title: Phishnet: Real-Time URL Analysis for Zero-Day Phishing Detection Using Ensemble Learning
authors: Gaurav Sarraf, Vibhor Pal
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The phishing attack is a matter that is continually threatening the internet users and could result into financial losses, identity theft and other security lapses. The success of the prevention of phishing URLs lies in the automated detection of these types of phishing. The research study demonstrates the new tool to detect phishing URLs and uses some of the Machine Learning (ML) tools. This study presents a powerful ML classification of phishing URLs, and it is based on the Web Page Phishing Detection Dataset. Two ensemble (V1) and baseline (V2) models were generated, which consist of combination of Random Forest (RF), XGBoost, LightGBM and CatBoost and Decision Tree (DT), Naive Bayes (NB) and Logistic Regression (LR) respectively. The performance assessment based on accuracy (Acc), precision (Prec) and recall (Rec), F1-score, AUROC, Cohen Kappa and MCC proves the superiority, with 95.32% accuracy and balanced scores in all metrics of V1, compared to classic classifiers, such as DT, LR, Multinomial Naive Bayes (MNB) and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN). The strength and generalization ability of the offered model is testified by the ablation experiments on the voting weights and learning rates, as well as the scaling comparisons. The framework provides a functional, efficient, and reliable system of real-time phishing detection, which has a huge practical aspect of cybersecurity defense enhancement and is scalable.

title: Scalable Smart City Analytics Through SmartFusion-Grid: A Unified Data Engineering Approach
authors: Sai Santhosh Reddy Bhimu
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Urban areas have large amounts of heterogeneous data on different domains like air quality, traffic, and crime, and analyzing this data in real-time is a complex problem. The traditional approach to data analysis is not effective in dealing with large amounts of data, heterogeneous data, and data changing over time because they are not highly scalable, highly adaptable, and focus on a single domain. Keeping this in view, this research proposes a highly scalable data engineering system called SmartFusion-Grid, which is designed to integrate different data sources to achieve dynamic risk forecasting and decision support. The model effectively utilizes data preprocessing, recursive feature selection, and an ensemble-based classification method, where accuracy and computational efficiency trade-offs occur. It includes a real-time risk alert system, where risk conditions are detected and alerts are immediately sent out. The analysis of the proposed model suggests that it is highly effective and outperforms existing models, achieving high accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score of 96.6 %. It was validated that the proposed model can be an effective for improving real-time urban risk monitoring, scalability, and predictive integrity to provide a powerful platform for smart city analytics intelligence and sustainability.

title: Event-Driven Financial Close Automation Using Universal Journal and Embedded Analytics in SAP S/4HANA
authors: Kajendran Jayaram, Maheshwar Gourishetti
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: This paper will analyze the effects of automation of event-driven financial close, through the Universal Journal and embedded analytics in the SAP S/4HANA. Conventional financial close procedures are usually time consuming, full of reconciliation and most likely to contain manual errors. SAP S/4HANA allows sustained accounting and close processes via real-time event triggers, integrated data model, and in-built analysis tools. The study is a mixed-method study, which takes a combination of quantitative measurement of performance and qualitative information of finance and SAP experts. The results show that the duration of close cycle time and the amount of work spent on reconciliation decreased considerably, and the error rate and the quality of data became better, as well as audit preparation and real-time visibility. With embedding analytics, exception handling is proactive during the process of decision support and close cycle. The paper concludes that event-driven automation is a fundamental paradigm shift in the financial close process, which allows quicker, more correct, and transparent financial reporting and redefines the role of the finance function as a more strategically oriented and value-oriented one.

title: Half Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)

title: Deconstructing Prompt Strategies: Evaluating Generative AI for Latency, Relevance, and Depth in Domain-Specific Tasks
authors: Vishwa Bhatt, Divya Thakar, Jerry Cervantes-Fernandez, Soo Dong Kim, Mira Kim, Jennifer Kim Jin
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: This study investigates how prompt strategies influence the performance of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) models, specifically ChatGPT-4o and DeepSeek. To measure performance, we used four core domains—Business, Healthcare, Neuroscience, and Mathematics—as test areas. A dataset of 1,000 prompts was categorized into five distinct prompt types: Simple, Meta, ReAct, Self-Consistency, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). We evaluated 2,000 AI-generated responses using a structured rubric covering Relevance, Accuracy, Complexity, and Runtime; each scored on a 5-point scale. We trained models to classify prompt types and predict response quality using both the textual content of prompts and human-assigned evaluation scores. Machine learning models, including XGBoost classifiers and Random Forest regressors, were used to estimate response quality and identify relationships among evaluation metrics. Correlation analysis revealed weak to moderate associations between scoring dimensions, while the predictive models showed strong alignment with human evaluations. Findings reveal that ChatGPT-4o excels in general-purpose accuracy and speed, whereas DeepSeek demonstrates greater consistency and complexity in domain-specific tasks. These results emphasize the critical role of prompt design in shaping generative AI behavior. Establishing a rubric-aligned evaluation framework is essential as these tools continue to be deployed across instructional and professional settings. This work supports the development of reliable, human-centered evaluation methods for integrating generative AI into education, tutoring systems, and real-world applications.

title: Intelligent Decision-Making for Patent-Standard Collaborative Transformation in the Power Industry: An Explainable Multi-Task Learning Framework
authors: Sanshan Zhao, Xiaoman Qi, Yong Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Co-operative transformation at patent standard of quality has become a critical part in the power industry, with technological innovation becoming faster and regulatory frameworks requiring a more formalised match between the emerging technologies and formalised standards. This paper strives to create an intelligent, understandable, and multifunctional decision-support system that would improve the precision, openness, and dependability of processes of patent-standard transformation. Quantitative research was used, which was based on a simulated multi-dimensional dataset of real-world patent and standard properties, and three analytical tasks were individually modelled: conversion potential classification, recommendation of standard type, and matching technical direction, with a structured multi-level method of technique of analysis. The findings illustrate very high predictive accuracy of all tasks, based on an accuracy rate of 84.5% on conversion potential classification when using XGBoost Classifier and a near-perfect rate of 98 on the standard recommendation using CatBoost Classifier and a very low RMSE of 0.086 in technical similarity estimation using XGBoost Regression, to show that the proposed framework was very effective in capturing the multi-layered relationship between patent attributes and standards requirements. The research finds that explanatory and integrated multi-task analytical methods would play a significant role in enhancing decision-making in the process of patent-standard transformation. Further validation of the framework is suggested by having real patent datasets and expert-labelled data included in future work. The empirical implication of this research is that this model can assist the regulators, standard agencies, and other stakeholders in the industry through automation and reinforcement of patent examination and standard compliance. The main weakness of this study is that it uses synthetic data, which requires empirical testing to be extended to a broader range of generalisation.

title: Design and Development of High Efficient Multiport Bidirectional DC-DC Converter for Charging Applications
authors: Preethiga. R, Sailesh. S, Sabari Vashan. V, K. P. Suresh, K. Vishnu Murthy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This project presents the design, modeling, and simulation of a MATLAB/Simulink-based on Multiport Bidirectional DC–DC Converter System that enables coordinated energy exchange between two electric vehicles (EVs) and the utility grid. The proposed topology comprises two independent Multiport Bidirectional converter ports, each interfaced to an EV battery, and a common DC-link that connects to the grid through a grid-interface stage. Each port contains dedicated current and voltage control loops and a PWM-based switching stage; a supervisory control unit orchestrates coordinated multiport operation, DC-link voltage regulation, and seamless transitions between Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) and Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) modes. The control architecture combines inner-loop current regulators, outer-loop DC-link voltage controllers PWM generation and gating synchronization are implemented in Simulink to produce robust switching signals for the semiconductor devices while avoiding inter-port interference and ensuring proper dead-time and timing alignment. Each EV’s Battery Management System (BMS) is modeled to monitor SOC, voltage, current, and temperature, and to impose protection and charging limits that the supervisory controller respects. A central contribution is the systematic analysis of eight operational modes arising from different ON/OFF combinations of the DC source and the two EV batteries (e.g., source → both EVs, EV1 discharging while EV2 charging, both EVs discharging to grid, etc.). For each mode we analyze power-flow direction, control response, DC-link stability, current sharing, and transition logic. MATLAB/Simulink results demonstrate stable DC-link voltage regulation, coordinated power distribution, effective SOC management.

title: Analysis of Voltage Fluctuations in Photovoltaic Power Stations Considering Meteorological Factor Weights on Similar Days
authors: Kexin Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: The dominant degree of meteorological factors affecting the output of photovoltaic stations varies under different weather conditions. Directly analyzing the output voltage using meteorological factors cannot accurately feedback its fluctuations. Therefore, a study was conducted on the analysis of voltage fluctuations in photovoltaic stations considering days with similar weights of meteorological factors. After calculating the maximal information coefficient (MIC) coefficient of the photovoltaic power generation impact factor, use it as a weight parameter. Select the TOP-N with the highest similarity to the analyzed day based on the weight of meteorological factors as the similar day for voltage waveform analysis. Calculate the distribution probability of random variables composed of meteorological factors on similar days, fit the power generation of photovoltaic stations within a time window, and output the voltage time series under rated current by sliding the time window. In the test results, the analysis of the output voltage waveform of the photovoltaic power station has achieved a high degree of fitting with the actual implementation, with an R2 of 0.90 or above, which can accurately feedback the fluctuation state of the voltage.

title: VIBE Project Management: An Autonomous AI-Driven Framework for Intelligent Agile Project Orchestration
authors: Subramanian Palaniappan, Goutham Kacheru
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Traditional agile project management methodologies rely heavily on manual coordination, human intervention, and reactive problem-solving approaches. This paper introduces VIBE (Virtual Intelligence-Based Execution) Project Management, a novel paradigm that leverages artificial intelligence to autonomously orchestrate the complete agile project lifecycle. Drawing inspiration from the emerging concept of "vibe coding" in software development, VIBE Project Management transforms tickets and user stories from static data artifacts into dynamic entities within an intelligent ecosystem. We present Writlix, a production implementation of this framework, which demonstrates autonomous requirement capture through conversational interfaces, intelligent task assignment based on real-time capacity analysis, proactive escalation management, and adaptive monitoring systems. Our approach reduces manual project management overhead by approximately 60-75%, while maintaining adherence to established agile principles including INVEST criteria and Gherkin-format acceptance criteria. This paper details the architectural foundations, intelligent agent subsystems, and practical deployment considerations of VIBE Project Management, positioning it as a transformative approach for organizations seeking to optimize their agile workflows through intelligent automation.

title: Modeling and Control of a Two-Agent Aerial System for Dynamic Throw-and-Catch Maneuvers
authors: Aashish Sahu, R. Prasanth Kumar
abstract: Cooperative multi-agent aerial systems are transforming aerial logistics and manipulation by enabling drones to perform dynamic physical interactions. This paper presents the complete modeling and control of a two-quadcopter system executing a high-speed throw-and-catch maneuver. A high-fidelity MATLAB simulation couples a throwing unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) that launches a ballistic payload with a catching UAV that autonomously intercepts it. The main challenge lies in achieving reliable interception of a fast, unpowered projectile under severe dynamic and latency constraints. Conventional reactive-pursuit controllers, which continuously track the projectile, were found to be unstable and computationally infeasible. To overcome these limitations, a lightweight Predictive Intercept Controller (PIC) is proposed, which computes a single fixed spatio-temporal intercept point using the projectile’s ballistic model and commands a tuned Proportional–Derivative (PD) controller to reach it. Monte Carlo evaluations demonstrate a 93.3% interception success rate across randomized launch conditions, with mean spatial and temporal errors below 0.12 m and 40 ms, respectively. The results confirm that the proposed predictive–feedback framework is robust, repeatable, and suitable for real-time implementation on embedded UAV platforms.

title: GeoNBeats: Unified Spatio-Temporal Neural Basis Expansion for Air Quality Estimation in Sparse Sensor Networks
authors: Vaibhav Joshi, Kapil Kumar Meena, Arkopal Kishore Goswami
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Reliable estimation of urban air pollution remains difficult in cities where monitoring stations are few and unevenly distributed. This paper presents GeoNBeats, a unified spatio-temporal learning framework for estimating PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> and PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</inf> under sparse sensor conditions. The proposed approach treats space and time within a single learning formulation by embedding fixed station location information directly into a neural time-series model, rather than applying spatial interpolation as a separate step. GeoNBeats is built on the N-BEATS architecture and extends it to condition temporal predictions on static geographic context, allowing a single model to learn shared pollution dynamics across all stations. The framework is evaluated using hourly air quality and meteorological data from seven regulatory monitoring stations in Kolkata, India, and is tested on an unseen station to assess its ability to infer pollution levels at unmonitored locations. Results show that the model captures both temporal variability and spatial differences with good accuracy, achieving R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values of 0.76 for PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> and 0.81 for PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</inf>. It is computationally lightweight and does not use dense sensor networks or graph topologies, making it suited for data-constrained urban contexts. GeoNBeats offers a practical pathway toward city-wide air quality estimation using limited monitoring infrastructure.

title: Research on Grid Fault Detection Technology Based on BeiDou Technology
authors: Zhenwu Xie, Xiaoqian Liu, Yulei Jia, Yi Zhou, Xinlei Chen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Renewable Energy and Electrical Technology (ICREET)
abstract: This paper proposes a power grid fault detection method based on Beidou space-time synchronization technology. Combining high-precision timing with synchronized phasor measurement, this method can accurately locate and quickly identify distribution line faults. The system utilizes a dynamic time warping algorithm to extract transient waveform features, significantly improving the accuracy of fault diagnosis. The method is suitable for multi-point coordinated monitoring of medium-voltage distribution systems.

title: A Legally Trustworthy and Sustainable AI Framework for Automated Legal Systems
authors: Aarthy Jonathan Kennedy, Arun D Raj, A. Sivanandam, Krishna Kishore Padala, Chesha P. Chellappan, Sreeja B G
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The growing integration of artificial intelligence into the court systems has become a matter of serious concerns in terms of law, morality, and ecology. Current AI methods of automated legal uses are more interested in predictive quality and less concerned with regulation compliance, justice, and environmental influence. In this paper, the authors come up with a compliance-conscious machine intelligence system that would facilitate legally reliable and ecologically sound AI in automated legal systems. The framework directly incorporates the legal limitations, ethical governance structures, and environmental impact measures in the machine learning training and evaluation process. The model is proposed as a multi-dimensional evaluation program, which considers models in predictive performance, compliance with regulations, fairness, explainability, and energy efficiency. The experimental findings have shown that the offered approach greatly enhances the levels of legal compliance and ethical alignment but preserves the highly competitive predictive performance and minimizes the levels of energy consumption and carbon emissions, in comparison with traditional models. These results indicate that it is possible to implement AI systems, not just effective but legally justifiable, ethically accountable, and environmentally friendly in actual legal contexts.

title: DOTGraph: CLIP-Driven Feature Disentanglement and Optimal Transport based Graph Learning for Few-Shot Segmentation
authors: Shreya Biswas, Zhaozheng Yin
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Few-shot semantic segmentation aims to build robust models that segment unseen objects using only a few labeled examples. Existing FSS approaches, which rely on semantic feature matching, often suffer from Background Bias, Pose-Scale Discrepancy Bias, and the inability to capture fine object details. These limitations hinder their ability to generalize to novel categories, especially in scenarios with high intra-class variability and fine-grained object structures. To overcome these challenges, we propose DOTGraph, a novel framework that incorporates CLIP-driven feature Disentanglement and Optimal Transport-based Graph learning for robust few-shot segmentation. We evaluate DOTGraph on PASCAL-5<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sup> and COCO-20<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sup>, achieving state-of-the-art performance with improvements in various few-shot settings. Our results demonstrate that DOTGraph effectively mitigates Background Bias, improves feature alignment, and enhances fine-grained segmentation. Code is available at https://github.com/shreyab1111/DOTGraph.

title: ICONIC-444: A 3.1-Million-Image Dataset for OOD Detection Research
authors: Gerhard Krumpl, Henning Avenhaus, Horst Possegger
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Current progress in out-of-distribution (OOD) detection is limited by the lack of large, high-quality datasets with clearly defined OOD categories across varying difficulty levels (near- to far-OOD) that support both fine- and coarse-grained computer vision tasks. To address this limitation, we introduce ICONIC-444 (Image Classification and OOD Detection with Numerous Intricate Complexities), a specialized large-scale industrial image dataset containing over 3.1 million RGB images spanning 444 classes tailored for OOD detection research. Captured with a prototype industrial sorting machine, ICONIC-444 closely mimics real-world tasks. It complements existing datasets by offering structured, diverse data suited for rigorous OOD evaluation across a spectrum of task complexities. We define four reference tasks within ICONIC-444 to benchmark and advance OOD detection research and provide baseline results for 22 state-of-the-art post-hoc OOD detection methods.

title: Agri-Aid: A Hybrid Embedded IoT-AI Framework for Smart Irrigation and Plant Disease Detection
authors: Balaji E, Giritharan S.S, T. V. Narmadha
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The lack of agricultural productivity is frequently caused by irregular irrigation, diseases in plants, and inhaled harmful gases which may influence not only crops, but also farmers. This paper introduces Agri-Aid, a cross-domain embedded system that implements an IoT sensing system with lightweight machine learning to offer real-time field surveillance and decision support. The platform will combine STM32-based soil and environmental measuring, ESP32-driven communication and support of LoRa, and ESP32-CAM to identify plant-disease on the device. The measurements of soil moisture, temperature, humidity, methane, carbon monoxide, and air-quality are picked along with the images of leaves, that are gathered in real-time. Disease detection at the edge is possible through a small convolutional model trained on both custom field samples and images of Plants. Agri-Aid automatically controls irrigation, sends environmental warning, and transfers data to cloud dashboards to be accessed remotely based on sensor patterns and model predictions. Field tests indicate the sensor works reliably in the field, can communicate over long distances, and is accurate in disease-classification with acceptable precision in small scale farms. The system is a cheap, convenient solution that assists on a healthy crop production, minimizes human labor and increases environmental consciousness in the rural farming atmosphere.

title: Dynamic Traffic Signal Control for Emergency Vehicle Prioritization
authors: Sandeep Waghmare, Jayashree Katti, Neha Karadkar
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Ambulance detection is a critical requirement for modern traffic management systems, especially in a crowded city where every minute of quick ambulance service can save a life. Traffic congestion, delayed clearance of signals, and dependency on manual intervention cause the ambulances to mostly reach their destinations beyond the scheduled time. Traditional detection methods such as siren-based or sensorbased systems suffer from limitations including environmental noise interference, short detection range, high installation costs, and maintenance complexity. These challenges highlight the need for an intelligent, automated, and infrastructure-independent solution that can reliably identify emergency vehicles in realtime using existing traffic surveillance systems. The system proposes the usage of a vision-based method involving real-time detection of ambulances using deep learning techniques. The method or system resizes and normalizes Video frames extracted from traffic cameras by a high-speed object detection system. This detection system has the ability to handle lighting conditions and partial occlusions. After an ambulance has been identified, an automatic traffic signal control system turns on the traffic signal to green after a brief interval. YOLOv8-based ambulance detection system achieved an accuracy of 94% with confidence scores ranging from 35-40%.

title: ProSeg-Net: A 2D and 3D U-Net Framework for Prostate MRI Segmentation
authors: Rithik Kumar, Vikas Mittal, Paurav Goel, Ayesha Siddiqha Mukthar
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The accurate segmentation of the prostate gland from MRI images is an important task in computer-assisted prostate cancer diagnosis. Deep learning methods like U-Net can achieve excellent results for prostate gland segmentation tasks. Nevertheless, there is further work needed to explore the reproducibility of these techniques in various dimensional setups. This paper presents ProSeg-Net, which is a unified method for prostate gland segmentation using 2D and 3D versions of the UNet model. The 2D U-Net architecture is employed for slice-based segmentation of the PROMISE12 data, while the 3D counterpart is employed for the volumetric segmentation of the NCI-ISBI 2013 data. Both architectures have undergone the same processing facilitating a fair comparison, where performance is measured by the Dice Similarity Coefficient. The experiment results have proven high accuracy in segmentation of the data sets with strong competitiveness, suggesting that U-Net models act effectively when implemented well. Experiment validation reveals that the proposed solution offers Dice Similarity Coefficient of 0.80 for PROMISE12 using 2D U-Net model and 0.81 for NCI-ISBI 2013 using 3D U-Net model. This work can be considered an open and practical benchmark, which can be used by researchers and healthcare practitioners looking for a trusted MRI segmentation pipeline based on conventional deep learning models.

title: Learning Action Hierarchies via Hybrid Geometric Diffusion
authors: Arjun Ramesh Kaushik, Nalini K. Ratha, Venu Govindaraju
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Temporal action segmentation is a critical task in video understanding, where the goal is to assign action labels to each frame in a video. While recent advances leverage iterative refinement-based strategies, they fail to explicitly utilize the hierarchical nature of human actions. In this work, we propose HybridTAS - a novel framework that incorporates a hybrid of Euclidean and hyperbolic geometries into the denoising process of diffusion models to exploit the hierarchical structure of actions. Hyperbolic geometry naturally provides tree-like relationships between embeddings, enabling us to guide the action label denoising process in a coarse-to-fine manner: higher diffusion timesteps are influenced by abstract, high-level action categories (root nodes), while lower timesteps are refined using fine-grained action classes (leaf nodes). Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets, GTEA, 50Salads, and Breakfast, demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance, validating the effectiveness of hyperbolic-guided denoising for the temporal action segmentation task.

title: Data-Driven Lipschitz Continuity: A Cost-Effective Approach to Improve Adversarial Robustness
authors: Erh-Chung Chen, Pin-Yu Chen, I-Hsin Chung, Che-Rung Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: As deep neural networks (DNNs) are increasingly deployed in sensitive applications, ensuring their security and robustness has become critical. A major threat to DNNs arises from adversarial attacks, where small input perturbations can lead to incorrect predictions. Recent advances in adversarial training improve robustness by incorporating additional examples from external datasets or generative models. However, these methods often incur high computational costs, limiting their practicality and hindering real-world deployment. In this paper, we propose a cost-efficient alternative based on Lipschitz continuity that achieves robustness comparable to models trained with extensive supplementary data. Unlike conventional adversarial training, our method requires only a single pass over the dataset without gradient estimation, making it highly efficient. Furthermore, our method can integrate seamlessly with existing adversarial training frameworks and enhances the robustness of models without requiring extra generative data. Experimental results show that our approach not only reduces computational overhead but also maintains or improves the defensive capabilities of robust neural networks. This work opens a promising direction for developing practical, scalable defenses against adversarial attacks.

title: Style-Friendly SNR Sampler for Style-Driven Generation
authors: Jooyoung Choi, Chaehun Shin, Yeongtak Oh, Heeseung Kim, Jungbeom Lee, Sungroh Yoon
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Recent text-to-image diffusion models generate high-quality images but struggle to learn new styles, which limits the personalized content creation. In response, style-driven generation has become a popular task, wherein users supply reference images capturing the target style, complemented by text prompts that specify stylistic cues. Fine-tuning is a common approach, yet it often blindly utilizes pre-training configurations without modification, especially for noise schedules defined in terms of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), which determines the amount of image information available at each denoising step. We discover that stylistic features predominantly emerge at low SNR range, leading current fine-tuning methods using regular noise schedules to exhibit suboptimal style alignment. We propose the Style-friendly SNR sampler, which focuses the fine-tuning on low SNR range where stylistic features emerge. We demonstrate improved generation of novel styles that cannot be described solely with a text prompt, enabling high-fidelity personalized content creation.

title: Detection-Driven Object Count Optimization for Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
authors: Oz Zafar, Yuval Cohen, Lior Wolf, Idan Schwartz
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurately controlling object count in text-to-image generation remains a key challenge. Supervised methods often fail, as training data rarely covers all count variations. Methods that manipulate the denoising process to add or remove objects can help; however, they still require labeled data, limit robustness and image quality, and rely on a slow, iterative process.Pre-trained differentiable counting models that rely on soft object density summation exist and could steer generation, but employing them presents three main challenges: (i) they are pre-trained on clean images, making them less effective during denoising steps that operate on noisy inputs; (ii) they are not robust to viewpoint changes; and (iii) optimization is computationally expensive, requiring repeated model evaluations per image.We propose a new framework that uses pre-trained object counting techniques and object detectors to guide generation. First, we optimize a counting token using an outer-loop loss computed on fully generated images. Second, we introduce a detection-driven scaling term that corrects errors caused by viewpoint and proportion shifts, etc., without requiring backpropagation through the detection model. Third, we show that the optimized parameters can be reused for new prompts, removing the need for repeated optimization. Our method provides efficiency through token reuse, flexibility via compatibility with various detectors, and accuracy with improved counting across diverse object categories.

title: Deep Learning–Driven Intelligent System for Early and Accurate Skin Cancer Diagnosis
authors: Monelli Ayyavaraiah, Dasari Sreenath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Skin cancer, particularly melanoma, poses a significant global health challenge, and early diagnosis remains crucial for improving survival rates. Traditional dermatoscopic examination, though effective, is prone to inter-observer variability and limited scalability. In this work, we propose a deep learning–driven intelligent system for early and accurate skin cancer diagnosis that integrates image-based and patient-level information. The framework employs a U-Net++ segmentation module to isolate lesion regions, followed by a hybrid CNN– Transformer backbone to jointly model local texture features and global contextual dependencies. To further enhance diagnostic precision, metadata fusion incorporates clinical attributes such as age, gender, and UV exposure. The classification layer leverages a combined weighted cross-entropy and focal loss function to address class imbalance, while Grad-CAM and SHAP explainability modules provide transparent and clinically interpretable predictions. Experiments conducted on the ISIC and HAM10000 datasets demonstrate that the proposed system achieves 95% accuracy, 0.94 F1-score, and 0.98 AUC, outperforming widely used baselines such as ResNet50, DenseNet121, and EfficientNet. The results highlight the robustness and clinical relevance of the framework, making it a promising tool for real-world dermatological applications.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Differential Privacy on the Reliability of Machine Learning Models: A Case Study on LendingClub Loan Data
authors: Ozgur Ardic, Gunes Aydindogan, Golnoosh Babaei, Paolo Giudici, Talu Karagoz
abstract: Machine learning (ML) models are increasingly used in peer-to-peer (P2P) lending to automate credit scoring and decision-making. However, concerns around model reliability including accuracy, fairness, explainability, robustness and data privacy remain critical barriers to trust these leading models. In this study, we examine how differential privacy (DP), a formal privacy-preserving framework, affects the reliability of ML models in the P2P lending context.

title: SAFE Metrics for Neural Inferences at the Edge
authors: Paolo Giudici, Alessandro Piergallini, Danilo Pietro Pau
abstract: The growth of Artificial Intelligence applications based on edge computing requires to develop risk management models that can balance opportunities with challenges, especially in high-stakes scenarios. In this paper, we contribute to the development of risk models presenting two integrated statistical metrics that can measure the “Accuracy” and the “Security” of Artificial Intelligence models developed at the edge. Developing Deep Neural Networks at the edge requires a lot of resources (e.g. time, money and knowledge), making them prized intellectual properties. Neural Network Watermarking (NNW) offers a valid technique to safeguard Neural Networks from malicious attacks. NNW refers to the process of inserting a secret mark into the weights of a NN. When watermarking NN it is important to assess both the performance and the robustness of watermark models under various conditions. This to give to the stakeholders the freedom to choose the strength of the watermark. These contributions aim to foster safer deployment of AI technologies at the edge.

title: Sentiment Analysis of Public Feedback on Government Schemes using Social Media
authors: Yesaswini Arikatla, T. Lakshmi Surekha, Hemalatha Jonnala, Swetha Talluri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Public sentiment is an essential part of many governments in analyzing their welfare programs effectiveness. This study introduces a transformer-based system that detects public sentiment through social media feedback about the various government schemes, like the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) program data that researchers collected from YouTube and Twitter. The system divides every comment and post into three distinct sentiment categories which include positive, neutral, and negative categories. To address dataset imbalance, a data balancing method is used to correct the dataset imbalance problem because it helps to present minority groups more accurately while achieving unbiased results. The system uses a BERT-based transformer model which performs multi-class sentiment analysis through text normalization and subword tokenization methods. Stratified sampling methods is used to conduct both model training and evaluation activities. The experimental results show that class imbalance treatment leads to better macro F1-score results which enable more equitable performance ratings across different sentiment groups. The trained model operates through a Flask-based API which connects to a web dashboard that provides instant sentiment assessment and trend observation and data presentation. The proposed system shows how contextual transformer architectures work for monitoring public sentiment at large scale and backing data-based governance operations.

title: Style Mate: A Comparative Analysis of Outfit Compatibility Predictor in Fashion Technology Employing Machine Learning Models
authors: Shivaprasad B R, Sudesh Rao, Jayanthi M G, Prasad Krishna Murthy, Murulikrishna H, Prashanth Kannadaguli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The e-commerce industry is quickly evolving because of the AI implementation. The AI intelligence is required to predict an intelligent outfit suggestion to improve the customer experience and it also enhances the customer personalization. The approaches for Machine learning are very effective to dominate the fashion recommendation problems. This study evaluates performances of 4 classical machine learning technique - XGBoost, Random Forest (RF), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Naïve Bayes - in analyzing the outfit compatibility between clothing items like pants and shirts.. A collected resources was pre-processed and feature engineered with visual indicators such as color histogram, texture and shape embeddings. The models are trained and validated while performances are evaluated through Accuracy, Precision, Recall, ROC- AUC curve. Comparative analysis shows that XGBoost is highly suitable for fashion recommended systems, continuously outperforming Random Forest and KNN in terms of classification accuracy and generalization.

title: Standardizing and Facilitating Machine Learning Model Performance Comparison with the UFLAS Framework
authors: Henrique Menezes, Kalinka Castelo Branco
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Comparing the performance of Machine Learning (ML) models across different studies presents a persistent challenge in network security research due to inconsistent evaluation practices and dataset partitioning. These discrepancies frequently undermine the reliability and reproducibility of reported metrics. To address this issue, we present UFLAS: the Unified test Framework for machine Learning-based Attack identification Systems. UFLAS provides an automated, end-to-end software pipeline for the standardized training, testing, and objective comparison of ML models in network attack detection tasks. By harmonizing data loading, splitting, and balancing, the framework enables researchers to benchmark models fairly and reproducibly. Experimental results on benchmark intrusion detection datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of UFLAS in facilitating robust model ranking, though findings also highlight the ongoing necessity of careful validation to avoid overfitting and ensure real-world applicability. UFLAS thus aims to advance the reliability and comparability of ML research in cybersecurity, laying the groundwork for reproducible evaluation practices and future extension to additional domains

title: RemEdit: Efficient Diffusion Editing with Riemannian Geometry
authors: Eashan Adhikarla, Brian D. Davison
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Controllable image generation is fundamental to the success of modern generative AI, yet it faces a critical trade-off between semantic fidelity and inference speed. The RemEdit diffusion-based framework addresses this trade-off with two synergistic innovations. First, for editing fidelity, we navigate the latent space as a Riemannian manifold. A mamba-based module efficiently learns the manifold’s structure, enabling direct and accurate geodesic path computation for smooth semantic edits. This control is further refined by a dual-SLERP blending technique and a goal-aware prompt enrichment pass from a Vision-Language Model. Second, for additional acceleration, we introduce a novel task-specific attention pruning mechanism. A lightweight pruning head learns to retain tokens essential to the edit, enabling effective optimization without the semantic degradation common in content-agnostic approaches. RemEdit surpasses prior state-of-the-art editing frameworks while maintaining real-time performance under 50% pruning. Consequently, RemEdit establishes a new benchmark for practical and powerful image editing. Source code: github.com/eashanadhikarla/RemEdit.

title: A Low-Cost Smart IoT-Enabled Multimodal Sanitation System for Health Monitoring and Urinary–GI Disorders Detection via Bio-Waste Analysis
authors: Purba Pal, Chandril Mallick, Smita Das, Rik Mandal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Many rural and low-income communities lack affordable, continuous health screenings. Poor sanitation and slow diagnosis let infections and chronic diseases spread. This paper describes a low-cost Internet of Things (IoT) sanitation system that analyzes urine and stool in real-time to support health monitoring and early detection of urinary and gastrointestinal problems. The platform uses cheap sensors and open-source micro-controllers. It collects data without direct contact, sends it securely over wireless links, and stores it in the cloud for analysis. Machine learning examines the signals to spot small, early changes and issue alerts. The model achieved an overall system accuracy of 94%, with an average precision of 75%, average recall of 73%, and an F1-score of approximately 74%. The system protects user anonymity and is designed to use little power. It is built to scale, work with other systems, and be easy to use in rural homes and elder-care settings. The goal is to enable prevention, reduce pressure on clinics, and help public-health responses.

title: Improved Flood Management and Risk Communication Through LLM
authors: Imaya Ranaweera, Susara S Thenuwara, H Waruna H Premachandra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: This paper presents a multimodal flood decision-support system that combines Large Language Models (LLMs), a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline, and a Flood Knowledge Graph (FKG) for operational flood risk communication. The system ingests GIS layers, real-time hydrological sensor streams, meteorological forecasts, and social media posts into a Neo4j-based FKG, which is then queried by a RAG framework to ground LLM outputs in current and historically validated flood conditions. A human in the loop governance layer validates critical messages before dissemination, ensuring that generated alerts, summaries, and evacuation suggestions remain traceable and policy compliant. The hydrological forecasting component achieved a Nash Sutcliffe Efficiency (NSE) of 0.89, while knowledge graph grounding reduced factual hallucinations in LLM responses by 78.6% compared to a text-only baseline. The integrated system further supported dynamic risk classification and real-time visualization of optimized evacuation routes. These results demonstrate that the proposed architecture can transform raw, heterogeneous flood data into reliable, actionable intelligence for emergency managers, moving beyond generic chat responses toward deployable, decision-grade flood early warning and response.

title: Multiple-RIS-Aided Predictable V2V Communications in Graph Structure with Doppler Spread Suppression
authors: Xue Wang, Ruixin Cao, Dongchan Sun
abstract: Doppler spread and multipath propagation leads to delay spread cause severe phase and waveform distortion of the transmitted signal in the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) system. Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) can significantly mitigate phase shifts and channel fading by leveraging their reflective elements. Meanwhile, vehicular networking communication must address various factors such as Doppler effects, multipath delay spread, and received power. Multi-objective optimization incorporating RIS enables a comprehensive balance among these performance metrics. In this paper, we model the RIS-aided network as a graph structure within a multi-objective optimization framework by integrating graph theory, where graph information represents the physical characteristics of RIS. We formulate four objective functions for Doppler spread, delay spread, received power, and system energy efficiency, and reconstruct four objective functions with phase as the core. We construct a multi-objective optimization model based on graph Laplacian regularization and present an improved Graph-NSGA-II algorithm to efficiently solve the optimization problem. Simulation results demonstrate that the Graph-NSGA-II algorithm in multi-RIS cooperative systems achieves significantly reduced computational complexity and improved communication quality and phase optimization while maintaining a low Bit Error Rate (BER).

title: Autonomous Causal Discovery: Evaluating LLMs' Priors and Constraint Strategies for Reliability
authors: Lyuzhou Chen, Xiangyu Wang, Taiyu Ban, Derui Lyu, Qinrui Zhu, Xin Wang, Huanhuan Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Expert-guided Causal Structure Learning (CSL) incorporates prior knowledge to improve the accuracy of causal discovery, yet the acquisition of such knowledge is often restricted by the availability of human experts. While Large Language Models (LLMs) provide an alternative source of causal priors, LLM-derived knowledge can be inconsistent with the true causal structure due to hallucinations or contextual misinterpretations. This paper introduces a structural constraint measurement framework, which defines constraint strength and constraint quality to describe reliability and effectiveness, enabling a systematic evaluation of LLM-derived constraints. Using this framework, we evaluate five categories of structural constraints: Edge Existence (EEC), Edge Forbidden (EFC), Path Existence (PEC), Path Forbidden (PFC), and Order Constraints (OC). Our theoretical and empirical analyses demonstrate that while EEC offers high constraint strength, it exhibits low quality when derived from LLMs; conversely, PFC and OC provide a balanced trade-off between search-space pruning and reliability. Building on these insights, we propose a two-level CSL optimization framework that partitions the search space by node order and refines the structure using global path constraints. The results show that this framework provides an effective way to incorporate noisy LLM-derived priors into CSL, particularly in settings where expert knowledge is limited.

title: Electric Vehicles Impact on the Power Grid: A Review of Challenges, V2G Integration, and AI-Based Control Strategies
authors: Sahil V. Bansod, Rudresh P. Kadu, Madhuri A. Chaudhari
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The increasing use of electric vehicles (EVs) is changing travel patterns and, by extension, has significant implications for the electricity supply system. One of the most important issues related to EVs is their effect on the electrical network when a large number of vehicles are connected at the same time for charging. Although EVs help in lowering greenhouse gas emissions, their sudden demand surge can cause circuit overload. The transmission lines can be loaded with additional demand, especially during the evening peak hours. The voltage levels can show erratic variations if the charging is not managed. These issues are more probable to occur in the local distribution network than in the national network. The charging patterns of consumers show immediate charging as soon as they arrive at work, rather than managing the demand over time. This paper focuses on the implications of EV charging on the local network, particularly on the weaknesses of the local power grids. It covers charging stations, current regulation methods, stability issues, and harmonic distortion. In addition, it explores the idea of vehicle-to-grid (V2G) power flow and artificial intelligence-based smart charging systems. The paper integrates the latest research to identify the key operational issues and their management strategies necessary for the stable and sustainable integration of electric vehicles into the electricity supply system.

title: $\text{MoS}_{2}$ /Barium Ferrite Nanocomposite: a Synergistic Electrode for Advanced Supercapacitor Applications
authors: Shivam Kumar Mittal, Udeshwari Jamwal, K. L. Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this work, a novel multidimensional 2D/0D nanocomposite based on flower-like molybdenum disulfide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MoS}_{2}$</tex>) and barium ferrite (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{BaFe}_{12} \mathrm{O}_{19}$</tex>) nanoparticles was developed via one-step hydrothermal method for high-performance supercapacitor applications. The incorporation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \text{wt} \% \text{BaFe}_{12} \mathrm{O}_{19}$</tex> into the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MoS}_{2}$</tex> matrix significantly enhanced the electrochemical performance by improving electrical conductivity, redox activity, and structural stability. The MS-BFO10 electrode exhibited a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{s}}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$215 ~\mathrm{F} ~\mathrm{g}^{-1}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \text{mV} \mathrm{s}{}^{-1}$</tex>. Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy revealed reduced charge transfer resistance, while long-term cycling at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \text{Ag} \mathrm{g}^{-1}$</tex> showed outstanding stability with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 104 \%$</tex> capacitance retention and nearly 100 % coulombic efficiency over 5,000 cycles. Kinetic analysis confirmed that the charge storage mechanism is primarily capacitive in nature, supported by a high b-value of 0.91 and a capacitive contribution of up to 92 % at higher scan rates. These findings highlight the synergistic effect of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MoS}_{2} / \text{BaFe}_{12} \mathrm{O}_{19}$</tex> architecture, offering a promising strategy for designing advanced supercapacitor electrode materials with superior energy storage performance and long-term durability.

title: Impact Assessment of Fast DC EV Charging Station Integration in an Islanded Microgrid Using a Node Sensitivity-Based Approach
authors: Amrutha Raju Battula, Sagar Babu Mitikiri, Dushyant Sharma, Vedantham Lakshmi Srinivas
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study proposes a node-sensitivity-based methodology to leverage fast DC electric vehicle (EV) charging stations for enhancing power-system stability during grid-to-island transitions. The IEEE 14-bus system, modeled in PSS®E, serves as the testbed to evaluate the dynamic role of EV charging stations as auxiliary power sources. An analytical framework incorporating the load-reduction effectiveness (LRE) and enhanced load-impact sensitivity index (ELISI) identifies critical nodes vulnerable to voltage and frequency instability. The study also evaluates vehicle-to-grid (V2G) operation, wherein EV charging stations act as temporary power supplies during islanding events. Simulations show that strategically integrated EV charging stations improve transient stability and reduce system stress during grid disturbances. The results indicate a viable pathway for EV charging infrastructure to provide ancillary services, informing planning for resilience and emergency response.

title: Subsynchronous Oscillations Identification in Series-Compensated DFIG-Based Wind Farm Using Machine Learning Algorithms: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Sameer P. Meshram, Jawaharlal Bhukya, Soumyabrata Das
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In wind-integrated power grids, sub-synchronous oscillations (SSOs) are gaining attention due to their potential impact on system stability, particularly following several reported incidents in the United States and China. These events are primarily associated with the interplay between seriescompensated transmission lines and power electronic converters. SSOs arise when two or more components within the power system (PS) exchange energy at frequencies less than the nominal system frequency. The integration of wind power plants (WPPs) introduces additional complexities, making SSO one of the critical challenges in ensuring system stability. Notably, the SSO phenomena observed in real-world wind farms (WFs) are not yet fully addressed in the conventional SSO literature. This article offers an extensive review of the traditional classifications of SSOs as defined by IEEE, proposes an updated classification framework based on recent research developments, and evaluates various SSO monitoring and identification techniques. Furthermore, the limitations of existing methods are discussed. The paper aims to provide a practical perspective and outline future research directions, assisting PS planners and operators in achieving secure, economical, and sustainable grid operations.

title: Estimating Power System Inertia: A Study with Renewable Energy Integrated Power Networks
authors: Nitisha Sudhir Darwade, S.T. Nagarajan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: It is widely known that, with adoption of Renewable Energy Sources (RES) in the power system, overall inertia of system decreases. Continuous monitoring of system inertia is essential to prevent the failure of protection schemes and components during low system inertia. This paper summarizes the formulas used for calculation of inertia in power system. Further, it explains quantum of reduction in inertia with numerical calculation, when RES are integrated in the system. In addition, it proposes simple approach for calculation of synthetic inertia to be provided in presence of RES. The proposed approach for calculation of reduced inertia or synthetic inertia has been verified based on IEEE benchmark models.

title: Asymmetrical Dc-Link Voltage Based 51-Level Multilevel Inverter Topology with Less Switch Count
authors: Uday Patel, Siddharthsingh K. Chauhan, P. N. Tekwani
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: A multilevel inverter (MLI) topology based on, both, symmetrical (SYM) and asymmetric (ASYM) dc-link voltages is proposed in this paper. The proposed MLI topology generates 11-level output voltage under SYM dc-link conditions. By employing ASYM dc-link voltages, the same topology produces output voltage levels ranging from 13 to 51, thereby offering enhanced voltage resolution and improved output voltage waveform quality. The number of power semiconductor controlled switches, driver circuits, dc voltage sources, diodes, capacitors, and the total number of components in the proposed MLI topology are compared with those in recently published MLI topologies. The performance of the proposed MLI topology is evaluated using MATLAB. The results show that proposed MLI topology produces a higher voltage level with reduced total harmonic distortion. Additionally, the proposed topology provides a stable output voltage under various dynamic conditions. The multiple dc-link voltage sources of the proposed topology makes it a good choice for photovoltaic applications, microgrids, and also gridtied systems.

title: Five-Level Fault-Tolerant Inverter with Preserved Output Under Multiple Switch Failure
authors: Prajesh Niranjan, Vimlesh Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: As the demand for multi-level inverters (MLIs) increases for high-power applications, the number of electronic switches required by consumers who use these MLIs exceeds that of the conventional 2-level inverter. These semiconductor devices must be protected against any faults to ensure the continuity of the power supply. A new Fault-Tolerant (FT) MLI is proposed in this paper that can overcome both single and multiple opencircuit switch faults while preserving post-fault output power. The proposed inverter is tested over dynamic load conditions and on different modulation indices. The superior features of the proposed FT-MLI are lower voltage stress, less power loss, and higher efficiency, which fit best for industrial and consumer electronics-based applications. The proposed FT topology is tested on the SIMULINK tool and validated experimentally, followed by the comparative analysis. PLECS software is used to calculate the power loss and efficiency.

title: Solar-Powered Boost Converter Using PID Control for Efficient Speed Regulation of a BLDC Motor and Real-Time MPPT
authors: Mohit Singh, Naushad Hussain, Madhu Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Environmental concerns have led to an expansion in the implementation of renewable energy for a range of applications. The most viable solution appears to be photovoltaic (PV) technology, which converts sunlight into electricity and is an environmentally friendly option. The energy required to operate the BLDC motor is obtained from the solar panel. The speed control method for Permanent Magnet-based BLDC (PM-BLDC) motors is presented in this paper, which combines a P&O-based algorithm and Incremental conductance (Inc) with a PID controller. This method achieves robust speed regulation in changing operational conditions by combining the precision of PID control with the adaptive characteristics of Perturb and Observe (P&O) and Inc. algorithms. The energy required to operate the BLDC motor is sourced from the solar panel. However, the voltage output of a solar panel is not always constant. Therefore, a boost converter is necessary, as the solar panel's voltage may be insufficient to operate the BLDC motor. The converter elevates the voltage required to operate a BLDC motor.

title: Dual Parametric Fractional Controller for Unstable Fractional Order Processes
authors: Rammurti Meena, Sudipta Chakraborty, Vipin Chandra Pal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Recent studies in control theory have highlighted the significant interest in fractional order (FrO) plants with dead time. However, tuning fractional order controllers presents challenges due to the numerous tunable parameters and the lack of systematic tuning methods. Controlling unstable industrial processes with dead time is particularly challenging. Control structures incorporating dual-loop configuration have demonstrated greater effectiveness than conventional proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controllers in unity feedback loops for such processes. This paper presents a Smith-predictor (SP) based dual-loop control structure for FrO unstable processes with time delays. A key advantage of this design is its ability to be analytically tuned based on defined maximum sensitivity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M_{s}$</tex>) and phase-margin (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\phi_{m}$</tex>) specifications. The effectiveness of the proposed design is assessed by testing it with altered plant models, and then quantifiable performance indicators are derived. The findings indicate that the proposed design provides a more robust and improved closed-loop response compared to other contemporary approaches.

title: LTO Battery Fading Prediction Using Deep Learning
authors: Vaibhav Mandhyan, Darshan R, Sanka Saketh Ram, Sreenu Sreekumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The high thermal stability, rapid charge/discharge capability, and extended lifespan of Lithium Titanate Oxide (LTO) batteries enhanced their applicability in electric vehicles, grid storage, and other high-demand applications. However, LTO cells experience gradual capacity fading with repeated charge-discharge cycling, like other lithium battery variants. This impacts the overall performance and reliability. This necessitates accurate degradation behaviour prediction for developing intelligent battery management systems and ensuring operational safety. Therefore, this paper proposes deep learning models for forecasting the capacity fading trend in LTO batteries. Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), and four hybrid architectures: LSTM-GRU, LSTM-CNN, LSTM-GRU-CNN, and CNNGRU, are implemented for capacity fading forecasting. These models are trained and validated using real-world battery cycling data, and their performance is assessed based on metrics like Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Root Mean Square Error (RMSE). The comparative analysis highlights the strengths and trade-offs of each architecture, providing valuable guidance for selecting the most suitable models in practical battery health prediction scenarios.

title: Hybrid BO-LSBoost Model for Accurate Prediction of Remaining Useful Life of Li-Ion Battery
authors: Shivi Varshney, Bhavnesh Kumar, Alok Prakash Mittal
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Estimating the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) of lithium-ion batteries is important in maintenance scheduling, safety evaluations, and applications like electric vehicles or renewable energy storage systems. We present a Hybrid Bayesian Optimization - Least Squares Boosting (BO - LSBoost) model to estimate RUL; in addition, the hybrid framework utilizes an original feature selection methodology that combines mutual information (MI), statistically-ranked feature importance, and synthetic features based on battery voltage time series that provide additional predictive capacity and improve RUL estimation accuracy. In addition, Bayesian Optimization is used to optimize the hyperparameters of the LSBoost to reduce prediction error. The combined BO - LSBoost models has improved RMSE of 1.3210 compared to the baseline LSBoost RMSE of 4.2959. This work shows that the BO - LSBoost has greater prediction accuracy while being less computationally intensive than other learning models such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Artificial Neural Network (ANN). Overall, the BO - LSBoost architecture provides accurate, custom, and generalized estimates of RUL that could improve the functionality of Battery Management Systems (BMS) for grid scale Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS).

title: Indirect Field-Oriented Control of a Single-Phase Induction Motor Fed by a Three-Phase Inverter
authors: Boikuntha Bikash Buragohain, Parthajit Dutta, Soumya Samanta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design, modeling, and simulation of an Indirect Field-Oriented Control (IFOC) scheme for a Single-Phase Induction Motor (SPIM) which is fed from a three-phase inverter to eliminate the need of the capacitor. A three-phase inverter emulates a balanced three-phase supply to the inherently asymmetric SPIM, allowing it to be controlled like a symmetric machine and making it suitable for lowpower electric vehicle applications. Clarke and Park transformations are employed to achieve decoupled torque and flux control. Additionally, a flux weakening mechanism is integrated with speed and current regulation. The rotor angle and slip speed are estimated mathematically, eliminating the need for flux sensors. The complete control strategy is implemented in MATLAB/Simulink, and the dynamic performance is validated through simulation. Simulation results confirm rapid dynamic response and improved torque smoothness under varying load conditions.

title: Air-to-Ground Covert Communication With Location and Interference Uncertainty
authors: Hongchi Chen, Junsheng Mu, Na Deng, Haichao Wei, Nan Zhao
abstract: As uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) are widely used in many communication scenarios, the issue of security has also caused much concern due to the line-of-sight propagation. Therefore, in this paper, we study an air-to-ground covert communication system, where a UAV transmitter Alice transmits messages covertly to a ground receiver Bob under the communication behavior detection of a ground warden Willie with location uncertainty and concurrent interference from other ground environmental nodes whose locations follow a two-dimensional Poisson point process. Under this setup, we first provide the approximate probability distribution of the aggregated interference power from all environmental co-channel nodes to facilitate the covert analysis. Then, we derive the average covert probability separately for two cases: case 1 assumes that Willie knows the received power from Alice; case 2 assumes that Willie knows the probability distribution of the received power from Alice. Next, we derive the connection probability and the covert throughput which is the maximal transmission rate under the covertness and reliability constraints. Numerical results demonstrate the feasibility of air-to-ground covert communication with location and interference uncertainty. The results also show the average covert probability and covert throughput in case 2 are higher than that in case 1, especially for a sparse deployment of interfering nodes.

title: PCCNA: A Probabilistic Caching Strategy Based on Strong Content Consistency and Node Attributes
authors: Wenjing Jing, Quan Zheng, Chaoming Huang, Shuangwu Chen, Xiaobin Tan, Jian Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: In response to the growing challenges posed by excessive redundant traffic in cache-enabled networks (cache-enabled networks), this paper presents a novel probabilistic caching strategy termed PCCNA (Probabilistic Caching based on Content Consistency and Node Attributes). A key innovation of PCCNA is its incorporation of strong content consistency between caching proxies and backend servers into the caching decision-making process, addressing a critical gap in existing caching strategies. Unlike traditional approaches that often focus on a singular optimization criterion, PCCNA integrates multiple factors such as content popularity within the network and the centrality of caching proxies based on their attributes. By adaptively computing the caching probability for content of varying popularity while ensuring strong consistency, PCCNA enhances the efficiency of in-network caching. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that PCCNA outperforms established caching strategies in terms of cache hit ratio, server load, and user service latency across diverse network topologies. This research provides significant insights into the development of effective caching mechanisms, paving the way for optimized resource utilization and improved user experiences in cache-enabled networks environments.

title: A Hybrid Numerical Simulation–Data Driven Modeling Framework for Municipal Solid Waste Incineration Digital Twin Temperature Prediction
authors: Junfei Qiao, Jiabin Zhuang, Haixu Ding
abstract: High-fidelity computational fluid dynamics (CFD) models can accurately describe the complex thermo–chemical processes in municipal solid waste incineration (MSWI). However, their high computational cost makes them difficult to apply in online monitoring, prediction, and control of industrial incineration systems. To overcome this limitation, this article proposes a fast and dynamic MSWI modeling framework that combines physics-based simulation with reduced-order modeling and data-driven learning to enable efficient temperature field prediction. First, a mechanism-based numerical model of a mechanical grate MSWI furnace is developed by coupling solid-phase waste combustion on the grate with gas-phase combustion simulated in Fluent, generating physically consistent temperature field data. Second, considering mechanism constraints, industrial controllability, and computational cost, key operating variables are selected and an orthogonal experimental design is employed to construct a representative simulation dataset. Third, proper orthogonal decomposition is applied to reduce the dimensionality of high-dimensional temperature fields, extracting dominant thermal modes and corresponding modal coefficients. Finally, an improved interval type-2 fuzzy neural network is used to establish the nonlinear mapping between industrial operating variables and temperature modal coefficients, enabling rapid temperature field reconstruction and short-term prediction. Simulation results show that the proposed method can effectively predict the dynamic evolution of the furnace temperature field within a 3 s horizon after operating condition changes, while reducing the full-field reconstruction time to approximately 10 s. The proposed framework significantly improves computational efficiency while preserving physical consistency, providing a practical solution for online modeling and intelligent operation of MSWI systems.

title: Challenges and Research Directions for Large Language Model Inference Hardware_supp1-3652916.pdf
authors: David Patterson

title: GSDeformer: Direct, Real-time and Extensible Cage-based Deformation for 3D Gaussian Splatting_supp1-3687062.pdf
authors: Hongchuan YU
abstract: We present GSDeformer, a method that enables cage-based deformation on 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS). Our approach bridges cage-based deformation and 3DGS by using a proxy point-cloud representation. This point cloud is generated from 3D Gaussians, and deformations applied to the point cloud are translated into transformations on the 3D Gaussians. To handle potential bending caused by deformation, we incorporate a splitting process to approximate it. Our method does not modify or extend the core architecture of 3D Gaussian Splatting, making it compatible with any trained vanilla 3DGS or its variants. Additionally, we automate cage construction for 3DGS and its variants using a render-and-reconstruct approach. Experiments demonstrate that GSDeformer delivers superior deformation results compared to existing methods, is robust under extreme deformations, requires no retraining for editing, runs in real-time, and can be extended to other 3DGS variants. Project Page: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://jhuangbu.github.io/gsdeformer/</uri>

title: A Diversity-Preserving Multistrategy Differential Evolution Algorithm for 3-D Uncrewed Aerial Vehicle Offline and Online Path Planning_supp1-3683527.pdf
authors: Zhenyu Meng
abstract: With the increasing adoption of metaheuristic algorithms in uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV) path planning, the problem has evolved into a challenging optimization task. To address offline and online planning under diverse terrains and multiple obstacle conditions, this article proposes a diversity-preserving multistrategy differential evolution-based method for 3-D path planning. The proposed algorithm integrates three key mechanisms. First, the multistage success rate-based adaptive strategy balances global exploration and local exploitation across evolutionary stages. Second, the stagnant individual replacement mechanism enables stagnant individuals to escape local optima, mitigating premature convergence. Third, the diversity maintenance mechanism preserves diversity within the top population, thereby strengthening global search capability. Furthermore, a benchmark suite with 26 terrain scenarios is designed to comprehensively evaluate performance. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms six state-of-the-art methods in generating superior 3-D UAV paths for both offline and online planning. Finally, tests on the CEC2013, CEC2014, and CEC2017 benchmarks confirm its competitive performance on numerical optimization.

title: Liver Couinaud Segmentation: A Review on Existing Segmentation Techniques of the Couinaud Classification of Liver_supp1-3623914.pdf
authors: Gajendra Kumar Mourya
abstract: Liver Couinaud segmentation is crucial in biomedical image processing for treatment planning and surgical interventions of the liver parenchyma. This is because the liver Couinaud segments help provide clinicians with a clearer and specific diagnosis of the location of the diseased areas within the liver tissue. Technological advancements in the form of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models have greatly enhanced the process of liver Couinaud segmentation. The efficiency of ML and DL models showcases their performance with incredible results. Hence, this article discusses the state-of-the-art techniques for liver Couinaud segmentation from 2006 to 2024. The segmentation techniques are divided into semi-automatic and automatic. Additionally, the online medical imaging datasets suitable for developing algorithms and frameworks for liver Couinaud segmentation are presented with their descriptions, advantages, and limitations. Furthermore, the performance metrics that are commonly used for evaluating the performance of segmentation algorithms are thoroughly discussed. Finally, the research challenges and recommendations are accentuated for future researchers who intend to bridge the highlighted gaps in liver Couinaud segmentation.

title: Quantum-Inspired Machine Learning for High-Dimensional Data Optimization
authors: Arpana Chaturvedi, Siddharth Shankar Mishra, S. Nagaraju, Amit Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: High dimensional data in machine learning (ML) however is not yet fully tapped due curse of dimensionality, sensitivity to local minima and computational burden. In this paper, we will introduce and test a new QIGS algorithm for such problems on classical computer. The efficiency of QIGS is demonstrated to be benefiting from the amplitude–phase hybrid probabilistic encoding and gradient-assisted update for optimizing entangling operations, which can effectively balance global exploration and local exploitation. We test this experiment on synthetic benchmark functions (Rastrigin, Rosenbrock, Ackley up to 5,000 dimensions) and real-world data sets (genomic profiles with 20,531 features and BERT-based sentiment embeddings with 768 features). It is also shown that QIGS achieves a classification accuracy of 94.2% on the genomic data (Adam: 91.5%, SGD: 90.7%, PSO: 89.8%) and reduces the number of iterations for convergence by 35–40% compared to fastest baseline on synthetic benchmarks. In addition, on the BERT representations QIGS got a training loss of 0.083 in 120 iterations (vs Adam's loss of 0.091 in 180 iterations). These results establish that QIGS is not only a faster converging algorithm, but also one which produces solutions of higher quality for many problems, thereby making it an accessible segue between classical optimization and the future of quantum-inspired machine learning.

title: Predicting Workplace Depressive Symptoms based on Work Limitation Questionnaire and Wearable-Derived Indicators
authors: Hiroaki Suzuki, Takemi Matsui, Nobutoshi Kariya, Yusuke Obara, Shohei Sato
abstract: Early detection of depression in the workplace is essential to enable timely support and mitigate productivity loss. The 25-item Work Limitations Questionnaire (WLQ-25), widely used by employers, correlates with depressive symptoms but is insufficient on its own for screening.
