We developed practical models that integrate self-report with wearable-derived physiology. Twenty office workers completed the WLQ-25 and the Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9; reference standard) and wore a wrist device for 24 h to obtain total sleep time (TST) and mean heart rate during the first 60 min after waking (HR post-waking). Multiple linear regression showed that adding TST and HR post-waking to the 8-item WLQ short form (WLQ-8) substantially improved PHQ-9 estimation (adjusted R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.68) compared with WLQ-8 alone (0.38). A model that used WLQ-25 instead of WLQ-8 achieved adjusted R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.75 (ΔR<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.07 versus the WLQ-8 model; nested F-test p = 0.0498). Higher WLQ scores, longer TST, and lower HR post-waking were associated with higher PHQ-9 scores. These patterns may reflect reduced morning activity and blunted autonomic reactivity. The proposed approach—combining a brief 8-item questionnaire with simple physiological indicators—offers a feasible primary screening tool for occupational health programs.

title: TranSight: Dual-Attention Spatio-Temporal Network for Anomaly Detection in Intelligent Transportation Surveillance
authors: Rakesh Kumar, Arvind, Manish Kumar Sharma, Sumeet Saurav, Sanjay Singh
abstract: Video anomaly detection (VAD) is crucial in intelligent transportation surveillance, enabling the automated identification of irregular or unsafe behaviours in complex traffic environments. Challenges such as illumination changes, occlusions, and scarce labelled data hinder the capture of finegrained spatio-temporal patterns. This paper presents TranSight, an unsupervised attention-guided spatio-temporal learning framework for anomaly detection in transportation videos. TranSight employs a multi-scale Wider-ResNet encoder for hierarchical spatial representation, a Bi-directional Temporal Shift Module (Bi-TSM) for efficient motion encoding, and a Temporal Transformer to model long-range dependencies. A dual-attention refinement scheme, combining Residual Channel Attention Blocks (RCABs) and a Spatial-Temporal Attention (STA) module, enhances discriminative motion cues while suppressing background redundancy. The framework is optimized using a multi-loss strategy integrating pixel-level, gradient-based, perceptual, and contrastive objectives, balancing reconstruction fidelity with anomaly sensitivity. On the UCSD Ped2 dataset, TranSight achieves a video-level AUC of 98.0%, a global frame-label AUC of 30 ± 1.47, an anomaly ratio of 82.20%, and an overall AUC of 97.99%, outperforming transformer-based baselines with high robustness, interpretability, and efficiency for real-time intelligent transportation analytics.

title: Neuromarketing Applications: Evolving Trends and the Way Forward
authors: Siddhartha Sarkar, Sachin Patel
abstract: Neuromarketing integrates neuroscience with marketing to uncover subconscious drivers of consumer behavior. As traditional self-report methods often fail to capture implicit processes, neuromarketing offers more accurate insights through neuroimaging and physiological measures. This paper reviews the evolution, tools, and applications of neuromarketing, highlighting its contributions to understanding decision-making. It also outlines emerging trends and future research directions, emphasizing neuromarketing’s growing potential to advance marketing practice.

title: Development of Machine Learning Model for Automated Fruit Classification & Ripeness Detection
authors: Anshika Jindal, Monika Nayak, Rekha Manasa, Medha Shukla, K. A. Chinmaya
abstract: This paper delineates the development of two machine learning models: the first for the classification of fruits into distinct categories using the comprehensive Fruits 360 dataset, and the second for the determination of ripeness levels within a specific category, exemplified by bananas. The Fruits 360 dataset, encompassing over 90,000 images of 131 fruit and vegetable types, provides a robust foundation for the initial classification model. In contrast, the banana ripeness dataset, with its focus on various stages of banana maturity, enables the second model to discern between unripe, ripe, and overripe states with remarkable accuracy. The architecture of the models is based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). It is meticulously trained and validated to achieve high precision in both fruit categorization and ripeness detection. The results of this study not only demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed models but also highlight the transformative potential of machine learning in automating and enhancing agricultural processes.

title: ICoECIT 2026 Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)

title: Game Optimization Scheduling Method for Regional Power Grid of 220kV Substation Based on Information Entropy
authors: Meng Ren, Junbao Zhang, Cunliang Wei, Defa Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Multi-stakeholder game scheduling of power grid has high complexity, and the objectives of each stakeholder are very different. These different objectives make it difficult to find a solution that satisfies all parties completely in game scheduling. Therefore, this study takes 220 kV substation as the research object, and designs a game optimization dispatching method for power supply region. The power supply information mining is completed based on information entropy, and the power supply area data scheduling method is designed under the multi-mixed game, and the power supply area scheduling function is solved based on the Bat algorithm. The experimental results show that: Under the application of the proposed substation power supply regional network scheduling method, the power utilization rate is always above 95 %, and the power fluctuation is smaller, and the power generation cost in the power supply region can be significantly reduced, indicating that the research method can effectively optimize the interest game between the power grid multi-target entities.

title: Handwritten Compound Odia Character Recognition using Deep Learning Architectures
authors: Subham Sahoo, Umakanta Majhi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The Odia language is spoken by more than 50 million+ people and it has a very rich literary heritage that is recorded in handwritten manuscripts and centuries of historical records. These documents have, however, been hard to digitize. Digitizing these materials remains challenging due to the complex structures of Odia characters and substantial handwriting variation across writers. We investigate compound Odia handwritten character recognition using deep learning. Our custom dataset consists of 5,760 handwritten samples across 64 compound character classes, collected systematically using standardized grid-based sheets. A comprehensive preprocessing pipeline was implemented, including geometric correction, skew correction, perspective transformation, and adaptive thresholding to extract clean character images. We evaluated five deep learning architectures: VGG-16, ResNet-18, DenseNet-121, EfficientNet-B0, and MobileNet-V2 under two scenarios: standard testing and cross-writer generalization. DenseNet-121 achieved the highest standard test accuracy (96.64%). However, cross-writer experiments—testing on completely unseen writers—revealed EfficientNet-B0 demonstrated superior generalization (92.29% ± 2.16%) compared to DenseNet-121 (88.93% ± 5.06%).Performance differences are not statistically significant, according to statistical analysis using Mc-Nemar’s test and the paired t-test (p=0.503), which suggests similar cross-writer robustness. These results show that lightweight architectures like EfficientNet-B0 and MobileNet-V2 achieve competitive performance with significantly lower computational cost, making them appropriate for deployment in government administration, educational institutions, and archival preservation projects that have limited resources.

title: Predictive Health Monitoring of Solenoid Coils Under Humid Aging Conditions
authors: Thomas Roulleau, Killian Corbel, Hani Al Hajjar, Frédéric Lamarque
abstract: This study presents an experimental approach to predict failure of alternative current solenoid coils, by linking internal temperature variation with the wire insulation degradation state due to the water ingress. (Abstract)

title: Lightweight UNet++ Based Multi-Sensor Fusion for Automated Building Footprint Extraction
authors: Nagadi Leela Rao, Deepak Kumar, Shivani Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Building Footprints are two-dimensional boundaries that identify buildings on the ground-level surface of the Earth. These are useful for urban planning, infrastructure mapping, disaster management, etc. The extraction process can be auto-mated using deep learning methods, making it faster and more resistant to errors. Although optical images are widely used for this purpose, they are not as reliable in cloud-occluded, shadow-covered areas, and low-contrast regions. Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data, on the other hand, are immune to these problems, but can show an underwhelming performance when used alone, due to inherent speckle noise. This paper aims to explore the automatic extraction of building footprints by fusing the two modalities using a light-weight, early fusion-based UNet++ model. The SpaceNet 6 Multi-Sensor All-Weather Mapping dataset (MSAW) has been used for this purpose.

title: Multimodal Hybrid Attention-Gating Fusion Deep Learning Framework for Alzheimer’s Disease Classification
authors: Mohammed Rizwan Shaikh, Andrew Jeyabose, R. Vijaya Arjunan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis, staging, and progression assessment of Alzheimer’s disease (AD) remain challenging due to heterogeneous clinical manifestations and substantial overlap among disease stages. While deep learning approaches based on structural magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) have demonstrated encouraging performance, imaging-only models often fail to capture important clinical and cognitive factors that are essential for comprehensive disease characterization. This work proposes a multimodal deep learning framework that jointly integrates MRI-derived features with clinical biomarkers for improved AD classification and staging. Unimodal (MRI-only and clinical only) and multimodal models are systematically evaluated using subject-level-cross-validation and an independent test set across multiple binary and multi-class tasks, including AD vs cognitively normal (CN), AD vs mild cognitive impairment (MCI), stable MCI vs progressive MCI (SMCI vs PMCI), and multi-class disease staging. Experimental results show that multimodal fusion consistently outperforms unimodal models, improving MCI progression prediction from 59% to 75% and increasing MCI vs AD diagnostic accuracy from 92.31% to 93.85% achieving higher accuracy, improved generalization, and reduced performance variability across evaluation settings. These findings demonstrate that combining imaging and clinical information provides a more robust and clinically meaningful approach for Alzheimer’s disease diagnosis and prognosis.

title: Key Parameters Optimization of DC Short-Circuit Current in Hybrid MMCs for GW Level UHVDC Transmission Systems
authors: Xin Yuan, Junda Qin, Nan Deng, Qing Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Renewable Energy and Electrical Technology (ICREET)
abstract: The hybrid modular multilevel converter (MMC), which is composed of half-bridge submodules (HBSMs) and fullbridge submodules (FBSMs), has become a significant research focus in flexible high-voltage direct current (HVDC) transmission. This is due to its unique blocking-free DC fault ride-through capability. First, taking the ± 800 kV Gansu-Zhejiang UHVDC project as a representative case, this study describes the topology and operational principles of hybrid MMCs in the project. Next, the impact of both the FBSM proportion ratio and DC reactor parameters on short-circuit currents during system faults is analyzed. Finally, a PSCAD/EMTDC model based on GansuZhejiang project is built. Simulation results conclusively demonstrate that optimized FBSM/HBSM proportion ratios and appropriately selected reactor parameters can significantly limit short-circuit currents.

title: An Attention-Guided Multi-Scale Network for Brain Tumor Segmentation
authors: Tushar Chaurasia, Pranshu Jena, Umesh C. Pati
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The identification of the tumorous region in brain scans has been challenging due to the scarcity of quantitative data for evaluation, treatment planning, and monitoring disease progression. Manual detection of tumor regions has been a difficult task in brain tumor segmentation due to intricate boundaries and the varied characteristics of tumors. Conventional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) struggle to capture in-depth tumor features and the nuanced spatial relationships between tumor and non-tumor regions. Hence, this work effectively addresses the drawbacks of existing state-of-the-art frameworks by proposing an innovative, lightweight segmentation framework that employs the U-Net architecture integrated with a fine-tuned ResNet-34 backbone and the squeeze-and-excitation architecture. The focus on the tumor region is enhanced by the multi-head attention module, resulting in improved segmentation performance. The BraTS 2020 dataset is rigorously tested using the proposed framework, yielding a Dice score of 0.8247, which indicates competitive performance when compared to state-of-the-art models.

title: Sensing the Neural, Muscular, and Biomechanical Biomarkers to Evaluate Age-Related Neuromuscular Health
authors: Sankhalp NS, A Celestin Jerald
abstract: Age-related neuromuscular changes significantly influence the onset and progression of neuromuscular disorders. This emphasizes the importance of understanding normal age-related neuromuscular variations for improved prevention and intervention strategies. This study aims to analyze neuromuscular dynamics across the aging spectrum using multimodal approaches, including electromyography (EMG), electroencephalography (EEG), and force plate studies. The results obtained in this study portray significant differences in these biomarkers with age. The integration of data from EMG, EEG, and force plate analyses offers a comprehensive framework to identify normal aging, which can be used to identify predictive biomarkers and explore their applications in assessing neuromuscular health. This research holds promise for enhancing diagnostics, advancing targeted therapies, and contributing to the understanding of neuromuscular function in aging populations.

title: Dynamic Programming Optimization of LLM Cascade Ordering for Extracting Sleep Parameters from Mean Sleep Latency Tests
authors: Arash Maghsoudi, Nithin Weerasinghe, Amir Sharafkhaneh, Javad Razjouyan
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: The objective of this study is to develop and evaluate a scalable natural-language-processing (NLP) pipeline that accurately extracts mean sleep latency (MSL) from Multiple Sleep Latency Test (MSLT) reports in Veterans Health Administration (VHA) records, while minimizing hallucinations and computational cost. We analyzed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 3 3}$</tex> million VHA clinical notes containing the terms MSLT or multiple sleep latency (1999 - 2025). A logistic-regression classifier using bigram features first identified genuine MSLT notes. Verified notes were processed with a 4-bit-quantized Llama-3.1-8B large language model (LLM); prompts were clinician-guided, and extractions were run at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.2-1.8$</tex> temperature settings (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta=0.1$</tex>). To cut missrates without excessive compute, we framed temperature ordering as a dynamic-programming (DP) optimization that, under a predefined cost budget, chooses the cascade of LLM runs that maximizes overall accuracy. Manual annotation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 8 2 5}$</tex> randomly sampled notes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 4 6}$</tex> MSLT, 979 non-MSLT) served as ground truth. The note-identifier achieved mean accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=94.3 {\%}$</tex> and AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.981$</tex> (5-fold cross-validation). Filtering reduced hallucinations from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0. 8 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 5 \%}$</tex> and misses from 12.8% to 2.4%, raising single-pass extraction accuracy from 73.3% to 87.0%. The DP-optimized cascade further lowered the miss-rate while respecting the cost budget, outperforming any individual-temperature run across all metrics. Clinically, using the 8 -minute MSL cut-off, the final system achieved a sensitivity of 98% and a positive predictive value of 97%.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Computing for Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning on Edge Devices
authors: B. Dinesh, M. Thangamani, B. Paulchamy, Lavanya K, D. Anandakumar, Rajasekhara Babu L
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The edge computing environment faces a challenge in maintaining data privacy while performing machine learning tasks. The current solutions for privacy preservation are inefficient or do not provide security guarantees, causing a critical gap in the edge computing environment, which is resource-constrained. The aim of this research work is to design a hybrid quantum-classical computing system that addresses the challenges of privacy preservation and efficiency in machine learning tasks. We design a novel architecture combining variational quantum circuits for secure data encoding with classical neural networks for scalable inference, implemented through federated learning protocols enhanced with quantum differential privacy mechanisms. Experimental evaluation demonstrates a 35% reduction in communication overhead compared to traditional federated learning approaches while maintaining 99.2% classification accuracy across diverse datasets. The quantum-enhanced privacy mechanisms achieve provable security guarantees with information-theoretic bounds, outperforming conventional cryptographic methods by 28% in computational overhead reduction. This hybrid approach establishes a new paradigm for secure and efficient machine learning deployment on edge devices, enabling privacy-preserving artificial intelligence in healthcare, finance, and IoT applications.

title: UAV-assisted MinMax Entropy and Artificial Intelligence-based Semi Supervised Domain Adaptation Approach for Rice Disease Detection
authors: Harnoor Kaur Khehra, Baljit Singh Khehra, Arjan Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In this paper, a semi-supervised domain adaptation approach is proposed that leverages a MinMax entropy strategy to address domain shift in rice disease detection. The method integrates UAV technology and consumer electronics, enabling scalable, field-level disease monitoring and detection. UAVs facilitate rapid data collection across diverse environments, while consumer electronics enhance accessibility and deployment of detection models. Several CNN architectures, including general CNN models and pretrained architectures like VGG16, ResNet50, and AlexNet, were evaluated for their ability to adapt from a labeled source domain to a target domain with limited labels. The proposed approach minimizes cross-entropy loss in the source domain while maximizing entropy loss in the target domain, ensuring better generalization. Through k-fold cross-validation, the performance of the models was assessed based on training, validation, and test losses, as well as validation and test accuracies. Experimental results highlight the efficiency of the proposed method in improving domain adaptability and enhancing the reliability of rice leaf disease detection, offering a scalable solution for modern agricultural challenges.

title: PALMS+: Modular Image-Based Floor Plan Localization Leveraging Depth Foundation Model
authors: Yunqian Cheng, Benjamin Princen, Roberto Manduchi
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Indoor localization in GPS-denied environments is crucial for applications like emergency response and assistive navigation. Vision-based methods such as PALMS enable infrastructure-free localization using only a floor plan and a stationary scan, but are limited by the short range of smartphone LiDAR and ambiguity in indoor layouts. We propose PALMS+, a modular, image-based system that addresses these challenges by reconstructing scale-aligned 3D point clouds from posed RGB images using a foundation monocular depth estimation model (Depth Pro), followed by geometric layout matching via convolution with the floor plan. PALMS+ outputs a posterior over the location and orientation, usable for direct or sequential localization. Evaluated on the Structured3D and a custom campus dataset consisting of 80 observations across four large campus buildings, PALMS+ outperforms PALMS and F<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>Loc in stationary localization accuracy—without requiring any training. Furthermore, when integrated with a particle filter for sequential localization on 33 real-world trajectories, PALMS+ achieved lower localization errors compared to other methods, demonstrating robustness for camera-free tracking and its potential for infrastructure-free applications. Code and data are available at https://github.com/Head-inthe-Cloud/PALMS-Plane-based-Accessible-Indoor-Localization-Using-Mobile-Smartphones.

title: Feature-Disentangling RGB-NIR Fusion Network for Remote Driver Physiological Measurement
authors: Tayssir Bouraffa, Ziyuan Wang, Daniel Strüber
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) is a crucial technique for non-contact heart rate (HR) estimation using facial videos, gaining significance in driver monitoring systems where contact-based measurements are impractical. Existing rPPG methods often rely on either RGB or NIR data, each susceptible to limitations under motion artifacts and varying illumination in real-world driving scenarios. To address these challenges, we introduce a novel RGB-NIR fusion model tailored for robust rPPG and HR estimation in dynamic vehicle environments. Our approach features two main contributions, an NIR-specific decoder that facilitates effective cross-modal knowledge transfer from RGB to NIR, enhancing model adaptability, and a dual autoencoder architecture for efficient feature disentanglement and reconstruction, mitigating noise from driver motion and changing lighting conditions. Comprehensive evaluations, including inter- and cross-dataset testing and ablation studies across various driving and garage conditions, demonstrate that our model achieves superior performance on the MR-NIRP car dataset, showcasing significant robustness in complex vehicular environments.

title: FlowMorph: Revealing an Optimizable Flow Latent Space for Controlled Image Morphing
authors: Yan Zheng, Yi Yang, Lanqing Guo, Zhangyang Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present FlowMorph, a simple and training-free framework for geometry-preserving and semantics-aware image interpolation. The key idea is to separate two factors inside the flow model’s latent space: an offset that captures shape and geometry, and a one-step vector that carries semantic meaning. By keeping the flow model frozen and only optimizing these two variables, FlowMorph exposes a stable and interpretable neighborhood around each image. This leads to two complementary modes. Flow-Optimizer directly fits a source image toward a target image and naturally supports multi-objective combinations, producing stable reconstructions. Flow-Interpolation mixes the offset linearly and the semantic vector spherically, generating smooth and coherent transitions between images. Across a wide range of tasks including object morphing, pose changes, and scene transitions, FlowMorph outperforms prior interpolation-based methods. Quantitative experiments show that our method achieves lower perceptual error, better image fidelity, and smoother transitions. Landmark-based analysis further confirms that FlowMorph preserves geometry more effectively. We also ablate the effect of the backward step size, showing that longer steps increase semantic expressiveness and allow interpolations that move beyond trivial shape blending, enabling richer morphs across object positions and photo layouts. FlowMorph provides an interpretable and controllable tool for high-quality image morphing without the need for additional training.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Vision-Language Segmentation for Medical Imaging
authors: Aryan Das, Tanishq Rachamalla, Koushik Biswas, Swalpa Kumar Roy, Vinay Kumar Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We introduce a novel uncertainty-aware multimodal segmentation framework that leverages both radiological images and associated clinical text for precise medical diagnosis. We propose a Modality Decoding Attention Block (MoDAB) with a lightweight State Space Mixer (SSMix) to enable efficient cross-modal fusion and long-range dependency modelling. To guide learning under ambiguity, we propose the Spectral-Entropic Uncertainty (SEU) Loss, which jointly captures spatial overlap, spectral consistency, and predictive uncertainty in a unified objective. In complex clinical circumstances with poor image quality, this formulation improves model reliability. Extensive experiments on various publicly available medical datasets, QATA-COVID19, MosMed++, and Kvasir-SEG, demonstrate that our method achieves superior segmentation performance while being significantly more computationally efficient than existing State-of-the-Art (SoTA) approaches. Our results highlight the importance of incorporating uncertainty modelling and structured modality alignment in vision-language medical segmentation tasks. Code: https://github.com/arya-domain/UA-VLS

title: Privacy-Preserving Edge-Cloud Framework for Real-Time Crowd Detection and Forecasting
authors: Sanidhya Vijay, Dhruv Gehlot, Shikher Tewari, Samarth Mantri, Malvinder Singh Bali
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The conventional cloud-based crowd monitoring system faces issues of latency, bandwidth, and privacy due to the constant transmission of video. This paper introduces a hybrid edge-cloud framework focused on privacy. It combines real-time human detection, short-term crowd forecasting, and proactive timeslot allocation. The YOLOv11 detection runs locally on NVIDIA Jetson devices to create anonymized per-second JSON metadata with centroid-based deduplication. This approach removes the need to transfer raw images. Short-term occupancy estimates are generated via a hybrid forecasting ensemble that combines Transformer, LSTM, and XGBoost models. Bayesian fusion improves these predictions using user-intent signals. The predicted densities then go into an Integer Linear Programming scheduler that assigns visitor timeslots to maintain balanced occupancy and prevent congestion. To support ongoing model improvement without central data collection, the system uses federated learning with adaptive differential privacy and secure aggregation. Only encrypted and noisy model weight updates are shared. The framework monitors privacy loss using Rényi Differential Privacy accounting. This method guarantees privacy while keeping the model effective. As a result, the framework achieves a 95% reduction in bandwidth, less than 50ms inference latency, and a 25% decrease in peak occupancy while ensuring privacy.

title: An Adaptive Federated Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Explainable Deep Learning and Smart Decision-Making in Healthcare IoT Using MIMIC-IV
authors: Vinitha Vincent, Harshitha Saraswathi R S, Srikanth R, N.P. Dharani, M. UmaDevi, Sudharsan Kalyanakumar
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The fast growth of Internet of Things (IoT) in healthcare has allowed real-time patient data monitoring, but issues of data privacy, explainability, and adaptive decision-making are not yet resolved. This paper proposes AFNS-IoT, an Adaptive Federated Neuro-Symbolic IoT framework that integrates deep learning, neuro-symbolic reasoning, and reinforcement learning for intelligent and explainable healthcare decision-making. Leveraging the MIMIC-IV dataset, local IoT edge nodes are trained using federated deep learning models to preserve patient privacy, while a multi-objective weighted aggregation balances accuracy, latency, and device constraints. A neuro-symbolic fusion layer translates model outputs into interpretable symbolic rules, providing transparent decision pathways for clinical decision support. Furthermore, a reinforcement learning feedback loop adaptively refines the system based on real-world outcomes. Experimental results demonstrate that AFNS-IoT achieves the highest accuracy (0.94), F1-score (0.92), and AUC (0.95), outperforming all baseline models. Furthermore, it reduces inference latency to 140 ms, compared to 220 ms in centralized learning, while achieving superior energy efficiency (0.88). Unlike conventional deep learning and federated approaches that lack interpretability, AFNS-IoT provides very high interpretability fidelity through its neuro-symbolic fusion layer. The findings demonstrate how AFNS-IoT can be used as the next-generation framework of privacy, explainable and adaptive healthcare IoT systems.

title: OpenLVLM-MIA: A Controlled Benchmark Revealing the Limits of Membership Inference Attacks on Large Vision-Language Models
authors: Ryoto Miyamoto, Xin Fan, Fuyuko Kido, Tsuneo Matsumoto, Hayato Yamana
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: OpenLVLM-MIA is a new benchmark that highlights fundamental challenges in evaluating membership inference attacks (MIA) against large vision-language models (LVLMs). While prior work has reported high attack success rates, our analysis suggests that these results often arise from detecting distributional bias introduced during dataset construction rather than from identifying true membership status. To address this issue, we introduce a controlled benchmark of 6,000 images where the distributions of member and non-member samples are carefully balanced, and ground-truth membership labels are provided across three distinct training stages. Experiments using OpenLVLM-MIA demonstrated that the performance of state-of-the-art MIA methods approached chance-level. OpenLVLM-MIA, designed to be transparent and unbiased benchmark, clarifies certain limitations of MIA research on LVLMs and provides a solid foundation for developing stronger privacy-preserving techniques.

title: DreamMakeup: Face Makeup Customization using Latent Diffusion Models
authors: Geon Yeong Park, Inhwa Han, Serin Yang, Yeobin Hong, Seongmin Jeong, Heechan Jeon, Myeongjin Goh, Sung Won Yi, Jin Nam, Jong Chul Ye
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The exponential growth of the global makeup market has paralleled advancements in virtual makeup simulation technology. Despite the progress led by GANs, their application still encounters significant challenges, including training instability and limited customization capabilities. Addressing these challenges, we introduce DreamMakup – a novel training-free Diffusion model based Makeup Customization method, leveraging the inherent advantages of diffusion models for superior controllability and precise real-image editing. DreamMakeup employs early-stopped DDIM inversion to preserve the facial structure and identity while enabling extensive customization through various conditioning inputs such as reference images, specific RGB colors, and textual descriptions. Our model demonstrates notable improvements over existing GAN-based and recent diffusion-based frameworks – improved customization, color-matching capabil ities, identity preservation and compatibility with textual descriptions or LLMs with affordable computational costs.

title: A Modular Edge-Assisted Deep Learning System for Real-Time Intrusion Detection in Smart Surveillance
authors: M. Saranya, S Fazil, R Muthumari, B Nivethithan, S Meiyarul, R Ranjithkumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The smart surveillance systems demand smart solutions that have the capability to detect unauthorized or suspicious access with maximum accuracy and minimal latency. In the research, it is proposed that a suspected entry alarm system based on AI can be developed that would incorporate object recognition, behavior, and notifications provided by the IoT to enhance the success of real-time monitoring. Using the assistance of edge computing, the system exploits deep learning approaches in intrusion recognition to deliver low-latency inference and cloud servers so that it can afford scalability. Experimental validation represents that the performance is vigorous with an IoU of 0.78, and ROC-AUC of 0.94 that denote good localization of objects, proper intrusion classification, as well as reliable occurrence of anomalies. In addition, the system took an average of 1.8 seconds to respond. It has been built with its architecture divided into modular architecture such as preprocessing, tracking and alert management that enables it to suit varied smart surveillance setups. Overall, the developed solution provides a scalable and efficient framework that improves security surveillance, reduces the false alarm, and makes proactive decision-making in the smart surveillance systems.

title: AI-Based Technique for Odia-to-English Language Translation Using LSTM: Breaking the Communication Barriers
authors: Sasadhara Sahoo, Rojalina Priyadrashini, Ram Chandra Barik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Neural machine translation (NMT) for low-resource languages remains a significant challenge due to limited parallel corpora and linguistic complexities. This paper presents an LSTM-based Seq2Seq model with attention for Odia-to-English translation. The proposed model integrates a bidirectional LSTM encoder with an attention-enabled decoder, enabling effective capture of contextual dependencies in morphologically rich Odia sentences. The system was trained on a curated parallel Odia–English corpus and evaluated using BLEU scores on a held-out validation set. Experimental results demonstrate that the model achieves reasonable translation quality for short and medium-complexity sentences, while highlighting challenges in handling rare words, long sentences, and domain-specific terminology. Comprehensive analysis, including attention visualizations, loss convergence trends, and sentence-level BLEU distributions, provides insights into model behaviour and translation limitations. Future improvements involving sub word tokenization and Transformer-based architectures are proposed to further enhance translation accuracy. This work establishes a foundational framework for advancing low-resource Odia-English neural machine translation and serves as a benchmark for future research.

title: Detection of Alzheimer's Disease Using Deep Learning and Interpretability Using Grad-CAM
authors: Sanjukta Mohanty, Swaraj Priyadarsini, Arup Abhinna Acharya, Debabrata Sahoo, Soumyaranjan Das
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease is a debilitating neurodegenerative disorder which progressively affects cognition, memory, and daily functioning. Early detection is critical for slowing disease progression and implementing timely interventions, yet current diagnostic techniques including manual MRI analysis and clinical assessments are often timeconsuming, subjective, and dependent on expert interpretation. These limit points highlight the need for an automated, efficient, and objective diagnostic approach. This study introduces a deeplearning based model to classify MRI scans automatically into different Alzheimer's disease stages (Normal, Mild, Moderate, Severe), addressing the inefficiencies of traditional diagnostic methods. The proposed framework integrates EfficientNet for 2D feature extraction and a 3D Convolutional Neural Network (3D CNN) for volumetric analysis, enabling high-precision classification while maintaining computational efficiency. To enhance interpretability and trustworthiness, the model incorporates Grad-CAM visualization, allowing clinicians to pinpoint affected brain regions and understand the model's decisionmaking process. With an accuracy target of more than 98.5%, the model demonstrates robust performance across diverse MRI datasets, ensuring generalizability and reliability. Extensive experimentation and validation show its potential for scalability and real-world deployment in hospitals and research facilities. By leveraging AI and deep learning in neuroimaging, this research presents an advanced, automated diagnostic solution that can significantly improve early Alzheimer's detection, reduce diagnostic subjectivity, and assist medical professionals in delivering more effective patient care.

title: Bootstrap-Ensembled Neural Networks for Robust Stellar Temperature and Age Predictions
authors: Marco Tarantino, Loredana Prisinzano, Nicoletta D'Angelo, Francesco Damiani, Giada Adelfio
abstract: This study presents a machine learning approach to predict the effective temperatures and their accuracy of premain sequence stars, which are essential for deriving stellar ages through isochrone fitting and starspot-dependent evolutionary models. We trained a Neural Network on high-quality spectroscopic temperatures from the Gaia-ESO Survey, using Gaia DR3 and 2MASS photometry as input features. To estimate predictive uncertainty, we implemented a Neural Network with bootstrap procedure, where each model was warm-started using the parameters of an initial network trained on the full dataset with K-fold cross-validation. This allowed accurate and robust temperature predictions for large stellar populations lacking spectroscopic data, with strong performance in the lowtemperature regime. The predicted temperatures were used to build Hertzsprung-Russell diagrams and derive stellar ages of young clusters by starspot evolutionary models, achieving good agreement with spectroscopic benchmarks and independent methods, such as lithium equivalent widths.

title: Integrating Metabolic Pattern Analysis and Ketone Dynamics with AI-Based Strength Training for Lower Limb Health Prediction in Working Women
authors: B. Devimano, S Ravikumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The interaction of Multi Omics Brain- Derived Neurotrophic factor insight with personalized AI Strength Training for working women’s lower limb health and Longterm outcome prediction wellness metrics by combining AI models with brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) as a positive wellness factor, hedonic hunger as an negative wellness factor with highly personalized and effective lower limb leg-strengthening metrices with time restricted feedings for working women professionals of advanced middle aged, especially those seeking to improve lower body strength, mobility, fat lose and longevity. BDNF is a protein that, in human is a key molecule involved in plastic changes related to learning and memory, the resistance training, functional exercises, and high intensity interval training can significantly increase the BDNF levels in women, which in turn supports muscle growth, recovery and neural adaptation in the legs which enhances both physical and cognitive outcomes. A regression target of predicting an individual’s hedonic hunger score classification which is the major causes of weakness in lower limb leg among women, caused because of childhood experiences, picky eating addiction can be analysed with the deep learning algorithms. AI powered personalized lower limb leg strengthening training increases the BDNF value of the brain that decreases the hedonic hunger value with help of the time restricting feeding of women of advanced middle aged that promotes fat lose, Lower limb strengthening that leads to healthy lifestyle of longevity.

title: PADM: A Physics-aware Diffusion Model for Attenuation Correction
authors: Trung Kien Pham, Hoang Minh Vu, Anh Duc Chu, Dac Thai Nguyen, Trung Thanh Nguyen, Thao Nguyen Truong, Mai Hong Son, Thanh Trung Nguyen, Phi Le Nguyen
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Attenuation artifacts remain a significant challenge in cardiac Myocardial Perfusion Imaging (MPI) using Single-Photon Emission Computed Tomography (SPECT), often compromising diagnostic accuracy and reducing clinical interpretability. While hybrid SPECT/CT systems mitigate these artifacts through CT-derived attenuation maps, their high cost, limited accessibility, and added radiation exposure hinder widespread clinical adoption. In this study, we propose a novel CT-free solution to attenuation correction in cardiac SPECT. Specifically, we introduce Physics-aware Attenuation Correction Diffusion Model (PADM), a diffusion-based generative method that incorporates explicit physics priors via a teacher–student distillation mechanism. This approach enables attenuation artifact correction using only Non-Attenuation-Corrected (NAC) input, while still benefiting from physics-informed supervision during training. To support this work, we also introduce CardiAC, a comprehensive dataset comprising 424 patient studies with paired NAC and Attenuation-Corrected (AC) reconstructions, alongside high-resolution CT-based attenuation maps. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PADM outperforms state-of-the-art generative models, delivering superior reconstruction fidelity across both quantitative metrics and visual assessment.

title: Roadkill Hotspots and Policy Advocacy (RHPA): A Multi-Layered AI and IoT Approach for Real-Time Wildlife Preservation
authors: Lokesh Patra, Indigibilli Harshit, Saumendra Kumar Mohapatra, Apurva Singh, Mihir Narayan Mohanty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Roadkill episodes are a crucial point where vehicle safety risks and ecological damage converge. The frequency of wildlife-vehicle incidents has increased due to rising urbanization, posing a threat to commuters and causing a major loss of biodiversity. In this study, a novel three-dimensional (3D) approach called “Roadkill Hotspots and Policy Advocacy” (RHPA) is proposed. In contrast to conventional radar-based systems, RHPA creates a preventive and responsive ecosystem by combining Computer Vision (YOLOv5xu), Deep Learning (DenseNet121 and EfficientNet-B3), and the Internet of Things (IoT). Both CNN architectures are trained individually to categorize subjects as “Alive” (High Risk) or “Dead” (Low Risk), achieving an overall accuracy of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\approx} {9 5 {\%}}$</tex>, and YOLOv5xu for real-time detection. Additionally, the system incorporates a policy-level dashboard to detect blackspots and an ESP32-based actuation layer for real-time driver warnings. Pilot testing indicates a potential 36% reduction in roadkill incidents.

title: Predictive Analytics in Smart Manufacturing with OptiSenseNet and Superior Sensor Data Integration
authors: Syed Hassan Imam Gardezi, Sankarganesh P, Ban Safar Khalaf, Ingilala Sharona, S.T. Santhanalakshmi, I Infant Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The huge amount and diversity of sensor information produced in contemporary settings continue to pose challenges to predictive analytics in smart manufacturing The existing approaches often fail to effectively process and analyze this data, which in turn leads to imprecise outlook and inaccurate decision-making father son It was born to go far. OptiSenseNet is the product that uses CNN and LSTM networks to offer effective solutions to these issues. The model is designed in such a way that it uses CNN layers to detect spatial features in sensor data and the LSTM components detect temporal features. This two-sided approach enables the analysis of both spatial and time elements of the OptiSenseNet in greater detail, which captures more information about how the devices behave and interact with the performance. One of the most important innovations of OptiSenseNet is that a temporal bypass connection between the LSTM module is combined. This aspect maximizes the seize of the long-term period dependencies via leveraging everyday styles which are made of all day fluctuations which supplements the overall predictive performance of the model. The process involves a systematic pipeline, which comprises statistics standardization, processing through CNN and LSTM layers with continuous model refinement through a process of iteration and blunders backpropagation. The OptiSenseNet has an impressive accuracy charge of 99.1, which proves that it can contribute to the proactive choice-making and optimization of business in intelligent manufacturing. OptiSenseNet will be a far-reaching leap in predictive analytics as it will overcome the limitations of traditional methods and adopt Industry four. Zero principles. It stimulates innovation and promotes operational excellence, which makes it a critical tool in the context of sophisticated sensor statistics generation and selection to support ways of production in the state of the art.

title: Text Recognition Using Neural Radiance Fields
authors: Madhav Sharma, Hukam Chand Saini, Samiksha Agarwal, Akash Saxena, Pushpendra kumar Sikarwal, Shalini chaudhary
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Text reputation in complex scenes is still a difficult problem due to various lighting conditions, occlusions, and mental distortions. In three-dimensional environments, text appears on curved or non-planar surfaces, rendering conventional OCR methods useless. Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs), originally designed for three-D scene reconstruction, offer a new method for textual content reputation by allowing immoderate-constancy volumetric rendering. Using deep analysis models to extract text records and reconstruct three-dimensional textual content systems from two-dimensional photographs, this paper explores the possible adaptation of NeRFs for text popularity. We evaluate NeRF-based full text recognition on both synthetic and real-world datasets, demonstrating that it performs better than conventional techniques when dealing with intricate text distortions and occlusions.

title: Dual-Active-Bridge Converter with ANFIS Controller
authors: Oakkar Min, Rishabh Dev Shukla, Shilpa Gupta
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Dual Active Bridge (DAB) Converter is one of the most commonly used configurations for handling DC output in Electric Vehicle (EV) chargers and energy storage systems due to its potential for converting and transferring power, as well as its ability to isolate components in both directions. However, the standard PI controller is not able to respond quickly enough and lacks enough stability to keep the voltage regulated while also reacting to reference inputs. This paper describes a model for an ANFIS based controller and compares its performance against a PI controlled version. The model developed in the study uses an ANFIS based control scheme for a dual active bridge DAB converter that converts 800 V input voltages into adjustable output reference voltages of 396-387 Vdc. The simulation performed with MATLAB/Simulink has shown a reduction of approximately $23 \%$ in settling time and $20 \%$ improvement in rise time over the PI control method which completely eliminates any overshoot associated with PI controller response and also achieves a decrease in output voltage ripple of nearly $30\%$ when compared to PI controller methods. The output voltage closely tracks the reference input voltage with minimal steady-state error. The ANFIS controller developed in this study has demonstrated better stability and dynamic performance than traditional PI and fuzzy controllers for other DAB converters used in EVs and renewable energy applications in currently available literature.

title: Flex-Knee: A Low-Cost Passive Haptic Model for Injury Assessment Training
authors: Marco Moran-Ledesma, Robert Burns, Oliver Schneider, Mark Hancock
abstract: We present the design and development of a lowcost ($ 400 CAD), scalable, and modular haptic knee model to support training in manual musculoskeletal assessments of injuries. Our model aims to help inexperienced practitioners learn the Valgus test, a clinical test to assess medial collateral ligament (MCL) injuries. The valgus test is typically taught on classmates or patients, which limits available training time and exposure to anatomical diversity and injury conditions. Designed with a certified clinician, our model uses a generic mechanical joint, silicone approximating human skin look and feel, a metamaterials-based approach to reproduce MCL injuries, and pressure sensors to capture user-applied forces. We evaluated the model in three stages: internal calibration with our collaborating clinician to configure it for four conditions (healthy, grades 1-3) using the staircase procedure; acceptance testing with 6 additional clinicians; and a showcase with students in a kinesiology class. Certified professionals distinguished healthy, partially-torn, and fully-torn MCLs with sensitivities of 0.83, 0.75, and 1.00; students also recognized these differences, though refinements are needed for grades 1 and 2. Participants praised the model's skin texture and shape. This work highlights the potential of low-cost haptic simulators to deliver standardized and repeatable training experiences.

title: Network Traffic Classification Based on Hadoop Using Gradient Boosting Algorithm
authors: Hmway Hmway Tar, Thiri Shwe Sin Htun
abstract: The proposed system presents a system for network traffic classification that combines Apache Hadoop for processing large data with the Gradient Boosting Classifier (GBC) algorithm. The proposed framework processes web server logs to categorize traffic into High, Medium, and Low priority flows, aiming to improve resource allocation and anomaly detection. The research highlights a gap in existing systems and handles with Gradient Boosting that gets high accuracy for complex pattern recognition in traffic flows. By leveraging the distributed processing power of Hadoop, the system transforms raw, messy network data into structured insights. The integration significantly improves decision-making, optimizes resource allocation, and helps identify critical trends and anomalies in network traffic, offering a robust advancement in big data and machine learning applications for network management.

title: Thermal Analysis of Multiphase Electromagnetic Coupler for High Power Contactless Charging System Considering Core and Shielding Effects
authors: Sukumar Das, Apurv Kumar Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a thermal analysis approach for a high-power multiphase inductive power transfer (IPT) system, with a focus on the thermal behavior of the magnetic core and electromagnetic shielding materials. The study incorporates detailed electromagnetic and thermal co-simulation using FEA in ANSYS to evaluate the spatial heat distribution across core, winding, and shielding structures. The influence of eddy current loss, core loss, and ohmic heating in conductors is analyzed. The thermal model is validated against experimental data and is used to optimize design of the magnetic core, coil geometry and shielding layout to minimize hotspots and improve thermal dissipation. Results demonstrate that appropriate selection of core and shielding can significantly reduce localized heating, enabling the system to operate safely at higher power levels.

title: EPDMC 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)

title: A Decentralized Crypto Launchpad on Binance Smart Chain
authors: Bidyutmala Saha, Priyanka Chakraborty, Sayan Nath, Debtanu Roy, Arna Saha, Anwesa Ghosh, Dewan Habib, Akash Dey
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: This project is a blockchain-based decentralised application that allows cryptocurrency owners to launch new currencies while investors may make first investments in them. The charge of crypto in the foreign websites is high due to currency differences. This is a Make in India initiative and also supports the Digital India campaign by making Indian people more accustomed to the digital market. It emphasizes security, transparency, and community participation to build a trustworthy and sustainable launch pad ecosystem. A block chain consists of a chain of blocks, where each block contains an immutable record of all the transactions or data. The application can easily access transactions by smart contracts. In this paper, our pinpoint is to build a decentralized crypto launch pad based on the Binance Smart Chain (BSC) block chain technology, which is known for its speed, security and low fees. A decentralized wallet add-on has been integrated with the website, so to buy new coins from us, customers need to have a MetaMusk or trust wallet, which they need to connect with our website.

title: Distributed Ledger Technology in Healthcare: Advancing Governance Architectures and Performance Engineering in Decentralized Ecosystems
authors: Sunil Kumar R M, Raghavendra M Devadas, Mrutyunjaya M S, Praveen Gujjar J, Sowmya T, Vani Hiremani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Healthcare data infrastructures remain fragmented, insecure, and characterized by low trust, hindering interoperable, patient-centric care. Centralized architectures struggle with multi-stakeholder governance, fine-grained accountability, and performance at scale under real-world workloads. Distributed ledger technology (DLT) promises immutability, distributed trust, auditability, and strong provenance, yet most healthcare implementations focus on narrow use cases, ad-hoc governance choices, or limited performance benchmarking. This paper designs and empirically evaluates an integrated governance architecture and performance engineering framework for healthcare DLT ecosystems. The approach combines (i) a theoretical synthesis of DLT governance and health data governance, (ii) architecture and protocol design for permissioned and hybrid platforms (e.g., Corda, Hyperledger Fabric, Ethereum-based frameworks, edge–blockchain hybrids), and (iii) testbed implementations with multi-scenario benchmarking across local, cloud, and edge-integrated deployments. Results from representative use cases—EHR interoperability, m-health/IoT streaming, and self-sovereign identity and consent—show that DLT-enabled governance can strengthen integrity, accountability, and patient control while achieving acceptable throughput–latency envelopes compared with centralized baselines, subject to explicit trade-offs between decentralization, audit granularity, and scalability. The paper distils design guidelines and reusable governance–performance patterns for large-scale, interoperable DLT health ecosystems, clarifying when to Favor platform choices, consensus mechanisms, and data-management strategies given domain-specific requirements.

title: Design and Implementation of an AI-Powered Autonomous Security Robotic Dog with Integrated Face Recognition
authors: Mohammed Mujeerulla, Shakkeera L, Zafar Ali Khan N, Hemanth G, Aditya Sharma, Dashami S, Harsha Vardhana T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Traditional security systems mostly use fixed surveillance cameras, which often result in persistent blind spots and provide little ability to respond in real time to security threats. To address these limitations, we present the design and implementation of an autonomous robotic security dog, which utilizes recent progress in mobile robotics and artificial intelligence. The robot was built on a specially designed, 3D-printed four-legged frame powered by 12 servo motors for flexible movement, all managed by a Raspberry Pi 5 computing unit. Its intelligence centers on a real-time face recognition system, for which we developed and compared two approaches: a lightweight option using Haarcascades with a Python library and a more advanced method based on the Insight Face deep learning framework paired with Facebook’s AI similarity search tools. In our analysis, the Haarcascade system achieved an average recognition rate of 92.4 % when running on a Raspberry Pi 5, whereas the Insight Face solution reached 95% accuracy on a high-end CPU workstation. This study confirms the potential of a modular robotic platform that strikes a practical balance between computational efficiency and recognition performance in active security environments, while also providing a solid foundation for future upgrades, such as autonomous navigation, more sensors, and responsive real-time behaviours.

title: Language Integration in Fine-Tuning Multimodal Large Language Models for Image-Based Regression
authors: Roy H. Jennings, Genady Paikin, Roy Shaul, Evgeny Soloveichik
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) show promise for image-based regression tasks, but current approaches face key limitations. Recent methods fine-tune MLLMs using preset output vocabularies and generic task-level prompts (e.g., "How would you rate this image?"), assuming this mimics human rating behavior. Our analysis reveals that these approaches provide no benefit over image-only training. Models using preset vocabularies and generic prompts perform equivalently to image-only models, failing to leverage semantic understanding from textual input. We propose Regression via Transformer-Based Classification (RvTC), which replaces vocabulary-constrained classification with a flexible bin-based approach. Unlike approaches that address discretization errors through complex distributional modeling, RvTC eliminates manual vocabulary crafting through straightforward bin increase, achieving state-of-the-art performance on four image assessment datasets using only images. More importantly, we demonstrate that data-specific prompts dramatically improve performance. Unlike generic task descriptions, prompts containing semantic information about specific images enable MLLMs to leverage cross-modal understanding. On the AVA dataset, adding challenge titles to prompts substantially improves our already state-of-the-art image-only baseline. We demonstrate through empirical evidence from the AVA and AGIQA-3k datasets that MLLMs benefit from semantic prompt information, surpassing mere statistical biases. We validate RvTC across two different MLLM architectures, demonstrating consistent improvements and method generalizability. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: DynaSplit: Latency-Aware Dynamic Model Partitioning for Large Language Model Inference in the Edge-Cloud Continuum
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Sandeep Shivam, Amit Kumar Padhy, Bharadwaj Vulugunda, Chaitanya Kulkarni, Chandrashekhar Medicherla
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have unlocked new possibilities for interactive intelligence on mobile and edge platforms. However, achieving sub- 300 ms end-to-end latency in real-world mobile environments remains an unsolved systems challenge. Pure cloud inference suffers from unpredictable round-trip delays, while fully local execution on commodity edge hardware is infeasible for multi-billion-parameter models. Existing edge-cloud LLM partitioning systems rely on static policies that ignore dynamic bandwidth fluctuations, interlayer activation variance, and the non-linear interactions of quantization and tensor parallelism. As a result, their latency degrades by up to 47<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>1Static partitioning: 710ms p95 vs. DynaSplit: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$290 \text{ms}=(710-290) / 290 \approx 145 \%$</tex> latency increase or <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1/1.45 \approx 47 \%$</tex> performance degradation. under typical 4G/5G volatility. We present DynaSplit, a latency-aware runtime system that dynamically selects (i) transformer-layer partition points, (ii) per-layer quantization levels, and (iii) cloud-side tensor-parallel degrees at fine-grained 100 ms intervals. DynaSplit incorporates a sub-millisecond performance model, a hybrid greedy-beam search optimizer with a search space reduction of >99.8%, and an asynchronous execution engine that overlaps GPU compute and RDMA transfers with up to 91 % pipeline utilization. Evaluated on an RTX A4000 edge node paired with a cloud A100 GPU and real 4G/5G-Fi traces, DynaSplit achieves 2.1-3.8 reduction in tail latency over DeepSpeed-Inference, PowerInfer, Orca, and vLLM for LLaMA-3-8B, Mixtral-8×7B, and Qwen-14B. To our knowledge, DynaSplit is the first system to consistently meet <300ms p95 latency for 8-14 B models on commodity edge hardware.

title: Design and Simulation of Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Machine Learning Models for Accelerated Big Data Classification
authors: Nida Afreen Rizvi, Shadab Pasha Khan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The research article presents a Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Machine Learning Model (QEHMLM) designed to accelerate data classification in Large-Scale Big Data Environments (LSBDE). The proposed framework integrates Quantum Computing (QC) with Classical Machine Learning Techniques (CMLT) including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Neural Network (Artificial-ANN) to overcome the computational and scalability limitations of classical approaches when processing High-Dimensional Datasets (HDD). The model leverages quantum feature encoding, entanglement-based transformations, and classical optimization to enhance accuracy and convergence speed. Simulations conducted using IBM Qiskit and TensorFlow Quantum on the Mobile Telecom Network Traffic Dataset validate the industrial applicability of the suggested approach in Applied Operational environment. The QEHMLM achieves notable improvements, recording 97% accuracy, 95% F1-score, and nearly 20% reduction in computational time compared to classical counterparts. These outcomes confirm that Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems (HQCS) provide a scalable, efficient, and high-performance paradigm for next-generation Big Data analytics and intelligent computational ecosystems.

title: HalluciSafe: Entailment-Driven Hallucination Suppression in LLM-Assisted Manuscripts
authors: Abirami A, S. Ponmaniraj
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The paper presents a hallucination suppression framework, HalluciSafe, that is an entailment-based system that upholds the suppression of hallucinations during the generation of academic manuscripts with the help of large language models (LLM). The system is applied in the form of the multi-stage pipeline, incorporating prompt-level risk control, claim-level verification, and human-in-the-loop correction. The LLM-generated manuscript drafts are initially broken down into factual claims as atoms with dependency-based claim extraction deployed during implementation. All of the claims are checked by a transformer-based Natural Language Inference model using human-written text and a reference corpus. It uses a Prompt Risk Scoring Algorithm to actively limit risky and unconstrained prompts prior to generation and avoids hallucination-prone results. Neutral that are contradicted claims are candidate to confidence masking and are refined, through author intervention over a structured refinement process. HalluciSafe experimental evaluation on the LLM-generated academic drafts indicates that the hallucination detection accuracy of HalluciSafe is 91.0%, surpassing by more than 11% the F1-score of only using NLI. Early risk control further lowers the incidence of hallucinations by over 60% of the high-risk prompts. The findings validate the fact that HalluciSafe is effective in reducing hallucination to a proactive and curative control process, which facilitates credible and dependable scholarship writing supported by the help of the LLM.

title: Hardware Security Modules in the Modern Era: Design, Scalability, and Integration with Emerging Technologies
authors: Dinesh Punde, Mandeep Singh, Balwinder Singh
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: In today’s increasingly interconnected digital landscape, securing sensitive data is of paramount importance. Hardware Security Modules (HSMs) have emerged as a critical solution to safeguard cryptographic keys and ensure secure data management. HSMs provide physical protection against cyberattacks and unauthorized access by offering a dedicated, tamper-resistant hardware environment for cryptographic operations. This paper explores the role of HSMs in modern security infrastructures, examining their design, implementation, and challenges in real-world applications. It also discusses the future directions of HSM technology, including advancements in cryptographic algorithms, the integration of machine learning for enhanced threat detection, and their expanding role in securing emerging technologies such as blockchain and IoT. Key challenges such as cost, scalability, and integration with modern technologies are highlighted, along with potential solutions to address these barriers. The paper concludes with recommendations for organizations considering HSM adoption and suggests areas for further research to enhance the effectiveness and accessibility of HSM technology.

title: SafeguardGS: 3D Gaussian Primitive Pruning While Avoiding Catastrophic Scene Destruction
authors: Yongjae Lee, Zhaoliang Zhang, Deliang Fan
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has advanced novel view synthesis, but its densification process leads to an excessive number of Gaussian primitives, which negatively impact rendering speed and memory usage. Although many 3DGS pruning techniques have been proposed to address this issue, a comprehensive analysis is still lacking. In this paper, we are the first to categorize 3DGS pruning techniques into two approaches: scene-level importance-threshold-based pruning and pixel-level importance-rank-based pruning, defined by their scope of importance calculation (scene-level vs. pixel-level) and their pruning criteria (threshold vs. rank). Our studies reveal that while the former leads to disastrous quality drops under extreme Gaussian primitive decimation, the latter not only sustains high rendering quality but also provides a natural pruning boundary, i.e., a safeguard for Gaussian pruning. We further propose multiple pruning score functions. From our extensive studies on various pruning score functions, we discover that color similarity with blending weight is the most effective factor for identifying insignificant primitives. In our experiments, our proposed method, SafeguardGS, with the selected score function achieves the highest PSNR-per-primitive performance under extreme pruning setting, retaining only about 10% of the primitives from the original 3DGS scene, i.e., 10× compression ratio.

title: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning Models for Hypoxemia Classification
authors: Sujata Ray, Debasmita Pradhan, Niranjan Kumar Ray
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Hypoxemia, is a critical medical condition which indicates the lower oxygen level in blood. Early prediction of hypoxemia is highly important for timely medical intervention as it may lead to health disasters including brain injury, heart and kidney damage. Machine learning models are widely and effectively used in almost all domains including healthcare. In this work a comparative analysis of machine learning models, including Logistic regression, Gradient Boosting, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machines (SVM), are used to predict hypoxemia severity using dataset including, heart rate, patient oxygen saturation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{SpO}_{2}\right)$</tex>, and other vital signs. Performance metrics such as precision, recall, accuracy, and F1-Score are computed to find the model predicts the severity of hypoxemia efficiently. From the results it is observed that the performance of Random Forest is better than other models compared with an average accuracy of 83% and accuracy of 88% for severe class.

title: PSA-MIL: A Probabilistic Spatial Attention-Based Multiple Instance Learning for Whole Slide Image Classification
authors: Sharon Peled, Yosef E. Maruvka, Moti Freiman
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Whole Slide Images (WSIs) are high-resolution digital scans widely used in medical diagnostics. Due to their immense size, WSI classification is typically approached using Multiple Instance Learning (MIL), where a slide is partitioned into individual tiles, disrupting its spatial structure. Recent MIL methods often incorporate spatial context through rigid spatial assumptions (e.g. fixed kernels), which limit their ability to capture the intricate tissue structures crucial for an accurate diagnosis. To address this limitation, we propose Probabilistic Spatial Attention MIL (PSA-MIL), a novel attention-based MIL framework that integrates spatial context into the attention mechanism through learnable distance-decayed priors, formulated within a probabilistic interpretation of self-attention as a posterior distribution. This formulation enables a dynamic inference of spatial relationships during training, eliminating the need for predefined assumptions often imposed by previous approaches. Furthermore, we introduce a diversity loss that promotes complementary spatial representations across attention heads and a spatial posterior-pruning strategy that reduces computational cost for long WSI sequences while preserving performance. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets and tasks show that PSA-MIL outperforms current baselines and achieves state-of-the-art results with substantially lower computational overhead. Our code is available at https://github.com/SharonPeled/PSA-MIL.

title: Fake News Detection Using LLM and RAG
authors: Praveen Gujjar J, Raghavendra M Devadas, Nikitha K, Lubna Ambreen, Sowmya T, Sunil Kumar R M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The increasing complexity and volume of user-generated content demand intelligent systems that not only analyze sentiment effectively but also demonstrate autonomous decision-making capabilities. This research proposes an Agentic AI Design Framework for sentiment analysis, integrating the principles of agency, autonomy, and adaptability within artificial intelligence systems. Unlike traditional sentiment analysis models that rely heavily on static rules or supervised learning, the agentic framework empowers AI models to dynamically interpret context, self-optimize learning pathways, and make independent decisions based on evolving data patterns. The framework combines multi-agent systems, reinforcement learning, and context-aware natural language processing (NLP) techniques to enhance both the accuracy and the responsiveness of sentiment classification. Experimental results across diverse datasets show significant improvements in adaptability to new domains and robustness against noise compared to conventional approaches. This study positions agentic AI as a transformative step in designing next-generation sentiment analysis systems capable of autonomous growth and deeper contextual understanding, opening avenues for broader applications in social media monitoring, customer feedback analysis, and beyond.

title: InteracTalker: Prompt-Based Human-Object Interaction with Co-Speech Gesture Generation
authors: Sreehari Rajan, Kunal Bhosikar, Charu Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating realistic human motions that naturally respond to both spoken language and physical objects is crucial for interactive digital experiences. Current methods, however, address speech-driven gestures or object interactions independently, limiting real-world applicability due to a lack of integrated, comprehensive datasets. To overcome this, we introduce InteracTalker, a novel framework that seamlessly integrates prompt-based object-aware interactions with co-speech gesture generation. We achieve this by employing a multi-stage training process to learn a unified motion, speech, and prompt embedding space. To support this, we curate a rich human-object interaction dataset, formed by augmenting an existing text-to-motion dataset with detailed object interaction annotations. Our framework utilizes a Generalized Motion Adaptation Module that enables independent training, adapting to the corresponding motion condition, which is then dynamically combined during inference. To address the imbalance between heterogeneous conditioning signals, we propose an adaptive fusion strategy, which dynamically reweights the conditioning signals during diffusion sampling. InteracTalker successfully unifies these previously separate tasks, outperforming prior methods in both co-speech gesture generation and object-interaction synthesis, outperforming gesture-focused diffusion methods, yielding highly realistic, object-aware full-body motions with enhanced realism, flexibility, and control. (https://sreeharirajan.github.io/projects/InteracTalker/)

title: OpenCV-Based Real-Time Multi-Modal Threat Detection and Alert System for Public Safety Applications
authors: S. P. Chokkalingam, Teja Thatiparthi, LaxmiNarayana Kaya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The introduction of efficient systems which can respond quickly and accurately will result in the safety of the people in crowded or dangerous places. A real-time Threat Detection and Alert System is presented in this paper, which is implemented using OpenCV and utilizes computer vision and deep learning to recognize hazards such as guns, fights, or flames. The intention is to create a system that can ’see’, ’hear’ and ’move’ just like a human being to understand its environment. Live video will be taken using OpenCV, and then every frame will be processed to focus the attention on the important parts of the scene. While the YOLOv8 deep learning model identifies weapons, the 3D Convolutional Neural Networks (3D-CNNs) monitor human’s gestures to classify actions as Fighting or Aggressive. An audio-based CNN will similarly be listening for shocking sounds—screams or gunshots—for instance—to corroborate the camera’s detection. The combination of all these inputs is done through a weighted Decision model which gives a single score indicating the likelihood of a threat’s presence. The system will immediately trigger a loud alarm sound and display a red warning on the screen if this score goes beyond a predetermined limit, thus instantly notifying the closest people to either evacuate or stay safely. The tests carried out in real-life situations showed that the system is very effective, with high accuracy and almost no delay. It is rapid, reliable, and easy to implement, thus making it ideal for locations such as public surveillance systems, train stations, schools, or even smart city infrastructure. By combining visual, movement, and sound analysis, this system offers an intelligent and responsive Approach to public safety beyond previous capabilities.

title: Empowering Source-Free Domain Adaptation via MLLM-Guided Reliability-Based Curriculum Learning
authors: Dongjie Chen, Kartik Patwari, Zhengfeng Lai, Xiaoguang Zhu, Sen-Ching Cheung, Chen-Nee Chuah
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Existing SFDA methods struggle to fully use pre-trained knowledge and often rely on a single model’s predictions or handcrafted prompts, limiting robustness under domain shift.
