title: Fully Data-Driven Inductance Reconstruction for Position-Sensorless Control of Switched Reluctance Motors
authors: Gyuwon Kim, Huibeom Youn, Minje Cho, Jaepil Ban
abstract: This study proposes a fully data-driven rotor position estimation method for sensorless control of switched reluctance motors (SRMs) under diverse operating conditions. The entire control process is performed online, spanning from initial startup to full-cycle position estimation without any prior motor information. For the initial startup and low-speed operation, a threshold-triggered slope drive (TTSD) method is proposed. While driving using TTSD, inductance extrema are computed and stored to construct an unsaturated inductance profile. Subsequently, an inductance vector method is used for estimating rotor position and is performed concurrently with the reconstruction of the energized phase inductance using an unsaturated inductance profile. Through this method, the rotor position is accurately estimated over the entire cycle, regardless of high-load operating conditions. Furthermore, an adaptive method for compensating the reconstructed inductance is also introduced to achieve robust estimation even under bus voltage fluctuations, without requiring a voltage sensor. The proposed method can be implemented through a simple online estimation process with minimal memory usage. Experimental results confirm its capability to achieve accurate rotor position estimation and robust operation without relying on model parameters, offline data, or additional hardware.

title: An Improved and Provably Secure EDHOC Protocol Supporting the Extended Canetti–Krawczyk (eCK) Security Model
authors: Awaneesh Kumar Yadav, Madhusanka Liyanage, An Braeken
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Transport Layer Security (TLS) is considered to be the most used standard security protocol for the Internet of Things (IoT). However, as TLS was originally designed for computer networks, it is not optimal with respect to efficiency. Therefore, a new protocol called Object Security for Constrained RESTful Environments (OSCORE) has been standardized for securing constrained devices. Currently, the Ephemeral Diffie Hellman Over COSE (EDHOC) protocol, which is a key exchange protocol to define a session key used in OSCORE, is also in the process of being standardized. This paper shows that the four authentication modes of the EDHOC protocol are vulnerable in the extended Canetti–Krawczyk (eCK) security model, which is a common security model used in IoT. In addition, also resistance to Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks is weak. Taking this into account, we propose two new variants of EDHOC. The first variant, EDHOC2, is able to overcome both issues but has a slightly higher cost for communication, computation, storage, and energy consumption. The second variant, EDHOC3, offers only additional protection in the eCK security model and has, on average, similar, even better performance in one authentication mode, compared to EDHOC. Additionally, the Real-Or-Random (ROR) logic and Scyther validation tool are employed to ensure the security of the designed variants. Furthermore, a prototype implementation is conducted to demonstrate the real-time deployment of the designed versions.

title: Joint Activity Detection and Channel Estimation for Asynchronous mMTC: A Model-Driven GNN Approach
authors: Junyu Xie, Jing Guo, Zhong Zheng, Lou Zhao, Zesong Fei
abstract: This work investigates the joint activity detection and channel estimation (JADCE) problem in asynchronous up-link massive machine-type communications (mMTC), where a model-driven graph neural network (GNN) approach is developed under the variational inference framework. Specifically, we first formulate the JADCE problem as an equivalent channel estimation task and derive its optimal variational distribution. Then, all potential access time slots of each device are modeled as nodes to construct a homogeneous graph, and the learnable GNN layers are integrated with the variational inference process to efficiently infer the associated hyperparameters. To further reduce the computational complexity and enhance estimation performance, a residual-based aggregation strategy and a multi-task update architecture are incorporated into the GNN design. Finally, simulation results show that our approach achieves better performance in both channel estimation and activity detection compared with benchmarks, while maintaining acceptable computational complexity and strong scalability.

title: Information-Theoretic Sensor Control for Multiple Extended Target Tracking Using Adaptive Hölder Divergence
authors: Yating Liu, Guoqing Qi, Yinya Li, Andong Sheng
abstract: This article proposes an adaptive sensor control strategy within the GGIW-LMB filtering framework for elliptical extended target tracking, aiming to jointly optimize kinematic states, target extent, and cardinality. With improving sensor resolution, shape information (e.g., target size) has become increasingly important for tracking and decision-making. In practice, however, heavy clutter, missed detections, and time-varying target numbers significantly degrade the accuracy of target state and cardinality estimation. To address these challenges, a closed-form approximation of the Hölder divergence is derived specifically for the GGIW-LMB filtering framework, which enables efficient information-theoretic sensor control without particle-based numerical methods. Building on this result, an adaptive Hölder-divergencebased sensor control strategy is developed, in which the divergence parameter is dynamically adjusted according to uncertainties in target existence and state estimates. The proposed algorithm jointly accounts for kinematics and shape parameters of elliptical extended targets and improves robustness in challenging scenarios. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated through simulation results.

title: SpineVLM: A Markdown-Guided Structured Fine-Tuning Framework for Spine X-ray Report Generation
authors: Dong Liu, Wenhui Li, Ning Xu, Guoge Han, Rui Hao, Xianzhu Liu, An-An Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Automated medical report generation in specialized fields like spine radiography is constrained by data scarcity and high annotation costs. Consequently, existing multimodal large language models (MLLMs) struggle in these settings, often missing minute, scattered spinal abnormalities. We introduce SpineVLM, a data-efficient framework for structured spine X-ray report generation. The framework is built upon the newly constructed SXRG dataset, comprising 10,468 image-report pairs developed via a hierarchical AI-assisted annotation pipeline. To optimize learning under limited data, we propose Markdown-Guided Structured Learning (MGSL), which reformulates unconstrained free-text synthesis into a structured completion task, acting as a strong regularizer. Furthermore, an unsupervised Region-Focused Inference (RFI) module powered by foundation models (DINOv2) isolates the vertebral column to enhance the perception of subtle lesions without requiring manual spatial annotations. Evaluated on a 7B-parameter vision-language backbone, SpineVLM achieves strong performance against ten baseline multimodal models across standard linguistic metrics. In a double-blind reader study, the system achieved a diagnostic F1-score of 0.866, comparable to specialist performance, while reducing clinical reporting time by over 41%. By open-sourcing the dataset and codebase, we provide, to our knowledge, the first quantitative benchmark for automated spine radiography report generation, together with a structured framework for this data-limited setting. All data and code will be publicly released at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/LiuDongDaniel/SpineVLM</uri>.

title: Decoding Cancer Tissues: A Comparative Deep Learning View of Breast Histopathology
authors: Sanya Malik, Maanasvee Khetan, Reya Oberoi, Hima Deepthi Vankayalapati
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Worldwide, breast cancer continues to be one of the riskiest killers of women, so early and correct diagnoses will be pivotal to treatment. As the field progresses in deep learning, detection of histopathological images has revolutionized the development of automated, reliable and scalable diagnostics.In this study, we present a thorough comparative analysis of six deep learning image classification architectures, including, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), ResNet50, DenseNet, EfficientNetV2-B0, MobileNetV1, and InceptionV3 in classifying breast cancer histopathological images from the BreaKHis dataset. We report each model performance based upon various key metrics of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score in order to evaluate e Diagnoses Capability and computational efficiency of models. Our study identified significant differences between framework performances, with the transfer models out performing CNN models in accuracy and generalization ability. We provide an insight into the strengths of utilizing deep learning framework architectures for medical imaging and provide new directions for more powerful AI-assisted diagnostic processes.

title: Generative AI-Driven Framework for Zero-Day Attack Prediction in Next-Gen Cybersecurity Using FusionGuardNet with DualInstinct Optimizer
authors: Nilesh Dnyaneshwar Bhandarwar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The rapid escalation of zero-day attacks has become one of the most critical threats in modern digital ecosystems, exposing vulnerabilities even in advanced cybersecurity infrastructures. These unpredictable and evolving attacks demand adaptive, intelligent, and proactive defense mechanisms capable of identifying zero-day attack. To address this challenge, this paper introduces a hybrid generative AI-powered framework named FusionGuardNet, with the DualInstinct Optimizer (FGN-DIO), designed to enhance the predictability and resilience of next-generation cybersecurity systems. The proposed FusionGuardNet integrates multiple deep learning principles, enabling simultaneous learning of spatial, temporal, and contextual dependencies for robust pattern recognition. To achieve efficient convergence and adaptive parameter optimization, the DualInstinct Optimizer, combining swarm intelligence and cooperative search strategies, ensures stable learning and superior generalization. Experimental evaluation on the CICIDS2017 dataset demonstrates that the proposed model achieves higher accuracy (97%), precision (97%), recall (97%), and F1-score (97%) compared to conventional techniques. Overall, the FGN-DIO provides a scalable and intelligent solution for zero-day attack prediction, establishing a new direction for proactive and resilient next-generation cybersecurity systems.

title: Investigation of Graphene Oxide Coating Methods in Laser-induced Graphene-based Humidity Sensor
authors: Aruloli Kathirvel, Kalpana Settu
abstract: Graphene oxide (GO) is an important sensing material in the fabrication of Laser-induced graphene (LIG) based humidity sensors for health monitoring. The adhesion quality of GO is exceptional, allowing it to adhere to various substrates for an extended period. However, the coating of GO before and after the laser irradiation of the polyimide (PI) film contributes significantly to the performance of the humidity sensor. Herein, two different existing fabrication techniques were used to develop capacitive-based humidity sensors. One humidity sensor was fabricated by coating the GO on PI film before the irradiation of the laser to form an interdigitated capacitor (GO/LIG-IDC), and another by coating the GO on an interdigitated capacitor (LIG-IDC/GO). The fabricated humidity sensor was tested for humidity levels varying from 20% to 90% relative humidity (RH). The GO/LIG-IDC humidity sensor shows better sensitivity of 27.97 pF/%RH compared to the LIG-IDC/GO humidity sensor with the sensitivity of 11.73 pF/%RH.

title: A Federated Learning Framework for Quality Inspection and Anomaly Detection in AI-Enabled Manufacturing Systems for Sustainable production
authors: K. Thiruppathi, Gururama Senthilvel P, M. Arivukarasi, P. Kalaivani, G. Vennila
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This study introduces a blockchain-enabled federated learning system for intelligent, privacy-preserving quality assessment in dispersed industrial contexts. To handle non-IID and heterogeneous datasets, the suggested system uses multimodal feature extraction from visual, vibration, and temperature data with adaptive federated optimisation. Smart contracts and a blockchain trust layer avoid data tampering and model poisoning by verifying model modifications transparently. Experimental assessments employing casting, electronics, and assembly datasets show 32% accuracy, 14.2% precision, and 16.8% recall advantages over centralised models, coupled with 40% lower communication costs. Results confirm system scalability, energy efficiency, and resilience, making it suitable for real-time industrial implementation. The proposed system provides safe, interpretable, and decentralised Industry 4.0 quality assurance and predictive maintenance.

title: AI-Driven Adaptive Turbo Decoding Framework for Dynamic IoT Communication Environments
authors: P.Balakrishna, Pramod Kumar P
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) enables large-scale connectivity across diverse devices, but maintaining reliable communication under dynamic channel conditions remains a significant challenge. Conventional turbo decoders, though effective in 4G and 5G systems, operate with fixed parameters and lack adaptability when faced with fluctuating signal-to-noise ratios (SNR), interference, and fading—typical in IoT networks. This paper presents an AI-driven adaptive turbo decoding framework that enhances decoding efficiency and reliability through intelligent parameter tuning. The proposed model integrates a reinforcement learning (RL) agent with the Log-MAP turbo decoding algorithm to dynamically optimize iteration count and decision thresholds based on real-time channel estimations. Simulations in MATLAB and Python demonstrate that the AI-enhanced decoder achieves up to 25% improvement in bit-error rate (BER) and 30% reduction in decoding latency compared to traditional methods. The framework offers a scalable, software-based solution for energy-efficient, adaptive communication in IoT and future 6G networks, eliminating the need for hardware modification.

title: Dual-Frequency Ultrasonic Method for Wide-Range, High-Precision Non-Contact Vibration Measurement
authors: Haneesh Sankar T P, Nithya P, Rajesh K R
abstract: This paper presents a novel dual-frequency ultrasonic method for non-contact vibration measurement. Conventional techniques such as laser vibrometry and pulse-echo detection, while widely used for non-contact vibration measurement, are sensitive to alignment or surface reflectivity and often fail in harsh environments. Single-frequency ultrasonic phase-tracking can achieve micrometer resolution, but its measurable amplitude is limited to half the acoustic wavelength, restricting its use for larger vibrations. The proposed method transmits two closely spaced ultrasonic tones and tracks their differential phase, producing a synthetic wavelength much larger than the carrier. This extends the unambiguous amplitude range to the centimeter scale while retaining micrometer-level sensitivity. Because only the phase modulation caused by target motion is tracked, the measurement is independent of absolute distance. The frequency spacing is software-tunable, enabling adaptive trade-offs between range and sensitivity. A theoretical framework, simulations, and validation results confirm low error for amplitudes from 1 µm to 1 cm. Requiring only narrowband transducers and simple processing, the method is robust against temperature, radiation, and electromagnetic noise, making it suitable for structural health monitoring, machinery diagnostics, biomedical sensing, and hazardous environments.

title: Remote Healthcare Monitoring for the Aging Population: A Systematic Review of Datasets, Techniques, and Challenges
authors: Abha Tewari, Dhananjay Kalbande, Sanchi Khopkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The rapid growth of the aging population has increased the demand for innovative healthcare solutions, particularly in fall detection, vital sign monitoring, and activities of daily living. Older adults are more vulnerable to health concerns, and timely intervention through remote monitoring systems Can help mitigate the risks that come with sudden illnesses. and falls. The paper describes a systematic literature survey. (SLS) of remote monitoring technologies for elderly healthcare. focusing on the datasets, techniques, and methodologies published. During the period 2019-2025, the survey highlights the integration of deep learning models and telehealth technologies to provide continuous and real-time care to the elderly, supporting global health objectives. We analyze 15 publicly available datasets, examining their characteristics, sensor modalities and preprocessing techniques, and applicability to machine learning and deep learning. (DL) models. Main challenges include diversity of datasets, real-world Applicability, and relevant privacy concerns are discussed along with potential solutions such as multi-sensor fusion, hybrid models. and edge computing. The findings provide guidelines for researchers and practitioners, while providing a roadmap for future advances in Remote healthcare monitoring of the aging population.

title: A DeBERTa-Based Claim Verification Framework with Integrated Retrieval and NLI Reasoning
authors: Manthri Parthiv, Kethavath Sai Naik, Ms. Prachi Dusa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In today’s world, internet usage has gone up drastically, and we have a rapid spread of misinformation online, which is difficult for users to identify as factual content or fabricated claims, leading to large-scale public confusion and reducing trust in digital news sources. To address this issue, this paper presents a DeBERTa-based claim verification framework that integrates multi-source evidence retrieval with Natural Language Inference (NLI), which enables real-time fact-checking. The system retrieves evidence from trusted news platforms like GNews, Wikipedia, and research corpora through a hybrid pipeline that combines keyword-based and semantic search methods.The retrieved documents are then ranked and passed to a fine-tuned DeBERTa model trained on the FEVER dataset, which helps classify claims as SUPPORTS, REFUTES, or NOT ENOUGH INFO. To enhance explainability, the framework provides brief evidence snippets and an LLM-generated explanation of the model’s reasoning, thus making this system a reliable approach for fact verification in dynamic news environments.

title: Learning spatio-temporal feature representations for video-based gaze estimation
authors: Alexandre Personnic, Mihai Bâce
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Video-based gaze estimation methods aim to capture the inherently temporal dynamics of human eye gaze from multiple image frames. However, since models must capture both spatial and temporal relationships, performance is limited by the feature representations within a frame but also between multiple frames. We propose the Spatio-Temporal Gaze Network (ST-Gaze), a model that combines a CNN backbone with dedicated channel attention and self-attention modules to fuse eye and face features optimally. The fused features are then treated as a spatial sequence, allowing for the capture of an intra-frame context, which is then propagated through time to model inter-frame dynamics. We evaluated our method on the EVE dataset and show that ST-Gaze achieves state-of-the-art performance both with and without person-specific adaptation. Additionally, our ablation study provides further insights into the model performance, showing that preserving and modelling intra-frame spatial context with our spatio-temporal recurrence is fundamentally superior to premature spatial pooling. As such, our results pave the way towards more robust video-based gaze estimation using commonly available cameras.

title: Kullback-Leibler Adversarial Training for Robustness Against Word-Substitution Attacks in Transformer-Based Arabic Text Classifier
authors: Hanin Alshalan, Banafsheh Rekabdar
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: Transformer-based models significantly advanced natural language processing (NLP), yet they remained vulnerable to adversarial attacks. While existing research primarily focused on English, a notable gap existed in evaluating and defending models for morphologically rich languages like Arabic. To address this, we proposed a Kullback-Leibler divergence-based adversarial training method (KL-AT) to enhance the robustness of Arabic Transformer-based models against score-based wordsubstitution attacks. KL-AT regularized the learning process by aligning model predictions between clean and adversarial inputs, enabling the model to resist perturbations to the greatest extent possible. We evaluated our method using the AraBERT model on two large-scale Arabic datasets, HARD and BRAD. Experimental results showed improved classification accuracy and resilience against two strong score-based attacks, TextFooler and PWWS. Specifically, KL-AT achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 \%}$</tex> increase in accuracy under both attacks TextFooler and PWWS, respectively. These findings highlighted the effectiveness of KL-AT in defending Arabic NLP classifiers against adversarial attacks at the word level.

title: An Evaluation of Zero-Trust Security Models on Institutional Data Protection in Zimbabwean Universities
authors: Gladman Jekese, Thanks Hondoma, Netsayi Chabata, Kundayi G Chinyongo, Bridget Munyoro
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: With Zimbabwean universities adopting digitalization through online learning environments, cloud-based research data storage and administrative systems, safeguarding sensitive institutional data has become an utmost priority. This research assesses the effect of Zero-Trust Security Model implementation in strengthening data protection among public and private universities in Zimbabwe. Traditional perimeter-based security proves inadequate against the intelligent persistent attacks, insider threats and increasingly mobile and decentralized IT infrastructures. The Zero-Trust model fundamentally revamps the security approach based on an assumption of compromise and the need for continuous authentication of all users, devices and traffic irrespective of location. The research adopts a mixed-methods approach consisting of technical audits of current security architectures and interviews with IT directors, system administrators and cybersecurity officers in five large Zimbabwean universities. Analysis of incident reports, access logs and data breach metrics are further utilized to establish a correlation between Zero-Trust policy implementation and data leak mitigation. Results shows that institutions that have implemented core Zero-Trust features report fewer instances of data breach and better detection of unusual behavior. Yet, there are still obstacles that hinders institutions from rolling out Zero-Trust solutions. This study offers a readiness model and implementation plan specific to higher learning institutions in resource-scarce situations. The findings of this study are important to policymakers, university senates and IT governance boards intent on updating their security posture. The study further contributes to national and regional discussion on cyber-readiness of universities in the pursuit of Fourth Industrial Revolution objectives as well as safe digital learning environments.

title: Dynamic Global Illumination for Interactive Gaussian Splatting Scenes in Real Time_supp1-3689199.mp4
authors: Sheng Li
abstract: We present a real-time pipeline to approximate global illumination in interactive or dynamic scenes, including both 3D Gaussian models and conventional meshes. Building on a formulated surface light transport model for 3D Gaussians, we address key performance challenges through a fast compound stochastic ray-tracing algorithm and a hardware 3D Gaussian rasterizer, and we implement multiple RTGI (real-time global illumination) techniques for 3D Gaussians. Our pipeline enables real-time rendering of interactive scenes featuring editable materials, lights, meshes, and 3D Gaussian models, effectively capturing multi-bounce diffuse and one-bounce glossy light transport. Our approach covers a wide range of dynamic light sources, including area lights, directional lights, and environmental lighting. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that our approach can efficiently render global illumination for both 3DGS models and hybrid models combining meshes and 3DGS, all in real time. Our pipeline highlights the potential of 3D Gaussians in real-time global illumination, and offers insights into performance optimization.

title: Multiphysics-Informed Adaptive Personalized Federated Learning for Battery State-of-Health Estimation
authors: Jiayin Xiao, Yujie Wang, Song Wang, Yin-Yi Soo, Zonghai Chen
abstract: Accurate state-of-health estimation is critical for the safe and reliable operation of Lithium-ion batteries, but developing robust data-driven models is often hindered by data privacy concerns and the distributed nature of the data. Federated learning (FL) offers a privacy-preserving solution, yet its performance is severely challenged by the statistical heterogeneity and data imbalance inherent in real-world distributed battery data. Furthermore, the opaque nature of conventional FL models impedes the cross-institutional trust required for collaborative training. To address these limitations, this article proposes a novel multiphysics-informed adaptive personalized federated learning (MP-APFL) framework. At the client level, we first develop a multiphysics-informed, multistage aging partial differential equation (PDE) that combines analytical models for key degradation mechanisms, such as solid electrolyte interphase formation, thermal effects, and lithium plating-induced “knee points,” with a data-driven term to account for complex unmodeled dynamics. Subsequently, this governing PDE is embedded as a soft constraint within a multiphysics-informed neural network via an adaptively weighted composite loss function that synergistically enforces physical consistency, maintains parameter realism, and ensures trend plausibility by penalizing unphysical jumps while permitting valid recovery, guiding each local model to learn physically plausible and interpretable aging dynamics. At the system level, we design MP-APFL to employ a privacy-preserving hybrid architecture with a shared encoder and personalized multiphysics components. This is orchestrated by an adaptive strategy featuring Information value-driven client prioritization for efficient client selection and physics-guided and stability-aware encoder aggregation for robust model fusion. Comprehensive experiments on laboratory-generated and public datasets demonstrate that MP-APFL significantly outperforms prominent FL benchmarks, particularly in highly heterogeneous and imbalanced settings.

title:
CoL
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    A: Convolution-free Local Linear Attention for SpatioTemporal Event Processing
authors: Yusuke Sekikawa, Jun Nagata, Itsumi Araki, Andreu Girbau
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Linear attention is sparse, recurrent, and GPU-parallel; these are essential features for processing sparse data from event-based cameras. We argue that locality is missing to efficiently model event-to-event relationships for continuous spatiotemporal perception. We propose CoL<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>A by introducing locality into linear attention without using a computationally demanding convolution operation. The key idea for the convolution-free formulation is restricting the positional embedding local convolutional kernel into the special class which can be decomposed into two global positional embeddings that can be absorbed into query and key; this replaces convolution with a local sum. To the best of our knowledge, CoL<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>A is the first to equip sparsity, recurrence, GPU parallelism and locality, simultaneously. We demonstrate CoL<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>A’s effectiveness on dense, high-temporal-resolution (> 1000 fps ) prediction task from events, demonstrating real-time capability while maintaining competitive results over the conventional method. https://github.com/DensoITLab/CoLA

title: When ‘YES’ Meets &lsquo;but&rsquo;: Can AI Comprehend Contradictory Humor in Comics?_supp1-3688191.pdf
authors: Yu Yin
abstract: Understanding humor, especially when it involves complex and contradictory narratives requiring comparative reasoning ability, remains a significant challenge for large vision language models (VLMs). This limitation hinders AI's ability to engage in human-like reasoning and cultural expression. In this paper, we investigate this challenge through an in depth analysis of comics that juxtapose panels to create humor through contradictions. We introduce the YESBUT, a novel benchmark with 1,262 comic images from diverse multilingual and multicultural contexts, featuring comprehensive annotations that capture various aspects of narrative understanding. Using this benchmark, we systematically evaluate a wide range of VLMs through four complementary tasks spanning from surface content comprehension to deep narrative reasoning, with particular emphasis on comparative reasoning between contradictory elements. Our extensive experiments reveal that even the most advanced models significantly underperform compared to humans, with common failures in visual perception, key element identification, comparative analysis, and hallucinations. We further investigate text-based training strategies and social knowledge augmentation methods to enhance model performance. Our findings not only highlight critical weaknesses in VLMs' understanding of cultural and creative expressions but also provide pathways toward developing context-aware models capable of deeper narrative understanding through comparative reasoning.

title: Remote Sensing for Understanding the Vertical Dimension of Urban Landscapes: A review on data, products, methods, and prospects
authors: Haiming Qin, Weiqi Zhou, Zhe Zhu, Qihao Weng
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Magazine
abstract: Quantifying the vertical structure of urban landscapes is the prerequisite to understand its social and ecological impacts. Remote sensing provides an important tool for data acquisition and pattern analysis on the vertical structure of urban landscapes. Therefore, a comprehensive review of how remote sensing can facilitate the quantification of the vertical structure of urban landscapes is not only desirable for the remote sensing community but also for the increasing number of scholars who are broadly interested in 3D related issues. Here, we present a comprehensive review of the current statuses of data, products, methods, and applications for quantifying the vertical dimension of urban landscapes using remote sensing. We further discussed the challenges now facing and future research needed. Specifically, we first reviewed and compared the remote sensing data that could be used to extract the vertical information of urban landscapes, including passive optical data, LiDAR data, SAR data, and street view imagery. We then presented a detailed list of the digital surface model (DSM) and ground object height products derived from remotely sensed data that are readily used for quantifying the vertical structure of urban landscapes, ranging from global-scale products to local ones with finer resolution. We also reviewed key approaches for urban vertical structure analysis, which included urban land cover classification, analysis of the vertical structure of urban architectures, greenspace, and comprehensive landscapes, as well as analysis of the dynamics of urban vertical structure. Additionally, we synthesized applications of urban vertical structure in six core fields: urban heat islands and microclimates, disaster risks, biodiversity, energy and resource consumption, human settlements and social equity, and urban management and planning. Finally, we discussed current challenges and future opportunities, such as limitations and tradeoffs of remote sensing technologies for vertical analysis, extracting vertical information by fusing multi-source remote sensing data, deep learning for high-resolution and high-quality DSM generation, establishing a comprehensive index system for urban vertical structure quantification, monitoring the dynamics of 3D urban landscapes based on long-time-series data, exploring the human perception of 3D urban landscapes, and addressing uncertainty and robustness. To our knowledge, this paper is the first work that comprehensively integrates these elements around the urban vertical structure. It provides abundant information on the quantification of the vertical structure of urban landscapes using remote sensing and offers valuable insights for the remote sensing and 3D research community.

title: Enhancing SME Financial Accuracy through Prompt Engineering Services to Address Manual Recording Challenges (Phenomenological Study)
authors: Khalid Muhammad Alfarisi, Riza Rizqiyah
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: The majority of Indonesian Small to Medium Enterprises (SMEs) still remain on using manual financial recording despite digital tools being widespread and available to access, resulting in hindered efficiency and data inaccuracies. AI technology are currently on the rise, creating new jobs on tuning AI to fit individual needs. Earlier studies predominantly focuses on AI adoption in large enterprises, overlooking prompt engineering potentials as a bridge on smaller enterprise digitalization. This research gaps are addressed through this research by studying the lived experiences and operational workflows of seven Small to Medium Enterprise (SME) owners and accountant to identify resistance factors and adoption perquisites through a phenomenological approach. High AI enthusiasm and low technical readiness are revealed in this research, currently 70% of the participants uses manual/hybrid recording. The research also reveal that tiered prompt engineering service monetization to be crucial, it helps improving financial accuracy by 27.3% and predictability by 34.5%. This research shows scalable monetization processes that match highlevel computational intelligence with technical and resource-based limitations of emerging economies, therefore supporting SDG 8 and SDG 9.

title: Flying Blind: In-Ground Effect Enabled Haptic Teleoperation of Uncrewed Aerial Vehicles
authors: Nicholas C. Schneider, Jacob M. Anderson, Mathias A. Schoen, Kam K. Leang, Edoardo Battaglia
abstract: Uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly deployed in environments with limited visibility, such as smoke, dust, rain, or fog. In these conditions, conventional visual or range sensors provide little useful information to the operator. To address this, we present a new approach that converts aerodynamic in-ground effect (IGE) interactions into haptic feedback cues. These cues are derived from variations in UAV power without relying on sensors that may fail in low-visibility conditions. We describe the modeling, hardware, and software used to measure and render IGE-based haptic feedback, and evaluate participants' ability to discriminate heights in a user study. Results show users can distinguish different ground distances using only the haptic cues, highlighting the potential of IGEbased feedback in UAV teleoperation. Finally, we present a demonstration experiment where a user is flying the UAV blindly along a path through IGE haptic feedback, showcasing our system's ability to aid navigation under low-visibility conditions.

title: Clustering Disaggregated Household Consumption Expenditure for Policy Targeting: Evidence from Malaysia's HES 2022
authors: En Lee, Thian Song Ong, Yvonne Lee
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: This paper investigates whether disaggregated household consumption can be used to identify households facing budget constraints by applying machine learning clustering on household-head observations from Household Expenditure Survey (HES) in Malaysia. Unsupervised clustering is performed using K-means and Gaussian Mixture Models to segment households based on their consumption profiles. We transform the original <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3}$</tex> disaggregated consumption expenditure features into per-capita scale using OECD equivalence scale. To address the inherent highly right-skewed and sparsity data, we augment the dataset with two more binary features and ten engineered summary features. The performance of K-means and Gaussian Mixture Models are then evaluated across four alternate preprocessing pipelines (Robust scaling, Winsorization + Robust scaling, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{K}$</tex>-distance trimming, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{K}$</tex>-distance trimming <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{+}$</tex> Winsorization). Following preprocessing, dimensionality reduction is performed by using Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and clustering quality is then assessed across different preprocessing pipelines using silhouette score. Results suggest the presence of four stable and interpretable clusters for policy recommendations. Among them, two clusters are identified as budget-constrained groups with low total spending and disproportionately high allocations of Food and Beverage spendings. The best-performing pipeline is K-Means model on the robust pipeline with silhouette score of 0.7031. These findings demonstrates that disaggregated consumption data, combined with simple engineered features allow the K-Means model to effectively segment households into well separated clusters. This study contributes to evidence-based policymaking by supporting target intervention such as cash transfer for budget-constrained groups. Moreover, this study also presents a reproducible preprocessing and clustering pipeline to handle sparse and highly skewed data.

title: Humanizing the Machine: A Study of Chatbot Continuance Intention in the Indonesian Banking Sector
authors: Keith Kanto, Nathanael Joshua Heriyando, Valdo Reyes Kurniadi, Santy
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: While banking chatbots are widely deployed, the drivers of long-term continuance in emerging markets remain under-researched. This study addresses this gap by integrating the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) and the Expectation-Confirmation Model (ECM) to examine chatbot's continuance usage in Indonesia. Analyzing survey data via PLS-SEM, the results show that Information Quality and Expectation Confirmation are critical to Customer Satisfaction, while Perceived Usefulness, Trust, and satisfaction most strongly predict continuance intention. The findings reveal that service quality does not significantly impact trust or satisfaction, suggesting a fundamental shift toward functional and cognitive evaluations in AI driven environments. This research demonstrates that conventional service quality metrics have limited explanatory power for autonomous agents. Practically, banks should prioritize information accuracy and expectation management over traditional service dimensions to ensure sustainable digital strategies.

title: Industrial Worker Safety Enhancement Through IOT-Driven Fatigue Detection Using Machine Learning
authors: K. Elavarasi, D. Dhivya, B. Sabeshna
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: The Industrial Worker Safety Enhancement through IoT-Driven Fatigue Detection Using Machine Learning project is aimed at uplifting the level of occupational safety and health of workers in industrial environments by an intelligent, real-time fatigue monitoring system. The proposed system uses several IoT-based wearable sensors, like a heart rate monitor, body temperature sensor, motion sensor, and eye-blink or EEG sensor, to record the physiological and behavioural changes of the workers. The recorded data are sent by the ESP32 microcontroller to the cloud platform, where continuous monitoring is done. A machine learning model, which is already trained, compares the data from the sensors to find out the earliest signs of fatigue, stress, or any abnormal activity patterns that may lead to danger to the worker’s safety. The system sends the worker and supervisor the alerts about the detected condition that is fatigue or irregularity through mobile or webbased dashboards along with a buzzer or vibration signal for the onsite notification. The entire information is stored for subsequent analysis and prediction of the trend. By the integration of IoT-driven data acquisition with machine learning-based prediction, the system provides the means for the prevention of the employee’s interventions; thus, the fatiguerelated accidents are reduced and productivity is improved. This is a project that shows the successful and scalable approach of the management of industrial safety by the use of intelligent technology-driven fatigue detection and monitoring.

title: Analytical Technology Infrastructure and Decision Making Efficiency in the Retail Sector
authors: Arya Kemal, Chato Meyer Alexander, Muhammad Ridho, Robertus Nugroho Perwiro Atmojo
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: This study aims to determine the extent to which the proposed model and data-driven technological aspects influence decision-making effectiveness in the retail industry. The analysis focuses on three independent variables-Big Data, Data Quality, and Data Analysis-evaluated against the dependent variable of decision-making effectiveness. The empirical findings demonstrate that all three variables have a significant impact on decision-making effectiveness, as evidenced by the hypothesis testing results: Data Analysis (T-statistics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.550; \mathrm{P}$</tex>-value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.000$</tex>), Big Data (T-statistics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=2.246; P$</tex>-value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.025$</tex>), and Data Quality (T-statistics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=3.255; \mathrm{P}$</tex>-value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.001$</tex>). These results provide strong empirical evidence that data analytics capabilities and data management quality play a crucial role in supporting strategic decision-making processes within the retail sector.

title: Q-AuthNet: A Hybrid Quantum-Classical Neural Framework for Deepfake Authentication with Explainability
authors: Uroosa Fatima Zaidi, Rakesh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: This paper presents a Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Machine Learning Model (QEHMLM) designed to accelerate data classification in Large-Scale Big Data Environments (LSBDE). The proposed framework integrates Quantum Computing (QC) with Classical Machine Learning Techniques (CMLT) including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Neural Network (Artificial-ANN) to overcome the computational and scalability limitations of classical approaches when processing High-Dimensional Datasets (HDD). The model leverages quantum feature encoding, entanglement-based transformations, and classical optimization to enhance accuracy and convergence speed. Simulations conducted using IBM Qiskit and TensorFlow Quantum on the Mobile Telecom Network Traffic Dataset validate the industrial applicability of the suggested approach in Applied Operational environment. The QEHMLM achieves notable improvements, recording 97% accuracy, 95% F1-score, and nearly 20% reduction in computational time compared to classical counterparts. These outcomes confirm that Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems (HQCS) provide a scalable, efficient, and high-performance paradigm for next-generation Big Data analytics and intelligent computational ecosystems.

title: A Lightweight Real-Time Multilingual Video Communication System using On-Device Speech Translation
authors: Rajat Kumar, Kuldeep Malviya, Avijit Mondal, Priya Pandey, Pravin Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Real-time multilingual interaction during mobile video calls still difficult to achieve due to strict latency, fluc-tuating network conditions, and the limited resources capacity of handheld devices. Although recent speech translation systems show high linguistic accuracy, many studies highly depend on cloud-based pipelines or text-only captions, which disrupt conversational flow and increase deployment cost. We design and implement a lightweight speech-to-speech translation system integrated directly into live video calls. Our system combines ondevice speech recognition and speech synthesis with a lightweight online translation service, coordinated via parallel pipeline that operates independently of the core audio–video stream. To avoid degradation of call quality, translated content is exchanged using a dedicated real-time data channel rather than the audio channel itself. Experiments conducted on physical Android smartphones under realistic network conditions over Wi-Fi and mobile data networks show end-to-end translation latency of approximately 0.5 to 2.0 seconds across multiple language pairs. Subjective user evaluations yield a mean opinion score of 4.2, indicating that the translated speech is sufficiently clear and natural for everyday conversational use.

title: Fully Unsupervised Self-debiasing of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
authors: Korada Sri Vardhana, Shrikrishna Lolla, Soma Biswas
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Text-to-image (T2I) diffusion models have achieved widespread success due to their ability to generate high-resolution, photorealistic images. These models are trained on large-scale datasets, like LAION-5B, often scraped from the internet. However, since this data contains numerous biases, the models inherently learn and reproduce them, resulting in stereotypical outputs. We introduce SelfDebias, a fully unsupervised test-time debiasing method applicable to any diffusion model that uses a UNet as its noise predictor. SelfDebias identifies semantic clusters in an image encoder’s embedding space and uses these clusters to guide the diffusion process during inference, minimizing the KL divergence between the output distribution and the uniform distribution. Unlike supervised approaches, SelfDebias does not require human-annotated datasets or external classifiers trained for each generated concept. Instead, it is designed to automatically identify semantic modes. Extensive experiments show that SelfDebias generalizes across prompts and diffusion model architectures, including both conditional and unconditional models. It not only effectively debiases images along key demographic dimensions while maintaining the visual fidelity of the generated images, but also more abstract concepts for which identifying biases is also challenging.

title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Linux Distribution
authors: Varnitha Venkatesh, Shreyaa S M Kudremane, Vismitha H, Ritika S Nair, Syed Matheen Pasha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: In this research work, the classic cryptography methods on which modern digital security relies are threatened by an existential risk due to the development of quantum computing. When large-scale quantum computing emerges, algorithms such as RSA, ECC, & Diffie-Hellman, having protected data for several decades, might soon turn vulnerable. This has created a direct need across the globe to migrate to post-quantum cryptographic techniques that can also resist attacks by adversaries, both classical and quantum. In this paper, we authors use the Yocto Project to develop and deploy a PQC-enabled Linux distribution. The system presents a hybrid encryption architecture for quantum-safe file security, combining symmetric encryption using AES-256 with simulated key encapsulation based on CRYSTALS-Kyber. The creation of a special meta-layer known as meta-pqr allows Yocto to be expanded with PQC-specific recipes, system services, and cryptographic tools. A completely automated file encryption daemon transparently encrypts newly created files in real-time while monitoring filesystem events without requiring user input. Platform independence and reproducibility are assured since the entire system is designed, packaged, and tested in a controlled embedded environment built around a QEMU. With thorough functional verification, runtime evaluation, and security assessment, the proposed PQC Linux distribution exhibits reliability, low-latency encryption, stable resource consumption, and seamless integration with embedded Linux workflows. This work provides a validated, modular, and extensible operating system blueprint for PQC-enabled systems that can be used as a foundation for additional research and deployment in quantum-resilient infrastructures.

title: Show Me: Unifying Instructional Image and Video Generation with Diffusion Models
authors: Yujiang Pu, Zhanbo Huang, Vishnu Boddeti, Yu Kong
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating visual instructions in a given context is essential for developing interactive world simulators. While prior works address this problem through either text-guided image manipulation or video prediction, these tasks are typically treated in isolation. This separation reveals a fundamental issue: image manipulation methods overlook how actions unfold over time, while video prediction models often ignore the intended outcomes. To this end, we propose ShowMe, a unified framework that enables both tasks by selectively activating the spatial and temporal components of video diffusion models. In addition, we introduce structure and motion consistency rewards to improve structural fidelity and temporal coherence. Notably, this unification brings dual benefits: the spatial knowledge gained through video pretraining enhances contextual consistency and realism in non-rigid image edits, while the instruction-guided manipulation stage equips the model with stronger goal-oriented reasoning for video prediction. Experiments on diverse benchmarks demonstrate that our method outperforms expert models in both instructional image and video generation, highlighting the strength of video diffusion models as a unified action-object state transformer. Our code will be available at https://yujiangpu20.github.io/showme/.

title: An Attention-Based and YOLO Hybrid Vision Framework for Real-Time Road Obstacle Detection and Driver Distraction
authors: S Revathi, N Satish, P Balu, S Mohammed Navas, M Velusamy, N Rajeshkannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: A real-time, context-aware system that can concurrently monitor driver behavior and identify dynamic road barriers is necessary to ensure road safety. This research presents a novel hybrid framework that combines EfficientNet-B2 improved with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention for distraction classification with YOLOv7-tiny for obstacle detection. By combining cross-domain data from both driver and road perspectives, the system is further enhanced by Hierarchical Attention Distillation (HAD) and Gradient-Sensitive Spatial Encoding (GSSE). The Hybrid Firefly - Whale Optimization Algorithm (HFWOA) is used for optimization in order to balance low-latency performance and detection accuracy on embedded devices such as Jetson Xavier NX. In real-time at $\boldsymbol{\geqslant} \mathbf{3 0}$ frames per second, evaluation results demonstrate an accuracy of 94.6% for distraction classification, mean average precision (mAP) of 0.99, and IoU up to 0.97 in road obstacle recognition. Adaptive fusion across views based on scene criticality is ensured by the Contextual Voting Attention (CVA) module. A high-fidelity perception model appropriate for incorporation into autonomous or ADAS systems is offered by this dual-stream ensemble framework. The suggested approach lowers the likelihood of accidents brought on by distraction or missing driving indications by concurrently analyzing driver states and external threats.

title: Ephemeral Bastion: A Lightweight Zero-Trust Framework utilizing Blockchain-Backed Auditing and JIT Certificate-Based Authentication
authors: Ramu Para, S.M. Udhaya Sankar, Aswanth Babu, N Kishan Jai Soorya, G Harikumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Cloud-natives infrastructure and perennially remote administration has broadened the attack surface inside regular perimeter based security types. Static SSH keys and longlived passwords remain as a major form of credential, despite the security risk they carry for theft, brute force and lateral movement attacks. Compounding this problem, centrally managed SQL audit logs can be easily modified by privileged insiders and destroy forensic non-repudiation. In response, we present Ephemeral Bastion, a lightweight zero trusting lightweight just in time (JIT) certificate based SSH authentication and SHA-256 hash chained audit ledger. A custom Python Certificate Authority is used to issue short lived session certificates ($\Delta t=300 \mathrm{~s}$), and thus reduces the credential exposure window length by 98.4% compared with static-key deployments. Kernel level EXECVE events received by Linux auditd are sent over an append-only and single-node ledger over TLS secured sockets with 100% tamper detection. When compared with static SHH keys, SQL-based logging, and Ethereum-based private blockchain (EPB), the framework consumes 68.2% fewer resources than EPB and increases the service load balancing index by 15.57%. By having data on authenticated identities experimentally visually tied to unchangeable command data, Ephemeral Bastion paints over that blind spot to identity audit with out actually incurring the consensus costs that ground within the construct of fully decentralised blockchains.

title: AI-Based Internship Recommendation Engine for the PM Internship Scheme
authors: D. Rosy Salomi Victoria, L Gokulraj, R Hem Kumar, S. Roselin Mary, S Nithya Roseline
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The proposed research is an artificial intelligence-based hybrid internship recommendation engine to be applied in the context of the Prime Minister Internship Scheme. The abundance of internship opportunities, as well as the varying conditions of eligibility makes it hard to find good internships, especially among the first-generation and rural learners. The proposed system tackles this issue by integrating the eligibility rule filtering, skill-related similarity analysis and a preference-sensitive weighted scoring to provide individual internship recommendations. The aspects that are taken into account during the recommendation include educational qualifications, courses, specialization, academic performance, skills, urban preference, sector, and work mode. Cosine similarity is used to measure skill relevance to allow partial and meaningful skills matching as opposed to strict keyword matching. A weighted scoring system gives priority to the relevance of the skills with also location and sector preferences. The model does internal weight sensitivity analysis, which, however, does not impact user interaction to facilitate robustness. The system delivers few and very relevant internships as well as easy to understand explanations that increase user confidence and faith. The proposed method is suitable to the large-scale implementation in the public sector as experimental assessment can provide better recommendation relevance, transparency, and accessibility than traditional key-word based filtering systems.

title: Smart Vehicle Black Box for Enhanced Road Safety and Crash Investigation
authors: M Dinesh Kumar, R Srikumar, S Sahaya Sam, P Sharon Raj, M Indumathi, S Revathy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Growing severity of road accidents and the lack of reliable post-crash evidence require smart vehicular surveillance systems that can provide real-time analytics and forensic data storage. A smart vehicle black box design has been introduced, which combines the concepts of multi-sensor signal imprinting, physics-guided impact modeling, and embedded crash intelligence to achieve road safety and accuracy in investigation. Its system utilizes a tri-axial inertial sensor, forceenergy severity estimation, collision discrimination, and probabilistic collision, as well as on-device classification to confirm the crash events and activate the emergency communication. The accuracy of collision detection was more than 98%, and false event suppression was more than 95%, as experimentally tested. The latency of impact confirmation was less than 12 ms, whereas embedded inference took not more than 4.1 ms. The response time to dispatch emergency alerts with end-to-end was 214 ms, and the forensic data integrity was 99.97% under the high-impact conditions. The proposed framework is highly effective in enhancing crash recognition reliability, response timeliness, and evidentiary strength. It can be deployed in intelligent transportation systems that need reliable safety analytics and legally sound crash reporting.

title: Enhancing Cloud Reliability Through Multi Agents Based Self-Healing Architecture
authors: Rajat Pundir, Yuvraj Kabadwal, Sujal Thapa, Chandradeep Bhatt, Anupam Singh, Rohan Verma
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: India is one of the major economies across the world but intensively hit by a peculiar challenge which is traffic congestion - high pedestrian density, diverse mix of vehicles, and unpredictable choke points. This leads to increased waiting times, CO2 emissions, and fuel use. This system leverages real time data camera feeds to perceive the current traffic state and uses Computer Vision (CV) which acts as an eye of this system, converts this raw data into actionable data on vehicle count and speed, then model this structure into interconnected graph, to recognize these intersection a graph Neural Network(GNN) with an attention mechanism (GAT) is used; this identifies which intersection is the most significant at any given time, Structured data is transferred to the main Muti Agent (DRL) framework. Through this framework each traffic signal acts as an autonomous agent and trained with a Multi Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (MAPPO) algorithm, which helps to decentralize the control, and to learn cooperative control policies that helps to effectively work as coordinated team to minimize the congestion and delays. The proposed framework is validated through SUMO simulations that replicate the diverse traffic conditions characteristic of Indian metropolitan cities, including varied vehicle categories, seasonal demand patterns, and pedestrian flow. In a comparative analysis model resolves the contemporary RL based controller and fixed time, achieving a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 25\%$</tex> in queue length, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 15\%$</tex> reduction in estimated emission, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 20 \%$</tex> reduction in average travel time. The proposed system is modular, scalar, and infrastructure-agnostic, providing pragmatic and future-proof architecture for intelligent traffic.

title: Knowledge Distillation for Cataract Classification Using a ViT Densenet Framework
authors: Shobana T S, Rashmi K B, Megh Mayur M R, Mohd. Viquaruddin Anas, Vedant Khandelwal, Karthik N H
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Cataracts are one of the major causes of preventable blindness globally and the elderly are particularly at risk for the disease, especially in low and middle income countries. The World Health Organization(WHO) reports that cataracts cause almost half of all blindness worldwide, and it is predicted that this will rise because of aging populations and inadequate access to early healthcare services. Conventional diagnosis through slit lamp examination requires trained professionals and specialised equipment that makes large scale screening difficult in resource limited settings. Fundus imaging is a workable alternative for large-scale screening as it is inexpensive, widely available, and easy to implement. In this work, a strong binary cataract classification framework with deep learning is proposed in a Knowledge Distillation paradigm. A Vision Transformer ViT B 16 is applied as the teacher network to train a compact DenseNet 121 student model. To overcome the extreme class imbalance that exists in the ODIR 5K dataset, a balanced training corpus was developed that includes 7000 images of the fundus, 3000 of which are of a cataract case or 4000 of a non cataract case, based on clinically valid data augmentations. Model evaluation was carried out on a totally isolated test set of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2, 5 4 7}$</tex> images to ensure unbiased and reliable performance evaluation. Through the knowledge distillation process, the student model exhibited improved feature robustness and achieved a test accuracy of 98.19%. This marks a significant increase compared to the baseline DenseNet 121 model and its CLAHE enhanced version. The proposed system attained a precision of 98.96% and a sensitivity of 97.41%, both of which are particularly important for automated screening systems where reducing missed diagnoses is important. Comprehensive benchmarking against traditional machine learning techniques like PCA based Random Forest and XGBoost in addition to modern convolution neural networks architectures shows that the proposed method outperforms existing techniques. These results confirm that the knowledge acquired by transformer based models can be successfully transferred to light models, which can achieve accurate results in cataract detection in the hardware with lesser resources.

title: BHARTI Brain Hyperspectral Attention ResNet U-Net with Transparent Interpretation Using Grad-CAM and Guided Grad-CAM
authors: Hariharan R, Anna Sian, Parv Gupta, Harsh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Accurate brain tumor segmentation from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is vital for diagnosis, surgical planning, and patient monitoring. Manual annotation is laborious and subject to observer variability, highlighting the need for automated methods. While deep learning models such as U-Net have achieved strong results, their limited interpretability reduces clinical trust. This work presents an interpretable segmentation framework that combines an Attention Residual U-Net with explainable AI techniques. Residual connections ensure stable training, while attention gates enhance tumor localization. A lightweight preprocessing pipeline employing CLAHE, Otsu’s thresholding, and morphological operations is introduced for skull removal and image enhancement. Data scarcity is addressed through synthetic augmentation using random rotations and Gaussian noise. Model transparency is achieved via Grad-CAM and Guided Grad-CAM, which generate multi scale heatmaps to visualize decision critical regions. Experimental results demonstrate that the framework achieves nearly 98.915% training accuracy with a reduced loss of 0.045. The Dice coefficient (0.919) and IoU score (0.850) demonstrate strong segmentation performance, enabling accurate isolation of brain structures and precise tumor boundary delineation. By combining spectral spatial learning with explainability, the method remains robust to noise and intensity variations while producing interpretable results consistent with radiological assessment.

title: Robogripfusion: a Hybrid Learning Framework for Grasp Prediction in Robotics Using Multisensory Input
authors: Xavier Louis, Prathikshaa C, Bhavana Selvamani, Kabil Chella Pandian, Ajay Vishva Raj, Karthick K
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Progression of robotic grasping requires accurate, context-sensitive, adaptive models to operate on different object geometries/distances of interaction. In this study we introduce RoboGripFusion, a new hybrid learning framework that uses multisensory data such as RGB, depth, and tactile modalities to categorize optimal robotic grasps. The framework combines visual and tactile information using a Transformer based encoder and uses a dual-branch hybrid network for classification as well as regression-based affordance predictions. In order to improve adaptability, reinforcement learning through Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) improves grip tactic with tactile feedback and, adversarial domain adaptation fills the gap between the simulation and real-world deployment. Compared against the best-in-class models under five major metrics including Grasp Success Rate, Top-1 Prediction Rate, Mean Grasp Score, Pose Accuracies and inference time, RoboGripFusion always outperforms. It gained 93.27% Grasp Success Rate, Top-1 Prediction Accuracy of 91.84 % and Pose Estimation Accuracy of 89.56 % but it came with low Inference Time of 28.70 ms. This hybrid architecture enhances robustness not only but also shows high real time applicability in precision critical manipulation tasks. The proposed system defines a new standard of multi-sensory robotic grasping and provides scalable solution for future industrial, assistive and autonomous robotic systems functioning in dynamic and unstructured environments.

title: An Intelligent IoT-Enabled Physiological Surveillance Framework for Early Cardiac Risk Detection and Emergency Intervention
authors: V. Gomathi, K Nandha, M Dhinesh Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The Heart Attack Monitoring and Controlling System has been proposed because it offers real-time monitoring of crucial physiological variables, such as heart rate, body temperature, heart beat rate (hbr), and SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, in a network of integrated sensors with a NodeMCU microcontroller. The wireless transmission of the data is through Wi-Fi to an Android application, which allows users to configure the individual settings of every parameter. Above and below those readings, instant alerts are sent to the patient and other emergency contacts, which is important to avert serious consequences of the situation. An Electrodermal Activity (EDA) sensor tracks the variation in the skin conductance with stress or pain, as an extra indication of patient discomfort in conjunction with vital signs. Its portability enables it to be monitored under a variety of settings, which improve patient comfort and autonomy and reduce dependence on hospitals. Taking several sensor sensors and an interactive display, this system allows the early warning against health hazards, especially heart attacks, facilitating proactive healthcare control and quick emergency calls in a dependable and convenient way.

title: CHIMERA: An Agent-Based Business Intelligence Framework
authors: Muskan Sethi, Arshit Goel, Santan Ratna
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Business intelligence (BI) is a critical component of modern decision-making, enabling organizations to transform raw data into actionable insights. However, traditional BI systems often rely heavily on manual processes, specialized technical expertise, and static reporting workflows, which limit accessibility and slow down insight generation. In this paper, we present Chimera, an agent-based business intelligence framework designed to automate the end-to-end BI pipeline from raw tabular data to multi-modal analytical outputs. Chimera employs a coordinated set of specialized agents responsible for data cleaning, schema inference, automated exploratory data analysis, visualization generation, narrative summarization, and conversational querying. By integrating large language models with structured data processing, Chimera enables natural language interaction with datasets while reducing human intervention and analytical overhead. The framework produces interactive dashboards, textual summaries, and conversational insights from a single CSV input. We evaluate Chimera through qualitative analysis, structural ablation studies, and comparative evaluation against traditional BI workflows, demonstrating its potential to improve accessibility, interpretability, and efficiency in business intelligence tasks. This work contributes toward the broader goal of democratizing data analytics by lowering technical barriers and accelerating insight delivery.

title: An Efficient Approach for Intrusion Detection in Networks using VQC
authors: Naveenkumar S, Anwar Basha H, Mariselvam A K, Madhan Veeramani, S. Sasikumar, V M Senthilkumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: This article presents a viable strategy for intrusion detection in contemporary networks that applies to a Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC), with the potential of improving a more substantive response to the continual evolution and realities of speed in targeted harassing techniques. Given the increasing dynamic nature of both network traffic and adversary evasion techniques in recent networks, expert-based classical machine learning models often cannot provide high confidence detection accuracy without extreme computation costs. To address this gap, the proposed VQC-based machine learning framework adopts quantum feature mapping and parameterized quantum circuits to capture the subtle, high-dimensional behavior of network traffic out of reach of conventional or the default strategy of most classical classifiers. The model was trained and evaluated on a benchmark intrusion detection dataset, achieving competitive performance across all evaluation metrics. The VQC achieved an accuracy of 90.15%, with a precision of 88.9%, recall of 91.45% and F1 score of 90.16%, indicating the effectiveness of the model in detecting both normal traffic and attacks. In addition to high accuracy, a major outcome of this study was computational efficiency. The model only took 42.31 seconds of training time which is dramatically less than traditional deep-learning methods require for training, and the 3.89 second inference time shows the VQC's capability to perform in near-real-time for live networks. According to the findings, VQC provides a potential avenue for high-performing, lightweight, and scalable intrusion detection. This research positions quantum-enhanced learning as a tangible and meaningful avenue to strengthen network security in the post-classical era.

title: A Hybrid Explainable Deep Learning and Linguistic Feature Framework for Psychological Character Analysis
authors: V Parkavi, Meera Karthikeyan Nair Sathikumari, R. Rajmohan, A. Palaniappan, V. Srija, R. Kanagaselvam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: This study introduces XPsyNarrNet, a hybrid deep learning and linguistic feature framework designed for psychological character analysis in English literary narratives. Traditional literary analytics often lack systematic computational modeling of characters’ psychological traits, especially when dealing with introspective, metaphor-rich narratives. To address this gap, the proposed system integrates a persona-infused transformer for psychological embedding, a graph neural network for narrative role inference, and a novel linguistic-psychometric interaction tensor (LPIT) for multimodal feature fusion. The framework also incorporates a context-aware emotion drift calibration module (CTDF) to manage sentiment inconsistencies across time and a Neuro-Linguistic Attribution Mapping (NLAM) layer for explainable outputs. The system was trained on an annotated corpus of classic and contemporary literature and evaluated using precision, recall, $\mathbf{F 1}$-score, and human interpretability. Results show an overall classification accuracy of 96%, with F1-scores exceeding 0.93 across key psychological traits such as depression, anxiety, and resilience. The explainability layer demonstrated high agreement with human analysts, averaging 0.81 overlap in rationales. This work bridges computational linguistics, psychology, and literary studies, offering a scalable and interpretable solution for narrative understanding. Future extensions may include multilingual corpora, dynamic character arc modeling, and educational deployment in literature and psychology curricula.

title:
Analysis and Performance Evaluation of a Compact Hybrid CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    —VOC Gas Sensor
authors: Jegadeesan Subramani, A Mukhila, S Nidhisaa, K Nisha
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: This paper presents the structural and functional design of a simulation–based hybrid sensor developed for the simultaneous detection of carbon dioxide and volatile organic compounds. Non-dispersive infrared (NDIR) for CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> detection and a Metal-Oxide Semiconductor (MOS) configuration for VOC sensing are two different sensing mechanisms that are combined into a single framework in the suggested model. Compact geometry, effective optical alignment, and material selection are prioritised in the design to guarantee exact gas diffusion and thermal stability. To reduce cross-interference and preserve independent calibration routes between the optical and chemical modules, a layered sensor design is implemented. To improve the even distribution of target gases across both sensor devices and fine-tune chamber size, thermal and flow simulations are employed. Adaptable to embedded system platforms and integrated circuit packaging, the hybrid topology offers low power consumption and modular scalability. Sensitivity, structural simplicity, and manufacturability are all balanced in the overall design approach, which lays the groundwork for nextgeneration hybrid gas sensors.

title: PathoSynth-XR: Biomedical Text-Conditioned Diffusion with Cross-Attention for Rare Cancer Histopathology
authors: Nilanjana Bose, Saurabh Bilgaiyan, Chiranjib Parida
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Instigating the deep integration of BioGPT and diffusion model for zero-shot and few-shot classification of breast cancer subtypes which is rare. It is highly significant for human society because it addresses several critical challenges. The diagnosis of rare breast cancer subtypes is hampered by the scarcity of data in histopathology. We propose the first framework that integrates BioGPT deeply with diffusion models via crossattention, embedding biomedical knowledge directly into the generative process. This produces high-fidelity synthetic images across 16 subtypes, that mainly includes invasive micro papillary carcinoma. Synthetic data is proven to be highly effective. It improved the classification accuracy to 96.8% with an additional boost of 3.25% when augmentation is done in real slides. Medically aware generative AI can help bridge the data scarcity gap, providing new tools to support more accurate and equitable cancer diagnosis, particularly in rare and hard-to-detect cases.
