title: Optimized Ferrite Design for Wireless Charging of Electric Vehicles a Cost-Efficient Approach
authors: Ragul R, R. Nakkeeran, C. Bharatiraja
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technology is gaining traction across various applications, from electric vehicles, owing to its inherent reliability, efficiency, and flexibility. Ferrite materials are integral in WPT systems, enhancing magnetic coupling, suppressing electromagnetic interference, and preventing magnetic energy leakage, thereby boosting charging efficiency. This paper explores different arrangements of ferrite materials within WPT systems to optimize performance and considers their cost-effectiveness. Ferrites, characterized by their high-frequency performance, high magnetic permeability, and electrical conductivity, are crucial for channeling magnetic flux, minimizing interference, and reducing eddy current losses. Various arrangements of ferrite can significantly improve the inductions and increase power transfer. Simulation results indicate that strategic ferrite arrangements increase flux through the coil, thereby improving coupling at most orientations. The cost-efficiency of using ferrites is demonstrated through reduced power losses, improved system performance, and enhanced shielding effectiveness. However, precautions must be taken to mitigate mechanical stress, shock, and high-temperature effects on ferrites. By carefully selecting and arranging ferrite materials, cost-effective WPT systems can be achieved with improved efficiency and enhanced power transfer capabilities.

title: Modeling, Control, and Performance Analysis of a Five-Phase Induction Motor with SPWM
authors: Ayantika Guria, Arka Dutta, Kartick Chandra Jana
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Five-phase induction motors (IMs) are attracting increasing attention in modern drive systems due to their inherent fault tolerance, reduced torque ripple, and improved efficiency compared to conventional three-phase machines. This work presents the complete modeling and simulation of a five-phase IM drive in MATLAB/Simulink. The drive is supplied by a five-phase two-level voltage source inverter (VSI) controlled via sinusoidal pulse-width modulation (SPWM), ensuring balanced output voltages but resulting in stator currents dominated by the 3rd and 7th harmonics. The motor model incorporates detailed electrical and mechanical parameters obtained from experimental characterization tests, enabling realistic performance evaluation. The system is analyzed under various load conditions, with simulation results demonstrating accurate speed tracking and smooth torque response despite the harmonic content. These results highlight the potential of five-phase IM drives for high-reliability and high-performance applications, including electric propulsion, aerospace systems, and advanced industrial machinery.

title: Optimal EPS Control for Minimum Backflow Power and Current Stress for Dual Active Bridge Converter
authors: Jahangeer Ahmad Dar, M. K. Pathak
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: When a dual-active bridge converter (DAB) is operated with single-phase shift modulation (SPS), the converter suffers from high backflow power and current stress. These issues arise, especially during low power operation or wide voltage ratios, leading to losses in the converter and thereby decreasing its efficiency. To simultaneously minimize both backflow power and current stress, the proposed paper introduces the optimal EPS modulation. In this paper, both modes of EPS are discussed, and the optimal combination of phase shifts is determined. The optimal combination is obtained using the method of Lagrange multipliers. Lastly, a hardware prototype is developed to validate the proposed algorithm.

title: Techno-Economic Feasibility Study of a Rooftop Solar PV System for EV Charging at University Campus Building
authors: Krishna Kumar Saini, Diptanjan Sengupta, HD Mathur
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: With India's rapidly evolving electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem and increased adoption in academic campuses, deploying clean energy infrastructure is essential. This paper presents an extended techno-economic analysis of a grid-tied rooftop solar photovoltaic (PV) system with battery backup at a hostel building on a university campus. The system is designed exclusively to cater to EV charging loads through solar energy. PVsyst 8.2 was used for simulation, and a 16.2 kWp PV array, 16.2 kWh battery, and a 20 kW inverter setup were modelled. Simulation outputs include annual energy generation, performance ratio, economic metrics (LCOE), and carbon savings. The study also references broader campus energy sustainability objectives and carbon neutrality goals.

title: Real-Time Lane Assist System Using Computer Vision with Adaptive PID Control
authors: Devadharshan D, Hrithick E, S Shyam Ganesh, Dhanvin S, Karthika R
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: With the growing need for Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), this work proposes a real-time lane assist system based on classical computer vision approaches combined with an adaptive Proportional Integral Derivative (PID)controller. Contrary to deep-learning-based lane detection, this system employs edge detection and Hough Transform to detect lanes, followed by real-time lane deviation calculation. One new contribution of this research which makes it novel is introducing a lane confidence value to adaptively adjust PID control parameters in real time while driving. This provides smoother and more stable steering control while, there is a change in road conditions. The system was evaluated in the CARLA simulator (Town10) and operated with steady lane recovery and low-latency visual feedback, thus having potential for embedded real-time applications.

title: Enhanced Two-Port Model-Based Study and CCM/DCM Boundary Condition for LCC-LCC IPT Topology
authors: Jalaj Kumar, Suvendu Samanta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Inductive power transfer (IPT) converters are attracting significant attention. The design of the IPT converter is generally done employing the first harmonic (FH) approximation due to the formation of the resonant tank network. However, under certain operating conditions, the harmonics in the converter become more pronounced, especially in the discontinuous conduction mode (DCM). As a result, the FH approximation leads to high inaccuracy in these operating conditions. The performance of the converter in the above-mentioned operating conditions is insufficiently addressed in the literature. This paper uses an enhanced two-port model-based study, which accurately examines the LCC-LCC IPT converter in DCM. The CCM/DCM boundary condition for the IPT topology is reported. The mathematical approach is synthesized so that the actual and estimated output voltage and current have an error below 2 %. A hardware prototype of the topology is built. The experimental results validate the proposed analytical method.

title: Optimal Reactive Power Dispatch Using Incomprehensible but Intelligible-in-Time Logics
authors: Rahul Kumar, S. Sivasubramani
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Efficient management of power flow is crucial for the effective operation of power systems, aiming to enhance safety, reliability, and efficiency while adhering to operational constraints. This study specifically focuses on improving the coordination of reactive power to achieve energy efficiency and voltage consistency. The research employs two key computational environments: network-related simulations and eigen value based small-signal stability analysis are performed on the DIgSILENT PowerFactory platform, while heuristic optimization tasks are executed in MATLAB using parallel processing techniques. To address the challenge of optimal reactive power dispatch (ORPD), a novel metaheuristic, the Incomprehensible but Intelligible-intime (IBI) logics algorithm, is utilized for multi-objective reactive power optimization (RPO). The effectiveness of the approach is validated on the IEEE 39-bus New England system, where the proposed algorithm is benchmarked against the sine cosine algorithm (SCA) and particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm. Comparative results demonstrate that the proposed method not only improves computational efficiency and robustness but also enhances dynamic stability, as evidenced by eigen value analysis of system modes under different dispatch strategies.

title: Current Mismatch-Based Newton-Raphson Power Flow Algorithm for Autotransformer Fed Traction Power Supply System
authors: Pradyumna Pradhan, Biswarup Das
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Power flow analysis plays a vital role in the planning, operation, and performance evaluation of traction power supply systems (TPSS). However, the unique topology of autotransformer (AT)-fed traction networks presents modelling and computational challenges compared to conventional transmission systems. This paper proposes a current mismatch-based Newton-Raphson (NR) power flow algorithm for AT-fed TPSS, in which the power flow equations are reformulated in terms of current mismatches. The proposed power flow algorithm's accuracy is validated through case studies on representative AT-fed TPSS configurations, with results compared against equivalent MATLAB SIMULINK models. The close agreement between the two confirms the validity of the proposed approach.

title: Eddy Current Sensor Based Smart Control System for Optimizing Industrial Zinc Coating Process Using Adaptive Machine Learning and IIoT
authors: Tarun K. Das, Chinmay Bera, Chandan Dutta, Prem K. Das, Nitish K. Bhakat, Kedar Bhave, Sarmishtha P. Sagar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Smart control systems and sensor technology equipped with advanced Machine Learning (ML) algorithms, automation, and Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) technologies offer transformative potential for optimizing zinc coating processes in real-time. An integrated eddy current sensor is used for in-situ measurement of thin zinc coating weight (∼ 50–150 GSM) in galvanized iron (GI) steel wires at an industrial facility. However, as manufacturing systems grow more complex, zinc coating requires precise control of process parameters such as coating weight, and line speed to ensure optimal quality and efficiency. The present study addresses an industrially relevant problem by developing an advance sensor technology that integrates eddy current sensing, ML-driven automation and IIoT-enabled systems to promote sustainable smart manufacturing. It showcases the successful integration of smart monitoring and control systems into harsh industrial environments, facilitating enhanced optimization of galvanization processes. It uses an adaptive PID controller based on reinforcement learning to assess dynamic response, adaptability, and robustness. In this work, a single RBF neural network has been realized to improve learning efficiency. It approximated both the Actor's policy function and the Critic's value function simultaneously. The inputs of this network are the system error and its first and second-order differences. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed controller is highly efficient for dynamic and complex nonlinear systems. It exhibits excellent adaptability and robustness, against conventional PID controllers. In line with NDE 4.0 and IIoT, the sensor facilitates precise, real-time assessment of material integrity and coating quality. This research highlights the advances in sensor technology by integrating it into harsh industrial environments, promoting smart manufacturing and optimize industrial galvanization processes.

title: A Resilient NN Assisted Switching Control for Networked Control Systems Under DoS Attacks
authors: Manmahendra Singh Daksh, Puneet Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Denial-of-Service (DoS) attack is one of the most common types of cyberattacks which can severely impact the control loop performance. In this work, a DoS attack on a nonlinear process control loop is investigated, and a probabilistic packet loss scenario has been simulated, affecting the feedback path of the control loop. To enhance the Networked Control System (NCS) resilience towards DoS attacks, this work proposes a Resilient NN Assisted Switching Control (RNASC) framework. The RNASC framework consists of an online Neural Network (NN) estimator to mimic the plant dynamics, along with a conventional Proportional Integral (PI) controller. A notable feature of the proposed control scheme is its switching capability from the feedback signal to the estimated plant output from the neural network. Multiple packet loss probabilities (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\rho$</tex>) have been considered to mimic the severity scenarios of the DoS attack, and the performance of the proposed controller is assessed with Monte Carlo simulations for setpoint tracking, robustness, and plant operation with measurement noise. Through extensive simulation studies, it was found that the proposed RNASC framework provides 90% lesser integral of absolute error (IAE) compared to the existing Neural Network Assisted PI controller (NAPIC) for the worst case scenario of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\rho=0.90$</tex>, which validates the effectiveness of the proposed RNASC in mitigating the effects of DoS attack on an NCS.

title: Control-Oriented Design of a Standalone Pv-Battery-Inverter System with Dynamic Load
authors: Pranati Rani Purohit, Shaswat Chandra Mishra, Arnab Ghosh, Pravat Kumar Ray
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Voltage stability is a major difficulty in photovoltaicbased power systems, which must deal with variable solar input and dynamic load profiles. This paper presents a simulation of a PV-battery system that employed a Model Predictive manage (MPC) approach to precisely manage the DC bus voltage. A boost converter is connected to a photovoltaic (PV) array, and an algorithm called Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) controls it. To talk to a battery system, you need a non-isolated bidirectional converter. The proposed MPC algorithm dynamically optimizes control actions in response to predictions of system behavior, and it actively manages the bidirectional converter. Regardless of fluctuations in generation and consumption, this ensures that the DC bus voltage stays constant at 48 V. A series of tests are performed on the system to evaluate its functionality under realworld scenarios. These tests include nonlinear and time-varying loads, among others. The MPC method outperforms the conventional PID-based control in terms of stability, handling system limitations more effectively, and dynamic reaction speed. The system is completed with an inverter that supplies a single-phase AC load and is connected to the DC bus. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed control architecture accomplishes both effective power sharing and consistent voltage regulation.

title: Federated Multi-Stage Market Clearing with Integrated Probabilistic Forecasting for Decentralized Energy Systems
authors: Surya Karan Singh, Amit Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The ongoing decentralization of electricity markets caused by distributed energy resources (DERs), renewable variability and multi-sided participation highlights the shortcomings of the traditional centralized market design. To tackle this challenge, we propose a federated six-stage multi-sided market clearing framework that closes the loops between forecasting, stochastic bidding, intra-day re-dispatch, real-time balancing, congestion and loss reconciliation and settlement. The FL framework is integrated with the state-of-the-art probabilistic forecasting models: DeepAR, PatchTST and N-BEATS. Market clearing integrates stochastic-robust optimization and conditional value-at-risk techniques for economic, operational and fairness efficiency. By continuously linking these forecasting-optimization-settlement feedback loops, the model enables autonomous flexibility allocation, improved price stability and reduced imbalance costs. Simulations on synthetic NREL-based data show an approximate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 6. 3 \%}$</tex> reduction in imbalance costs, lower price volatility and a load RMSE of 0.84 MW, surpassing the centralized and two-stage benchmarks. The method is scalable, privacy-preserving and mathematically rigorous, thereby providing a solid foundation for the next generation of decentralized markets for electricity.

title: Short-Term Electricity Price Forecasting Using RFECV-Based Feature Selection and GA-Optimized BiLSTM
authors: Ashish Prajesh, Prerna Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Accurate electricity price forecasting has become increasingly important in deregulated power markets, where generators, retailers, and other market participants rely on precise price estimates for bidding strategies and risk management. However, electricity prices exhibit strong volatility, seasonality, and complex nonlinear interactions with demand, generation mix, fuel costs, and other exogenous factors. These characteristics limit the effectiveness of conventional statistical approaches for time-series modeling. To solve these issues we have put forth a deep learning based forecasting framework which is built on a Bidirectional Long Term Memory (BiLSTM) network which is able to capture past and also future temporal relationships in sequential data. Also we report that we used a Genetic Algorithm for hyperparameter tuning of the BiLSTM model which in turn enables us to systemically explore the search space and identify very good configures. Also we used Recursive Feature Elimination with Cross Validation (RFECV) to choose the most relevant input variables which in turn reduced redundancy, improved generalization performance and also we saw a reduction in overfitting. The proposed framework is evaluated through comprehensive experiments and benchmarked against several established forecasting models. Comparative results demonstrate its robustness and competitive accuracy in short-term electricity price prediction.

title: SFKH-GC: Harnessing the Power of Full-Neighborhoods Information for Large-Scale Attributed-Graph Learning_supp1-3689771.pdf
authors: Wenxuan Tu
abstract: With the increasing complexity of data in various fields, large-scale graph learning draws significant attention due to its importance in understanding complex relationships and structures. Nevertheless, it faces two major challenges: (1) expensive computational costs caused by frequent message-passing (MP), and (2) limited expressive capacity due to over-smoothing. Most existing methods address the first challenge by employing node sampling techniques to decrease computational costs, which can lead to an incomplete representation of the graph structure, thereby diminishing overall performance. While decoupled Graph Convolution Networks (GCNs) effectively handle the over-smoothing problem, they still fail to solve the computational cost challenge. In this paper, we re-examine the design of the decoupled GCN from a theoretical perspective and reveal that its ability to alleviate over-smoothing largely arises from capturing richer multi-hop neighborhood information, rather than from the decoupled design, which is the prevailing consensus. Motivated by this finding, we propose a Scalable Full-Neighborhoods K-hop Graph Convolution (SFKH-GC) to simultaneously address the aforementioned challenges. The core idea is to allocate separate memory space for the neighborhood representation obtained in each MP iteration. Specifically, by performing K steps of MP on the original feature matrix, the multi-hop neighborhood information is concatenated, providing rich input for subsequent feature transformation (FT). This process is designed to be independent of the training phase, significantly reducing the number of MP operations and preventing the creation of complex integral graphs during forward and backward propagation. Given the absence of MP operations during training, this method not only permits mini-batch learning but also facilitates the flexible application of common visual or sequential models (rather than graph models). Extensive experiments on seven benchmark datasets demonstrate that SFKH-GC achieves state-of-the-art performance, with up to +7.68% ACC improvement on Cora and +4.02% on Computer compared to the best existing methods, while substantially reducing memory usage and training time. Both theoretical and empirical results validate the efficiency, scalability, and performance of the proposed method.

title: Explainable Machine Learning for Breast Cancer Diagnosis Using Feature Grouping, Model Comparison, and Interpretability Methods
authors: Ananjan Maiti, Nirupam Saha, Sayan Bag, Shrabana Pal, Riya Banerjee, Ankush Mondal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Breast cancer is one of the leading causes of death around the world. Having it accurate and on time is essential to save lives. Diagnostic processes relying on prediction often come at the cost of explainability. Thus, limiting their clinical acceptance. The proposed framework includes a robust architecture composed of many building blocks, with special consideration for the classification of breast cancer. The benign and malignant classes in the dataset are unevenly distributed or imbalanced. To be specific, there are 357 non-cancerous and only 212 cancerous instances. This is a significant difference that will affect the classifiers' learning. The data set is enhanced with complex morphological and textural fingerprints, which give a complete picture of the samples. After going through a detailed feature selection process using PCA and t-SNE, four classifiers were designed with extreme care. Various standard machine learning methods are applied to complete classification tasks in a credit risk model. These classifiers include logistic regression, random forests, gradient boosting, and a shallow neural network. Each classifier is a machine learning model (or algorithm) of its own, developed by modeling the data with features. The global interpretations in SHAP identified the mean radius feature as the most important, whereas LIME identified specific-case local features. The evaluation and testing of the subgroups were carried out fairly. The study's findings show that ML models are interpretable, can achieve high diagnostic accuracy, and are likely to provide transparent diagnoses.

title: JOCA: Task-Driven Joint Optimisation of Camera Hardware and Adaptive Camera Control Algorithms
authors: Chengyang Yan, Mitch Bryson, Donald G. Dansereau
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The quality of captured images strongly influences the performance of downstream perception tasks. Recent works on co-designing camera systems with perception tasks have shown improved task performance. However, most prior approaches focus on optimising fixed camera parameters set at manufacturing, while many parameters, such as exposure settings, require adaptive control at runtime. This paper introduces a method that jointly optimises camera hardware and adaptive camera control algorithms with downstream vision tasks. We present a unified optimisation framework that integrates gradient-based and derivative-free methods, enabling support for both continuous and discrete parameters, non-differentiable image formation processes, and neural network-based adaptive control algorithms. To address non-differentiable effects such as motion blur, we propose DF-Grad, a hybrid optimisation strategy that trains adaptive control networks using signals from a derivative-free optimiser alongside unsupervised task-driven learning. Experiments show that our method outperforms baselines that optimise static and dynamic parameters separately, particularly under challenging conditions such as low light and fast motion. These results demonstrate that jointly optimising hardware parameters and adaptive control algorithms improves perception performance and provides a unified approach to task-driven camera system design. Code is available via our project page at https://roboticimaging.org/Projects/JOCA/.

title: The Role of Error in Enhancing Probabilistic Quantum Deletion
authors: A. Nancy, S. Balakrishnan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: In this paper, the probabilistic quantum deletion machine constructed from the NB-deletion machine, controlled by the deletion probability p and the error probability q. It is observed that, under specific conditions, controlled errors can enhance the deletion process: when p is minimal and q is maximal, both deletion and retention fidelities reach their optimal values. Compared to the original NB-machine, this probabilistic machine performs better in both deleting and preserving states. Although the output states do not violate Bell’s inequalities, they still achieve teleportation fidelities above the classical limit. The entanglement of machine’s output state appears only when p > 0, and the mixedness increases with p. Altogether, these results indicate that controlled error can enhance certain non-local features of a probabilistic deletion machine.

title: Hybrid EfficientNet-SVM Model for Analysis of Health Insurance Contract Breaches: An Academic Perspective
authors: J. Gayathri, Behin R.V, M. Sandeep Kumar, D Prerna, Sri Supraja S N, Vardini Venket
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Health insurance contract breaches are a critical issue, often leading to legal disputes between policyholders and insurers. This study presents a novel hybrid model combining EfficientNet and Support Vector Machine (SVM) for the legal analysis of health insurance contract breaches. The model is designed to identify and classify breaches in health insurance contracts, leveraging advanced feature extraction and classification techniques. Data was sourced from public legal records, judgments, and dispute summaries, followed by preprocessing steps such as anonymization, tokenization, and vectorization using TF-IDF and Word2Vec. The hybrid EfficientNet-SVM model integrates deep learning and machine learning to extract features from text-image data and classify cases into “Valid Breach,” “Invalid Breach,” and “Ambiguous” categories. The model achieved an overall classification accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2. 8 \%}$</tex>, outperforming standalone EfficientNet (89.5 %) and SVM (85.3 %). This demonstrates the effectiveness of the hybrid approach in capturing the complexities of legal contract analysis. The study provides a scalable and interpretable solution for legal practitioners and policymakers, aiding in dispute resolution and regulatory compliance. Future enhancements include expanding the dataset to cover additional regions and integrating the model into user-friendly tools for real-time legal analysis.

title: A Novel Multi-Priority Encoder with Efficient Dynamic Reordering and Broad Input Compatibility
authors: A. Subbulakshmi, K Ebinesh, V Sankaranarayanan, D. Denceli
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: The Priority encoders are fundamental components in high-speed digital design and facilitate effective data processing and generation of control signals. Traditional topologies of priority encoders are hindered by high latency and power consumption due to high switching activity and suboptimal logic structures. Adhering to these limitations, this work presents a new topology of a multi-bit priority encoder with new dynamic approaches to priority selection. The suggested architecture includes a strong input selection framework with smooth compatibility support for different input sizes (8-bit, 16-bit, and 32-bit). A leading-one detector (LOD) system is also employed to enhance priority detection, and a masking decoder is employed to eliminate the highest-priority bit, enabling progressive extraction of priorities until the entire set is processed. Implementation and simulation were carried out using Cadence Virtuoso at a 90 nm technology node. The priority encoder proposed has a very low latency of 0.272 ns, with a total power consumption of 0.6 mW. Furthermore, the design shows a Power-Delay Product (PDP) of $\mathbf{2 5 f J}$, representing very high energy efficiency in comparison to traditional methods.

title: Intelligent Cold Storage Monitoring for Perishable Fruits
authors: Balarengadurai C, Inchara Prasad, Hitha M N, Havisha V Sarovar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: In order to maintain the quality of fresh perishable fruits after harvest, it is necessary to possess ideal and consistent cold storage environments. The latest cold storage environments are applying a combination of human management and control systems that lack the precision necessary to detect subtle environmental variations that lead to spoilage and premature ripening. This paper addresses a methodology for monitoring cold storage environment based on a specifically designed intelligent cold monitoring system consisting of a distributed sensor network and data-driven analytical models. This system permits measurement of temperature, humidity, gas accumulation, and air quality on a continuous basis, thereby providing a comprehensive account of the cold storage environment. When certain measurements are integrated with machine learning tools, they are capable of identifying outliers, forecasting outcomes and enabling participation in timely decision-making. A laboratory scale cold storage chamber evaluated the proposed system over multiple fruit batch experiments. Based on quantitative results, the proposed system demonstrates a 18-25% increase in shelf life, a nearly 30% decrease in spoilage, and a 10-12% energy savings compared to the current approach of monitoring temperature through set thresholds. Future scalability of the proposed innovative system will also allow for application in commercial/industrial refrigeration facilities and other low-cost system architectures.

title: Machine Fault Identification Using a Digital Twin-Based Augmented Vision Framework with IoT-Enabled Sensors
authors: K. Elavarasi, Z. Mohammed Javith, N. Imran, K. RajKumar
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: In modern industries, machines serve as the backbone of production. Any unexpected downtime can lead to a complete halt in operations, resulting in significant losses in productivity and revenue. Ensuring the continuous and efficient operation of machinery is therefore a critical aspect of industrial maintenance. The proposed system introduces a Digital Twin (DT)-based Augmented Vision (AV) framework designed to support predictive maintenance by integrating IoT-enabled sensors, real-time visualization, and fault diagnostics. The system employs a DT scanner to capture and visualize the operating condition of machines, providing a color-coded output—green for normal, orange for warning, and red for critical conditions such as abnormal vibration, overcurrent, overheating, or excessive noise. The approach combines IoT-based data acquisition with DT visualization to deliver an intelligent, low-cost, and efficient solution for monitoring and maintaining industrial equipment. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed system improves diagnostic accuracy, reduces service time, and ensures enhanced operational reliability environments.

title: Smart AI-Driven Logistics Optimization and Sentiment Analysis the Role of Technology Trust and Organizational Readliness in Enhancing Business Performance
authors: Muhammad Irvan Firdaus, Murniati
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: The adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) in logistics is expected to enhance efficiency and business performance, yet empirical evidence remains inconsistent, particularly in emerging economies. This study investigates the roles of technology trust and organizational readiness in shaping the relationship between AI adoption and logistics outcomes, while viewing sentiment analysis as a perceived AI-enabled analytical capability rather than a technical tool. Using a quantitative explanatory approach, data were collected from 100 logistics and supply chain professionals in Indonesia and analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The findings show that organizational readiness has a strong positive impact on logistics efficiency, while AI adoption and technology trust exert weaker, indirect effects. Although perceived sentiment analysis capability supports efficiency, it does not directly enhance logistics performance. Overall, the results emphasize that successful AI-driven logistics transformation relies more on organizational preparedness and user trust than on technology adoption alone.

title: Event-based Graph Representation with Spatial and Motion Vectors for Asynchronous Object Detection
authors: Aayush Atul Verma, Arpitsinh Vaghela, Bharatesh Chakravarthi, Kaustav Chanda, Yezhou Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Event-based sensors offer high temporal resolution and low latency by generating sparse, asynchronous data. However, converting this irregular data into dense tensors for use in standard neural networks diminishes these inherent advantages, motivating research into graph representations. While such methods preserve sparsity and support asynchronous inference, their performance on downstream tasks remains limited due to suboptimal modeling of spatiotemporal dynamics. In this work, we propose a novel spatiotemporal multigraph representation to better capture spatial structure and temporal changes. Our approach constructs two decoupled graphs: a spatial graph leveraging B-spline basis functions to model global structure, and a temporal graph utilizing motion vector-based attention for local dynamic changes. This design enables the use of efficient 2D kernels in place of computationally expensive 3D kernels. We evaluate our method on the Gen1 automotive and eTraM datasets for event-based object detection, achieving over a 6% improvement in detection accuracy compared to previous graph-based works, with a 5× speedup, reduced parameter count, and no increase in computational cost. These results highlight the effectiveness of structured graph modeling for asynchronous vision. Project page: eventbasedvision.github.io/eGSMV.

title: Personalized Healthcare Assistant for Lung Cancer Detection Using Hybrid Machine Learning Approach
authors: M Sitha Ram, Bhanu Chander Balusa, Phaneendra Kanakamedala, Vejendla Meenakshi, Chikoti Trisha, Allada Shankar, Vemuri Praveena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Lung cancer is one of the main cause of cancer related death globally so the early detection techniques are really essential for reducing the deaths globally every year. In order to diagnose lung cancer, our research proposes a hybrid machine learning technique which uses XGBoost for tabular data analysis and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for histopathological image classification. The suggested approach makes use of two datasets: a histopathological image dataset with 25,000 images of lung cancer, and a lung cancer dataset with 15 clinical and demographic variables. While the CNN model showed 91.06% training and validation accuracy for image classification whereas the XGBoost model achieved 98.39% accuracy for tabular data set. we used Streamlit to create a web based tool for this research project for real time prediction. The hybrid approach is great for early lung cancer screening programs and early lung cancer detection and support systems.

title: AI-Powered Visibility Enhancement System for Safe and Intelligent Driving
authors: Vangimalla Roopeswara Sai Reddy, Nivethitha Vijayaraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Road safety is an overriding issue in all parts of the world, especially where visibility is highly undermined, thus increasing the risks of vehicle crashes and loss of lives. Quite often, conventional driver aid technologies do not provide any uniform functionality during nighttime, foggy, or rainy weather when visual obscuration is high. Therefore, in this paper, a visibility enhancement system reinforced with AI is outlined, which aims to optimize the perception of driving in real-time and thus, counteract poor video quality and alleviate bad weather conditions. The suggested architecture will use convolutional neural networks along with generative adversarial networks to reconstruct these images with high quality in visual imagery based on noisy or opaque inputs. The system supports low-latency inference using edge-based processing, and the cloud orchestration, which should be based on the usage of the Google Cloud AI, supports the large-scale optimization of the model. Empirical validation results of the system on benchmark datasets like KITTI, BDD100K, and Foggy Cityscapes evidenced that the system is higher in terms of performance measures, like the highest signal-to-noise ratio, structural similarity index, and speed of inference. The conclusions drawn from the experiment show that there are significant improvements in the accuracy of object detection and overall scene interpretability in cases of unfavorable visibility. The implications of these findings are that AI-mediated visibility improvement has a huge potential to advance the next-generation advanced driver-assist systems and autonomous driving platforms, which in turn would help to develop a safer transportation ecosystem.

title: A Unified ViT-BiLSTM-CTC Framework for Multilingual Handwritten Text Recognition
authors: Gande Varun Kumar, G Harshavardhan, Lekshmi S. Raveendran
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: In India many people still use handwritten documents in many daily scenarios for example in the form of notes,forms,medical documents and agreement papers,so when these documents are being referred by somebody else there might be a chance of misread and misunderstandings,so converting these documents and making a digital version is a crucial task to deal with this problem. Existing systems or website do not provide a one stop solution for this problem, these existing systems only provide support for english language but not for indic languages. In our OCR system,we tested Telugu,Tamil,Hindi and English scripts,and our model handles most of the characters correctly,our system uses a VIT(vision transformer) for extracting visual features of a image and a BiLSTM-model for analyzing front and back sequences and arranging characters in the output as a sequence. The total system consists of preprocessing,segmentation,recognition,and document generation. Results show that the approach performs well across different styles of handwriting and languages.we achieved a character error rate of 5 % and a word accuracy of 75 %, these results show it performs acceptably for many real life situations.

title: Intelligent Vehicle Engine Lock Using Alcohol and Heart Rate Monitoring
authors: M. Ishwarya, AnandhajothiNalini. R, Visalatchi. K, Vicshiya Sherin. V, Manimehalai. S, Velan. K
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The Connected Vehicle safety framework and monitoring system that aims to provide driver comfort and safety, as well as prevent the occurrence of accidents that are a common cause of the impairment of the driver's abilities or health anomalies. The system has a heart rate sensor, temperature sensor, and alcohol detection module besides an ESP8266 Wi-Fi controller for on-the fly monitoring and control. Once the driver puts his finger on the sensor module, body temperature and heart rate are measured by the system, and an alarm is activated if the temperature is above 45 degrees Celsius. At the same time, an alcohol sensor is looking for the level of intoxication. Using the Blinkit application, all sensor data are sent to a web dashboard, which shows the car's health and the driver's condition in real time. The principle of a car will only be activated when all three parameters such as heart rate, temperature, and alcohol are at normal levels and gives accuracy up to 98 %. If there is any abnormality, the engine will be kept at a standstill, and a warning will be sent through the web interface. This is an excellent and very allowed way of demonstrating the implementation of IoT driven safety system that can help in accident prevention, remote control, and the promotion of responsible driving through technology immersion.

title: AI-Powered Real-Time Vehicle Detection and Traffic Analysis using YOLOv3 and OpenCV
authors: S Niresh Kumar, A. Hemalatha, P Malathi, T.N. Charanya, K Antony Kumar, P Rajendran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Vehicle detection and classification is a very important task in traffic monitoring and autonomous driving and intelligent surveillance. Real-time detection and classification of various vehicle types, including cars, trucks, buses, and motorcycles, is essential. This can actually make the road safer. This can actually help in the traffic flow and the police. However, the traditional way of vehicle detection faces some problems like when one vehicle is blocking another vehicle, when the light is poor, when the city is very complicated. So, we need to develop a system. This research aims to develop a better system for vehicle detection and classification by using OpenCV and YOLOv3. The main objective of this research is to develop a model which can detect and classify different kinds of vehicles in an efficient way. The system which we are proposing can look at the video in time. Can identify different kinds of vehicles very fast and accurately in different kinds of traffic situations. This research uses OpenCV to process images and YOLOv3 to detect objects in time. YOLOv3 is very good at detecting objects. This research uses YOLOv3 to detect objects like vehicles in images. Then we classify them based on their appearances. A fine-tuned YOLOv3 model is used for vehicle detection and classification. The system is developed in Python, with OpenCV for video processing and deep learning frameworks for model implementation. Results show effective performance under various conditions. This system is effective at processing video feeds with little delay. This makes it effective for real-time applications. This research is helpful in the management of traffic because we have a way to monitor vehicles automatically. This can be used in transportation systems as well as security surveillance. In conclusion this research shows that using OpenCV and YOLOv3 together is a way to detect and classify vehicles in time.

title: Secure Cyber Infrastructure Design to Protect Customer Data in Modern Digital Commerce Systems
authors: P. Brindha, E. Saranya, Ganesh Kumar R, G. Manikandan, J. David Sukeerthi Kumar, Mohit Tiwari
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Strong, scalable, and smart customer data protection security mechanisms are required to support the continuously increasing number of online financial transactions. This paper proposes a novel decentralized cyber defense framework that combines Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) specifically designed on the blockchain, and a baseline anomaly detection model based on autoencoders. The method proposed combines ECC for lightweight encryption, Hyperledger Fabric for distributed access policy enforcement, and deep learning for high-fidelity behavioral anomaly detection. There are significant improvements in key performance metrics through experimental validation over a simulated transaction dataset. Access control decisions are enforced by the system with a 97.4 percent success rate, and latencies for encryption-decryption are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 1. 6}$</tex> milliseconds. Baseline systems were outperformed by anomaly detection by almost 8 %, with a precision of 96.2 %. Compared to baseline systems, there was an increase in end-toend transaction throughput by 37.6 %. The results show that the system can handle high user volume scenarios securely without much impact on performance.

title: AI-Based Document Leak Attribution System
authors: Kayalvizhi P, Jayaprakash J, Dharanidharan C, Balasunil P
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: With the increasing flow of organizational operations digitalization, the threat of sensitive data leakage has increased immensely, and it is frequently posed by the actions of malicious insiders, the unwitting error, or the external cyberattack. The conventional security controls that include role-based access control, history records, and encryption are not effective outside of the controlled environment and ultimately become useless after the release of documents to third parties. The paper suggests an AI-based Multi-Mode Document Leak Attribution System that has the capability of offering accurate and post-distribution accountability. The system inserts invisible, unnoticeable watermarks into all of the documents by using an integration of natural language processing (NLP)-based text manipulations, metadata addition, and steganographic algorithms and produces durable and resistant fingerprints. The documents have a secure Token ID which is stored in encrypted database and hence they can be traced. When a leaked document is detected, the forensic engine looks at all the layers of the watermark, compares the patterns extracted with the database and creates a high-confidence attribution report, which contains the source of the leak, the time of the leak and the confidence. In unmatched cases, this system enables deep analysis and human-assisted review to make sure that everything was investigated. This framework can improve the security of an organization, increase its forensic, and offer scalability in identifying and attributing insider-induced leaks of documents because it closes the gap between the access control and post-distribution monitoring.

title: Eff-GRot: Efficient and Generalizable Rotation Estimation with Transformers
authors: Fanis Mathioulakis, Gorjan Radevski, Tinne Tuytelaars
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We introduce Eff-GRot, an approach for efficient and generalizable rotation estimation from RGB images. Given a query image and a set of reference images with known orientations, our method directly predicts the object’s rotation in a single forward pass, without requiring object- or category-specific training. At the core of our framework is a transformer that performs a comparison in the latent space, jointly processing rotation-aware representations from multiple references alongside a query. This design enables a favorable balance between accuracy and computational efficiency while remaining simple, scalable, and fully end-to-end. Experimental results show that Eff-GRot offers a promising direction toward more efficient rotation estimation, particularly in latency-sensitive applications. Code is available at: https://github.com/fmathiou/eff-grot.

title: Q-GenZ-AIQuanta: A Quantum-Enhanced Evaluation Model for Next-Gen Text-to-Text Generative AI in Knowledge Management System
authors: Mohammad Imroz Malik, Navin Kumar Agrawal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The mesmerising evolution of Generative Artificial Intelligence (Gen.AI) has transformed knowledge management, yet evaluating its reliability and contextual precision remains a critical challenge. This paper proposes GenZ-AIQuanta, a hybrid quantum–classical (Q–C) evaluation model designed for text-to-text generative AI systems. The model integrates classical linguistic metrics—BLEU (Biilingual Evaluations Study) and BERT.Score (Bi-direction Encoder Representation from Transformer Score)—with quantum-derived semantic measures such as QSS (Quantum Semantic Similarity) and QSF (Quantum State Fidelity). Implemented using Python, Qiskit, and HuggingFace Transformers, the system enables multidimensional analysis via a hybrid aggregation engine. Experimental outcomes exhibit significant improvements in accuracy, contextual coherence, and explainability. Moreover, the EC (Explainability Coefficient) highlights enhanced interpretive reliability across evaluation layers. The proposed Q–C hybrid architecture establishes a scalable, explainable, and quantum-enhanced foundation for assessing next-generation GenAI models within knowledge-centric environments.

title: Occlusion Boundary and Depth: Mutual Enhancement via Multi-Task Learning
authors: Lintao Xu, Yinghao Wang, Chaohui Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Occlusion Boundary Estimation (OBE) identifies boundaries arising from both inter-object occlusions and self-occlusion within individual objects. This task is closely related to Monocular Depth Estimation (MDE), which infers depth from a single image, as Occlusion Boundaries (OBs) provide critical geometric cues for resolving depth ambiguities, while depth can conversely refine occlusion reasoning. In this paper, we aim to systematically model and exploit this mutually beneficial relationship. To this end, we propose MoDOT, a novel framework for joint estimation of depth and OBs, which incorporates a new Cross-Attention Strip Module (CASM) to leverage mid-level OB features for depth prediction, and a novel OB-Depth Constraint Loss (OBDCL) to enforce geometric consistency. To facilitate this study, we contribute OB-Hypersim, a large-scale photorealistic dataset with precise depth and self-occlusion-handled OB annotations. Extensive experiments on two synthetic datasets and NYUD-v2 demonstrate that MoDOT achieves significantly better performance than single-task baselines and multi-task competitors. Furthermore, models trained solely on our synthetic data demonstrate strong generalization to real-world scenes without fine-tuning, producing depth maps with sharper boundaries and improved geometric fidelity. Collectively, these results underscore the significant benefits of jointly modeling OBs and depth. Code and resources are available at HERE.

title: Enhanced Liver Tumor Segmentation and Feature Extraction Using Gaussian Filtering and LICA on CT Images
authors: D. Anandkumar, S. John Justin Thangaraj, C. Alamelu, M. Vallikkannu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Liver cancer is among the major causes of cancer related deaths in the world and this is partly caused by the late stages of diagnosis and inability to detect it early through effective means. Effective segmentation and sound feature detection of liver CT images play a significant role in enhancing the credibility of the diagnosis in addition to therapy choices. This paper will offer a superior approach that combines Gaussian filter and Linear Iterative Clustering Algorithm (LICA) to effectively demarcate and extract quantitative characteristics of liver tumors. The image pre-processes start with the use of Gaussian filtering on the CT images to suppress noise and preserve important anatomical information. The ROIs are manually defined based on local intensity minima to enhance focused segmentation. LICA is used to perform effective clustering according to pixel intensity and spatial closeness, and thus, accurate description of tumor extent is achieved even in complex and low-contrast regions. Following segmentation, key geometric properties (such as area) are defined using the technique, and entropy, energy, contrast and homogeneity from Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) based texture descriptors are used to quantitatively describe the shape of the tumor. The experimental results over the multiple CT image samples have demonstrated that the proposed method can achieve good segmentation accuracy and refined features for further classification. Tumor detection is consistently achieved over a wide range of tumor sizes and contrast levels with low false detection rates. In addition, its high computational efficiency allows the possibility of real-time integration in the clinical environment. Unlike deep learning models, the proposed system is fully interpretable thus making it more suitable for healthcare industry, where the need for explanation is crucial.

title: Framework for Explainable Learning in Federated 3D Multimodal Cancer Detection
authors: D.Nagadurga Naveen, N.Ankith Yadav, P. Rajalakshmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The uneven nature of the multimodal data, absence of sizable, centralized datasets brought on by privacy and data-sharing regulations across healthcare organizations make it difficult to diagnose cancer early using medical imaging. Traditional deep learning models frequently use centralized training and single-modality data, which restricts generalization and puts private patient data at risk. In order to get over these restrictions, this research suggests a Federated 3D Multimodal Explainable Learning Framework that uses distributed 3D medical imaging and clinical data to enable collaborative and privacy-preserving cancer diagnosis. The framework enables efficient learning from multimodal inputs like CT, MRI, and patient metadata by integrating Transformer-based encoders for cross-modal feature fusion and Convolutional neural networks(CNNs) in three dimensions for the extraction of spatial features. A federated aggregation strategy (FedAvg) synchronizes local models from many medical institutions without sharing raw data, protecting patient privacy.Additionally, by emphasizing the important areas and characteristics affecting the diagnosis, explainable AI modules like Grad-CAM and attention visualization offer interpretability. According to experimental assessment, the suggested strategy offers transparency and complies with healthcare privacy regulations while outperforming conventional centralized and unimodal methods for accuracy, F1-score, and resilience. This technique creates a new path for safe and explicable AI-based cancer detection by bridging the gaps between data privacy, multimodal integration, and model interpretability.

title: Explainable Multimodal Vision Transformer for CKD Prediction and Stage Classification
authors: V Nivethitha, K S Manoj, K Sai Poojitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a progressive disease that is mostly asymptomatic and remains in an undiagnosed state until it reaches an advanced stage of the disease, thereby increasing morbidity and mortality rates and creating a healthcare burden for patients around the world. Hence, it is of prime importance that CKD be identified at an early stage in order to stratify the risk of progression of the disease. Recent works have shown the presence of microvascular markers related to renal disorders in retinal fundus images, allowing for a non-invasive CKD screening method. In this manuscript, we present an explainable multimodal deep learning approach unifying retinal Fundus images and structured clinical data for early CKD diagnosis, staging, and classification. To do this, a Vision Transformers ViT is used for the extraction of global features from retinal images, combined with a deep neural network for the processing of clinical variables, like HbA1c levels, blood pressure, creatinine levels, and estimated glomerular filtration rate. The learned features are combined and optimized for all tasks simultaneously as part of a multi-task learning approach. To improve explainability and trustworthiness of the results, a gradient-based method for explainability is employed as part of the proposed approach, namely Grad-CAM. Experimentation carried out confirms that the proposed approach outperforms traditional CNN-based as well as unimodal schemes for CKD detection, and this establishes that this approach could potentially be used as a trustworthy tool for CKD detection as a decision-support system.

title: Automated Down Syndrome Detection from Fetal Ultrasound Images Using Deep Learning: A MobileNetV2-Based Approach
authors: Vidhyasagar B S, Veeramallu Naga Sai Sandeep Kumar, Katta Naga Manikanta
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Down syndrome is one of the most common chromosomal abnormalities affecting fetal development. Early detection through ultrasound screening is essential for clinical decision-making and parental counseling. This paper presents a deep learning-based automated system for detecting Down syndrome from fetal ultrasound images using transfer learning with MobileNetV2 architecture. The approach analyzes nine critical anatomical markers: nuchal translucency (NT), nasal bone, intracranial translucency (IT), cisterna magna (CM), thalami, midbrain, palate, nasal tip, and nasal skin. The proposed model was trained and validated on a comprehensive dataset of 1,684 ultrasound images with 9,433 anatomical annotations and achieved an accuracy of 75.32%, sensitivity of 96.02%, specificity of 48.53%, and AUC-ROC of 0.8577. The high sensitivity reported herein indicates that the model is very effective as a screening tool because it identified 96% of cases of Down syndrome, with a negative predictive value of 90.41%. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms baseline methods and provides clinically relevant insights for prenatal screening programs.

title: Effectiveness of Online Learning Platforms Enhanced by Virtual Reality and AI Tutoring Systems
authors: G. Gowtham, K. Yasotha, R Siddharth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Despite the impact of technology on the learning process, online education is still plagued by a number of challenges, including a lack of student engagement, impersonal feedback mechanisms, and the absence of physical presence. This research proposes a new paradigm in which the use of AI teaching technologies and VR can help solve the challenges currently affecting online education and boost its efficacy. Virtual reality is a powerful tool in the pursuit of increased student engagement, motivation, and retention due to its immersive and engaging functionality. The AI educator takes a student-centric approach that is akin to one-on-one mentoring in addition to the ability to provide student feedback and the ability to continuously monitor student performance. This new paradigm combines intelligence and immersion in a unified approach, effectively bridging the gap between traditional online learning environments and e-learning. This new approach resulted in a significant improvement in the retention of course materials, satisfaction levels, and learning outcomes when compared to traditional online learning environments. This research highlights the revolutionary potential of the convergence between VR and AI technologies and proposes a new approach to the creation of efficient and customized online learning environments.

title: Optimization of the LEACH Protocol in Wireless Sensor Networks Using Hybrid Grey Wolf and Moth Flame Optimization Algorithms
authors: Mallepula Sai Teja Goud, Akula Revanth, Askani Harish Sagar, Saroja Kumar Rout, Nikhila Kathirisetty, S Satheesh Kumar
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The general life span of WSN and Industrial WSN or remote health monitoring WSN depends exclusively on how well it can manage the depleting batteries in resource-constrained environments. Network longevity is primarily constrained by limited energy resources. Though conventional LEACH tries to save power by clustering nodes, it is a stochastic process of selecting Cluster Heads independent of the current status of the nodes, which often results in premature energy holes and network partitioning in dense deployments. In this paper, a hybrid protocol has been proposed for overcoming this variability by integrating the predatory social hierarchy of Grey Wolf Optimization with the navigational convergence of Moth Flame Optimization. Though simple, it is an effective logic to combine global exploration capability of GWO with precise local exploitation provided by MFO. Instead of being dependent on a random probability, Cluster Heads will now be selected based on optimal energy distribution and proximity metric. The strategy of hybridization could also be justified using the results as well. Compared to traditional methods and a single optimization strategy, the suggested system improves the Packet Delivery Ratio by 22.4% and the overall network lifetime by 18.6%. This plan reduces the average energy dissipation every round by 15.3% while producing a uniform residual energy distribution.

title: EfficientNet-DR: A Deep Learning Approach for Diabetic Retinopathy Detection and Classification
authors: Navyansh Kothari, Aryan Anand, Sandeep Kumar Satapathy, Shruti Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is one of the main causes of preventable vision loss, especially among working-age populations. In this paper, an automated detection and multiclass classification system for DR is proposed, which is optimized for deployment on low-end devices with very confined hardware and limited software capabilities. For the purpose of this paper, we relied on the first ever published dataset representing the Indian population, IDRiD, that comprises pixel-level annotations of DR lesions and normal retinal structures. All images are also classified on the basis of DR severities ranging from 0 (no DR) to 4 (proliferative DR), which are essential while giving precise diagnoses and the need for intervention. Preprocessing techniques including data cleaning, resizing, normalization, transformations, and data augmentation form an integral part of our methodology to significantly improve model performance. The trained and validated model of EfficientNet-B0 achieved a testing accuracy of 84.2 % for all the DR stages. The clinical utility of these results highlights the necessity to apply the idea of deep learning instruments to ophthalmology to more adequately screen system DR and patient outcomes.

title: 3D Superquadric Splatting
authors: Daniel MacSwayne, Aleš Leonardis, Jianbo Jiao
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Gaussian Splatting has proven to be an effective algorithm for novel view synthesis and 3D reconstruction from multi-view images. However, the underlying volumetric primitive—the ellipsoidal Gaussian—has limited expressive capabilities, leading to difficulties in 3D modelling (especially geometry such as edges, corners, and high curvature). To address this limitation, in this paper, we introduce Superquadric Splats (SQS), an extended class of volumetric primitives, as a superset of Gaussian Splats, to model more detailed geometry. We treat superquadrics as volumetric distance functions rather than level-set surfaces. A non-trivial differentiable rendering pipeline is developed to support this. Extensive experimental analysis on multiple datasets validates the effectiveness of the proposed SQS approach, showing both enhanced visual and geometric performance compared to Gaussian-based splatting (with more than 1dB in PSNR and prominent geometric improvement). Project page can be found at: https://daniel-macswayne.github.io/3DSQS/

title: Miniaturized Dual-Band Patch Antenna for Reliable Sensing Applications
authors: Soham Karak, Swapnaleena Biswas, Sneha Shaw, Ch. Raghavendra, A.Jhansirani, Gobinda Sen
abstract: This work presents the design and execution of a triangular, inexpensive microwave sensor for multiband sensing applications. The suggested sensor makes use of a Triangular Ring Resonator (TRR) as radiating patch, which gives a high Q factor. The design with footprint of size 9 mm × 31.32 mm make it very small and hence useful for portable applications. The sensor's substrate is chosen as a low loss Arlon material with a dielectric constant of 2.5 and a loss tangent of 0.0013. In order to characterize various oil samples, toluene, and ethanol etc., the sensor provides a strong sensing characteristic at the ISM bands with a high Q value of 21.7. The highest sensitivity of 16.75% is obtained with proposed sensor by submerging it within the sample material and hence make it very convenient for real-time monitoring in industrial, agricultural, and healthcare applications because to their affordability and small size, especially when it comes to guaranteeing food safety. The efficiency of the sensors is confirmed by experimental results, which also show that they have the potential to be widely used in a variety of sensing applications.

title: Real-Time Crop Sensing and Localization for Precision Agriculture
authors: Rakesh M D, Rohith Rajesh, Rudraswamy S B
abstract: This work introduces a real-time intelligent framework for subterranean crop sensing, depth estimation, and object tracking, designed to advance precision agriculture. Leveraging a custom-trained YOLOv11n model, the system accurately identifies crops such as beetroot, radish, and potatoes from RGB imagery and live video feeds. To determine spatial positioning, the approach integrates MiDaS a monocular depth estimation technique which infers Z-axis coordinates without the need for specialized depth sensors. The fusion of YOLO bounding box centers with MiDaS-generated depth maps enables estimation of actual crop-to-camera distances. Norfair-based multi-object tracking assigns unique IDs and preserves crop identity across sequential frames, ensuring consistency in dynamic agricultural settings. The end-to-end system delivers comprehensive spatial awareness vital for autonomous harvesting, targeted input application, and yield estimation. A user-centric Gradio interface supports intuitive interaction and visualization, making the tool accessible to both experts and farmers. Emphasizing scalability and adaptability, the framework can be easily customized for different crop types through dataset retraining. By supporting efficient resource utilization and promoting smart farming practices, this work directly contributes to key United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, including SDG 2, SDG 9 and SDG 12.

title: Optimization of Low-Noise Amplifier Circuits for Enhanced Sensor Signal Integrity
authors: Dayarnab Baidya, Athul Krishnan, Mitradip Bhattacharjee
abstract: A Low Noise Amplifier (LNA) acts as an important component in sensor system design for wireless sensing applications. In this direction, this paper demonstrates a novel approach to optimizing LNA design using Grey Relation Analysis (GRA), a multi-objective optimization method. This technique was used to increase gain (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21</inf>), and stability factor (K), and decrease input reflection coefficient (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>), reverse voltage gain (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</inf>), output reflection coefficient (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">22</inf>), and Noise Figure (NF) for the LNA circuit. The LNA circuit was simulated in the Advanced Design System (ADS) tool. The obtained optimized input values are R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> = 665 Ω, R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>= 12 Ω, L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> = 2 nH, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf> = 1.2 pF, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</inf> = 1.2 pF. In this situation, outputs had magnitudes as S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>= -14.007 dB, S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">22</inf>= -17.023 dB, S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</inf>= - 17.752 dB, S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21</inf>= 11.390 dB, K = 1.210, NF= 0.882 dB at 4 GHz. Furthermore, an analysis of variance (ANOVA) was performed to determine the impact of inputs on LNA outputs. This study suggests that the contribution of L3 is significantly high, with 98.62%.

title: Double-Sided Laser-Induced Graphene Memristors for Thermally Tunable Neuromorphic Computing
authors: Soha Maqbool Bhat, Fazian Tariq Beigh, Dhiman Mallick
abstract: In this work, the fabrication and characterisation of double-sided laser-induced graphene (D-LIG) memristors on non-adhesive polyimide (PI) substrates are presented. SEM, XRD, and Raman analyses confirm the formation of few-layer, porous graphene with low defect density (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> ~0.57, I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2D</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> ~0.62) on both surfaces. Electrical measurements show non-linear hysteresis indicative of memristive switching, while infrared thermography under 8 mA bias reveals self-regulated Joule heating and efficient vertical thermal transport through the PI layer. The strong electro-thermal coupling between dual LIG layers enables controlled resistance modulation and stable operation. These findings highlight the potential of D-LIG memristors as scalable, thermally adaptive platforms for neuromorphic and reservoir computing applications.

title: Scalable Fabrication of Double-Sided Laser-Induced Graphene for High-Sensitvity Triboelectric Pressure Sensors
authors: Tausif Kamal, Faizan Tariq Beigh, Ankur Goswami, Dhiman Mallick
abstract: Laser-induced graphene (LIG)-based sensors are durable, lightweight, and highly versatile, making them ideal for a wide range of potential applications, including healthcare, environmental monitoring, and industrial sensing. This study investigates the feasibility of utilizing LIG as both the electrode and the triboelectric layer in self-powered pressure sensors. Using CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> laser technology, LIG is fabricated via a single-step, chemical-free laser ablation process on polymeric substrates, offering a scalable and cost-effective method for developing triboelectric nanogenerators (TENGs). In this work, double-layer LIG structures with tailored resistances were fabricated on polyimide (PI) substrates, enabling independent control over the conductivity and charge storage properties of the electrode and triboelectric layers. This approach eliminates the need for additional materials, as distinct layers are formed directly on the top and bottom surfaces. A double-layer LIG-based TENG (DTENG) was developed using this configuration, with CYTOP employed as the counter triboelectric material. The performance of the DTENG was compared with that of a conventional copper-based TENG (CTENG) and a single-layer LIG-based TENG (STENG). Comparative analysis revealed that the DTENG exhibited a significantly enhanced peak voltage of 4 V. Finally, the fabricated devices were evaluated for pressure sensing, and their responses were analytically compared. The high sensitivity of 29.31 mV/kPa demonstrates that DTENGs are promising candidates for high-performance self-powered sensing systems.

title:
Benchmarking YOLOv12-A
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    F for Robust Detection of Underwater Objects
authors: Rajat Amat, Mourina Ghosh, Amitava Nag, Robin Pathak
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: In order to solve issues including turbidity, poor contrast, and color distortion, this study benchmarks the YOLOv12-A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>F model for reliable underwater object recognition. The model was trained and assessed on an underwater dataset encompassing varied item classifications and varying optical conditions. YOLOv12-A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>F obtained strong performance, with a precision of 85.6%, recall of 73.8%, mAP@50 of 81.5% and mAP@50-95 of 49.5%, exhibiting dependable localization and classification. With only 29.0 million parameters, the model stays efficient while delivering comparable accuracy. These findings demonstrate that YOLOv12-A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>F is a powerful and portable solution for problems involving underwater perception.

title: Explainable Multimodal Emotion Recognition Using Integrated Eye-Tracking and Galvanic Skin Response Signals
authors: Abhinav Rana, Kiran Jot Singh, Divneet Singh Kapoor
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Emotion recognition is an important part of affective computing and human-computer interaction. This paper suggests a multimodal emotion recognition system that can be explained with eye-tracking and galvanic skin response (GSR) measures to enhance the accuracy and understandability of classification. Eye-tracking capabilities are used to record visual attention and cognitive involvement, whereas GSR measures are used to estimate autonomic emotional arousal. The collection of synchronized multimodal data was performed with the stimuli which evoked emotions, they were preprocessed and features were extracted, and multimodal fusion was performed. Emotion classification was performed with the help of machine learning models, and explainable artificial intelligence methods were also implemented to explain the decisions of the models. The experimental findings indicate that multimodal approach is much better than the unimodal systems, and early fusion is the most accurate. The predominant role of the GSR amplitude and eye fixation measures is pointed out by explainability analysis and is expected according to the existing psychophysiological theories. The suggested architecture is strong, transparent, and has prospective applications in the real world like affect-aware systems, mental health monitoring, and adaptive human-computer interaction.

title: Distributed Computing Paradigms: Comparative Review of Cluster, Cloud, and Grid Technologies
authors: Venkatesh Bhat, Diya Shetty, Fredrina Saldanha, Chandushree B S, Daneshwari S Jammaladini
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: As distributed computing continues to evolve, it has become a cornerstone for efficiently processing massive data and executing complex workloads across diverse infrastructures. Among the dominant paradigms—cluster computing, grid computing, and cloud computing—each offers distinct architectures, operational models, and performance characteristics. This paper presents a structured comparative review of these paradigms, emphasizing their strengths, limitations, and application domains. Clusters deliver high performance and low latency for tightly coupled workloads; grids enable large-scale collaboration across heterogeneous, federated environments; and clouds provide elasticity, scalability, and cost-effective provisioning. Furthermore, the study discusses hybrid and emerging models driven by AI-based scheduling, containerization, and sustainability-oriented designs. The analysis highlights that no single paradigm universally outperforms the others; instead, optimal selection depends on workload characteristics, scalability requirements, and operational goals.

title: Proceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Renewable Energy and Electrical Technology (ICREET)

title: Cluster-based Pseudo-labeling for Semi-Supervised LiDAR Semantic Segmentation
authors: Qingju Guo, Shuang Li, Jing Geng, Binhui Xie, Jiawei Shan, Wei Li
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
