abstract: The costly annotation process has driven the development of semi-supervised learning (SSL) approaches. Existing semi-supervised LiDAR segmentation methods typically process entire point clouds directly, aiming to assign labels to all points at the scene scale. However, the large number of points, combined with their sparse and irregular nature, makes it challenging to learn scene-level optimization objectives, especially in SSL settings where labeled data are insufficient. This paper presents a Cluster-based pseudo-LAbeling Semi-Supervised technique, called CLASS. CLASS is designed to divide point clouds into several small, pure clusters, thereby decomposing challenging scene-scale segmentation task into more manageable cluster-scale classification and segmentation tasks, enabling the generation of high-quality pseudo labels for unlabeled data. CLASS possesses three key properties. i) Task simplicity: our pseudo-labeling process is based on simpler cluster-scale classification and segmentation tasks, resulting in ease of learning. ii) Labeling effectiveness: CLASS can generate pseudo-labels comparable to ground truth using only approximately 10% labeled data. iii) Universal versatility: CLASS exhibits flexibility regarding LiDAR representations (e.g., BEV, voxel, and range view). Comprehensive experiments on popular LiDAR segmentation benchmarks demonstrate its superiority.

title: Generative AI Meets 6G and Beyond: Diffusion Models for Semantic Communications
authors: Hai-Long Qin, Jincheng Dai, Guo Lu, Shuo Shao, Sixian Wang, Tongda Xu, Wenjun Zhang, Ping Zhang, Khaled B. Letaief
abstract: Semantic communications mark a paradigm shift from bit-accurate transmission toward meaning-centric communication, essential as wireless systems approach theoretical capacity limits. The emergence of generative AI has catalyzed generative semantic communications, where receivers reconstruct content from minimal semantic cues by leveraging learned priors. Among generative approaches, diffusion models stand out for their superior generation quality, stable training dynamics, and rigorous theoretical foundations. However, the field currently lacks systematic guidance connecting diffusion techniques to communication system design, forcing researchers to navigate disparate literatures. This article provides the first comprehensive tutorial on diffusion models for generative semantic communications. We present score-based diffusion foundations and systematically review three technical pillars: conditional diffusion for controllable generation, efficient diffusion for accelerated inference, and generalized diffusion for cross-domain adaptation. In addition, we introduce an inverse problem perspective that reformulates semantic decoding as posterior inference, bridging semantic communications with computational imaging. Through analysis of human-centric, machine-centric, and agent-centric scenarios, we illustrate how diffusion models enable extreme compression while maintaining semantic fidelity and robustness. By bridging generative AI innovations with communication system design, this article aims to establish diffusion models as foundational components of next-generation wireless networks and beyond.

title: Hyde: Inferring Cardiovascular Hemodynamics Using BCG for Cross-user Hypertension Detection
authors: Yandao Huang, Cong Li, Chenggao Li, Lin Chen, Junyao Peng, Weibin Cheng, Qian Zhang, Kaishun Wu
abstract: Hypertension, a leading contributor to global mortality, affects 1.28 billion adults worldwide, and its early detection is essential to prevent adverse outcomes. However, relying solely on blood pressure for hypertension detection proves insufficiently accurate. Recognizing this limitation, we have identified a range of multi-dimensional hemodynamics as novel biomarkers that can offer more comprehensive cardiovascular insights for detecting hypertension. Specifically, we propose Hyde, a cross-user hypertension detection system that leverages ballistocardiogram (BCG) signals to infer multi-dimensional hemodynamics and naturally detect hypertension without disrupting users' daily routines. Hyde features a multi-task, multi-branch, unsupervised domain adaptation learning framework, enabling simultaneous prediction of five hemodynamic biomarkers in the auxiliary regression task. This design addresses several challenges, including unlabeled target domain, single-source bias, and complete negative transfer. We implement a flexible optic fiber sensor mat to collect high-quality BCG signals and deploy it in a four-month clinical trial involving 85 subjects with different conditions. The results show that Hyde can accurately identify hypertension with an average accuracy of 97.65%, outperforming state-of-the-art approaches by 21.65% $\sim$ 38.16%. To further validate Hyde's reliability, we test the system with another 33 participants under natural, unconstrained sleep conditions, revealing consistent performance. These findings highlight Hyde's potential for long-term, non-contact hypertension assessment, offering valuable clinical implications for personalized healthcare and improving cardiovascular outcomes.

title: Topology-Preserving Deep Hashing for Ultrafast Drone-Dominated Object Detection
authors: Luming Zhang, Guifeng Wang, Zhiming Wang, Ling Shao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Drone (or unmanned aerial vehicle) has been extensively applied in many modern artificial intelligence systems in the past decade. In this work, we propose a novel deep hashing framework that can detect objects from drone-captured pictures extremely fast. Our method can intrinsically and flexibly encode various topological structures from each target object, based on which multiscale objects can be discovered in a view- and altitude-invariant way. Moreover, by leveraging <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$l_{F}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$l_{1}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> norms collaboratively, the calculated hash codes are robust to low-quality drone pictures and possibly contaminated semantic labels. More specifically, for each drone picture, we extract visually/semantically salient object parts inside it. To characterize their topological structure, we construct a graphlet by linking the spatially adjacent object patches into a small graph. Subsequently, a binary matrix factorization (MF) is designed to hierarchically exploit the semantics of these graphlets, wherein three attributes: 1) deep binary hash codes learning; 2) contaminated pictures/labels denoising; and 3) adaptive data graph updating are seamlessly incorporated. Accordingly, a manifold-regularized feature selector is adopted to further obtain more discriminative deep hash codes. Finally, the selected hash codes corresponding to graphlets within each drone photograph are utilized for ranking-based object discovery. Comprehensive experiments on the DAC-SDC, MOHR, and our self-compiled dataset have demonstrated the competitive speed and accuracy of our method.

title: A Compact Circularly Polarized Array Enabled by a Slot-Coupled Feeding Network
authors: Jingjing Mao, Andrea Michel, Shigang Zhou, Jianying Li, Paolo Nepa
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a compact circularly polarized (CP) antenna array enabled by a slot-coupled feeding network. By introducing open-ended branches and gradually widened terminals in inverted-F radiating elements, miniaturization of the antenna is achieved. The proposed CP feeding network exploits the inherent 180° phase difference of the non-resonant slot and incorporates 90° phase-delay lines to realize sequential CP excitation. When the feeding network is integrated with the radiating elements, an additional resonance is introduced, expanding the impedance bandwidth of the array from 5.5% to 14.9%. In addition, a Z-shaped slot is employed to effectively extend the axial-ratio bandwidth compared with a conventional straight slot. Simulated and measured results demonstrate that the proposed antenna achieves 4.5% CP bandwidth (845-884 MHz). The size of the antenna is only 0.231 × 0.231 × 0.064 λ03. The proposed design avoids the use of lumped elements or high-permittivity materials, providing a practical solution for compact CP arrays.

title: A Novel Filtering Method for Industrial Target Tracking Using Millimeter-Wave Radar
authors: Chenwei Fan, Rui Zheng, Shuhong Liang, Min Wu, Xilin Rui, Zibao Lu, Fangdong Li, Xiaoyao Zheng
abstract: In this paper, a novel target tracking and filtering method is proposed to address the challenges of millimeter-wave radar perception in industrial environments, including multipath interference, high noise levels, and stringent real-time requirements. A velocity-constrained multidimensional Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) clustering algorithm is developed to improve clustering accuracy and stability by exploiting the triangle inequality to accelerate processing and reduce false alarms. For target tracking, a constant-velocity (CV) motion model is adopted, and radial velocity information is incorporated to derive a nonlinear measurement equation for constructing an extended Kalman filter (EKF) framework. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method improves clustering accuracy by 19%, achieves 94% clustering stability, and increases clustering speed by approximately eight times. Additionally, compared with the DBSCAN+KF-based tracking method and the GTRACK method, the proposed method achieves a higher tracking success rate and more accurate robot tracking.

title: A Frame-Theoretic Approach to Robust Filter Pruning in Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Pradyumna Pradhan, Pradip Sasmal, Ramunaidu Randhi
abstract: Filter pruning method is an effective approach to eliminate redundant filters in convolutional neural networks. Most of the existing techniques rely on threshold parameters for filter selection and employ multi-shot pruning, resulting in increased computational overhead. In addition, many methods primarily optimize test accuracy while overlooking robustness to noise, which is critical in practical and safety-critical applications. Since Incoherent Unit Norm Tight Frames (IUNTFs) are inherently robust to noise, we propose a filter pruning technique that removes redundant filters such that the retained set approximates an IUNTF structure. This structure enhances noise robustness and promotes the extraction of diverse and complementary features. Unlike conventional approaches, the proposed framework requires no threshold tuning and adopts a one-shot pruning strategy. Experimental results on the CIFAR-10 dataset using VGG-16, ResNet-56, and GoogLeNet demonstrate that the proposed method achieves robustness under noise and improved accuracy compared to existing approaches while pruning 85.12%, 57%, and 59% of the parameters and 69.66%, 57%, and 58% of the FLOPs, respectively.

title: Real-Time Vision-Based Disease Detection Using Edge- Optimized Deep Learning Models
authors: Jayamala Hipparkar, Altaf Mulani, Vaibhav Godase, Swapnil Takale, Rahul Ghodake
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Real-time vision-based disease detection has gained significant attention due to its potential to support early screening and rapid clinical decision-making. However, most existing deep learning models are computationally intensive and unsuitable for deployment on edge devices with limited processing power, memory, and energy resources. This limitation restricts their practical use in point-of-care and resource-constrained environments. In this paper, we propose a real-time vision-based disease detection framework using edge-optimized deep learning models. The proposed approach leverages lightweight convolutional neural network architectures and model optimization techniques to achieve an effective balance between classification accuracy and inference efficiency. Publicly available medical image datasets are used to evaluate the framework, and performance is assessed in terms of accuracy, inference latency, frames per second (FPS), and model size. Experimental results demonstrate that optimized models significantly reduce computational overhead while maintaining competitive detection performance, making the proposed framework suitable for real-time edge-based disease screening applications.

title: SymNet: A Multi-Task Network for Joint Radio Map Reconstruction and Transmitter Localization
authors: Lyuzhou Ye, Thanh Dat Le, Yan Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurately predicting directional radio maps is essential for wireless applications, yet prior approaches primarily focus on omnidirectional signals and typically treat transmitter localization and signal map reconstruction as separate tasks. In omnidirectional settings, predicting the maximum signal location often coincides with the transmitter position, which limits the need for explicit joint modeling. However, in directional propagation where angular effects, reflections, and building occlusions play critical roles, this assumption no longer holds. To address this gap, we propose SymNet, a unified framework that jointly predicts directional radio maps and transmitter locations from sparse signal measurements. SymNet incorporates a prediction head for transmitter localization alongside radio map reconstruction, enabling simultaneous learning of both tasks. This joint formulation leverages their complementary information and leads to consistent improvements over treating them separately. Experiments on challenging directional scenarios demonstrate that SymNet outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving superior accuracy in both radio map reconstruction and transmitter localization.

title: Energy Optimization and Trust-Based Security Model for Enhanced WSN Performance
authors: Neela Kiranmai, R Raja Sekhar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) find their applications in essential fields like environmental monitoring, healthcare, and defence yet they have been having a consistent challenge concerning the restricted power resources and exposure to security threats. In order to deal with these problems, the given paper offers a dual-module integrated framework that will include the energy optimization and improvement of the trust. The former relies on LiqOG-Net (Liquid Neural Networks + Osprey Optimization + GRU) to perform adaptive clustering and efficient selection of cluster head to minimize the energy loss and prolong the lifetime of the network. The second module relies on LiteSBLA-Net ( SqueezeNet + Bi-LSTM + Adam optimizer ) to develop a powerful trust management and anomaly detector system that can detect and counteract attacks, including Blackhole, Sybil, Selective Forwarding, Replay, Jamming, and Sinkhole. The experimental findings indicate vast improvements in the experimental period as the network life cycle is achieved by a significant percentage, the average energy usage is minimized, and more than 98 percent of the detection of malicious nodes is achieved, which is testament to the fact that the proposed framework will provide sustainable, secure, and high-performance WSNs operations.

title: Training-free Detection of Text-to-video Generations via Over-coherence
authors: Jonathan Brokman, Oren Rachmil, Omer Hofman, Roy Betser, Amit Giloni, Roman Vainshtein, Hisashi Kojima
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Text-to-video generative models have emerged as powerful tools in content creation, capable of synthesizing highly realistic videos from textual prompts. However, the rapid advancement of these models introduces significant security and trust concerns - adversaries can now fabricate convincing videos, while existing detection methods struggle to generalize to unseen generative techniques. This is due to existing approaches’ reliance on supervised learning, requiring continuous dataset curation and retraining, which is impractical given the fast-paced evolution of generative models. In this work, we introduce the first training-free detection method for text-to-video generations, eliminating the need for labeled training data or prior exposure to generation techniques. Our approach exploits a fundamental weakness in text-to-video models: Unnatural temporal over-coherence in frame transitions. By leveraging a novel time-coherence detection criterion, our method identifies subtle inconsistencies in video embeddings, capturing temporal artifact patterns that reliably distinguish AI-generated content from real videos. We extensively evaluate our approach on an extended combination of benchmarks, amounting to 23 video generative models and 54.4K diverse videos, demonstrating that it outperforms the existing baselines, and shown to be effective on unseen generative models. This work establishes a new direction for training-free detection of text-to-video generated content, providing a scalable and time resilient solution.

title: Low-Power Intelligent Safety Tool Cabinet: Integration of Electro-Osmotic Dehumidification and IoT Management System
authors: Ke Feng, Daoze Lin, Jinlan Xu, Zhongxiu Weng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a lowpower zero-drainage intelligent safety tool cabinet integrating electro-osmotic solid adsorption dehumidification technology and IoT-enabled management system. Aimed at addressing the limitations of traditional cabinets, including high energy consumption, manual drainage requirements, and inefficient dehumidification <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.3 \mathrm{L} / \mathrm{D})$</tex>, this study introduces a novel system architecture combining electro-dynamic dehumidification and smart tool lifecycle management. The core innovations include: (1) a composite adsorbent material optimized through molecular dynamics simulation and 3D printing, achieving 0.51L/D dehumidification capacity in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex> space at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35^{\circ} \mathrm{C} / 85 {\%} \text{RH}$</tex>; (2) a fuzzy PID control algorithm enabling ± 3%RH humidity accuracy with energy consumption reduced by 85%; (3) an IoT platform with RFID-based tool tracking and two-factor authentication for automated inventory management. Field validation in 15 cabinets across 7 power tool warehouses demonstrated a 40% reduction in tool damage incidents and 60% improvement in operational efficiency. This work provides a sustainable solution for real-time humidity control and digital traceability of safety tools, meeting industry standards while opening new avenues for AI-driven predictive maintenance and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> integration.

title: Forget Less by Learning Together through Concept Consolidation
authors: Arjun Ramesh Kaushik, Naresh Kumar Devulapally, Vishnu Suresh Lokhande, Nalini Ratha, Venu Govindaraju
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Custom Diffusion Models (CDMs) have gained significant attention due to their remarkable ability to personalize generative processes. However, existing CDMs suffer from catastrophic forgetting when continuously learning new concepts. Most prior works attempt to mitigate this issue under the sequential learning setting with a fixed order of concept inflow and neglect inter-concept interactions. In this paper, we propose a novel framework -Forget Less by Learning Together (FL2T) - that enables concurrent and order-agnostic concept learning while addressing catastrophic forgetting. Specifically, we introduce a set-invariant inter-concept learning module where proxies guide feature selection across concepts, facilitating improved knowledge retention and transfer. By leveraging inter-concept guidance, our approach preserves old concepts while efficiently incorporating new ones. Extensive experiments, across three datasets, demonstrates that our method significantly improves concept retention and mitigates catastrophic forgetting, highlighting the effectiveness of inter-concept catalytic behavior in incremental concept learning of ten tasks with at least 2% gain on average CLIP Image Alignment scores.

title: Analysis of Object Detection in Optical Remote Sensing Images using YOLO Frameworks
authors: Darshan Shankar R, Niharika G, Dilip Kumar Dalei, Narayan Panigrahi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Automatic analysis and Interpretation of remote sensing images is a growing area of interest among geospatial researchers and engineers. This is quite evident by observing the unprecedented growth in geospatial data and systems last decade. There are many important tasks in remote sensing image interpretation - Scene Classification, Object Detection, Change Detections etc. The object detection task in remote sensing has wide applications in both civilian and military applications. Modern remote sensing devices have made it possible to capture high-resolution spatial-temporal images of Earth's surface. Analysis of these large and complex datasets allows us to understand and interpret complex phenomena on and around earth, but the size and complexity of these datasets limit the manual methods. Deep Learning based methods has potential to handle these large and complex datasets for post analysis. Automatic object detection techniques are now feasible due to the rise in deep learning.YOLO frameworks are the industry-standard methods of object identification in images and videos. Their application in remote sensing has been on the rise, owing to their success in other domains of image. It has evolved in several versions with varied capabilities and applications. Their applicability in remote sensing image analysis is quite evident and is being researched word-wide. The current paper also explores possible solutions and challenges of fitting these models for object detection in remote sensing images. It focuses the study and analysis to understand the architecture of these models and their implementation strategy for Optical remote sensing images. The models comprise previous important versions of YOLO, including very recent YOLO 11 models.

title: HiMix : Hierarchical Visual-Textual Mixing Network for Lesion Segmentation
authors: Soojin Hwang, Jaeyoon Sim, Won Hwa Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Lesion segmentation is an essential task in medical imaging to support diagnosis and assessment of pathologies. While deep learning models have shown success in various domains, their reliance on large-scale annotated datasets limits applicability in the medical domain due to labeling cost. To address this issue, recent studies in medical image segmentation have utilized clinical texts as complementary semantic cues without additional annotations. However, most existing methods utilize a single textual embedding and fail to capture hierarchical interactions between language and visual features, which limits their ability to leverage fine-grained cues essential for precise and detailed segmentation. In this regime, we propose Hierarchical Visual-Textual Mixing Network (HiMix), a novel multi-modal segmentation framework that mixes multi-scale image and text representations throughout the mask decoding process. HiMix progressively injects hierarchical text embedding, from high-level semantics to fine-grained spatial details, into corresponding image decoder layers to bridge the modality gap and enhance visual feature refinement at multiple levels of abstraction. Experiments on the QaTa-COV19, MosMed-Data+ and Kvasir-SEG datasets demonstrate that HiMix consistently outperforms uni-modal and multi-modal methods. Furthermore, HiMix exhibits strong generalization to unstructured textual formats, highlighting its practical applicability in real-world clinical scenarios.

title: DoTA: Latent Distribution Conditioned Data Attribution for Diffusion Models
authors: Ninad Joshi, Vivek Srivastava, Shirish Karande
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Diffusion models have emerged as the backbone of several modern generative AI models for effective visual content generation. However, their opaque nature raises fundamental questions about which training samples are responsible for specific generations, especially in applications involving bias detection, model auditing, and dataset curation. Data attribution seeks to identify the training samples that highly influence the output of generative models, a task that becomes especially challenging when targeting fine-scale attributes for attribution. Prior work has focused on broad concepts such as global features or entire images, often overlooking the nuances of fine-grained attributes and relying on group-based strategies that dilute individual influence. We propose a novel latent distribution conditioned method DoTA for data attribution. DoTA presents an effective search space pruning technique based on the latent distribution matching between the generated and training data for effective and controlled attribution. We demonstrate the effectiveness of attribution through extensive quantitative and qualitative evaluations across challenging settings such as counterfactual evaluation and robustness to adversarial attack.

title: MooTrack360: A Novel Fisheye Camera Dataset for Robust Multi Dairy Cow Detection and Tracking
authors: Rasmus Gjerlund K. Christiansen, Toan Van Nguyen, Lasse Rose Malskær, Leon Bodenhagen, Dirk Kraft
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: MooTrack360 is a novel top-down fisheye dataset designed to support the development of robust camera surveillance and monitoring systems in large-scale, real-world environments. While centered around continuous livestock monitoring of Holstein dairy cows, the dataset addresses general challenges in computer vision such as fisheye distortion, overlapping fields of view, variable lighting, and occlusions. It includes 102,747 annotated cow instances across 1,500 images, each labelled as "standing" or "lying", along with a 1-hour annotated video sequence for tracking evaluation. Several unannotated sequences are included for development and analysis. The dataset enables detection and tracking under conditions ranging from daylight to infrared-assisted nighttime imaging, and facilitates both application-specific and generalized model evaluation. A calibration pipeline based on the Double Sphere Camera model is provided to support distortion correction and precise spatial localization. An ac companying end-to-end training framework further addresses challenges such as illumination changes and occlusions. Benchmarks using state-of-the-art detection and tracking methods demonstrate the dataset’s potential to advance research in non-invasive, camera-based monitoring across domains. The complete dataset—including annotated images, tracking sequences, sample videos, calibration footage, and the full codebase, is available at: https://zenodo.org/records/15470268.

title: Digital Forensic AI You Can Explain: A Case Study on Video Source Camera Identification
authors: Maryna Veksler, Kemal Akkaya, Selcuk Uluagac
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: In recent years, artificial intelligence (AI) has significantly impacted digital forensics, yet its broader deployment remains limited due to the difficulty of explaining AI decisions. Explainable AI (XAI) presents a promising solution to increase transparency and trust, but its application in digital forensics is still underexplored. In this work, we present a practical and structured explainable digital forensics AI (xDFAI) approach tailored to the forensic task of video source camera identification (VSCI). Our method enables forensic examiners to interpret the behavior of AI models, assess whether decisions are driven by intended logic or arise from random or content-dependent artifacts, and establish the integrity and reliability of explanations. We implement and evaluate this approach on two state-of-the-art VSCI models, providing step-by-step analysis of explanation quality, spatial consistency of high-impact features, and content dependence. Our results reveal that although models achieve strong classification accuracy, their explanations lack spatial stability and are impacted by video content, raising concerns about forensic reliability. To support reproducibility and future research, we provide an open-source implementation. This work underscores the potential of XAI to improve transparency in digital forensics and highlights the challenges of interpreting and presenting results. Our study takes an important step toward the operational deployment of xDFAI in multimedia forensics.

title: ClusterMine: Robust Label-Free Visual Out-Of-Distribution Detection via Concept Mining from Text Corpora
authors: Nikolas Adaloglou, Diana Petrusheva, Mohamed Asker, Felix Michels, Markus Kollmann
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Large-scale visual out-of-distribution (OOD) detection has witnessed remarkable progress by leveraging vision-language models such as CLIP. However, a significant limitation of current methods is their reliance on a pre-defined set of in-distribution (ID) ground-truth label names (positives). These fixed label names can be unavailable, unreliable at scale, or become less relevant due to in-distribution shifts after deployment. Towards truly unsupervised OOD detection, we utilize widely available text corpora for positive label mining, bypassing the need for positives. In this paper, we utilize widely available text corpora for positive label mining under a general concept mining paradigm. Within this framework, we propose ClusterMine, a novel method for mining positive labels. ClusterMine is the first method to achieve state-of-the-art OOD detection performance without access to positive labels. It extracts positive concepts from a large text corpus by combining visual-only sample consistency (via clustering) and zero-shot image-text consistency. Our experimental study reveals that ClusterMine is scalable across a plethora of CLIP models and achieves state-of-the-art robustness to covariate in-distribution shifts. The code is available at https://github.com/HHU-MMBS/clustermine_wacv_official.

title: Use HBAR to Extract the ${k}_{\textit{eff}}{}^{{2}}$ and the Q Value of FBAR
authors: Danyu Mu, Weipeng Xuan, Gang Liu, Rui Ding, Feng Gao, Hao Jin, Jikui Luo, Wenjun Li, Lingling Sun, Shurong Dong
abstract: This article proposes a fitting method based on the harmonic bulk acoustic resonator (HBAR) for precisely extracting the effective electromechanical coupling coefficient (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${k}_{\textit {eff}}{}^{{2}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and quality factor (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$Q$</tex-math> </inline-formula> value) of a film bulk acoustic resonator (FBAR). This method successfully simulated the impedance of the FBAR based on an Al<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{{0}.{7}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>Sc<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{{0}.{3}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>N film after release by precisely fitting the HBAR impedance and obtained its <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${f}_{p},{k}_{\textit {eff}}{}^{{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$Q$</tex-math> </inline-formula> value. The experimental results show that compared with the measured results of FBAR, the relative error of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${k}_{\textit {eff}}{}^{{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> predicted by the HBAR is less than 5%, and the relative error of the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${Q}_{p}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> value is less than 25%, the relative error of the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${Q}_{s}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> value is less than 26%. This work provides an efficient and reliable characterization tool for the process optimization and performance prediction of the FBAR based on HBAR.

title: Concord: Concept-Informed Diffusion for Dataset Distillation
authors: Jianyang Gu, Haonan Wang, Ruoxi Jia, Saeed Vahidian, Vyacheslav Kungurtsev, Wei Jiang, Yiran Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Dataset distillation (DD) has witnessed significant progress in creating small datasets that encapsulate rich information from large original ones. Particularly, methods based on generative priors show promising performance while maintaining computational efficiency and cross-architecture generalization. However, the generation process lacks explicit controllability for each sample. Previous distillation methods primarily match the real distribution from the perspective of the entire dataset, whereas overlooking concept completeness at the instance level. The missing or incorrectly represented object details cannot be efficiently compensated due to the constrained sample amount typical in DD settings. To this end, we propose incorporating the concept understanding of large language models (LLMs) to perform Concept-Informed Diffusion (Concord) for dataset distillation. Specifically, distinguishable and fine-grained concepts are retrieved based on category labels to inform the denoising process and refine essential object details. These concepts can be applied to any diffusion-based DD framework to enhance both the controllability and interpretability of the distilled image generation, without relying on pre-trained classifiers. We demonstrate the efficacy of Concord by achieving state-of-the-art performance on ImageNet-1K and subsets. Code is released at Concord.

title: Regulation of Magnetic Properties in Fe-based Composite Ribbons by External Magnetic Field Coating
authors: Z.L. Ye, H.R. Dong, M.Q. Li, Z.J. Zhang, J.R. Wang, X. Li, Z.J. Zhao
abstract: In this study, Fe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">73.5</sub>Cu<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub>Nb<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>Si<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">13.5</sub>B<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">9</sub> amorphous ribbons were used as substrates to fabricate FINEMET/Fe₅₀Ni₅₀ composite ribbons using three deposition techniques: conventional magnetron sputtering (CMS), transverse magnetic field-assisted sputtering (TMF) and perpendicular magnetic field-assisted sputtering (PMF). We aimed to clarify the mechanism how the orientation of an external magnetic field during deposition regulated surface morphology, magnetic hysteresis behavior, and the giant magnetoimpedance (GMI) effect of the resulting composite ribbons. The results demonstrated that magnetic field-assisted deposition promoted the directional alignment of magnetic particles and significantly enhanced the magnetic properties of the films. TMF facilitated the formation of a well-ordered transverse magnetic domain structure, increasing the maximum GMI ratio to 55.57%, approximately 15 % higher than the 40.50% obtained with CMS. Meanwhile, PMF yielded a more refined magnetic domain structure, resulting in the lowest coercivity among the three methods and a maximum GMI ratio of 62.25%, which substantially improved both soft magnetic characteristics and magnetic-field response speed. The magnetic field-assisted sputtering strategy proposed in this work enabled active control of magnetic domain orientation during the film preparation process, effectively overcoming the limitations of post-deposition treatments. This approach offered a promising pathway for developing high-performance GMI composite materials with great potential in weak magnetic field detection and information storage application.

title: DermEVAL: A Dermatologist-Reviewed Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Hongjin Zhao, Weihao Li, Zhenyue Qin, Ge-Peng Ji, Yang Liu, Tom Gedeon, Nick Barnes
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Clinical photographs play a vital role in conversational computer-aided diagnosis, particularly in dermatology. However, existing skin disease benchmarks contain limitations like insufficient dataset size, the sole presence of categorical labels, the lack of expert inspections, and limited diversity in annotations. To address these shortcomings, we introduce DermEVAL, a large-scale benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the performance of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in dermatology. Our benchmark includes image-text pairs depicting 16 distinct skin diseases, featuring a total of 11,347 representative images drawn from various dermatological datasets, carefully selected and annotated with the guidance of dermatologists. DermEVAL enables two primary tasks: visual question answering (VQA) and medical report generation (MRG), designed to simulate real-world medical diagnostics. We evaluate the performance of MLLMs in dermatology using multiple metrics, including traditional metrics and GPT-4V-based assessments. Our results indicate that accurately diagnosing skin diseases remains challenging for state-of-the-art MLLMs. We also demonstrate that fine-tuning MLLMs using DermEVAL significantly improves their performance on dermatology-related image–text tasks.

title: Delay-Compensated Double-Loop IDFD Controller for EV-Assisted Renewable-Integrated Thermal Power Systems Amid Cyber Threats
authors: Deepak Kumar, Mohamed Alkhatib, G. Lloyds Raja, Omar Al Zaabi, Khalifa Al Hosani, Utkal Ranjan Muduli
abstract: Modern automatic generation control (AGC) systems suffer from instability as well as delayed corrective actions in response to frequency and tie line power aberrations due to communication time delays (TD) and cyber threats. Additionally, the sporadic nature of the renewable energy sources (RES), electric vehicles (EVs) and energy storage systems further introduces complexities in AGC. Therefore, this work proposes a double-loop-based integral dual fractional derivative (IDFD) controller to enhance robustness and stability in EV-assisted dual-area thermal power systems (TPS) under such adverse conditions. The IDFD controller is developed via the internal model control theory grounded on the fractional-order model of the TPS. The performance-robustness tradeoff is improved through the judicious selection of the single tuning parameter (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha$</tex-math></inline-formula>) based on gain and phase margin specifications with birds-of-prey-based optimisation by minimising integral time absolute error of area control error. The proposed controller is validated using both simulation and hardware-in-the-loop implementation to show its practical feasibility. Case studies are performed on dual-area TPS and a realistic IEEE 39- bus test system, demonstrating superior dynamic response and enhanced resilience against cyber threats, TD, parametric uncertainties, multiple-step load disturbance, and non-linearities.

title: Investigation on the Single-Event Burnout Mechanism and Hardening Design in p-GaN Gate AlGaN/GaN HEMTs
authors: Danmei Lin, Xuefeng Zheng, Xiaohu Wang, Longbing Yi, Rui Han, Kai Liu, Hao Zhang, Yingzhe Wang, Xiaohua Ma, Yue Hao
abstract: Single-event burnout (SEB) has become a critical reliability concern for p-gallium nitride (GaN) gate AlGaN/GaN high-electron-mobility transistors (HEMTs) operating in radiation environments. In this work, the SEB mechanism of conventional p-GaN gate AlGaN/GaN HEMTs has been systematically investigated, and a novel hardening design has been proposed to suppress device burnout. Under Ta-ions irradiation with a linear energy transfer (LET) of 63.8 MeV<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula>cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/mg, the 650 V p-GaN gate HEMT exhibited SEB when the drain voltage reached 390 V, with the failure region located near the drain edge. TCAD simulation revealed that the burnout is caused by the high electric field near the drain region. Based on the SEB mechanism, a hardened p-GaN gate HEMT with a drain-connected n-AlGaN cap layer has been designed and verified through TCAD simulation. The proposed structure effectively modulates the electric-field distribution, significantly reducing the peak electric field near the drain after heavy-ion incidence. As a result, the SEB threshold voltage increases from 400 to 700 V under a LET of 0.6 pC/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m, demonstrating a significant improvement in SEB characteristic compared to the conventional p-GaN gate AlGaN/GaN HEMT.

title: A 3-D Magneto-Thermal Coupling Model for Ferrite Toroidal Cores Based on Vector Magnetic-Circuit Theory
authors: Haodong Wang, Zheng Wang, Wei Qin, Ming Cheng, Xiang Ma, Chengbo Li
abstract: Accurate prediction of magnetic loss and temperature rise is critical for design of ferrite toroidal cores. Compared to three-dimensional finite-element analysis (FEA), equivalent magnetic circuit (EMC) based modeling methods map regular-shaped geometries into lumped magnetic circuit elements, thus achieving higher computation efficiency while maintaining good accuracy for field analysis. However, conventional magnetic-circuit models with only reluctance is unavailable for magneto-thermal coupling analysis. Based on the vector magnetic-circuit theory, this paper proposes a three-dimensional (3-D) magneto-thermal coupling model for ferrite toroidal cores. By constructing a discrete magnetic-circuit mesh containing both reluctance and equivalent magnetic reactance, the proposed modeling method achieves fast solutions for power loss and the 3-D distribution of magnetic flux density simultaneously. Furthermore, the magnetic-thermal coupling analysis is achieved by exchanging data with a similar 3-D thermal network. Both simulation and experiments have verified that the proposed modeling method enables accurate prediction of magnetic and thermal fields, but also significantly reduces computation time and memory requirements compared with FEA.

title: CraftSVG: Multi-Object Text-to-SVG Synthesis via Layout Guided Diffusion
authors: Ayan Banerjee, Nityanand Mathur, Josep Lladós, Umapada Pal, Anjan Dutta
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating SVGs from text is a challenging vision task, requiring diverse yet realistic depictions of the seen as well as unseen entities. Existing research has been mostly limited to generating single-object rather than comprehensive scenes comprising multiple elements. In response, CraftSVG introduces an end-to-end framework for creating SVGs depicting entire scenes from a textual description. Utilizing a pre-trained LLM for layout generation from text via iterative in-context learning, CraftSVG introduces a per-box mask latent mechanism for accurate object placement. A fusion mechanism is developed to integrate the attention maps, employing a diffusion U-Net for coherent composition, which accelerates stroke initialization. Recognizing the importance of abstract SVGs in communication, we incorporated an MLP-based mechanism to simplify SVGs, with alignment and perceptual loss via differential rendering and opacity modulation to improve aesthetics. CraftSVG outperforms previous methods in abstraction, recognizability, and detail, as depicted by its CLIP-T: 0.5013, Aesthetic: 7.0779, score. The code is available at github.com/CraftSVG.

title: Electrical Consumption Data Aggregation Impact on REC Analysis: A Real Case Study Including Industrial and Commercial Users
authors: Valentina Consolo, Roberto Rugani, Elena Mura, Marco Raugi
abstract: Renewable energy communities (RECs) are emerging as key contributors to grid decarbonization and energy poverty alleviation. However, the accuracy of REC performance assessments strongly depends on the resolution of input consumption data. Since shared energy is defined hour by hour, an accurate analysis of a REC should be based on an hourly simulation of the community’s energy flows. While detailed hourly data are ideal, they are often unavailable in practical applications. This study addresses the gap by evaluating how different levels of consumption data aggregation affect both technical and financial outcomes in REC feasibility studies. A real-world REC case study in central Italy, including commercial and industrial users, was simulated using a MATLAB-based model across four scenarios: hourly measured data and three synthetic profiles based on monthly time bands, monthly totals, and annual consumption. The results show that higher aggregation leads to significant losses in hourly accuracy, particularly in the presence of large prosumers with seasonal load variations. Still, monthly aggregated models—especially those disaggregated by time bands—yield robust approximations of long-term financial metrics. As expected, the estimation of hourly shared energy from aggregated data introduces information loss and unrealistic dynamics. Nonetheless, monthly banded profiles provide a reliable compromise. Future work should improve disaggregation methods—e.g., through typical load curves by building use—and extend validation across diverse REC configurations to refine control strategies and planning.

title: WWE-UIE: A Wavelet & White Balance Efficient Network for Underwater Image Enhancement
authors: Ching-Heng Cheng, Jen-Wei Lee, Chia-Ming Lee, Chih-Chung Hsu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Underwater Image Enhancement (UIE) aims to restore visibility and correct color distortions caused by wavelength-dependent absorption and scattering. Recent hybrid approaches, which couple domain priors with modern deep neural architectures, have achieved strong performance but incur high computational cost, limiting their practicality in real-time scenarios. In this work, we propose WWE-UIE, a compact and efficient enhancement network that integrates three interpretable priors. First, adaptive white balance alleviates the strong wavelength-dependent color attenuation, particularly the dominance of blue-green tones. Second, a wavelet-based enhancement block (WEB) performs multi-band decomposition, enabling the network to capture both global structures and fine textures, which are critical for underwater restoration. Third, a gradient-aware module (SGFB) leverages Sobel operators with learnable gating to explicitly preserve edge structures degraded by scattering. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that WWE-UIE achieves competitive restoration quality with substantially fewer parameters and FLOPs, enabling real-time inference on resource-limited platforms. Ablation studies and visualizations further validate the contribution of each component. The source code is available at https://github.com/chingheng0808/WWE-UIE.

title: Low-Voltage Distribution Network Line Loss Rate Prediction Based on Gradient Boosting Decision Trees
authors: Tian Wen, Kai Cuiying, Liu Huiying, Hu Jingyu
abstract: In the field of line loss prediction for low-voltage distribution networks, conventional models are largely constrained by static feature analysis frameworks, which limits their ability to capture the combined effects of dynamic load fluctuations and topological heterogeneity on line losses. To address this limitation, this study proposes a Distributed estimation of the fusion of Bagging in Gradient Boosting Decision Tree prediction (DB-GBDT) model, which integrates dynamic feature extraction with distributed estimation. First, a high-dimensional hybrid feature space is constructed by temporally aligning static line parameters with dynamic operational data and performing feature interaction, resulting in a feature matrix that explicitly incorporates spatiotemporal coupling information. Next, distribution areas are adaptively grouped based on feature similarity, and lightweight GBDT sub-models are independently trained within each group. Global prediction is then achieved through dynamic weighted aggregation of the sub-model outputs. Experimental results on the LVDN-LLD and PGLP-Bench datasets demonstrate that the proposed model achieves an AUC of 0.965 (95% CI: 0.958–0.972), representing a 4.4% improvement over standard GBDT. The F1-score reaches 92.3% under dynamic operating conditions, while the inference speed is 21% faster than that of LightGBM. Compared with traditional methods such as SVM, the proposed approach reduces generalization error by 62%, significantly enhancing prediction stability under complex network topologies.

title: Numerical Analisys of Quasi-Periodic Concentric Hexagonal Ring Antenna Array Based on the Thue-Morse Sequence
authors: F. P. Chietera, W. U. Usmani, L. Mescia
publish: IEEE Journal of Radio Frequency Identification
abstract: This work investigates performance enhancement in phased antenna arrays arranged using a quasi-periodic concentric hexagonal ring array structure. The proposed PAA employs an unconventional geometric layout in which antenna elements are placed on concentric hexagonal rings, with inter-ring spacing determined by the Thue–Morse sequence. The impact of this quasi-periodic variation on array performance is systematically analyzed in terms of half-power beamwidth, sidelobe level, and scan capability, focused on the grating lobes behavior. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed concentric hexagonal rings array configuration yields measurable improvements if compared with standard ones, especially in terms of grating lobes mitigation, highlighting its potential for advanced phased array applications.

title: Adversarial Machine Learning: A 20-Year Survey of Attacks, Defenses, and Standards
authors: Benhur Tekeste, Khalil Al-Hussaeni, Benjamin C. M. Fung, Ibtesam Alawadhi, Claude Fachkha
abstract: Adversarial Machine Learning (AML) presents a significant barrier to the large-scale deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in safety-critical environments. While early research focused on algorithmic robustness, the field has evolved into a complex intersection of security, assurance, and policy. This paper presents a comprehensive, multidisciplinary survey of the AML landscape, covering over 250 peer-reviewed contributions. We implement a lifecycle-oriented taxonomy that maps attack vectors and defense mechanisms to specific stages of the AI pipeline from data collection to deployment, expanding the traditional Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA) triad to include Governance and Regulation.We identify critical research gaps, including certified robustness for Natural Language Processing (NLP), and emerging threats in Generative AI. To ground these theoretical insights in practice, we analyze five domain-specific case studies: Autonomous Vehicles, Medical AI, Financial Systems, Natural Language Processing (NLP), and the Internet of Things (IoT). Uniquely, this survey bridges the gap between academic literature and industrial practice by mapping technical AML findings to emerging standards, including the NIST AI Risk Management Framework (RMF), MITRE ATLAS, and ISO/IEC 42001. We conclude by providing a roadmap for researchers, practitioners, and regulators to build verifiable, trustworthy, and compliant AI systems.

title: Morphology-Driven and Symmetry-Guided Geometric Segmentation of White Blood Cells with Self-Consistency Reliability Assessment
authors: P Umarani, P Viswanathan
abstract: Accurate segmentation of blood cells in microscopic images is a fundamental step in automated hematological analysis, yet it remains challenging because of staining variability, illumination inconsistencies, imaging noise, and severe cell overlap. This paper presents a unified, fully automated morphological and symmetry-based framework for white blood cell region segmentation in blood smear images. The proposed method integrates Laplacian-of-Gaussian-based contrast enhancement, hybrid color-space analysis in RGB, CIELab∗, and HSV domains for candidate region extraction, hierarchical segmentation refinement, and marker-controlled watershed for instance-level separation of touching and overlapping cells. To support annotation-free internal assessment, a novel Self-Consistency Reliability Index (SCRI) is introduced by combining stage-wise structural stability, boundary-alignment fidelity, and geometric shape reliability. Experimental evaluation on 50 high-resolution microscopic blood smear images yielded 1,422 detected regions, corresponding to an average of 28.44 cells per image. The mean Dice coefficients were 0.8777 for Level 2 versus Level 3 and 0.9997 for Level 3 versus final segmentation, while the corresponding mean IoU for the final refinement stage was 0.9993. Boundary-alignment evaluation produced a precision proxy of 0.7890, recall proxy of 0.0571, and F1 proxy of 0.1045. In addition, consensus analysis between refinement stages achieved precision, recall, and F1-score values of 1.0000, 0.9993, and 0.9997, respectively. The mean shape reliability was 0.6884, and the overall SCRI was 0.5975±0.0204. These findings demonstrate strong internal consistency and stable geometric performance across the dataset. The framework provides an interpretable and data-efficient alternative for microscopy image segmentation, although external validation against annotated ground-truth datasets remains necessary for definitive accuracy assessment.

title: PSD Estimation Based Enhanced Kalman Filter for Target Tracking With Singer Acceleration Noise
authors: Xiaodi Zhou, Jiaolong Wang, Chengxi Zhang, Choon Ki Ahn
abstract: This study proposes a new adaptive Kalman filter for high-precision cooperative target tracking based on the Singer acceleration model. In cooperative scenarios, the maneuver time constant <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha$</tex-math></inline-formula> is known in advance through mission planning or communication between targets, allowing a physically consistent and structure-preserving noise modeling. Traditional approaches often necessitate the estimation of the full noise covariance matrix, which can be computationally intensive and prone to inaccuracies and spurious state correlations. To overcome this limitation, this work elaborates a novel online power spectral density (PSD) estimation scheme. Using known <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha$</tex-math></inline-formula> to reduce the number of unknown variables, the new approach can improve the accuracy and reliability of the estimation. Numerical experiments in cooperative target tracking demonstrate that the refined algorithm achieves robust adaptability to dynamic noise that varies over time, provides high-precision state estimates, and maintains low computational complexity.

title: A Compact 75–105 GHz Symmetric-Cell-Based Distributed Attenuator With Broadband Phase Compensation
authors: Yang Liu, Nengxu Zhu, Jinxin Li, Xuan Li, Zhifu Hu, Meilin He, Keyuan Chen, Fanyi Meng
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter introduces a compact distributed attenuator with low phase error, employing a novel second-order phase correction (SOPC) technique. In each attenuation unit, two switch transistors are symmetrically distributed at both ends of the transmission line to form a second-order network, effectively compensating phase deviations without extra area overhead. The analysis and design of the attenuator are described. The proposed 5-bit attenuator is fabricated in a 130-nm SiGe BiCMOS process. Measurement results demonstrate that over 75–105 GHz, the attenuator achieves an insertion loss of 3.1–4.6 dB and a 12.8 dB tuning range with 0.4 dB step size. The rms phase error is <2° from 75 to 95 GHz, with a maximum value of 3.2° across the entire frequency band, while the compact device area is only 0.08 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Robust Brain Tumor Segmentation with Incomplete MRI Modalities Using Hölder Divergence and Mutual Information-Enhanced Knowledge Transfer
authors: Runze Cheng, Xihang Qiu, Ming Li, Ye Zhang, Fei Richard Yu, Chun Li
abstract: Multimodal MRI (magnetic resonance imaging) provides critical complementary information for accurate brain tumor segmentation. However, conventional methods struggle when certain modalities are missing due to issues like image quality, protocol inconsistencies, patient allergies, or financial constraints. To address this, we propose a robust single-modality parallel processing framework that achieves high segmentation accuracy even with incomplete modalities. Leveraging Hölder divergence and mutual information, our model maintains modality-specific features while dynamically adjusting network parameters based on available inputs. By using these divergence and information-based loss functions, the framework effectively quantifies discrepancies between predictions and ground-truth labels, resulting in consistently accurate segmentation. Extensive evaluations on the BraTS 2018 and BraTS 2020 datasets demonstrate superior performance over existing methods in handling missing modalities, with ablation studies validating each component's contribution to the framework.

title: Perceptually Weighted Late Fusion for Robust Binocular Gaze Estimation in Unconstrained Environments
authors: Ngawang Choeda, Biranchi Panda, Phub Namgay
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The variation in asymmetric illumination and artifacts in ocular regions poses a significant challenge for gaze estimation when moving from controlled laboratories to a realworld environment, despite its promising applications in multiple domains. The standard gaze estimation methods either assume a perfect ocular symmetry or use simple fusion techniques, often overlooking minor interocular asymmetries. This paper introduces a perceptually weighted late fusion framework inspired by human perception for binocular gaze estimation, which implements a SoftMax-weighted fusion of each eye's gaze estimates within a dual-stream LightGazeNet architecture, using the Weber contrast between the sclera (white) and iris (colored) regions of the eye as a perceptual quality metric. Experiments on 15,000 samples from 15 participants in the MPIIGaze dataset show that the proposed method reduces the mean Euclidean error to 60.63 mm, achieving a 6.67% and 12.53% improvement compared to “Left-Eye-Only” and “Right-Eye-Only” baseline models, in addition to reducing the error variance on both axes.

title: Benchmarking Traditional and Deep Sequential Models for Text Sentiment Analysis
authors: Silvi Avayan, Anna Mikayelyan, Sachin Kumar, Pradeep Kumar Mallick
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Sentiment analysis (SA) helps identify people's opinions and emotions from written text. This paper aims to test and compare machine learning, deep learning, and sequential modeling techniques for sentiment classification by utilizing the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 K}$</tex> Movie Reviews (IMDb) dataset. Four standard baselines (Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression, Linear SVM, Random Forest) are used for comparison and further extended to deeplearning and sequential models. Moreover, an attention-based encoder-decoder is also included in the analysis. The results reveal that the classical models, such as Linear SVM (90.22 %) and Logistic Regression <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(90.00 \%)$</tex>, achieved strong accuracy with less training time and computational cost. However, deep learning architectures performed better overall, with the Feedforward Neural Network and CNN reaching 97.92 % and 97.27 % accuracy, respectively. The attention-based encoder-decoder and Bi-LSTM-GRU also performed well by capturing contextual and sequential relationships. Overall, the study highlights the balance between computational efficiency and predictive performance, and provides useful guidance for selecting appropriate sentiment analysis models based on accuracy and resource availability.

title: Perception Intelligence-Based Neuro-Fuzzy Framework for Real-Time Autonomous Vehicles Using Monocular Vision
authors: G Paramesh, G Arulkumaran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Autonomous vehicle decision-making using monocular vision remains challenging due to perception uncertainty and the limited explainability of deep learning models. The Neuro-Fuzzy Perception Intelligence Framework for real-time autonomous driving presented in this paper combines an Interval Type-2 Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (IT2- ANFIS) with monocular visual perception. Geometric reasoning determines relative distance and orientation without the need for additional depth sensors, while a YOLO-based perception module pulls obstacle and lane information from single-camera input. The IT2-ANFIS controller processes this information and generates stable, comprehensible steering and speed decisions by explicitly modeling uncertainty and encoding human-like driving principles. Robust lane perception and risk-aware decision-making in difficult circumstances are further validated by visual results. The suggested framework provides a useful, comprehensible and effective monocular-based autonomous navigation solution.

title: Integrating Spatial Diffusion and Influence Maximization for Marine Biodiversity Monitoring
authors: Sangita Dutta, Abeer Lal Nandi, Deepmalya Acharya, Himanshu Kumar, Avik Kumar Das, Uddipan Ghosh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Marine biodiversity monitoring is increasingly constrained by limited resources, vast ecological complexity, and dynamic environmental change. Traditional approaches to survey site selection, such as random seeding, Degree centrality, or PageRank heuristics, provide computational efficiency but fail to capture ecological dynamics, spatial spillover, and temporal variation. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel framework for spatially aware influence maximization in marine biodiversity monitoring. In our model, survey stations are represented as nodes in a symmetric k-nearest-neighbor (kNN) network, with edges weighted by geodesic distance decay to simulate ecological diffusion. Species spread is modeled using the Independent Cascade (IC) process augmented with geographic decay functions, enabling realistic representation of biodiversity spillover across survey sites. We introduce a minimal-K decision rule that identifies the smallest set of seed stations needed to achieve near-maximal biodiversity coverage, thereby supporting cost-effective planning under logistical constraints. Extensive experiments on real-world reef survey datasets demonstrate that our framework consistently outperforms baseline strategies including Degree, PageRank, and Random seeding across multiple coverage metrics and temporal evaluations. The results highlight the scalability, robustness, and ecological validity of our approach, offering a practical tool for adaptive conservation planning and real-time marine biodiversity management.

title: Deep Learning-Based Detection and Classification of Mango Leaf Disease Using Image Processing Techniques
authors: K. Sai Lokesh, S. Shanthi
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Mango production is important for the agricultural economy while foliar diseases such as sooty mold, powdery mildew, bacterial canker, and anthracnose adversely affect the yield and quality of mango. The early and accurate identification of mango foliar diseases is necessary for management and to minimize crop losses. In this study, we utilize convolutional neural network architectures in concert with sophisticated image processing to propose a deep learning-based methodology for the automatic identification and classification of mango leaf diseases. Preprocessing image processing techniques, photo quality improvements, image noise removal, establishing image consistency, and data augmentation, were performed systematically to enhance the diversity of elicited feature sets from images of healthy and diseased mango leaves. Four different evaluation measures accuracy, F1 score, precision, and recall were used to assess the performance of three deep learning methods applied to the images, like CNN, DenseNet, and ResNet. From the results of our tests, we can confidently say that CNN will outperform with an accuracy of 99%, DenseNet also achieves the best accuracy of 98%, and ResNet gets low accuracy of 97%. This study establishes a strong basis for the early detection of diseases in mango crops and opens possibilities for the better usage of deep learning.

title: Campus Lifestyle Analytics and Academic Success Using IoT-Based Behavioural Tracking
authors: G. Swarnalatha, S. Mohan, G Saabakarini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: With the increase in the number of IoT devices in schools, there has been an opportunity to utilize behavioral analytics to further analyze and enhance students’ academic performance. Combing lifestyle indicators in one analytics system for predicting academic success, this study presents an innovative IoT-based behavioral monitoring framework. The framework would consider variables such as attendance habits, library use, usage of study spaces, sleep patterns, and social interactions. The proposed method leverages mobile sensing, RFID-tagged campus facilities, and intelligent wearables to capture non-intrusive insights into students’ daily lives in real-time, as opposed to existing survey-based or self-reported studies. Students, instructors, and educational administrators are all served by the data that is captured when machine learning techniques are utilized to identify correlations between students’ lifestyle habits and academic performance. An improved grade point average is positively related to regular lifestyle measures such as adequate sleep, optimizing study time, and regular exercise. On intelligent campus networks, this research shows the potential of internet-of-things (IoT) powered behavioral analytics as an integrated, scalable approach toward enhancing students’ health, personal learning, and academic performance in the long term.

title: SeqFeedNet: Sequential Feature Feedback Network for Background Subtraction
authors: Yu-Shun Huang, Jing-Ming Guo, Yi-Xiang Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Background subtraction (BGS) is a fundamental task in computer vision with applications in video surveillance, object tracking, and recognition. Despite recent advancements, many deep learning-based BGS algorithms rely on large models to extract high-level representations, demanding significant computational resources and leading to inefficiencies in processing video streams. To address these limitations, we introduce the Sequential Feature Feedback Network (SeqFeedNet), a novel supervised algorithm for BGS in unseen videos that operates without additional preprocessing models. SeqFeedNet innovatively incorporates time-scale diverse sequential features and employs a feedback mechanism for each iteration. Moreover, we propose the Sequential Fit Training (SeqFiT) technique, enhancing model convergence during training. Evaluated on the CDNet 2014 dataset, SeqFeedNet not only achieves ∼ 5 times increase in inference speed but also outperforms F-Measure scores of the leading supervised algorithms, making it highly suitable for real-world applications. Our experiment demonstrates that SeqFeedNet surpasses state-of-the-art network without pre-trained segmentation model by 3.83% F-Measure on the CDnet 2014 dataset. Leading the way to establish a new benchmark for efficient and effective BGS in unseen videos. The code is released at https://github.com/tw-yshuang/SeqFeedNet.

title: Conjuring Positive Pairs for Efficient Unification of Representation Learning and Image Synthesis
authors: Imanol G. Estepa, Jesús M. Rodríguez-de-Vera, Ignacio Sarasúa, Bhalaji Nagarajan, Petia Radeva
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: While representation learning and generative modeling seek to understand visual data, unifying both domains remains unexplored. Recent Unified Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) methods have started to bridge the gap between both paradigms. However, they either extract information from discriminative pretrained models or rely solely on semantic token reconstruction, which requires an external tokenizer during training — introducing a significant computational overhead. In this work, we introduce Sorcen, a novel Unified SSL framework, incorporating a synergic Contrastive-Reconstruction objective. Our novel Contrastive objective, leverages the generative capabilities of Sorcen and eliminates the need for additional image crops or augmentations during training. Sorcen "generates" contrastive positive samples, called Echoes, directly in the semantic token space using the reconstruction objective. This on-the-fly Echo generation, enables Sorcen to operate exclusively on precomputed tokens, eliminating the need for an online tokenizer during training. Sorcen significantly reduces the computational overhead by 60.8% compared to token reconstruction SoTA. Extensive experiments on ImageNet-1k demonstrate that Sorcen outperforms the previous Unified SSL SoTA by 0.4%, 1.48 FID, 1.76%, and 1.53% on linear probing, unconditional image generation, few-shot learning, and transfer learning, respectively. Additionally, Sorcen establishes as a new single-crop MIM SoTA in linear probing and achieves SoTA performance in unconditional image generation, highlighting significant improvements and breakthroughs in Unified SSL models <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: GPU Accelerated Near-Field Electromagnetic Simulations for Microwave Hyperthermia Treatment Planning Using GprMax
authors: Rahul Choudhary, Kavitha Arunachalam
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Hyperthermia (HT) treatment elevates tumor temperature to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0}-\mathbf{4 4}{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, enhancing the effectiveness of conventional cancer therapies. Microwave antennas, or applicators, are widely used to achieve localized heating, and they require patient-specific hyperthermia treatment planning (HTP) based on electromagnetic (EM) simulations. Most reported work relies on commercial solvers, which add cost and limit accessibility. In this study, we propose a GPU accelerated opensource HTP framework using gprMax, a finite-difference timedomain (FDTD) solver originally developed for groundpenetrating radar. A cavity-backed patch antenna and a twoelement array operating at 434 MHz were simulated in a muscle equivalent digital phantom. Antenna reflection coefficient and specific absorption rate (SAR) distribution were extracted from the computed electric field distributions and compared with results from commercial solver Ansys HFSS. GPU acceleration resulted in an eight-fold decrease in simulation time relative to CPU-based computation in gprMax. The study demonstrates that gprMax can provide accurate and efficient EM simulations for microwave hyperthermia applicators, offering a cost-effective, and accessible alternative for clinical treatment planning.

title: An Interpretable Ensemble Neural and Symbolic Learning Framework for Pattern Discovery Across Literary Eras
authors: S. Nandhini, Bujji Babu Velagaleti, M. Udhayakumar, S. Padmapriya, Tahmeena Fatima, R. Kanagaselvam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The paper presents LitEraNet-X; an interpretable ensemble model integrating neural and symbolic learning to find patterns in literary works in five distinct periods of English literature: Renaissance, Romantic, Victorian, Modernist, and Postmodern. The framework combines a two-path architecture, which consists of a Transformer-LSTM neural network and a logic-based symbolic inference engine, which is informed by a Literary Device Knowledge Graph (LDKG). It analyzes texts using literary-conscious preprocess, motif matching, semantic drift modeling and emotion trajectory analysis. The model classifies eras with a maximum accuracy of 91.36% in Romantic texts and has F1-scores of more than 0.84 at all periods. The most essential literary patterns and emotional lines are connected with neuro-symbolic fusion and understood through SHAP values, which helps provide obvious, scholarly-relevant information. Semantic drift tracker also shows conceptual shifts of key words (e.g., freedom, virtue) over time. The consistency of the dual interpretability is confirmed by experimental findings, which show a high level of motif alignment ($\Delta{\lt}0.05$) between neural attention and symbolic reasoning. The framework promotes a field of digital humanities, which combines statistical strength and the knowledge of the literary domain. It is designed in a way that is not only accurate and deep but also transparent- between the methods of computation and humanistic inquiry.

title: Identification of Key Genes in Prostate Cancer via RNA Sequencing and Network-Based Bioinformatics
authors: VasudevaRao Badana, Ramesh Chappa, Chalapathi Rao Tippana, JagadeeswaraRao G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Prostate cancer (PCa) stands as the second most common cancer diagnosis among men worldwide every year. Nearly 1.5 million men have been diagnosed with prostate cancer. Gene expression microarrays have identified multiple tumor markers and therapeutic targets for prostate adenocarcinoma (PRAD). Ribonucleic acid sequencing (RNA-seq), one of the most widely used transcriptomics technologies, is helpful for tumor diagnosis, prognosis, and treatment because it may demonstrate the relationship between complex biological processes and genetic mutations. Differential analysis of RNA-seq data is necessary to identify aberrant transcriptions; efficient techniques for differential expression analysis include Limma, EdgeR, and DESeq2. The goal of this work is to identify the hub genes linked to PRAD by doing an RNA-seq analysis using the DESeq2 and Limma packages. Here, we provide a comprehensive method for identifying genes that are differentially expressed (DEGs) between PRAD tissues and healthy tissues. We have taken the gene expression data for PRAD from the Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA), which includes 554 samples (502 tumor, 52 normal). A total of 2635 and 4348 DEGs were identified by the Limma and DESeq2 tools, respectively. Further, these DEGs were filtered based on their nature of expression and analyzed for hub gene identification through the protein-protein interaction (PPI) network, and we filtered the hub genes using the cytohubba plugin. In this study, we identified 11 hub genes that are significant in PRAD, and the gene ontology (GO) and pathway analysis of the hub genes were also presented. This thorough investigation shows how integrative genomics can be used to decipher intricate disease pathways and identifies new directions for bettering prostate cancer patient treatment.

title: SCALEX: Scalable Concept and Latent Exploration for Diffusion Models
authors: E. Zhixuan Zeng, Yuhao Chen, Alexander Wong
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Image generation models frequently encode social biases, including stereotypes tied to gender, race, and profession. Existing methods for analyzing these biases in diffusion models either focus narrowly on predefined categories or depend on manual interpretation of latent directions. These constraints limit scalability and hinder the discovery of subtle or unanticipated patterns.We introduce SCALEX, a framework for scalable and automated exploration of diffusion model latent spaces. SCALEX extracts semantically meaningful directions from H-space using only natural language prompts, enabling zero-shot interpretation without retraining or labelling.
