This allows systematic comparison across arbitrary concepts and large-scale discovery of internal model associations. We show that SCALEX detects gender bias in profession prompts, ranks semantic alignment across identity descriptors, and reveals clustered conceptual structure without supervision. By linking prompts to latent directions directly, SCALEX makes bias analysis in diffusion models more scalable, interpretable, and extensible than prior approaches.

title: Grid-Based Spatial Encoding for Multimodal LLMs in Facility Management
authors: Viviana Crescitelli, Koji Maeda, Mitsuki Ikuta
abstract: This paper presents ongoing research on a spatial analysis framework designed to improve the performance of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in real-time facility management, with parking lot monitoring as a representative use case. Although MLLMs perform well in object recognition, they often fail to capture precise spatial relationships, limiting localization accuracy. Existing methods address this issue but typically depend on specialized sensors, prebuilt maps, or complex embodied systems, which hinder deployment in resourceconstrained environments. To overcome these limitations, we introduce a grid-based spatial encoding method that embeds structured layouts directly into prompts, anchoring visual elements to explicit reference zones. This improves localization, obstruction detection, and reasoning in occluded or ambiguous scenes. In real-world parking scenarios, our approach increased space identification accuracy from 29% to 86% and obstruction detection from 15% to 85%. The system transforms ordinary surveillance into actionable spatial insights, supporting frontline workers and advancing safe, efficient, and sustainable operations in line with Japan's Society 5.0 vision. These preliminary findings demonstrate that prompt-based spatial grounding can significantly improve the spatial reasoning capabilities of MLLMs without retraining or additional sensors, and future work will extend validation to broader facility contexts.

title: Design and Comparative Analysis of PID Controllers on FPGA for Power-Sensitive Applications Using Various Arithmetic Architectures
authors: Nowreen Mukhtar, Liyaqat Nazir, Asifa Mehraj Baba
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This study investigates the design and implementation of Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controllers on FPGA hardware, focusing on the trade-offs between various arithmetic architectures (Ripple Carry Adders (RCA) and Carry Look-Ahead Adders (CLA)) across variable bitwidth architectures. The results demonstrate different performance characteristics by comparing area, power consumption, delay, and Area Delay Product (ADP). RCA demonstrates superior area efficiency, utilizing 2.35% fewer Look-Up Tables (LUTs) than CLA (e.g., 26942 vs. 27575 LUTs for 64-bit) and lower power consumption (1.255W vs. 1.398W for 64-bit). Conversely, CLA achieves higher operational speeds (155.424 MHz vs. 98.154 MHz for 8-bit) and reduced delays (6.434 ns vs. 10.188 ns for 8-bit). However, CLA incurs a 11.35% power penalty at 64-bit and exhibits 3.25% higher ADP at 64-bit, underscoring a critical speed-resource trade-off. These results emphasize the necessity of selecting adder architectures aligned with application priorities: RCA for power, and area-sensitive systems and CLA for latency-critical implementations. The study provides actionable insights for optimizing FPGA-based PID controllers, paving the way for future exploration of hybrid architectures to balance efficiency and performance.

title: Very-Large-Scale Integration Implementation using Multiplier Topologies with BTC Algorithm and Threshold Optimization for Efficient Computation
authors: R Manasa, Ahmad Al-Qerem, B Mouli Chandra, R Govindaraj, G. Karthik, Bharathi Panduri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) refers to the technology of designing and manufacturing integrated circuits that can accommodate thousands to millions of transistors on a single chip. This paper presents a VIMBTE model that achieves very high performance and hardware efficiency by integrating a drastically improved Block Truncation Coding (BTC) algorithm, a radix-4 Booth multiplier, and a highly customizable VLSI platform suitable for real-time multimedia processing. The main breakthroughs are an adaptive threshold optimization method that enhances Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) without adding to the compression cost and the implementation of $4: 2: 2$ subsampling to reduce the data redundancy level and retain the visual quality. The radix-4 Booth multiplier speeds up arithmetic operations and at the same time reduces the hardware complexity. The hardware implemented in VHDL employs open-source EDA tools for RTL analysis, optimization, binding, and verification. The performance analysis consists of normalized delay, power dissipation, delay, power consumption, and delay metrics.

title: Empowering Advanced Analytics: The Role of NLP and Semantic Modeling in Self-Service Risk Intelligence
authors: Vinoth Kumar Arumugam, Jeevitha Sakkarai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The growing tendency toward the use of unstructured textual data in organizational settings has added to the pressure to develop intelligent and user-friendly risk analytics that would be able to extract contextual and semantic risk indicators. To overcome this difficulty, a superior selfservice risk intelligence architecture is introduced, based on transformer-based natural language processing, ontologydriven semantic representation, and reinforcement-based prioritization. Its architecture applies the use of contextual semantic encoders, ontology-aligned concept mapping, semantic query interpretation, and adaptive risk scoring to facilitate explainable and autonomous risk intelligence. The evaluation using experiments shows that the ratio of the semantic concept alignment is $94.87 \%$, the contextual embedding stability is 0.931, and the latent risk discovery density is 0.882. The framework has a semantic query interpretation accuracy of $93.54 \%$, a self-service interaction index of 0.918, and a latency of risk insight production of 184 ms. The comparative analysis shows that the risk intelligence maturity index is higher, 0.94, and is characterized by better semantic awareness, flexibility, and explainability. These findings support the usefulness of semantic and NLP-based self-service analytics in terms of proactive, scalable, and context-driven risk intelligence.

title: SmartScore: A Deep Learning Approach for Predicting Final Scores in IPL Matches
authors: M V L N Raja Rao, Lakshmi Sathvika Devanaboina, Koppaka Gnanendra Chowdary, Karra Geethika, Mugata Dhanunjay Prabhakar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The dynamic and sequential nature of Twenty20 cricket means that predictions of final scores in Indian Premier League matches are still difficult because of the nature of the game. Conventional statistical models are normally unable to reflect the temporal correlations and quick momentum changes that take place within an innings. To overcome this drawback, it introduces SmartScore, a deep learning-based system of score prediction that predicts match progression with the help of Long Short-Term Memory networks. The system processes ball-by-ball IPL data and features like over, runs, wickets, teams, venue, pitch, and toss. Experimental analysis of the historical IPL dataset shows that the model proposed has an average absolute error of about 12 runs, and the majority of its predictions lie within a range of ±15 runs. The comparative analysis indicates that the LSTM model is better than linear regression and random forest in that it has a higher consistency score of 0.59. Prediction correctness can further be confirmed using venue-wise evaluation, which has a close value of 62%. These findings suggest that SmartScore is a valid and real-time score predictor and is a valuable analysis tool in the assessment of IPL matches.

title: AnyBald: Toward Realistic Diffusion-Based Hair Removal In-The-Wild
authors: Yongjun Choi, Seungoh Han, Soomin Kim, Sumin Son, Mohsen Rohani, Edgar Maucourant, Dongbo Min, Kyungdon Joo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present AnyBald, a novel framework for realistic hair removal from portrait images captured under diverse in-the-wild conditions. One of the key challenges in this task is the lack of high-quality paired data, as existing datasets are often low-quality, with limited viewpoint variation and diversity, making it difficult to handle real-world cases. To address this, we construct a scalable data augmentation pipeline that synthesizes high-quality hair and non-hair image pairs across diverse real-world scenarios, enabling effective generalization and scalable supervision. With this enriched dataset, we present a new hair removal framework that reformulates pretrained latent diffusion in-painting using learnable text prompts, removing the need for explicit masking at inference. In doing so, our model achieves natural hair removal with semantic preservation via implicit localization. To further enhance spatial precision, we introduce a regularization loss that guides the model to attend specifically to hair regions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AnyBald outperforms in removing hair while preserving identity and semantics across various in-the-wild domains. Our project page is here: https://vision3d-lab.github.io/anybald/.

title: A Spatial-Spectral Component Representation Network for Fine-Grained Mineral Classification in Hyperspectral Imagery
authors: Zhaoyu Wang, Mingming Xu, Shanwei Liu, Hongxia Zheng, Muhammad Yasir, Qinlong Tong
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Mineral mapping is a significant focus in remote sensing research, which aims to fully utilize spectral data for identifying and describing the composition of surface minerals. To gain a deeper understanding of mineral distribution within the study area, fine-grained hyperspectral image classification (HSIC) has emerged as an important research challenge. In mineral scenes where mineral co-occurrence and severe spectral mixing are prevalent, variations in mineral abundances largely govern discriminability, making abundance information essential for improving the reliability of mineral identification. Motivated by these considerations, we develop a spatial-spectral component representation network for HSIC, called SSCRNet, which introduces an Autoencoder (AE)-structured unmixing network to achieve complementary integration between abundance features and deep features extracted by the network. The SSCRNet enhances deep classification networks by introducing convolutional layers with different dilation rates, which enables the simultaneous capture of fine-grained mineral texture details and large-scale geological structural patterns in mineral areas. This design effectively improves the classification performance for complex surface types. Experimental results demonstrate that SSCRNet outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches in classification performance and robustness for mineral mapping scenarios. The code and datasets will be available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ZhaoYGeo/SSCRNet</uri>.

title: Differential RF-MEMS Enabled Power Divider Based Phase Shifters
authors: Zehan Guo, Padraig Fitzgerald, Dimitra Psychogiou
abstract: New types of co-designed differential RF-MEMS enabled power divider-based phase shifters (PDPSs) are reported. They are based on T-junction power dividers co-integrated with single-pole N-throw (SPNT) RF-MEMS switches that simultaneously control the power division and differential phase shift (Δφ) between adjacent antenna elements, allowing to steer the beam of an antenna array to N distinct directions. Scalability to alternative levels of Δφ and number of antenna elements are demonstrated through the implementation of four- and eight-way PDPSs. Practical realization aspects are discussed and validated through the design, manufacturing, and testing of two PDPS prototypes at X-band using commercially-available RF-MEMS switches. They exhibit the following RF performance characteristics: i) four-way PDPS: tunable Δφs: –39.2°, –106.2°, +105.1°, +38.8° and maximum phase shift range: 314.2° at 8 GHz with an insertion loss (IL): 3.09 ± 2.04 dB; ii) eight-way PDPS: tunable Δφs: –38.1°, –106°, +106.3°, +38.2° and maximum phase shift range: 741° at 8 GHz with an IL: 4.57 ± 2.08 dB.

title: Digital Twin-assisted Handover Scheme for Mobile Networks using Generative AI
authors: Lal Verda Cakir, Mehmet Ali Erturk, Mehmet Ozdem, Berk Canberk
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Handover management in mobile networks is challenged by high latency and reduced reliability in dense deployments and under user mobility. Here, existing schemes improve handover initiation by optimising the candidate handover at the decision time. However, these are applied after a non-negligible delay due to the control-plane signalling. Then, when applied, it may become invalid or degrade performance. To address this, we propose a Digital Twin (DT)-assisted handover scheme that performs predictive execution-time validation prior to the preparation of the Next Generation (NG)-based handover. To this end, the DT-What-If Generator (DT-WIG) is used to emulate short-horizon future network states under uncertainty. Here, the DT-WIG is a spatiotemporal graph generative model that uses variational latent sampling to generate counterfactual post-handover trajectories for the candidate handover decision. Then, the AMF estimates the failure and QoS risks associated with the candidate handover and approves/rejects it via standard-compliant signalling. With this, we form a policy-agnostic mechanism that runs on the underlying handover policy. Consequently, we evaluate performance using ns-3/5G-LENA trace generation and replay-based policy analysis, with OpenAirInterface-based signalling evaluation. The results show that the proposed method reduces the handover failure rate and handover interruption time while improving latency, jitter, throughput, and packet loss.

title: TM-Adapter: Temporal Merge Adapter for Efficient Global Temporal Modeling
authors: Woo Joo Hahm, Seungwoo Jang, Hyeon Tak Kim, Daeun Lee, Kwangsu Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We propose the Temporal Merge Adapter (TM-Adapter), a novel framework for image-to-video parameter-efficient transfer learning (PETL), specifically designed for temporal representation learning in video understanding. PETL has emerged as a practical strategy for adapting large-scale vision models to video tasks under limited computational budgets. However, existing PETL approaches suffer from local redundancy caused by highly similar consecutive frames, which limits the modeling of diverse temporal dependencies. To address this limitation, we introduce a lightweight merge-unmerge mechanism that temporally aggregates and redistributes token embeddings, enabling the model to capture diverse temporal patterns by mitigating redundancy. Furthermore, to effectively handle diverse temporal dependencies across different time scales, TM-Adapter introduces a single adapter module with two parallel branches, local and global adapters, each specialized in capturing complementary patterns at different temporal ranges. We validate our approach through experiments on Kinetics-400, Something-Something V2, and HMDB-51, demonstrating competitive performance compared to existing methods while maintaining high parameter efficiency.

title: Enhanced Ensemble Temporal Convolutional Network With Genetic Algorithm Optimization for Anomaly Detection in CAVs
authors: Elvin Ugonna Eziama, Omar Abou Kasm, Mahreen Nasir
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Malicious attacks on Connected and Automated Vehicle (CAV) systems can severely impact transportation safety and efficiency. They can manipulate sensor readings, disrupt service, or falsify environmental perceptions, leading to incorrect decisions and potential catastrophes. Detecting such anomalies is complex due to challenges such as false positives and negatives, sensor interference, resource constraints, and diverse types of anomalies. Existing traditional methods, including statistical approaches, signature-based detection, behavioral analysis, and network intrusion detection systems, often suffer from high false positive rates, sensitivity to noise, reliance on thresholds, and inability to detect evolving or subtle patterns. Recent machine learning and hybrid methods improve on traditional techniques but struggle with frequency bias and time scale selection, limiting real-time applications in CAVs networks. To address these challenges, we propose ET-DRF-GA, a novel method that combines an ensemble-based exploration of Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN), Differential Receptive Fields (DRF), and Genetic Algorithms (GA). This approach offers dynamic parameter adjustment via genetic algorithms, enhanced robustness and stability through ensemble-based model selection, and optimized sensitivity to different anomaly types using DRF. Experiments on benchmark datasets, Safety Pilot Model Deployment (SPMD) and Vehicular Reference Misbehavior (VeReMi), demonstrate the method’s robustness and effectiveness in anomaly detection for CAV systems, paving the way for future research in this critical domain.

title: Shared and Individual Resting-State MEG Network Signatures of Tinnitus Revealed by Holistic Graph Learning
authors: Payam S. Shabestari, Harry H. Behjat, Dimitri Van De Ville, Christopher R. Cederroth, Niklas K. Edvall, Adrian Naas, Tobias Kleinjung, Patrick Neff
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Engineering in Medicine and Biology
abstract: Tinnitus, the perception of sound without an external source, affects many individuals, yet its impact on the brain' functional connectome remains underexplored. Traditional functional connectivity (FC) methods, such as Pearson correlation, phase lag index, and coherence, rely on pairwise comparisons between activity of macro-scale brain regions, limiting holistic characterization. We used an approach that estimates the entire connectivity structure by analyzing all time-courses simultaneously, robust even for short recordings and suitable for real-time applications. Using resting-state MEG from tinnitus patients and controls, learned connectomes outperformed correlation-based ones in fingerprinting individuals across test/retest. Group analyses revealed altered FC across multiple frequency bands, impacting default mode, auditory, visual, and salience networks, indicating large-scale reorganization. Tinnitus exhibited highly individualized whole-brain FC profiles, highlighting the importance of individual variability and paving the way for personalized models to optimize patient-specific interventions.

title: Towards a 3D Thermal-Electrodynamic Simulation of Non-Insulated ReBCO Coils: The H-FoSTER Model
authors: Rinaldoni Davide, Bordini Bernardo
abstract: Accurately modeling the transient behavior of non insulated (NI) ReBCO superconducting coils is crucial for fully assessing their potential for high-field magnet applications. 3D Finite Element (FE) models are among the most promising approaches for capturing the thermal-electrodynamics of these coils. However, most popular mathematical formulations of Maxwell's equations for superconductors, such as the well-known H-φ formulation, are currently too computationally expensive to simulate large-scale systems like accelerator magnets. To address this challenge, we present a novel mathematical formulation that couples a 3D FE magnetic model with a 1D FE + 2D Finite Difference (FD) electric model, implemented in COMSOL Multiphysics. The formulation has been used to develop a model that simulates the electrodynamics of large ReBCO NI pancake coils. Although still under development, the model has been validated against other models across various test cases, and preliminary results demonstrate its ability to efficiently capture critical phenomena such as persistent current effects while significantly reducing the computational time. In this paper, we describe the electrodynamic model and present selected results benchmarked against more established approaches (e.g., the H φ formulation). Work is currently ongoing to integrate thermal behavior into the model.

title: Adversarial Decision-Making in Partially Observable Multi-Agent Systems: A Sequential Hypothesis Testing Approach
authors: Haosheng Zhou, Daniel Ralston, Xu Yang, Ruimeng Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: Adversarial decision-making in partially observable multi-agent systems requires sophisticated strategies for both deception and counter-deception. This paper presents a sequential hypothesis testing (SHT)-driven framework that captures the interplay between strategic misdirection and inference in adversarial environments. We formulate this interaction as a partially observable Stackelberg game, where a follower agent (blue team) seeks to fulfill its primary task while actively misleading an adversarial leader (red team). In opposition, the red team, leveraging leaked information, instills carefully designed patterns to manipulate the blue team's behavior, mitigating the misdirection effect. Unlike conventional approaches that focus on robust control under adversarial uncertainty, our framework explicitly models deception as a dynamic optimization problem, where both agents strategically adapt their policies in response to inference and counter-inference. We derive a semi-explicit optimal control solution for the blue team within a linear-quadratic setting and develop iterative and machine learning-based methods to characterize the red team's optimal response. Numerical experiments demonstrate how deception-driven strategies influence adversarial interactions and reveal the impact of leaked information in shaping equilibrium behaviors. These results provide new insights into strategic deception in multi-agent systems, with potential applications in cybersecurity, autonomous decision-making, and financial markets.

title: Switching Rollout-Enhanced Value Iteration With Stabilization Guidance for Active Vehicle Suspension Systems: Theory and Experiment
authors: Liangju Zhang, Xiang-Peng Xie, Ju H. Park
abstract: Active vehicle suspension systems (AVSSs) are critical to ride comfort and road-holding performance. However, conventional learning-based methods for solving optimal control problems often suffer from slow convergence and overly restrictive stability requirements, thereby limiting their practical applicability. To address these issues, a three-stage learning scheme, termed switching rollout-enhanced value iteration (SRVI), is proposed to obtain the optimal control policy for AVSSs. Unlike conventional value iteration methods that bound successive value function (VF) differences by the instantaneous utility, the proposed approach adopts a less conservative finite-window stability criterion, enabling earlier identification of stabilizing policies. Building on this, a rollout-based incomplete policy evaluation mechanism is developed to reduce redundant VF exploration by rapidly steering the VF estimate toward a near-optimal region. Subsequently, a monotonic value update scheme is employed to ensure stable convergence to the optimal solution. The effectiveness of the proposed SRVI method is validated through hardware-in-the-loop experiments on a nonlinear active suspension system. Moreover, the proposed method reduces training time by 38.8% compared with existing approaches, while the learned control policy improves ride comfort and road-holding performance, reducing vertical suspension cost by up to 9.9% and control energy consumption by up to 9.0%.

title: Valley-Preserving Spread-Spectrum Jitter for Quasi-Resonant Flyback Converters to Reduce Conducted EMI
authors: Ming-Chang Tsou, Qing-Mu Chen
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a spread-spectrum control method for a quasi-resonant (QR) flyback converter that reduces conducted electromagnetic interference (EMI) while preserving soft-switching in discontinuous-conduction mode (DCM). The proposed controller injects an adjustable and bounded jitter signal into the peak-current threshold, thereby modulating the switch on-time, varying the effective switching frequency, and dispersing the switching spectral energy. A direction-aware modulation algorithm is developed to limit cycle-to-cycle energy variation, mitigate interaction with the voltage-regulation feedback loop, and maintain output regulation. Both SIMPLIS simulations and experiments on a 65-W (20 V/3.25 A) QR flyback prototype validate the approach. Compared with a conventional frequency-spreading method, the proposed controller reduces the quasi-peak (QP) conducted EMI by up to 5 dBμV in the 400–700 kHz band, corresponding to an average broadband reduction of approximately 3 dBμV, while maintaining tight line and load regulation.

title: Bio-inspired Soft Crawling Robot HISOYAKA II: Adaptive Behavior Emerging from the Dynamics of a Redesigned Soft Arm
authors: Koki Hirota, Kazuyuki Ito
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: We investigate a mobile robot that locomotes using two long octopus-like soft arms in environments with many protruding objects. In this study, we improve the previous robot by redesigning the arms to enable non-uniform motion. A prototype is developed, and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed arms achieve more complex and adaptive motion than conventional designs, despite employing the same simple controller. The reachable area of the arm is approximately three times larger than that of the conventional design.

title: Analysis of Dual AC-DC Charging for Electric Vehicles using Fuzzy Logic Controller
authors: M. Yerri Veeresh, M S Shiva Kumar, Y V Siva Reddy, V.Nagabhaskar Reddy, M.Vasu, U V Sujala, K.Rajasekhara Reddy, K. Harish
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Recent breakthroughs in electric vehicle (EV) driving range, battery power density, and charger ratings, a number of difficulties remain that prevent mainstream EV adoption and parity with internal combustion engine vehicles. The key challenges are inadequate charging infrastructure, long charging times, potential grid overload and congestion, non-uniform EV charging standards, and costly capital expenditures. EVs are typically charged using a single charging input, whether AC or DC. However, in residential areas and regions without rapid charging stations, DC charging outlets are frequently underutilized, forcing EV users to rely on slower AC charging choices. Rooftop solar photovoltaic (PV) systems, battery energy storage systems (BESS), and vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) charging are emerging options that offer alternate DC charging channels. This paper presents a dual charging technique that uses a solar-PV array and a BESS to supplement the power provided by standard on-board AC chargers (OBC), allowing an EV battery to be charged simultaneously through both its AC and DC ports. The proposed methodology using FLC is simulated and studied in MATLAB/Simulink to demonstrate its feasibility and efficiency in lowering charging time while optimizing the use of existing distributed energy resources.

title: An Explainable AI-Based Hybrid SVM–CNN Ensemble Model Implementation for Intelligent Email Spam Detection
authors: Tanmoy Chanda, Biva Mondal, Subhashis Das, Soumendu Banerjee, Bappaditya Mondal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: In modern-day life, emails are mostly used as a source of communication across almost everywhere from business to education. Generally, emails can be classified into spam & ham (not spam) mail. Spam mail creates several problems in our daily life such as it wastes our time, slows down our devices, steals important information like passwords or personal details such as bank information, ATM card information, etc. Hence, the identification & removal of spam messages is a very serious & important challenge for most modern-day email systems. In that context, a hybrid ensemble model has been proposed that combines machine learning (ML) and a deep learning (DL) algorithm to detect spam mail from the recipient's email list. In this proposed technique, emails are not only classified but also provided in-depth metrics for fine-tuning the predictions, thus making it practical for real-life deployment. The proposed model is developed by combining the strengths of Support Vector Machine (SVM) with Deep Neural Network (DNN) & Convolution Neural Network (CNN) to achieve a recall value of 97.41%.

title: CAMP-VQA: Caption-Embedded Multimodal Perception for No-Reference Quality Assessment of Compressed Video
authors: Xinyi Wang, Angeliki Katsenou, Junxiao Shen, David Bull
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The prevalence of user-generated content (UGC) on platforms such as YouTube and TikTok has rendered no-reference (NR) perceptual video quality assessment (VQA) vital for optimizing video delivery. Nonetheless, the characteristics of non-professional acquisition and the subsequent transcoding of UGC video on sharing platforms present significant challenges for NR-VQA. Although NR-VQA models attempt to infer mean opinion scores (MOS), their modeling of subjective scores for compressed content remains limited due to the absence of fine-grained perceptual annotations of artifact types. To address these challenges, we propose CAMP-VQA, a novel NR-VQA framework that exploits the semantic understanding capabilities of large vision-language models. Our approach introduces a quality-aware prompting mechanism that integrates video metadata (e.g., resolution, frame rate, bitrate) with key fragments extracted from inter-frame variations to guide the BLIP-2 pretraining approach in generating fine-grained quality captions. A unified architecture has been designed to model perceptual quality across three dimensions: semantic alignment, temporal characteristics, and spatial characteristics. These multimodal features are extracted and fused, then regressed to video quality scores. Extensive experiments on a wide variety of UGC datasets demonstrate that our model consistently outperforms existing NR-VQA methods, achieving improved accuracy without the need for costly manual fine-grained annotations. Our method achieves the best performance in terms of average rank and linear correlation (SRCC: 0.928, PLCC: 0.938) compared to state-of-the-art methods. The source code and trained models, along with a user-friendly demo, are available at: https://github.com/xinyiW915/CAMP-VQA.

title: Machine Learning Based Hybrid RF–VLC Modulation Classification with RIS-Assisted Channels
authors: M. W. P. Maduranga, Durgesh Nandan, W. C. Nirmal, H. K. I. S. Lakmal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: This work targets Machine Learning based modulation classification system in a hybrid Radio Frequency (RF) and Visible Light Communication (VLC) environment. The setup was also supported with Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS). An indoor space of dimensions a 5 m × 5 m × 3 m was simulated and it contained a ceiling mounted LED, an RF transmitter and several RIS panels which help generate additional VLC paths by reflection. Five modulation formats were produced under AWGN over SNR levels ranging from 0 to 30 dB. Each sequence received was represented through time-domain statistics, selected FFT components, and I/Q-based features. A total of four ML classifiers, SVM, Random Forest, KNN and XGBoost were trained to identify the type of modulation. The classification accuracy increasing with SNR is shown by the results, with ensemble models achieving the highest accuracies. XGBoost shows an accuracy of 53% at 30 dB. RIS reflections provided a clear benefit at low SNR as it boosted received optical power, while causing minor degradation at high SNR due to small waveform distortions. With these findings, it is clear that unified RF–VLC modulation recognition is feasible and also shows the disadvantages introduced by RIS.

title: MSA Unet A Dual Scale Mining Self Attention Network for Accurate Segmentation in Echocardiographic Images for MI Diagnosis
authors: Anushalin P.S, P Arunachalam
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of cardiac structures from echocardiographic images is essential for the quantitative assessment of heart function. However, the low contrast, noise, and complex structure make the segmentation process more challenging. Recently, deep learning models with attention mechanisms have been widely used for segmentation. However, there is still room for improvement in boundary areas. To overcome the boundary segmentation problem, we propose a novel Dual-Scale Mining Self-Attention U-Net (DS-MSA U-Net) architecture that effectively addresses boundary issues by integrating multi-scale feature extraction, mining modulation, and self-attention mechanisms into the conventional U-Net model. The proposed Mining Self-Attention (MSA) module dynamically modulates the self-attention computation by incorporating a learned mining map, which highlights spatial regions of high uncertainty in cardiac boundaries and noise-affected areas. This mining map is derived from feature-level statistics, such as variance or estimated uncertainty, and is used to control both the query and key representations within the self-attention computation. In order to better represent features, at every level of the network a dual-scale extraction module is employed to obtain contextual information at different receptive fields and enhance the border delineation of cardiac chambers like the left ventricle and myocardium. The incorporation of multi-scale learning here guarantees that both global shapes and fine boundary details are maintained. Experimental assessments performed using publicly available echocardiography datasets confirm that the proposed DS-MSA U-Net performs better than baseline and state-of-the-art models. Having greater segmentation accuracy, the proposed approach presents a promising solution for accurate automated cardiac image analysis in clinical settings.

title: Boosting Unsupervised Video Instance Segmentation with Automatic Quality-Guided Self-Training
authors: Kaixuan Lu, Mehmet Onurcan Kaya, Dim P. Papadopoulos
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Video Instance Segmentation (VIS) faces significant annotation challenges due to its dual requirements of pixel-level masks and temporal consistency labels. While recent unsupervised methods like VideoCutLER eliminate optical flow dependencies through synthetic data, they remain constrained by the synthetic-to-real domain gap. We present AutoQ-VIS, a novel unsupervised framework that bridges this gap through quality-guided self-training. Our approach establishes a closed-loop system between pseudo-label generation and automatic quality assessment, enabling progressive adaptation from synthetic to real videos. Experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance with 52.6 AP<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50</inf> on YouTubeVIS-2019 val set, surpassing the previous state-of-the-art VideoCutLER by 4.4%, while requiring no human annotations. This demonstrates the viability of quality-aware self-training for unsupervised VIS. The source code of our method is available at here.

title: Key Point Iris Detection Using Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Sweta Jain, Devishree Naidu, Atharva Nagbhidkar, Atharva Pawar, Aryan Dhait
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The proposed research demonstrates a novel iris recognition solution using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) in the task of key point detection. Unlike the state-of-the-art solutions in the field, where the entire iris texture image is considered for analysis, the purpose of this solution is the effective and accurate detection of robust and distinct iris features like the iris boundary point, the pupil point, and other significant biologically based key points of interest. Additionally, the solution uses the ResNet152v2 CNN structure that proves efficient in extracting high spatial details of highresolution images. Furthermore, the CNN consists of down sampling and refinement steps that allow the gradual focusing of interest regions on the object of interest identification. This research highlights the viability of the iris recognition solution based on key point detection in biometric border control applications and other digital product identifications in the field of medicine. The Mean Squared Error for the model was 0.0027 on average, with a precision of 94.6 %, recall of 92.1 %, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> score of 93.3% for the test set.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Architecture for Secure Validation of Medicines and Consumer Products
authors: K. Maithili, S V Hemanth, S.Tejas Reddy, S. Amutha, Krishnaveni N, Minu Inba Shanthini Watson Benjamin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The issue of drugs and the things that people purchase on a daily basis are a huge problem since it inflicts pain on people and ruins the reputation of businesses. It also makes the buyers not trust much. typically the process of making and selling is, however, not very transparent. People can easily counterfeit records or dispose of them. to achieve this we can apply the notion of a computer known as blockchain. This system allows all the concerned parties to confirm whether a drug or other things that people purchase are authentic or not. It operates by record keeping of all that occurred to a product since it is manufactured to when it is sold in a book which no one is allowed to alter once written. A smart contract will record all the events that occur to an item automatically. This implies that the entire life of the item since it was in the factory to the customer is never hidden. each package has its QR code attached to its data in a ledger. As a buyer scans this code with their phone they can view away whether the item is real or not on Ethereum. We have a prototype that is Ethereum based. The contracts of this prototype are in Solidity. We also have a Python module that links the phone application to the blockchain.

title: Controllable Long-term Motion Generation with Extended Joint Targets
authors: Eunjong Lee, Eunhee Kim, Sanghoon Hong, Eunho Jung, Jihoon Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating stable and controllable character motion in real-time is a key challenge in computer animation. Existing methods often fail to provide fine-grained control or suffer from motion degradation over long sequences, limiting their use in interactive applications. We propose COMET, an autoregressive framework that runs in real time, enabling versatile character control and robust long-horizon synthesis. Our efficient Transformer-based conditional VAE allows for precise, interactive control over arbitrary user-specified joints for tasks like goal-reaching and in-betweening from a single model. To ensure long-term temporal stability, we introduce a novel reference--guided feedback mechanism that prevents error accumulation. This mechanism also serves as a plug-and-play stylization module, enabling real-time style transfer. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that COMET robustly generates high-quality motion at real-time speeds, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art approaches in complex motion control tasks and confirming its readiness for demanding interactive applications. Video results and code are available at https://comet-proj.github.io/.

title: Intelligent Meeting Insights Generator Using Speech-to-Text and LLM-Based Semantic Categorization Algorithm
authors: Madhushree. R, T.N. Prabhu, Gopalakrishnan. K
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Meetings produce large amounts of unorganized audio data, and therefore, manual data recording of decisions, action items, and discussion outcomes are inefficient, and prone to error. The given paper offers a Meeting Insights Generator powered by AI, which combines the speech recognition (ASR), semantic reasoning with a large language model (LLM), and sentiment analysis to turn meeting audio into structured and actionable insights automatically. The proposed framework uses semantic categorization and reasoning multi-stage pipeline, not using text-only summarization as traditional systems: modules of the proposed system are deployed in a hybrid local-cloud mode. It is a scalable, interpretable and cost-effective design. Simulated meeting and benchmark datasets like the AMI and ICSI Meeting Corpora are experimentally tested to provide better contextual relevance, lower redundancy and competitive results under ROUGE-L, Word Error Rate (WER), and sentiment accuracy measures. The findings have shown that the proposed system is quite suitable to the actual analysis of meetings in academic and organizational settings.

title: Learning Compact Video Representations for Efficient Long-form Video Understanding in Large Multimodal Models
authors: Yuxiao Chen, Jue Wang, Zhikang Zhang, Jingru Yi, Xu Zhang, Yang Zou, Zhaowei Cai, Jianbo Yuan, Xinyu Li, Hao Yang, Davide Modolo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: With recent advancements in video backbone architectures, combined with the remarkable achievements of large language models (LLMs), the analysis of long-form videos spanning tens of minutes has become both feasible and increasingly prevalent. However, the inherently redundant nature of video sequences poses significant challenges for contemporary state-of-the-art models. These challenges stem from two primary aspects: 1) efficiently incorporating a larger number of frames within memory constraints, and 2) extracting discriminative information from the vast volume of input data. In this paper, we introduce a novel end-to-end schema for long-form video understanding, which includes an information-density-based adaptive video sampler (AVS) and an autoencoder-based spatiotemporal video compressor (SVC) integrated with a multimodal large language model (MLLM). Our proposed system offers two major advantages: it adaptively and effectively captures essential information from video sequences of varying durations, and it achieves high compression rates while preserving crucial discriminative information. The proposed framework demonstrates promising performance across various benchmarks, excelling in both long-form video understanding tasks and standard video understanding benchmarks. These results underscore the versatility and efficacy of our approach, particularly in managing the complexities of prolonged video sequences.

title: Dynamic Feature Fusion In a Semi-Supervised Scene for Cardiovascular Risk Identification
authors: Turaka Rajesh, Suthir Sriram
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Predicting heart attacks isn’t just important—it’s urgent. You need models you can actually trust. That’s where our research comes in. We’re introducing Dynamic Feature Fusion in a Semi-Supervised Scene Framework. It’s a mouthful, but the idea is simple: we use multi-view data in a smarter way to make heart attack predictions sharper and more reliable. Right off the bat, SMILE tackles two big problems. First, there’s never enough labeled medical data. Second, not all types of data matter equally. Here’s what we do: the framework uses a dynamic fusion module that learns which data sources are more trustworthy, and it pays more attention to those. On top of that, we add a semantic mapping head, which helps the model build smarter features for easier classification. And with view-specific autoencoders, SMILE digs deep to pull the best low-level features from each kind of data. Because it constantly adjusts how much it trusts each data source, SMILE pulls out the strengths of every input and tones down the noise from anything low-quality. It doesn’t stop there. With so much unlabeled data floating around, SMILE leans into semi-supervised learning and pseudo-labeling, so it keeps learning even when expert-labeled examples are hard to come by. We put SMILE to the test against real-world heart attack prediction challenges. It beats other top semi-supervised, multi-view models—higher accuracy, better F1 scores, and stronger AUC. When we dig into what’s driving those results, it’s clear: the dynamic fusion and semantic mapping are making a real impact. For clinics without endless labeled data, SMILE gives them a reliable way to predict heart attacks—and honestly, it just works better.

title: Geo-Location Based Attendance Tracking Using Mobile Application and Geofencing Techniques
authors: G.Roja, Vidushi Bahuguna, Sadashiv Anavya, Kancharla Shresta Reddy, Hasavathu Pallavi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This paper presents a geo-location-based attendance tracking mobile application leveraging geofencing technology to ensure precise and automated attendance monitoring. Traditional attendance systems often face issues such as proxy marking, manual errors, and inefficiency. The proposed system integrates GPS, Google Maps API, and mobile application development tools to automate attendance verification within predefined geographical boundaries. Developed using React Native and Node.js with MySQL, the application supports both user and administrator interfaces for seamless attendance management. The solution minimizes manual intervention, ensures real-time updates, and enhances transparency in attendance tracking.

title: AuViRe: Audio-visual Speech Representation Reconstruction for Deepfake Temporal Localization
authors: Christos Koutlis, Symeon Papadopoulos
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of sophisticated synthetic audio-visual content, e.g., for subtle malicious manipulations, ensuring the integrity of digital media has become paramount. This work presents a novel approach to temporal localization of deepfakes by leveraging Audio-Visual Speech Representation Reconstruction (AuViRe). Specifically, our approach reconstructs speech representations from one modality (e.g., lip movements) based on the other (e.g., audio waveform). Cross-modal reconstruction is significantly more challenging in manipulated video segments, leading to amplified discrepancies, thereby providing robust discriminative cues for precise temporal forgery localization. AuViRe outperforms the state of the art by +8.9 AP@0.95 on LAV-DF, +9.6 AP@0.5 on AV-Deepfake1M, and +5.1 AUC on an in-the-wild experiment. Code at https://github.com/mever-team/auvire.

title: Artifact Comparison Analyzer: Evaluating Microservice Build Metrics for Performance and Efficiency Improvements
authors: Sharad Jain, Dileep Jain
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Microservice architecture has become a popular method of creating scalable, modular, and maintainable software systems that allow organizations to accelerate development processes and react fast to changes in business requirements. Nevertheless, the current studies are mostly concerned with runtime performance, energy usage, and anomaly detection, with no study done on build-time efficiency or artifact-level measures. This paper introduces the Artifact Comparison Analyzer that uses the MiSAR (MicroService Architecture Recovery) model to evaluate microservice architectures in a structured way by analyzing them as artifacts. Open-source systems that were publicly available were collected and transformed into platform-specific models (PSM) and platform-independent models (PIM) based on the concepts of Model-Driven Architecture and QVTo changes. Accuracy of a model and efficacy of a framework in reference to precision, recall and F1-score evaluation metrics were used to evaluate the accuracy of the model. The two representative applications examined through experiments, TrainTicket and MusicStore, prove that MiSAR has high accuracy (up to 99.6%), successful build processing, and better resource usage. The results of optimization show a decrease in the build time, the size of the artifact, dependency resolution time, and the consumption of CPU, which demonstrates the power of the framework to perform semi-automated recovery of architectures and performance improvement. The paper confirms that MiSAR is a viable, scalable and dependable approach to evaluate and enhance microservice-based architectures.

title: Generative AI for Video Trailer Synthesis: From Extractive Heuristics to Autoregressive Creativity

authors: Naina Chaturvedi, Ananda Gunawardena
publish: Proceedings of the Nineteenth ACM International Conference on Web Search and Data Mining
