An essential aspect of the framework is its smart learning module for long-term context reuse that relies on learning and memorization of derived context from past repetitive interactions, hence making the system more lightweight and reactive without overfitting the basic GenAI model. Expect the results to be improved agent preparedness, higher FCR, lower AHT, higher CSAT and better ability to offer personalized service recommendations. The paper introduces the system architecture, the MCP-GenAI interaction model, the context persistence mechanism, and presents practical implementation issues and future improvements.

title: A Study on Maternal and Child Health Monitoring system using IoT and Machine Learning
authors: Sunitha Tappari, N. Sreshta, Medha Sree Aare, Rakshitha Bheemanadhuni, A. Hinduja Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Pregnancy is a critical period, and the timely detection of physiological changes helps in the early prevention of complications. Wearable sensors measure essential parameters like heart rate, temperature, and SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, transmitting the data to the ThingSpeak cloud via Wi-Fi. It is analyzed for the differentiation of normal and abnormal conditions via a Random Forest algorithm, which generates alerts in case of deviations. This allows for early intervention, reducing risks, and enhancing maternal safety. It is low cost, compact, and user-friendly, thus allowing home-based monitoring without repeated visits to the hospital. It supports real-time visualization, record storage, and remote sharing of data with healthcare providers through cloud integration. Prototype testing confirms correct sensor readings and reliable ML predictions.

title: GeneVA: A Dataset of Human Annotations for Generative Text to Video Artifacts
authors: Jenna Kang, Maria Beatriz Silva, Patsorn Sangkloy, Kenneth Chen, Niall L. Williams, Qi Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Video generated by the current state-of-the-art generative models contain undesirable artifacts. We introduce GeneVA, the first large-scale dataset of human-annotated artifact bounding boxes in AI-generated videos. The dataset consists of 16,356 AI-generated videos, each labeled by a human annotator with per-frame artifact bounding boxes, their labels and descriptions, and video quality ratings. A custom data collection pipeline was developed in Prolific, and a novel taxonomy for spatio-temporal artifacts present in AI-generated videos was defined. The videos were from the VidProM [41] dataset, with text prompts from this dataset then used to generate an additional subset of videos using Sora. We trained an artifact detector and caption generator using a pre-trained image-based model, and a custom temporal fusion module. The dataset can be found at https://www.immersivecomputinglab.org/publication/geneva. We hope that datasets like GeneVA will encourage improvements in artifact detection in AI-generated video towards applications such as deepfake detection.

title: Privacy-Preserving Fraud Detection in Digital Payments Using Federated Learning
authors: Harshit Gulipalli, Hema Kakarlapudi, Moorathoti Achsah Himavarsha, Ainapuri Sri Nikhith, Doddi Chandana Gowri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The rapid expansion of digital payment systems has been matched by a rise in fraudulent activities, emphasizing the importance of secure and intelligent detection methods. This study proposes a federated learning-based weighted ensemble framework that improves fraud identification accuracy while maintaining confidentiality of transaction records. Rather than sharing sensitive data, individual clients develop local models using XGBoost, CatBoost, and AdaBoost, transmitting only model parameters for safe aggregation. The optimal ensemble weights are calculated using Non-Negative Least Squares (NNLS), resulting in contributions of XGBoost (0.0516), CatBoost (0.9480), and AdaBoost (0.0004), highlighting CatBoost’s primary influence. Tests on the OpenML Credit Card Fraud Detection dataset (284,807 transactions, 31 features) achieved 99.95% accuracy, 0.9691 ROC-AUC, 0.9180 precision, 0.7568 recall, and 0.8296 F1score, surpassing all individual base models. This methodology provides a viable solution for financial organizations, offering high detection performance alongside privacy protection and scalability in real-world digital payment environments.

title: Power Demand Forecasting
authors: Kirtiman Singh, Pranjal Singh, Ayush Singh, Deepali Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Forecasting electricity demand is difficult problem that has a multitude of factors associated with the environment. In this paper, we have presented development of a strong model for short term electricity demand forecasting that uses publicly available data from Kaggle. The observations in the data measured frequent periods of electric power use with environmental characteristics, such as temperature, humidity, dew point, wind, and pressure. Environmental features and generated time variables (year, month, day, and hour) and moving average variable was generated and used to help with forecasting trend aspects of consumption. A (SARIMAX) variable forecasted future values while controlling for the seasonality and dependence on other variables. Modeling the data with a SARIMAX approach allowed the model to identify daily and weekly cyclical usage patterns while considering forecasted weather conditions. The SARIMAX Model was developed using two years of data and was assessed for generalizability against a subsequent years worth of data. The SARIMAX obtained high forecasting accuracy for consumption behavior with expected shifts in peak load. This study provides evidence that data-driven approaches have promise for substantial improvement in energy management and operational forecasting within power systems.

title: Deep Neural Network Model-Based Ship Images Classification
authors: Vishnavi Eddu, Nilayam Kumar Kamila, Srimannarayana Mopidevi, Kavyasri Thatavarthi, Durga Prasad Pitti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Ship classification is vital for the navy or military to identify regional, national, or international ships. Since these ships are moving in sea areas or sea locations that are in dangerous places, identifying and classifying those ships in deepsea areas is challenging for Navy personnel. Although a few approaches are used to classify the ships, more techniques are still needed to identify and classify various ships using the ship image classification model. Thus, this paper considers a few deep learning approaches, such as Xception, EfficientNetB7, DenseNet201, etc., for classifying ships from deep-sea areas. The proposed techniques are demonstrated with a ship image dataset, and performance evaluation is considered to analyze the classification ship. The comparative performance is considered among approaches, and better performance is found in DenseNet201 with 96% accuracy.

title: Explainable Superintelligence NLP with Agentic AI for Transparent Policy Decision-Making in Cloud Systems
authors: Phani Rohitha Kaza
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Cloud systems are growing in scale and complexity, making policy decisions more critical for performance, security, and resource allocation. Traditional approaches to decision-making often work as black boxes, creating gaps in trust and transparency. This lack of clarity restricts adoption in sensitive domains where accountability and interpretability are essential. Superintelligent natural language processing (NLP) with agentic artificial intelligence (AI) offers a pathway to address these gaps by combining advanced reasoning with explainable outputs. The proposed work introduces an explainable superintelligence NLP framework that integrates agentic AI capabilities to guide transparent policy decision-making in cloud environments. The framework builds an interpretable layer over advanced NLP models, allowing decision flows to be traced and understood by human operators. Agentic AI modules actively adapt policies in real time by analyzing workloads, security alerts, and user demands. A hybrid algorithmic approach is used, combining graph neural reasoning with reinforcement-based policy updates, allowing both adaptability and clarity. Each policy action is paired with human-readable justifications, reducing the opacity of automated systems. To evaluate the framework, multiple metrics are applied. Accuracy in decision outcomes is measured against benchmark policy datasets. Transparency is assessed using interpretability scores that highlight alignment between system explanations and human understanding. Reliability is measured through policy consistency under stress-tested cloud workloads. Efficiency is quantified by monitoring response latency and computational cost. Experimental studies demonstrate significant improvements across these metrics, highlighting that explainable superintelligence NLP with agentic AI not only supports transparent decision-making but also improves operational trust and system stability. This work presents a practical step toward accountable, high-performing, and interpretable AI-driven governance in modern cloud systems.

title: Parameter Optimization of a Magnetic Fault Current Limiter Using Biogeography-Based Optimization
authors: Tirtha Sankar Daphadar, Tapan Santra, Amalendu Bikash Choudhury
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization study of a permanent-magnet biased fault current limiter (PMFCL) using the Biogeography-Based Optimization (BBO) algorithm. With the increasing expense of magnetic cores and rare-earth permanent magnets, reducing material usage while maintaining reliable fault suppression has become essential. In this work, the PMFCL design parameters are optimized through BBO to minimize device volume and improve performance. The optimization framework addresses three key objectives: (i) minimizing the physical size,(ii) reducing the first fault-current peak, and (iii) limiting voltage drop during normal operation. A prototype was constructed based on the optimized parameters, and its performance was validated experimentally. Both simulation and test results confirm that the BBO-optimized PMFCL provides effective fault current limitation while ensuring acceptable voltage regulation in steady state.

title: See, Record, Do: Automated Generation of UI Workflows from Tutorial Videos
authors: Adam Beauchaine, Craig Shue
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: System designers and developers need data-driven approaches for user-interface (UI) development and testing. They need trace-based workflows to support UI navigation agents and inputs for UI code generation. Given the high production costs of manually constructing such workflows, the UI agent research community has explored automated, fully-synthetic UI workflow generation [16]. However, there is an open need to characterize the fidelity and effectiveness of these synthetic approaches with respect to the current manual approaches.In this work, we aim to provide larger-scale synthetic workflows based on past human usage. We particularly focused on the desktop application modality since prior synthetic generation has mostly targeted mobile or web applications. Using video tutorials with permissive licenses, we derive associated UI behaviors to construct synthetic dataset of UI workflows in desktop applications. We provide data from these videos to a large language model (LLM) to generate a set of "task list" instructions that replicate the videos’ actions. We execute the task list instructions in a UI agent within an instrumented desktop operating system. Using the sensor data from that environment, we can enable simpler actuation scripts for replay of the tasks. Along with detailing our approach, we provide a dataset with over 5,000 workflows across a range of desktop applications with costs that are significantly lower than manual construction.

title: An Explainable Deep Learning Framework for Automated Bone Fracture Detection and Classification
authors: Vinay P, Pruthvi M Kadri, Mehak Sharma, Moulya Suvarna, Pooja
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The diagnosis of fractures and their classification are the most important factors in clinical assessment and planning of treatment. Manual interpretation of X-ray images is often time-consuming and error-prone. This work presents an AI-powered deep learning framework for automated fracture detection and classification using CNN. The system incorporates explainable AI techniques like Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and Canny edge detection to enhance interpretability and visual transparency. Furthermore, this framework has been validated in three stages: binary fracture detection, multi-class fracture classification, and explainability-based analysis. The experimental results obtained reveal that the proposed model using ResNet50 achieved a classification accuracy of 91.33%, outperforming the state-of-the-art baselines. This includes Grad-CAM and Canny overlays that improve visual understanding, thus supporting clinical decision-making and enhancing the trustworthiness of AI-driven diagnosis.

title: Privacy-Aware Federated Large Language Model Adaptation Using Encrypted Gradient Aggregation
authors: Gade Shiva Kumar, Basha Vankudothu, Abhishek Kumar Tiwari, V.P Gladis Pushparathi, B Sathiya, U D Prasan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated exceptional capabilities in language understanding and generation but pose significant risks when fine-tuned using sensitive, user-specific data. Conventional methods either compromise privacy or degrade performance due to excessive noise and inefficient encryption schemes. The article introduces the new privacy preserving fine-tuning framework, namely PP-EGA-LLM, which is an alternative to homomorphic encryption system but considers interpretability at the cost of gradient filtering during the federation process. Every client calculates encrypted gradients on their own and sums them up in the framework of Secure Multi-Party Computation without sharing the content of the data. SHAP-based filtering masks sensitive components of gradient before update of a model. On sensitive medical and legal datasets, PP-EGA-LLM is more accurate (93.4%) than baselines based on differentially private and encrypted models and has a much lower leakage risk (MIR: 0.18). The model shows nearly centralized performance and acceptable inference latency on edge devices. We are optimized with utility, privacy and efficiency and thus fit quite well in the real-world application in the privacy sensitive field. The architecture can upgrade cryptographically and is therefore post quantum threat extendible. The study is leading the art of secure and ethical fine-tuning of LLMs without losing the accuracy of the data.

title: Reviewers
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)

title: Hybrid Feature Selection Using Genetic Algorithm and Particle Swarm Optimization for Classification Problems
authors: Madhavilata Mangipudi, Naveen Pragallapati
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Selection of features is a process involving the enhancement of the quality and effectiveness of machine learning systems especially in the case of high dimensional data. The article is a description of a hybrid feature selection methodology that combines Genetic Algorithms (GA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to locate the most optimal combination of features in solving classification problems. The suggested method utilizes the global exploration power of GA and high-speed convergence ability of PSO. Studies were carried on various benchmark datasets in order to find out its effectiveness and compare it with singly applied GA, PSO, and traditional feature selection algorithms. The findings indicate that the hybrid approach of using GA-PSO is highly beneficial in increasing the accuracy of classification and minimizing the selected characteristics. Moreover, it is also repeatable over a number of classifiers, which shows that it can be used in medical diagnosis, finance, and text classification.

title: Parkinson’s Disease Prediction from Handwriting using Sequential CNNS
authors: N. Divya, Ankitha Kontemukkula, Azmeera Priyanka, Likitha Reddy Jillella, Sruthi Manikireddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Parkinson’s Disease(PD) is a progressive neuro muscular disease that affects movement and often begins with subtle changes in handwriting [1][3] (e.g., tremors or less large letters). Early identification of the PD can greatly affect treatment options and the quality of life. This project presents an uncomplicated, browser-based tool that applies Sequential Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to the analysis of handwriting [3], [7]. Users can either draw on a digital canvas, or upload images of their handwriting, and the system will analyze the handwriting to identify the presence of patterns that are consistent with or associated with Parkinsonism [6], and will output assessments (e.g., risk, confidence score, and visual explanations of the model’s focus). The device is is a lightweight, user-friendly alternative to current-devices that require expensive equipment and thorough clinical evaluations. This tool can be used remotely, which enhances virtual health initiatives and broadens outreach potential for early screening in under-served areas. This device provides a simple, easy, low- cost, and safe risk-assessment method that hopes to broadening access to facilitate early diagnosis of Parkinson's Disease.

title: Improving Out-of-Distribution Detection using Segmented Images and Cross-View Attention Fusion
authors: Alexander Politowicz, Sahisnu Mazumder, Bing Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Although out-of-distribution (OOD) detection has been extensively studied, it continues to face challenges in handling OOD data semantically similar to In-Distribution (ID) data. Part of the difficulty arises from the model’s inability to learn superior ID class discriminative features. We propose to improve this by segmenting input images into foreground and background views and combining them with the original input image (original view) in a multi-view learning approach. We present a novel method, called CASOD (Cross-view Attention of Segmented views for OOD Detection), that learns better discriminative information from all three views and subsequently, fuses them through a novel stacked cross-view attention mechanism to produce the final predictive feature representation. A feature-based OOD method is then applied to the final fused feature for OOD detection, giving major improvements over a range of strong baselines on various near- and far-OOD datasets. CASOD achieves state-of-the-art performance in various experimental settings with challenging ID and OOD datasets. Code for CASOD can be found at https://github.com/alexp205/CASOD.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Energy, Trust, and Congestion Optimization in IoT Networks with a Novel EnsemAG-Net Framework
authors: Koppunuri Swapna, V Thirupathi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The paper provides an integrated literature-based study and methodology development towards energy-efficient, trust-aware, and congestion-optimized IoT networking. Recent studies indicate that the challenges of uneven energy depletion, unreliable routing, accumulation of congestion during dynamic traffic conditions, have been persistent even in the review of recent research. To fill these gaps, a light Convolutional neural network is proposed to be used in these gaps, which is called EnsemAG-Net, a combination of AlexNet to learn the spatial states and GRU-based time predictions to assist intelligent clustering and routing. Initial simulations of an IoT network with 50-100 nodes reveal that EnsemAG-Net lowers the total energy consumption by 10-20, stability of trust by 15, fluctuation of congestion by 30 and network life by more than 1200 rounds. These initial results prove the promise of EnsemAG-Net and make this survey-and-model paper a unified resource and a baseline to the future of deep learning-based optimization of the Internet of Things.

title: A Hybrid Transformer Framework for Text-Visual Thyroid Nodule Segmentation in Ultrasound Diagnostics
authors: Hussainbi Shaik, Hussain Syed
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Correct segmentation of thyroid nodules in US images is crucial for accurate diagnosis and treatment planning. However, the performance of most current methods is suboptimal in terms of both high accuracy, and clear interpretability and generalization. In this work, we present Text-Vision Aligned Transformer (TVAT), a novel multimodal segmentation framework that combines state-of-the-art visual– language model and a neural architecture for addressing these challenges. The proposed methodology splits visual information into Broad-Scale and Detail-Scale sets of features. Text cues are combinational optimized with large-scale information to help reinforce semantic recognition. The broad-Type Extractor harvests abundant context features from a fixed vision– language backbone, and the detail- Type Refiner raises spatial accuracy by improving with residual U-Net fashion blocks. Experiments on our newly collected PKTNS dataset and other public datasets demonstrate that TVAT achieves competitive performance. Additional ablation studies reinforce the importance of text guidance in performance, especially when it is adaptive instead of fixed or absent prompts.

title: SOAF: Scene Occlusion-aware Neural Acoustic Field
authors: Huiyu Gao, Jiahao Ma, David Ahmedt-Aristizabal, Chuong Nguyen, Miaomiao Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: This paper tackles the problem of novel view acoustic synthesis along an arbitrary trajectory in an indoor scene, given the audio-video recordings from other known trajectories of the scene. Existing methods often overlook the effect of room geometry, particularly wall occlusions on sound propagation, making them less accurate in multi-room environments. In this work, we propose a new approach called Scene Occlusion-aware Acoustic Field (SOAF) for accurate sound generation. Our approach derives a global prior for the sound field learning through distance-aware parametric sound propagation modeling and then transforms it based on the scene structure learned from the input video. We extract features from the local acoustic field centered at the receiver using a Fibonacci Sphere to generate binaural audio for novel views with a direction-aware attention mechanism. Extensive experiments on the real dataset RWAVS and the synthetic dataset SoundSpaces demonstrate that our method achieves superior performance in spatial audio generation.

title: Short-Term Forecasting of BSE Sensex Using Linear Regression in Microsoft Excel: An Accessible Machine Learning Approach
authors: Pranavi E, T. Malathi Latha, Smitha Mahindrakar, J. Mamatha, Guguloth Sravanthi, K. Yamini Bhargavi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This study explores short-term forecasting of the BSE Sensex Index using linear regression, a foundational machine learning technique. Financial markets exhibit volatility and nonlinear patterns influenced by economic factors, yet data-driven tools enable reliable predictions. Historical yearly closing data from 2015 to 2024, supplemented by 2025 values up to November, were analyzed using Microsoft Excel’s built- in regression functions. The model achieved an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.93 and a MAPE of approximately 2.5%, indicating strong predictive accuracy. Quarterly forecasts for Q1–Q3 2026 project continued upward momentum. This approach demonstrates that accessible tools like Excel can deliver actionable insights for investors, analysts, and researchers, while highlighting the value of interpretable models in financial analytics.

title: Improved Skin Lesion Segmentation Using Dilated SegNet and Image Processing Techniques
authors: Chilukuri Vaishnavi, Adigopula Siva Balaji, Anitha Setti, Saladi Saritha, Yepuganti Karuna, A Shanthi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Among the most extensively distributed malignancies in the world, skin cancer is included; most of the cancer-associated death due to skin cancer is attributed to melanoma which is a very aggressive type of the skin cancer. Early and proper diagnosis is paramount in the successful management and less deaths. With the increasing popularity of the utilization of the dermoscopic imaging as one of the non-invasive diagnostic techniques, dermatologists are now able to examine the lesions more thoroughly and in a better way. The inspection of dermoscopic image through manual methods is tedious and can be subject to human error and incorrect diagnosis is likely to occur in many cases, thus, the proposed work provides a Dilated SegNet based segmentation framework that integrates the encoder-decoder (SegNet) depth and the contextual learning of dilated (atrous) convolutions. The enlargement of the receptive field of the network with dilated filters would not add any extra computational cost but would retain fine details and accumulate wide context. Also, the spatial cross-entropy loss ensures pixel-level pixel classification, which improves the convergence of the models and the minimalization of the lesion-to-background boundaries. The work helps to fill the gap between the deep learning techniques and practical clinical use. Future studies of this work should be focused on developing hybrid architectures that can work with multi-class skin condition analysis, adapt to a larger and more diverse dermoscopic data set, and interact with lesion classification systems.

title: EVTP-IVS: Effective Visual Token Pruning For Unifying Instruction Visual Segmentation In Multi-Modal Large Language Models
authors: Wenhui Zhu, Xiwen Chen, Zhipeng Wang, Shao Tang, Sayan Ghosh, Xuanzhao Dong, Rajat Koner, Yalin Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Instructed Visual Segmentation (IVS) tasks require segmenting objects in images or videos based on natural language instructions. While recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have achieved strong performance on IVS, their inference cost remains a major bottleneck, particularly in video. We empirically analyze visual token sampling in MLLMs and observe a strong correlation between subset token coverage and segmentation performance. This motivates our design of a simple and effective token pruning method that selects a compact yet spatially representative subset of tokens to accelerate inference. In this paper, we introduce a novel visual token pruning method for IVS, called EVTP-IV, which builds upon the k-center by integrating spatial information to ensure better coverage. We further provide an information-theoretic analysis to support our design. Experiments on standard IVS benchmarks show that our method achieves up to 5× speed-up on video tasks and 3.5× on image tasks, while maintaining comparable accuracy using only 20% of the tokens. Our method also consistently outperforms state-of-the-art pruning baselines under varying pruning ratios.

title: Fetal and Neonatal Cortical Surface Reconstruction with Anatomical Normal-guidance and Perceptual Enhancements
authors: Jiyang Lee, Woori Bae, U-Geun Ji, Hanyeol Yang, Jong-Min Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurate reconstruction of cortical surfaces from fetal and neonatal brain MRI plays a fundamental role in neuroscience research and clinical applications. Despite recent advances in deep learning-based approaches, accurate fetal and neonatal surface reconstruction remains challenging due to low tissue contrast, narrow sulci, and complex folding patterns. We present ANPE (Anatomical Normal-guidance with Perceptual Enhancements), a simple yet effective framework that enhances cortical surface reconstruction networks through three key aspects: Our method enforces anatomically plausible deformations by adaptively integrating normal vectors with velocity vectors to simultaneously capture global structure and fine-grained details, significantly improving geometric coherence. Furthermore, we amplify structural transitions and tissue boundaries without requiring explicit segmentation or signed distance functions to eliminate the dependency on additional processing steps, making our method more efficient and widely applicable. Finally, we utilize a context-aware loss function that transcends traditional point-wise losses by integrating a pre-trained feature extractor to capture hierarchical contextual and structural similarities, blending surface-based and image-based features to ensure anatomically meaningful reconstructions. Our simple yet efficient framework is shown to be an efficient, more accurate, and biologically informed approach, presenting a new baseline for fetal and neonatal cortical surface reconstruction.

title: A Multi-Factor Risk-Minimization Framework for Integrated Personal Safety and Navigation
authors: Niki Modi, Yash Shyam Wadhvani, Rishi Mehta, Tirth Borad, Prit Suthar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The concept of public safety is indeed a major issue in any urban setting but the current navigation solutions do not have a full-fledged risk evaluation and they focus more on velocity and distance rather than on the security of the user. Existing emergency applications are either a single-point of failure as is the case with only functional smartphones, or a reactive hardware device that does not incorporate intelligent data integration. SafeRoute is our suggested idea of a multi-factor risk minimization framework in the form of an integrated personal safety ecosystem. The system uses a Multi-Factor Safety Scoring (MFSS) model, which is a backend inspired model that takes into account five unique types of data inputs, such as crime reports, street lighting, and crowd density to compute dynamic safety ratings. The system uses GeoHash-based spatial discretization to create geographical data which it processed into a multi-objective routing system that produced route recommendations based on safety requirements. Moreover, the system has an incremental architecture which has also been integrated with a standalone IoT personal SOS device, which forms a backup emergency response capacity and operates even in cases where the main mobile application has shutdown. In the given paper, the methodological proposal of SafeRoute is determined, which allows creating a viable and comprehensive solution to ensure the safety of individuals and provide them with active travel safety options.

title: Visual Attention-based Modeling of Consumer Like/Dislike Reactions to Creative Contents
authors: Mithun B S, Tince Varghese, Somnath Karmakar
abstract: Understanding consumer preferences for creative contents, like advertisements, is a core driver of effective marketing and design strategy. Traditional preference assessment techniques rely heavily on self-reported responses, surveys or questionnaires, which are susceptible to bias, memory limitation and differences between stated opinions and true behaviour. Neuromarketing addresses these limitations by capturing behavioural and physiological signals to understand consumer’s natural response to creative contents. This research presents a novel, unobtrusive framework for predicting consumer like/dislike reactions to creative contents using blink rate variability-based visual attention signals captured through a standard RGB camera. The proposed methodology introduces two attention-based features, attention stability frequency score and attention stability power score, to effectively model visual attention dynamics and derive a quantifiable preference index in a natural viewing context. The effectiveness of the proposed approach has been demonstrated in predicting consumer like/dislike preferences and subsequently in A/B testing scenarios. This scalable, human-in-the-loop method integrates advertising and neuromarketing, providing insights for A/B testing and creative optimization to enhance consumer engagement and improved conversion rates.

title: Stress Detection and Suggestion for IT Professional Using Natural Language Processsing
authors: C Sivamurugan, P Gowsalya, Pathan Abdul Jilani, Y V Jwithesh Kumar, Rohith, N Pavan Sai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Stress has become a critical occupational hazard for IT professionals, who routinely face stringent deadlines, rapid task switching, and heavy cognitive workloads. Traditional stress-assessment methods such as questionnaires and periodic clinical evaluations are slow, subjective, and incapable of capturing real-time fluctuations. To address these limitations, this work proposes a multimodal, automated stress-detection system that integrates image processing, physiological pattern analysis, and Natural Language Processing (NLP) to enable continuous and objective monitoring of employee stress levels. Facial expression cues are extracted using Python libraries such as MediaPipe and OpenCV, which identify 33 facial landmark points as indicators of emotional strain. Textual inputs—such as user- provided statements or reflections—are processed through NLP models to identify stress-related sentiment and linguistic markers. These multimodal inputs are analysed using machine learning models, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for image analysis, Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs/LSTMs) for physiological trends, and transformer-based NLP models for textual interpretation. The fused outputs generate a unified real-time stress score, enabling early detection and intervention. The proposed system demonstrates strong performance across accuracy, precision, recall, and F1- score metrics, confirming its reliability for workplace deployment. By integrating AI-driven analysis into organizational wellness workflows, the system provides actionable insights that support healthier work environments and timely stress-management strategies.

title: CHAID Based Decision Tree for Intelligent Employee Performance Appraisal in Human Resource Management Systems
authors: S. Mohan Prabhu, Brijesh Singh, Mohammad Shahid Kamal, Aasif Aftab, Samisha B, S. Baskaran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Since the advent of liberalization, privatization, and globalization, it has become essential for organizations to attract potential candidates for specific positions and retain key employees to maintain an effective and competitive workforce. Currently, in any industry, regardless of size, there is a significant transformation in the functions of human resource management. In addition to its conventional functions, Human Resource Management has broadened its scope to evaluate and manage employee performance through a clearly defined systematic approach that has developed in response to advancements in the HRM field. SimpleImputer was employed to address missing values, while StandardScaler was utilized to standardize numerical features. PCA is used for feature extraction. The purpose of developing a decision tree model was to classify performance outcomes and identify critical assessment elements using the CHAID method. A useful tool for HR practitioners, the resultant model provides an interpretable framework for factor importance analysis and prediction. The reliability of the CHAID-based decision tree in evaluating employee performance was demonstrated by its high accuracy rate of 98.36%. In order to improve assessment processes, decision-making, and organisational competitiveness, the study emphasises the importance of incorporating analytical models into HRM systems.

title: Intelligent Machine Condition Monitoring using Triboelectric Vibration Sensor
authors: Aparna Sinha, Debanjan Das
abstract: The continuous operation of electrical machines in the manufacturing and automation industries demands vigilant monitoring to prevent failures and financial losses. Conventional methods rely on accelerometers for vibration data, which requires external power and limits comprehensive fault detection. Alternative studies involving Triboelectric Nanogenerators (TENGs) have surfaced, yet few have harnessed this technology for machine fault diagnosis. This study introduces a novel approach employing a TENG-based sensor system featuring a sphere-in-sphere design to capture machine vibrations. The sensor harnesses mechanical vibrations to generate electrical signals, circumventing the need for external power sources and addressing the limitations encountered in previous methodologies. Through rigorous experimentation and analysis, the proposed method achieves an exceptional fault detection accuracy of 99.48% using logistic regression algorithm. This substantial achievement underscores the efficacy and reliability of the TENG-based sensor system in accurately identifying machine faults, offering a promising avenue for proactive maintenance strategies and preventing potential failures.

title: Performance Benchmarking of Machine Learning Algorithms for Attention State Classification Using Physiological and Oculometric Features
authors: Abhinav Rana, Kiran Jot Singh, Divneet Singh Kapoor
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Proper evaluation of human attention is in use in applications like adaptive learning, driver monitoring, and human-computer interaction. In this research, a performance benchmark analysis of machine learning based on attention state classification is conducted through physiological and oculometric features. The physiological indicators and data on eye-tracking were recorded in the process of attention requiring tasks and analyzed to retrieve pertinent characteristics. Attention statuses were categorized as binary and multi-class depending on the conditions of the task and the measures of performance. Several machine learning models such as Support Vector Machines, Random Forest, and XGBoost were tested based on the five-fold cross-validation method. According to the results of the experiment, multimodal feature fusion is a highly effective method in relation to unimodal techniques in the accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The highest classification performance as well as stability was achieved by ensemble-based models especially XGBoost. The results demonstrate the success of the combination of physiological and oculometric measurements and offer a solid framework of benchmarking to inform the choice of models to be used in the real-world attention monitoring systems.

title: Design and Implementation of Low-Cost Highly Sensitive Portable Microwave Sensor for Detection of permittivity of Edible Oils
authors: Supratim Mondal, Sagnik Adhikary, Ch.Raghavendra, A.Jhansirani, Gobinda Sen
abstract: This work presents a unique approach to achieve higher sensitivity of differential microwave based dielectric sensors by integrating folded SRR (Split Ring Resonator) into a microstrip line. This method makes use of inter-resonator coupling, in which an analogous lumped circuit is represented as mutual capacitance. This mutual capacitance enhances the sensor’s sensitivity by altering the resonance frequency, enabling the detection of dipolar materials (Oils) through variations in the real permittivity of dielectric laminates. The proposed sensor is made up of a splitter-combiner microstrip section and PLA channel. The sensitivity of the novel sensor for identifying dielectric materials has significantly increased due to the separate PLA channel just over the SRR. From the simulation we can see that the electric fields are distributed at all the arms and two sides of that SRR. Here a low-loss Arlon material is used as a substrate which has the dielectric constant of 2.5 and a loss tangent of 0.002. It resonates in dual band. Materials Under Test (MUTs) are tested on both of the bands to achieve higher sensitivity. Characterizing various oil samples like mustard, castor and flaxseed oils are made possible by the sensor's high Q factor. When it comes for detecting oil samples, the sensor’s highest sensitivity is 1.6% (Flaxseed Oil). The main area of the resonator is regarded as a sensing region across the arms. It is ideal for real-time permittivity monitoring in industrial, agricultural, and healthcare applications especially in food safety due to its affordability and compact size. The efficiency of the suggested sensor is confirmed by experimental results, which also show how widely applicable they could be in a variety of sensing applications. real-time permittivity monitoring in industrial, agricultural, and healthcare applications especially in food safety due to its affordability and compact size. The efficiency of the suggested sensor is confirmed by experimental results, which also show how widely applicable they could be in a variety of sensing applications. suggested sensor is confirmed by experimental results, which also show how widely applicable they could be in a variety of sensing applications. real-time permittivity monitoring in industrial, agricultural, and healthcare applications especially in food safety due to its affordability and compact size. The efficiency of the suggested sensor is confirmed by experimental results, which also show how widely applicable they could be in a variety of sensing applications.

title: Design and Development of a Unmanned Surface Vehicle (USV) for Sludge Quantification and Inland Water Quality Assessment
authors: Dwivedula Narasimha Sri Sourabh, Vinayak Maharshi, M.Balakumaran, Himabindu Allaka
abstract: Lakes are vital for sustaining ecosystems and human life, offering drinking water, supporting agriculture, providing recreational opportunities, and serving as habitats for diverse species. Unfortunately, urban development, agricultural runoff, and climate change are increasingly degrading water quality and causing sludge to accumulate, which negatively impacts the lake health. Monitoring both water quality and sludge buildup is vital for effective lake management. Traditional manual sampling methods are time-consuming and offer limited spatial and temporal coverage. In this study, we present LIMUS (Lake Inspection and Monitoring Unmanned Surface Vehicle), a compact and cost-effective USV platform designed and developed to monitor water quality and quantify sludge accumulation. LIMUS is equipped with in-situ water quality sensors, a custom-built water sampling carousel, and an echosounder that estimates both depth and sludge buildup. Propelled by two thrusters, providing differential thrust and controlled via an onboard autopilot, the vehicle supports both manual and autonomous operation modes. The water sampling system, driven by a stepper motor and peristaltic pump, collects water at predefined locations. A Ground Control Station (GCS) enables mission planning, real-time data visualization, and data logging. Initial tests in a controlled pool environment demonstrated reliable handling, accurate sensor readings, mechanical robustness, and efficient sample collection. This integrated platform offers a scalable solution for detailed monitoring of inaccessible lakes, combining water quality assessment along with sludge quantification to track lake health.

title: AEON: Adaptive Embedding Optimized Noise for Robust Watermarking in Diffusion Models
authors: Muhammad Shahid Muneer, Simon S. Woo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The widespread use of synthetic image generation models and the challenges associated with authenticity preservation have fueled the demand for robust watermarking methods to safeguard authenticity and protect the copyright of synthetic images. Existing watermarking methods embed. Invisible signatures in synthetic images often compromise image quality and remain susceptible to multiple watermark removal attacks, including reconstruction and forgery methods. To overcome this issue, we propose a novel watermarking approach, AEON, which seamlessly integrates the watermark into the latent diffusion process and ensures the watermark aligns with scene semantics in the final image. Unlike existing invisible in-diffusion watermarking and traditional hash-based methods, our approach adapts the neural synthesized hash-based watermark to the semantics of the generated image during the intermediate diffusion process instead of embedding traditional hashes with the initial noise. Our proposed approach a) modulates the noise sampling in each diffusion denoising iteration through a learnable watermark embedding, b) optimizes consistency, reconstruction, and similarity loss, enforcing local and global alignment between the watermark structure and the underlying image content, and c) generates a strong watermark by allowing late embedding of the watermark in the diffusion process. Empirical results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in retaining quality and its robustness against cumulative adversarial attacks.

title: RAVU: Retrieval Augmented Video Understanding with Compositional Reasoning over Graph
authors: Sameer Malik, Ayush Singh, Moyuru Yamada, Dishank Aggarwal
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Comprehending long videos remains a significant challenge for Large Multi-modal Models (LMMs). Current LMMs struggle to process even minutes to hours videos due to their lack of explicit memory and retrieval mechanisms. To address this limitation, we propose RAVU (Retrieval Augmented Video Understanding), a novel framework for video understanding enhanced by retrieval with compositional reasoning over a spatio-temporal graph. We construct a graph representation of the video, capturing both spatial and temporal relationships between entities. This graph serves as a long-term memory, allowing us to track objects and their actions across time. To answer complex queries, we decompose the queries into a sequence of reasoning steps and execute these steps on the graph, retrieving relevant key information. Our approach enables more accurate understanding of long videos, particularly for queries that require multi-hop reasoning and tracking objects across frames. Our approach demonstrate superior performances with limited retrieved frames (5-10) compared with other SOTA methods and baselines on two major video QA datasets: NExT-QA and EgoSchema.

title: Performance Analysis of Univariate Garch Models Using Weather Data
authors: Bishnupriya Mallick, Jibendu Kumar Mantri, Niranjan Kumar Ray
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Weather data are affected by nonlinear characteristics of its variance, referred to as volatility. It is a key factor for accurate prediction of time series data. Volatility modeling is also vital for capturing the uncertainty and variability of weather patterns. Univariate GARCH methods are the statistical tools that helps in analysing and forecasting volatility of a single time series data. Therefore, in this research work, we have estimated and analysed performance of some selected univariate GARCH (Generalized Autoregressive Conditional Heteroskedasticity) models like sGARCH (Standard GARCH), eGARCH (Exponential GARCH), gjrGARCH (Glosten-Jagannathan-Runkle GARCH), TGARCH (Threshold GARCH), iGARCH (Integrated GARCH) and csGARCH (Component sGARCH) using three different distribution model such as student t, skew student t, and generalized error to find the best fit optimal model for volatility modeling. Temperature dataset of PURI district for a time period of January-2001 to December- 2020 is utilised for this purpose. The findings of the obtained result recommend that the outperforming eGARCH model has strong validity for the temperature data in volatility modeling using above mentioned three distinct distribution models.

title: LIC-UMNET: Lung Disease Classification via Dual Attention Unet and MobileNet
authors: Fareesa Amina, Krishnanaik Vankdoth
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Lung Disease (LD) refers to pathological conditions affecting the respiratory system, characterized by impaired gas exchange, airway obstruction, or lung tissue damage. A major challenge in classifying LD is overlapping symptoms across different conditions. A unique deep learning (DL)-based LIC-UMNET model is suggested to diagnose lung illnesses from chest X-ray (CXR) pictures in order to overcome these difficulties. The input lung CXR pictures are first gathered from the datasets and pre-processed utilizing contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE) in order to enhance the quality of the input images. The lung area is segmented using the Dual Attention U-Network (DAU-Net) before being sent into MobileNet for feature extraction. Following feature extraction, feature selection is done using whale optimization (WHO), and the fully connected (FC) layer then employs the pertinent characteristics to categorize the LD like pneumonia, COVID-19, lung cancer (LC), normal, and tuberculosis (TB). The Grad-CAM model is employed to produce heatmaps, emphasizing critical areas in medical images that are essential for precise virus detection. The classification accuracy of 98.86% for the proposed MobileNet is highly reliable for publicly available datasets. The LIC-UMNET model achieves an overall accuracy of 2.40%, 0.84%, and 1.04% compared to the existing method such as VGG19+CNN, MCSVM, and VGG19.

title: DocMind: Conversational PDF QA with RAG
authors: B J Rakshitha, Renil Dharuni, Likhitha M, S Lakshmi, V Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Traditional keyword searches offer no understa–nding of the semantics of the documents being searched, and Large Language Models (LLMs) are prone to hallucinations, making the extraction of relevant information from large PDF documents problematic. In this paper, we present DocMind, which is a framework for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for conversational question answering on PDF documents. The system combines semantic document parsing with token-based chunking, sentence-transformer embeddings, and HNSW-indexed vector retrieval. To find the relevant context for a document chunk, a user query is embedded in the same vector space, and the document chunk is provided to an LLM for generating a cohesive answer. DocMind, unlike standard RAG systems, has multi-turn conversational memory and a transparency feature that indicates when an answer is not justified by the document the user uploaded. Our experiments show DocMind outperforms BM25 and TF-IDF by a large margin in the BERT F1 score, ROUGE-L, and the faithfulness metric. DocMind, by semantic retrieval, combines controlled generation to enhance information accessibility and reduce hallucinations while providing reliable, transparent, and domain-agnostic question answering.

title: Exploring Quantum Computing Algorithms for Effective Task Scheduling in Computing Systems
authors: Prathamesh Vijay Lahande, Akshay Lokare, Indrajeet Kedari
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Task scheduling is a critical problem within the modern underlying computing infrastructure, where resource overload causes a direct impact on the cost and time performance metrics, leading to increased latency, excessive financial cost, and overall poor throughput. Traditional scheduling algorithms are normally fixed and lack the strong exploration and exploitation processes, hence limiting their effectiveness when faced with large problems in scheduling, which are NP-hard. The current work investigates Quantum Computing (QC)-algorithms including Grover, the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE) and Quantum Annealing (QA). This research work includes an extensive experiment conducted in the WorkflowSim simulator with heterogeneous task-scheduling performed with diverse Virtual Machine (VM) configurations. The performance-metrics considered for their comparison include cost and time, along with Relative Performance Index (RPI). The experimental results indicate that QC-algorithm QA achieves higher cost-efficiency, with an average cost reduction of 4 % compared to Grover and 25% compared to VQE, with a corresponding average reduction in time of about 14 % compared to Grover. VQE provides competitive behavior with particular VM configurations but provides poor stability behavior with a wider range of parameters. Statistical evaluations using five regression provides the Grover algorithm to have a superior predictive stability and stronger goodness-of-fit concerning cost and time compared to QA and VQE. Future research will be directed towards the design of hybrid quantum-classical scheduling systems and inclusion of additional performance indicators (including energy efficiency and throughput) and testing the mentioned systems on real quantum equipment to evaluate scalability and real-world feasibility in computational environments.

title: Scheduling Training-Inference Co-Location in Demand Response for Sustainable Edge AI_supp1-3686889.pdf
authors: Lei Jiao
abstract: In the pursuit of data privacy and reduced latency, the adoption of edge intelligence has surged. Meanwhile, the enormous increase in AI has resulted in significant energy consumption. Edge intelligence plays a crucial role in Energy Demand Response (EDR). However, existing edge intelligence falls short of meeting the demands of co-locating training and inference tasks while satisfying EDR. Specifically, the intertwinement between balancing energy consumption, system delay and model accuracy, and uncertain future inputs adds to the challenge of designing an online sustainable system for co-located training and inference tasks. To address these challenges, we propose a novel two-timescale system for co-locating training and inference EDR. Our approach satisfies EDR by strategically planning training schedules on macro-timescales and migrating inference requests between heterogeneous edges on micro-timescales while minimizing long-term cost. We introduce a novel online polynomial time algorithm that first breaks down the problem into two subproblems, which are subsequently solved using an online-learning-based fractional algorithm and a randomized roun ding algorithm, respectively. Rigorous analysis demonstrates that our approach achieves both sublinear dynamic regret and sublinear dynamic fit. Extensive trace-driven evaluations validate the practical superiority of our approach over multiple existing methods, highlighting its effectiveness in real-world scenarios.

title: Dynamic Semantic Graph-guided Cross-modal Attention Network for Open-Vocabulary Object Detection in Aerial Images
authors: Lingjun Li, Xinxin Zhang, Xiaoxu Feng, Yanbu Guo, Shigang Liu, Yali Peng, Xiwen Yao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Open-vocabulary object detection (OVD) in aerial images is critical for intelligent perception of multi-category objects in complex scenarios, yet still faces two key challenges: 1) the prevalence of long-tailed categories in aerial images domains leads to insufficient visual-semantic correlation modeling for novel classes due to annotation scarcity, and 2) complex backgrounds induce feature confusion and degrade the robustness of cross-modal semantic alignment. To address these issues, we propose a Dynamic Semantic Graph-guided Cross-modal Attention Network (DynaGraph-CrossAtt) for aerial open-vocabulary detection. The overall architecture of DynaGraph-CrossAtt is collaboratively constituted by two core components, i.e., a dynamic semantic-aware graph convolutional module (DSG) and a bidirectional cross-modal attention mechanism (BiCMA). Specifically, DSG constructs text semantic graphs to propagate fine-grained semantic relationships (e.g., part interactions, spatial dependencies) and dynamically updates graph node representations, thereby enhancing the contextual awareness and discriminability of text embedding, effectively alleviating the representation deficiency of novel classes. Subsequently, BiCMA receives the enhanced text semantic embedding output by DSG and performs cross-modal interaction with vision features. In BiCMA, the vision-to-text branch employs dynamic weight allocation to emphasize critical semantic nodes, while the text-to-vision branch leverages semantic graphs to guide vision feature focusing on object regions, suppressing background noise, forming a dynamic soft-alignment paradigm of “semantic-guided localization and vision feedback optimization” for cross-modal features. Experiments on aerial benchmarks demonstrate that DynaGraph-CrossAtt significantly improves OVD performance in complex aerial images scenarios.

title: Half Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)

title:
CRISP -
                    <u>C</u>
                    ompliant
                    <u>R</u>
                    OS2 Controllers for Learn
                    <u>i</u>
                    ng-Ba

                    ed Manipulation
                    <u>P</u>
                    olicies and Teleoperation
authors: Daniel San José Pro, Oliver Hausdörfer, Ralf Römer, Maximilian Dösch, Martin Schuck, Angela P. Schoellig
abstract: Learning-based controllers, such as diffusion policies and foundation models output low-frequency and discontinuous target states. Tracking such references smoothly requires a low-level controller to handle such high-level commands and convert them to joint torques, ideally in a compliant way to enable contact-richt interactions. To this end, we present <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">CRISP</small>, a lightweight C++ implementation of compliant Cartesian and joint-space controllers for the ROS2 control standard designed for seamless integration with learning-based policies and teleoperation. Through our CRISP_PY Python and CRISP_GYM Gymnasium interfaces, we provide a uniform way to interact with the controllers intended for data collection through teleoperation, policy training, and subsequent policy deployment. The controllers are compatible with any manipulator exposing a joint effort interface and have been validated on numerous platforms, including Franka Robotics FR3, Universal Robot UR5 and Duatic. Designed for rapid integration, flexible deployment, and real-time performance, our implementation provides a unified pipeline for data collection and policy execution, lowering the barrier to applying learning-based methods on ROS2-compatible manipulators. Details and extensive documentation are available at the <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://utiasDSL.github.io/crisp_controllers</uri> project website.

title: A Semi-Supervised Framework for Road Condition Assessment: From Minor Surface Distress to Post-Disaster Failures
authors: Eda Tsujimoto, Sunyong Eom, Tsutomu Suzuki
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Accurate road-condition segmentation is crucial for intelligent transportation systems (ITS), enabling autonomous driving and post-disaster recovery. Yet, most prior studies focus on binary or low-class segmentation, limiting applicability to complex scenes where subtle surface degradations and large-scale obstructions coexist. To address this gap, we present a unified framework for pixel-level segmentation across 11 road-condition classes using a hybrid dataset integrating the Post-Disaster Road Dataset Japan (PDRDD-J), the Social Media Image Dataset for Disaster Road Damage Object Detection (SoDR), and the Road Damage Dataset 2022 (RDD2022-J). The defined classes include background, road, manhole cover, alligator crack, linear crack, pothole, vehicle, natural blockage, structural blockage, sinkhole, and collapsed road. To learn this label space under limited supervision, we propose F-UNet, a Feature-Level Data Extractor (FLDE)-guided extension of U-Net integrated into a standard teacher–student self-training protocol and trained through progressive pairwise specialization. Unlike conventional U-Net variants that rely solely on global multi-class training, F-UNet derives FLDE guidance through pairwise road–damage patch training and injects it during decoding to better handle class imbalance and thin, low-contrast defects. Extensive experiments demonstrate consistent gains over strong baselines, supported by high-confidence class-wise analysis, confusion-matrix analysis, and error and uncertainty-based failure-mode characterization under disaster edge cases. On a held-out test set, the teacher model achieves 0.8652 mIoU, representing a 15.5% improvement over standard U-Net and outperforming U-Net++, DeepLabV3+, SegNet, and FCN under the same supervision setting. In the semi-supervised configuration, the student model further improves to 0.8727 mIoU. The F-UNet source code is available at https://github.com/EdaTsujimoto/F-UNET.

title: Multi-Criteria Collaborative GNSS Integrity Monitoring: Optimization, Spoofing/Jamming Detection, and Performance Evaluation
authors: Victor Vince, Dominique Heurguier, Alexandre Vervisch Picois, Jose Manuel Rubio Hernan
publish: IEEE Journal of Indoor and Seamless Positioning and Navigation
abstract: This study evaluates the performance of a collaborative, multi-criteria integrity control method in the presence of GNSS (Global Navigation Satellite System) jamming and spoofing. By simultaneously leveraging several sources of information, it enables the determination of whether the position of the receiver has been compromised by anattack. In particular, it combines PVT (Position, Velocity, Time) residuals with the carrier-to-noise density ratio (C/N0) to ensure a robust assessment of integrity across all available data. Its main strength lies in collaboration between receivers through comparison and information sharing. This approach is purely software-based, requiring no additional hardware, and is scalable to receiver populations of varying sizes, from small to large. This study uses real jamming data and simulated spoofing data. In both cases, the data used for PVT calculation, originating from different satellites, are real. The presented results based on data combining simulation and real data demonstrate that the method is promising.

title: Adaptive Resource Allocation for eMBB and URLLC Under Dynamic Backhaul Traffic: Puncturing or Slicing?
authors: Wanlu Zhang, Jingjing Luo, Fu-Chun Zheng, Lin Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: To satisfy the heterogeneous QoS requirements of eMBB and URLLC, network slicing and puncturing have emerged as two most widely-used resource allocation methods. However, under dynamic backhaul traffic (e.g., bursty eMBB and URLLC traffic), relying solely on either slicing or puncturing may cause performance degradation. This paper investigates adaptive resource allocation in integrated access and backhaul (IAB) networks to tackle the performance degradation resulting from eMBBandURLLCtraffic bursts in the backhaul. To release more backhaul communications resources, we first leverage edge caching to serve eMBB traffic at the edge as much as possible. Based on the caching decisions, we then allocate the backhaul resources according to the URLLC arrival patterns. Specifically, for sporadic URLLC arrivals, a greedy puncturing algorithm is proposed to minimize the delay. For bursty URLLC arrivals, we propose a hybrid algorithm that switches between puncturing and slicing methods to minimize the delay. To determine the timing for switching, we employ a deep reinforcement learning framework to learn the switching probability based on backhaul traffic states. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of our proposed algorithms in reducing URLLC delay while guaranteeing eMBB throughput. In particular, the proposed HPS algorithm achieves lower delay than other algorithms, especially under intensive URLLC traffic bursts.

title: Hybrid Classical–Post-Quantum Migration Framework for Brownfield SCADA Environments
authors: Ankit Gupta, Shilpi Mittal
abstract: Brownfield supervisory control and data acquisition environments face a migration problem that is materially different from enterprise information technology. Post-quantum cryptography must be introduced into systems that privilege safety, deterministic communications, long asset lifetimes, staged outages, vendor-certified configurations, and protocol heterogeneity. At the same time, the public standards landscape changed rapidly between 2023 and March 2026, including finalization of the NIST ML-KEM, ML-DSA, and SLH-DSA standards, publication of NIST guidance for key-encapsulation mechanisms, growth of national migration roadmaps, and active standardization of hybrid and pure post-quantum handshakes for Transport Layer Security and Internet Key Exchange. This paper develops a framework for hybrid classical-post-quantum migration in brownfield SCADA settings that is evidence-based, implementation-oriented, and explicitly staged. We distinguish coexistence from true cryptographic hybridization, quantify control-plane overhead using official algorithm sizes and current protocol drafts, and propose a conduit-first migration sequence that prioritizes gateways, remote access, management planes, trust anchors, and software-signing services before intrusive field-device replacement. The framework combines a scoring model for urgency and retrofit friction, protocol-specific migration patterns, and a worked brownfield roadmap with phase gates, rollback criteria, and procurement controls. The result is a practical path for operators who must begin quantum-safe preparation now, even where endpoint-native post-quantum support remains uneven.

title: A Grid Connected PV Inverter with Subarray Level Control in MPP and non-MPP Mode
authors: Ayush Chauhan, Hiruteamlak G. Kidanemaria, Arpit Tiwari, Subhendu Dutta
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a new power circuit topology and a dual-mode control strategy of a single-phase grid-connected solar PV inverter. This inverter is capable of seamlessly switching from MPP mode to non-MPP mode for independent regulation of active and reactive power of two series-connected PV subarrays. The inverter ensures that each of the two PV subarrays operates at their respective MPP points during MPP mode, enabling it to yield higher power from the PV array, especially under varying and mismatched atmospheric conditions. Apart from this, the inverter can also inject or absorb reactive power within its kVA limit. In non-MPP mode, the operating points of the subarrays are set by external active and reactive power references. The ability to control active power injection gives the inverter a flexibility to maximize its reactive power support (injection or absorption) without exceeding its total kVA rating. This seamless transition between MPP and non-MPP modes enhances operational flexibility, broadens the stability of the grid, and improves overall reliability of the power system. The description of the power circuit, operating principle, and control strategy of the proposed inverter are explained. A comprehensive simulation study has been carried out with a 2.5 kVA MATLAB-Simulink model of the proposed inverter to show its effectiveness.

title: Comprehensive Study Paper on the Role of Additive Manufacturing in Production of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor
authors: Asma Raza, Ujjwal Kumar Kalla
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Additive Manufacturing (AM) is revolutionizing the production of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs) by offering exceptional design flexibility, material efficiency, and rapid prototyping capabilities. This paper reviews key AM technologies, including Selective Laser Sintering (SLS), Selective Laser Melting (SLM), Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM), and Electron Beam Melting (EBM), and various other technologies highlighting their applications in PMSM manufacturing. These technologies enable the creation of complex geometries, lightweight structures, and optimized cooling systems that enhance motor performance and reduce material waste. Case studies, such as the Fraunhofer Institute's development of 3D-printed stators for electric vehicle (EV) motors and CRP Technology's customized rotors for drones, illustrate the practical applications and performance improvements achieved through AM. Despite its advantages, challenges like material limitations and precision issues remain. However, with ongoing advancements in materials, multi-material printing, and digital twin integration, AM holds great promise for the future of PMSM production, offering the potential for more efficient, custoomized, and sustainable motors for a wide range of applications.

title: Optimal Charge Scheduling for Economic Operation of Electric Vehicle Charging Station
authors: Samarendra Pratap Singh, Bishwajit Dey, Neeraj Kanwar, Srikant Misra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The load demand in the distribution system has been on the rise as a result of the deployment of electric vehicles (EVs) in the automobile sector in recent days. The charging management must be enhanced in order to address this burden demand. However, it requires several hours to fully charge an electric vehicle (EV). The promotion of electric vehicles (EVs) over conventional vehicles is a significant competition that involves the reduction of energy consumption and charging time. The energy consumption of the EV may be influenced by the condition of the itineraries, which must be considered prior to satisfying the energy demand. This work aims to minimize the total operating cost (TOC) for the owner of an electric vehicle charging station (EVCS) owner also termed as EV aggregator by optimally scheduling the charging capabilities of up to 200 EVs based on their cost-effective grid to vehicle (G2V) and vehicle to grid (V2G) operation. TOC includes the V2G cost, G2V cost and battery degradation cost (BDC) of the EVs. Five different types of EV are used for the study and four case studies solved using CONOPT solver of GAMS environment analyses the best charging schedule with minimum TOC.

title: Twin-Delayed DDPG-Tuned PIDF Controller for Load Frequency Control in Power Grid with Distributed Energy Resources
authors: Tumuluri Datta Venkata Sai, Sukriti Tiwari, Devesh Shukla
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative study of metaheuristic, deep reinforcement learning, and proposed hybrid approaches for Load Frequency Control in a multirenewable integrated two-area power system. A traditional Proportional Integral Derivative controller with a Filter is first implemented to tune the controller gains, along with metaheuristic algorithms such as Particle Swarm Optimization and Genetic Algorithm optimization. Particle Swarm Optimization and Genetic Algorithm optimization exhibited limitations in their ability to minimize steady-state error and achieve a shorter settling time. To address this, Deep Reinforcement Learning algorithms, Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient, and Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient, were integrated into the control loop. The controller using the TD3 algorithm delivered the most effective dynamic performance, providing quicker frequency recovery, minimizing tie-line variations, and enhancing resilience to load changes.

title: Coupled-Inductor Based Non-Isolated High Gain DC-DC Converter
authors: Raj Moyal, Jahangeer A. Dar, M.k. Pathak
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This research introduces a new non-isolated DC-DC converter topology that combines a coupled active-switched inductor network with a passive switched-capacitor configuration. The proposed configuration offers multiple benefits, such as an increased voltage gain, reduced voltage stress and current stress across the switching devices, and a lower component count when compared to similar high-voltage performance topologies. A comprehensive theoretical analysis is provided, accompanied by a performance comparison with existing designs. The converter's operation is comprehensively analyzed under both continuous conduction mode (CCM) and discontinuous conduction mode (DCM) conditions. Analytical findings are supported by simulation results acquired using PLECS software.

title: Enhanced Automation Using FPGA and IoT for Multi-Environment Applications
authors: Parvathy Harikumar, Harshavardhan Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This work presents the design and implementation of a hybrid home and industrial automation system using FPGA board and NodeMCU. The system features the LM35 for temperature monitoring and the LM393 for sensing ambient light. The system includes actuators including a DC motor to mimic a fan and three LEDs representing tube light, bulb and air conditioner. For industrial setting, a servo motor demonstrates to mimic the movement of robotic arm by rotating from 0° to 90°. The system prototype is demonstrated using Xilinx Vivado for FPGA implementation, along with Arduino IDE and Blynk IoT for NodeMCU-based control and monitoring. This implementation demonstrates the adaptability and parallel capability of FPGAbased automation in a variety of applications, including those in home and industrial sector.

title: Comparative Analysis of Phase Shift Modulation Strategies for Dual Active Bridge Converters
authors: T. Dheepaswarupan, R. Nakkeeran, C. Bharatiraja
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: World moves towards the path of electric vehicles, we have a drastically higher usage of electric vehicles, which has opened a new problem called higher electricity usage. Dual Active Bridge (DAB) theatres a pivotal part is a solution for this problem. The DAB is nothing but a converter it seen as one that can attain a bidirectional power supply. It is very popular among researchers these days. It's popular because of its capability of attaining Zero switching voltage (ZVS), Low switching losses, and also an easy-to-understand circuit design. Even though it is easily understandable, the DAB converter features a wide spectrum of topologies, which arises a challenge. There is a need for a wide variety of control and modulation strategies which is been customized for specific applications. Various methods of modulation techniques, but in this paper, we are focusing on the phase shift modulation techniques, which include Single phase shift control (SPS), Extended phase shift control (EPS), Dual phase shift control (DPS), and Triple Phase Shift control (TPS). This work, a Conventional DAB converter is been modelled using PLECS software, where a numerous amount of simulations has been run for each of the advanced techniques listed above, and the resulting performance metrics like voltage, current, switching losses, conduction losses, efficiencies, and more importantly temperature of all 8 power switches which have been used are been compared and evaluated to list their effectiveness.

title: Real-Time Optimization of Partially Shaded Solar Photovoltaic Modules Using Parameter-Optimized Global Maximum Power Point Tracking
authors: N S Ramanathan, Devangi, Naran M. Pindoriya, Pallavi Bharadwaj
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Solar photovoltaic (PV) emerges as a promising energy source to reduce carbon emissions. However, the performance of solar panels deteriorates during partial shading conditions. Therefore, tracking global maximum power point to maximize the power output of PV systems amidst changing environment conditions is crucial. This work proposes an advanced parameter-optimized global maximum power point tracking (PGMPPT) algorithm, with stable operation under varying ambient conditions. It follows the underlying principles of bio-inspired algorithms to track the global maxima. The parameter optimization involved in the proposed method paves the way to optimally track the global maximum power point with low processing time, high speed, low CPU utilization factor (CUF) and low computational memory requirement (CMR). The proposed optimization enables faster convergence with a speed improvement of 62.5-70%. It is implemented and validated in real-time using OPAL-RT simulator using existing cuckoo search optimization (CSO) as a baseline, yielding a high tracking speed of 440μs over other bio-inspired algorithms for the given conditions in the study.

title: Economic Operation of an Islanded Microgrid Using PSO-Tuned Droop-Controlled Distributed Generations Alongside Solar and Wind Generations
authors: Sk Mahammad Sorif, Supriya Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The operation of an islanded microgrid is critical to maintain the system frequency and node voltages within acceptable limits around the clock as the load and generation change throughout the day.
