The generations from solar PV and wind generators are not even available for a long time of the day, and vary significantly during their availability. So, droop settings of droop-controlled distributed generations are controlled precisely in the presence of solar PV and wind generators to balance the generation and load. The active and reactive powers of droop-controlled distributed generations are optimized for each hour of a day by PSO algorithm based on the economic dispatch of power from the generators while satisfying the system frequency, node voltages, and power constraints. The effectiveness of the methodology is tested through a 33-node radial islanded network.

title: Fault-Tolerant Radio Communication Framework for TCAS: Design, Implementation and Validation
authors: Raj Singh, Sandeep Kumar, Sanjay Motia, Manpreet Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Reliable and fail-safe radio communication forms the backbone of safety-critical railway operations, especially for system like the Train Collision Avoidance System (TCAS). However, achieving resilience in radio networks demands solutions to challenges such as wireless instability, real-time failover, secure session handling, and redundancy management. This paper presents a robust Fault-Tolerant UHF Radio Communication Framework that ensures uninterrupted train-to-trackside communication using full-duplex UHF radios, hot-standby dualmodem architecture, and application-layer packet switching. GPS-synchronized TDMA scheduling provides time determinism but serves as a supporting layer to the core radio infrastructure. The framework is validated through diverse failure simulations, demonstrating seamless switchover among redundant radios and time sources (GPS, RTC, CPU). It fulfils TCAS safety specifications and lays a scalable foundation for integrating emerging technologies like 5G and software-defined radios in future railway communication systems. The framework architecture also accommodates cross-layer optimizations to reduce handover delays and packet retransmissions. Its modular and interoperable design ensures backward compatibility with legacy TCAS deployments while being adaptable for future digital signalling paradigms.

title: Real-Time Vital Sign Monitoring with Dashboard Integration
authors: Harinath Aireddy, Sudhir R, Marriwada Harshavardhan, Nanditha D N, Javvaji Pavan Kumar, Namitha Papishetty, Sheshadri K, Dappili Vijay Kumar Reddy, Shravani N
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The development of real-time vital sign monitoring systems has become a key advancement in healthcare, focusing on the continuous tracking of health metrics such as SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, heart rate, blood pressure, and body temperature. The system employs advanced sensors integrated with a microcontroller to continuously collect data, which is then wirelessly transmitted to a central dashboard for real-time visualization. The dashboard provides a user-friendly interface to monitor health trends and detect any irregularities, enhancing the efficiency of healthcare delivery. With low power consumption and high accuracy, this system can improve patient outcomes, reducing the reliance on manual processes, and enhancing the accessibility of healthcare services.

title: From Point Clouds to Digital Narratives: A Generative AI Framework for Immersive Media Reconstruction and Cultural Storytelling of Classical Garden Heritage
authors: Shawei Zhang, Xuefei Li, Ran Guo, Ting Yang
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 2nd International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Digital Media
abstract: None

title: Clash of the Titans: A User Experience Comparison of Amazon, Apple, and Google Smart Home Technology
authors: David Wright, Sarah E. Hercula, Daniel B. Shank, Adam Rentz, Julianna Farr
abstract: None

title: Evaluation of an AI-Based Semantic Chatbot for Quran and Hadith Retrieval Using Expert Validation and NLP Performance Metrics
authors: Dian Pratiwi Wahyuddin, Amil Ahmad Ilham, Yuspiani
abstract: This study presents the design and evaluation of an artificial intelligence (AI)-based semantic chatbot developed to retrieve Quranic verses and Hadith references in response to thematic user queries. The system integrates a natural language processing (NLP) pipeline to interpret user intent and match it with a curated corpus of authenticated Islamic sources using a hybrid semantic retrieval approach based on TF-IDF and BERT embeddings. Evaluation was conducted through two complementary approaches: expert validation and technical performance measurement. Expert validation involved two domain specialists in Al-Qur'an and Hadith sciences who rated 30 thematic queries using a 4-point Likert scale across three criteria: authenticity, thematic relevance, and contextual accuracy. The technical evaluation used standard NLP metrics, including Precision, Recall, and F1-Score. The dataset consisted of 6,236 Quranic verses and 14,838 Hadiths from Sahih Bukhari and Sahih Muslim in Bahasa Indonesia. Results demonstrated a mean Precision of 0.78, Recall of 0.98, and F1-Score of 0.84, indicating the chatbot's high accuracy and retrieval comprehensiveness. Expert assessments further confirmed strong reliability, with an average validation score exceeding 85% across all criteria. These findings highlight the potential of semantic AI systems in supporting digital Islamic learning environments and automated religious information services. Future work will extend semantic understanding to multilingual queries and incorporate user-centered evaluation and broader tafsir integration to enhance contextual reasoning.

title: Orientation-Invariant Step Length Estimation for Arbitrary Indoor Trajectories
authors: Saptadipa Mazumder, Pampa Sadhukhan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: In this study we have implemented a new smartphone-based Orientation-Invariant Step Length Estimation (OI-SLE) method. OI-SLE is achieved through the development of three features: —orientation compensation using quaternion, slope-adaptive stride length estimation, and turn aware compensation. By converting sensor readings into the Earth Coordinate System and automatically adjusting for changes in direction and surface slope, the method performs well on straight paths, curves, stairs, and inclined surfaces. Tests in different walking conditions show that OI-SLE improves the accuracy of step length estimation by about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10-15 \%$</tex> compared to basic linear models, proving it works effectively for all kinds of indoor routes.

title: Optical emission spectroscopy (OES) for leak detection and impurity analysis for lab grown diamond (LGD) application
authors: Vikas Sharma, Priyadarshini Ghosh
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Diamond Industry based on the MPCVD (Microwave Plasma Chemical Vapor Deposition), HORIBA introduce an important tool EV 2.0 for Plasma & Chamber health monitoring and Advance process control. EV 2.0 spectroscopy provides the Recipe optimization in diamond growth process. This platform allows satisfying all the needs of in-situ plasma chamber health monitoring, fault analysis diamond growth process in the presence of different working gases in the chamber.

title: OpenCowID: Zero-Shot Visual Identification of Dairy Cows
authors: Omkar Prabhune, Younghyun Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurate identification of individual cows is essential to precision dairy farming. While computer vision offers a non-invasive alternative to ear tags and RFID systems, its practical deployment remains limited by the need for zero-shot identification in dynamic herds where test identities are unseen during training. In this work, we propose OpenCowID, a unified framework that addresses this challenge. First, we introduce a stochastic cow coat synthesis pipeline that efficiently generates large-scale, diverse images. Second, using the generated large-scale high-quality data, we present a centroid-guided feature learning strategy that forms a well-structured embedding space using virtual class centroids, enabling generalization to unseen identities. OpenCowID achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot and open-set identification on real-world cow benchmarks, without requiring any real labeled training data. This work contributes to the advancement of automated livestock monitoring, enabling robust, non-invasive identification. Code is available at https://github.com/neis-lab/OpenCowID.

title: The Cognitive Impact of AI Chatbots Dependency Among Students
authors: William Chandra, Andy Effendi
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: The usage of artificial intelligence (AI) chatbots in education has changed the way students access information and complete their academic tasks. While AI Chatbots offer efficiency and convenience in helping students perform various task, students may have not realized that they are slowly getting dependent on AI Chatbots and that dependency can affect their cognitive abilities. This research aims to examine the effects of AI Chatbots dependency on student's cognitive engagement and critical thinking. The significance of this study lies in its contribution to understanding how reliance on AI assistance may influence student's cognitive abilities. This research uses a quantitative approach by using a survey method. Research data were collected from 150 students across various educational levels who are currently enrolled in various learning institutions. The questionnaire were distributed online and designed to measure students levels of AI Chatbots dependency, cognitive engagement and critical thinking ability. The collected research data were analyzed using statistical techniques to identify the general patterns and the relationships between the variables. This research's results indicate that AI Chatbots dependency has a visible influence on student's cognitive engagement and their critical thinking abilities. Overall, higher levels of AI dependency is associated with reduced cognitive abilities, which suggests the need for more guided use of AI Chatbots in various learning institutions.

title: Honeypots to Spot Personal Account Credential Stuffing Attacks
authors: Trilochan Patra, Swarup Kumar Mitra, Ujjal Bhattacharya, Shampurna Rakshit, Alakesh Hazra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: The rate of credential-stuffing attacks is rising exponentially, as cybercriminals look for ways to break into accounts on different sites using stolen login credentials and passwords. Despite much research into safeguarding large enterprises from such attacks, there are few techniques focused on protecting individual accounts. This paper describes the development and use of honeypots for detecting credential stuffing attacks against personal web accounts (email, social media). This “The Spawn” Honeypots are used to make phoney accounts that act like real users to lure attackers into using stolen credentials. This research analyzes how well these honeypots identify and derive credential-stuffing techniques and focuses on the ease at which non-technical users can perform these attacks. Providing users with an accessible and affordable way to proactively detect and combat the threat of credential-based attacks. This paper aims to bring user personal cyber security closer to realization. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of the use of honeypots as an operational countermeasure against growing cyber-threats directed towards individual online identities. The paper addresses a gap in personal cyber security by presenting a widely available and cost effective way for an individual to detect and mitigate credential-based risks.

title: Enhancing Privacy-Preserving Healthcare Analytics via Quantum-Inspired Federated Optimization
authors: Mithaguru, Rakshita R, Joshuva Arockia Dhanaraj, Vegi Fernando A, Mude Nagarjuna Naik, Lakshmanan M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) allows a joint model training between decentralized data sources and does not involve the exchange of sensitive records, which is appealing in the context of health care. Nevertheless, the widely applied Federated Averaging (FedAvg) algorithm cannot perform well in the non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data, resulting in slow convergence and poor performance. In order to overcome this limitation, this paper examines a quantum-inspired aggregation strategy in which Quantum-Behaved Particle Swarm Optimization (QPSO) is considered. On an artificial PubMed-style text classification dataset split into four clients with Dirichlet non-IID splits, we compare FedAvg and QPSO after three communication rounds. The experimental findings indicate that QPSO has much better validation accuracy (0.978 vs. 0.618) and final test accuracy (0.956 vs. 0.624) and faster convergence. These findings show that the QPSO-based aggregation can add value to global model in federated healthcare analytics with heterogeneous data distributions.

title: Ai-Integrated Cyber Security Model for Intelligent Intrusion and Anomaly Detection in Next-Generation Communication Systems
authors: Kammari Srujana, Nemalipalli Madhu Sudhan, Sandrapati Vinodh Kumar, Yaganti Ravi Teja
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The next generation communication systems are becoming very susceptible to advanced cyber threats because of high-speed, heterogeneous, large scale. The conventional intrusion detection systems which are rule based are inadequate in relation to the zero-day attacks and evolving attacks. This paper suggests an AI-based Cyber Security Model that is a combination of deep traffic embedding, semantic log analysis, graph-based structural learning, supervised intrusion classification, and unsupervised anomaly detection in an ensemble decision fusion structure. The discussed hybrid architecture relies on the multimodal feature representation to provide greater detection robustness and flexibility. The experimental analysis based on CICIDS2017, UNSW-NB15, and simulated next-generation traffic proves to be better than single stand-alone SVM, DNN, and clustering-based approaches. The model had an accuracy of 98.5%, precision of 98.2% and recall of 98.7% and a false positive of about 0.1. The analysis of confusion matrix showed that there were only 15 false positives and 12 false negatives, which shows that classification is rather stable. The ROC and Precision-Recall tests prove that the discriminative capacity is strong at the imbalanced and bursty traffic scenarios. The findings affirm that a combination of supervised, unsupervised and semantic intelligence is much better in enhancing detection accuracy, strength and scalability of next-generation communication infrastructures.

title: Armos: A Proxy Based Security Architecture for Mitigating Unauthorized LLM Access to Web Applications
authors: Gundu Bhavana, Dhanyasree Thallapalli, S. Saravanan
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Large Language Models as Web scrapers cause a massive threat to the news and the media houses by even permitting the unauthorized extraction, reinterpretation, and even use of proprietary materials. Unlike traditional bots, these are sophisticated scrapers which use semantic reasoning, browser computerization, and simulation of human navigation behaviour, in this way beating conventional anti-bot defenses and augmenting the reputational risk because of hallucinated or falsely attributed content. This is a proxy based multi-layered proxy called Armos. middleware model which integrates active HTTP header. fingerprinting, behavioral monitor-TLS/JA3 anomaly detection. ing, honeypot-based deception, and machine learning-based deception. driven classification. Working as a mediating inspection. layer, Armos will allow detecting in real time and counter-adaptively. termeasures on malicious traffic by blocking, throttling, or premeditated fraudulent reaction. We perform an experimental assessment of its efficacy: alone, the use of static fingerprinting produced Accuracy on known bot and LLM client signatures is 97.8 percent and the behavioral and honeypot systems detected 91 percent of the. activities of adaptive LLM scrapers out of their inelastic and low-variance. timing patterns, such as 0.9 sec mean request interviewe. compared to 7.8 sec by humans. The final XGBoost incumbent. generated 96.5 % validation accuracy whose characteristics in Header. Assumptions of entropy, JA3 Mismatch and Behavioral Anomaly Score. greater than 70 percent of predictive value. These findings verify that Armos should provide a stalwart defense and at the same time retain fair authentication of authorized access, and its intrinsic feedback of learning loop will also ensure flexibility to varying scraping techniques and improve the safety of the digital journalism content within the times of generative AI.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning approach and Statistical Ensemble Model for Spatiotemporal Crop Yield Prediction
authors: N Satish, K Akash, A Kishor, M Kumaravel, Gokulraj, K Sabari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Prediction of crop yield is necessary to ensure food security, timely and accurate strategic agricultural planning and management of resources. The proposed research presents a new hybrid modeling system, which is Statistical Ensemble Stacked-Hybrid Machine Learning System (SES-HMS) and the aim of this paper is to predict crop yield using spatiotemporal environmental, meteorological, and geospatial data. It combines satellite-derived NDVI, soil parameters, rainfall, temperature, and past yield statistics via uncommon processing methods including Spatiotemporal Kriging, Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD), and Symbolic Aggregate approximator (SAX) to preprocess and upscale the data respectively. The ensemble structure is consisting of Bayesian Hierarchical Yield Modeling with sophisticated machine learning architectures such as Gradient Boosting, LSTM and Gaussian Process Regression. Quantile Regression Averaging is used to refine and conformal prediction is used to calibrate yield prediction to assure uncertainty estimation. Ranging experiments carried out in five different agro-ecological zones also indicate that SES-HMS is significantly more effective at prediction with a mean accuracy of 94.6 and lowering of RMSE by more than 18 % relative to conventional methods. The suggested approach guarantees the spatiotemporal compatibility of the predictions and demonstrates a high level of generalizability to various climatic scenarios. These findings confirm the possibility of using SES-HMS as a powerful, scalable and interpretable tool in data-scarce settings to determine precision agriculture and agricultural policy-making.

title: Unlocking Prospects of Digital Twin in Healthcare
authors: Nayanjyoti Deka, Anjana Raut, Swati Samantaray, Purnendu Bhushan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: This article discusses the application of artificial intelligence and digital twin technology with a focus on its use in healthcare and individualized treatment planning. Deep AI methods like convolutional neural networks and hybrid mathematical models facilitate dynamic, predictive virtual patient modeling, allowing anatomical segmentation, landmark detection, and treatment simulation with improved accuracy and efficiency. Substantial advancements are observed in various domains of medical science with multimodal imaging and real-time data integration. The work also assesses new ideas like blockchain-secured data management and the metaverse, with implications for data security and operational efficiency. Technical, clinical, and regulatory challenges are critically discussed, with recommendations for standardized protocols, AI applications, and effective privacy measures. The paper intends to explore AI-augmented digital twin platforms synergizing individualized, predictive, and preventive patient management, while shaping their responsible and feasible use in the clinical environment.

title: Evaluating Best Practices of Financial Management in Healthcare Service Sector for Integration with AI
authors: Rashmikanta Tripathy, Susanta Kumar Mishra, Sisir Ranjan Dash, Purwono Purwono, Amit Kumar, Ajitav Acharya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The present research is the conclusive part of a project which started with the overall objective to evaluate best practices of financial management for Indian healthcare service providers. The initial exploratory phase has been carried out as qualitative research in which we have discovered that financial management in Indian healthcare service sector is getting done with the use of artificial intelligence and other advanced technologies. Using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> the finance managers are now able to analyze huge amount of health data and then taking decision that consequently improving patient care, operational efficiency and outcomes. But we have also identified a research problem that the advanced technologies including AI which are getting used for financial management needs to be integrated with the best practices in the industry. So, this study is an attempt to map the best practices of financial management in the healthcare sector and recommend the research community about its findings. The findings of the present study will be unquestionably relevant for researchers, students, faculty members, technology experts and entrepreneurs in specific.

title: Blockchain and Graph Neural Network-Based Peer-to-Peer Energy Trading Mechanism for Smart Grids Integrating Renewable Energy Resources
authors: N. Selvam M. E., Nalin Kumar S P, Sanjaai U N, Sharmitha S P
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The growing adoption of renewable energy sources, including solar and wind in smart grids, creates a chance and also challenges to the field of decentralized energy management. In this project, a new framework of blockchain-based and Graph Neural Networks-based (GNNs) Peer-to-peer (P2P) energy trading is proposed that allows a secure, transparent, and intelligent energy exchange among prosumers. Within this model, producers and consumers of energy deal directly with each other without having to middlemen when exchanging excess energy. Smart contracts are trading processes that are represented by blockchain and can be automated to allow dynamic pricing, buyer-seller matching, and payment settlements that are not subject to modification, auditability, and lower transaction costs. In the meantime, GNNs learn complicated spatial and relational relationships in the energy network, and they are capable of predicting the patterns of renewable energy generation and consumption to guide optimal trade-off strategies. According to simulation findings, the proposed system is able to enhance the efficiency of the grid, minimize the energy waste, and facilitate the adoption of the sustainable energy, which proves that the proposed system could become the scalable solution to the intelligent and secure management of the energy in the smart grids.

title: An Efficient Retinal Vessel Segmentation Through Modified NOA Algorithm based Feature Selection and CrossViT for Feature Extraction
authors: M Soujanya, A Swathi, Imad Shalout, Preeti Khurana, G. Ramkumar, Chitukula Navya Sree
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Early identification of retinal vessel abnormalities is critical to diagnosing vision-related diseases. However, the methods currently in use are encountering difficulties differentiating small vessels and complex vascular patterns, especially when the data is unbalanced. This work, therefore, proposes the Enhanced Retinal Vessel Segmentation and Neuro-Classification (ERVSNC) model, which is based on multi-scale feature extraction, refined feature selection, and transformer-assisted convolutional learning. The main data undergo pre-processing and training through three benchmark datasets, DRIVE, CHASEDB1, and HRF, to guarantee the model’s robustness under varying imaging scenarios. A CrossViT architecture to capture detailed retinal features through a dual-branch attention mechanism. A CrossViT architecture to capture detailed retinal features through a dual-branch attention mechanism and a changed Northern Owl Optimisation (NOA) to get rid of the most irrelevant features, thus making the classifier more efficient, is employed. The simulation analysis is ERVSNC loss of 3.0, F1-Score of 0.98 at 4096 points, fitness value of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> to 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> at 300 iterations, ERVSNC average of 98%, 91%, 88%, and 84%, and accuracy calculations of 99%.

title: Boosting Brand Recognition Within Large Language Models and AI-Driven Search Systems
authors: Uma Uppin, Vibha Uppin
abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive understanding of how brand visibility occurs in AI Large Language Models (LLMs) and Augmented Engine Optimization (AEO). The review paper examines the fundamental differences between static pretrained LLMs and search-augmented systems, establishing strategic frameworks for monitoring, feedback implementation, and optimization strategies to enhance brand visibility in LLMs and search-augmented systems. Through systematic analysis of platform architectures, and optimization methodologies, this study reveals that brand awareness correlates with AI mentions [4]. The findings demonstrate that approximately 9 0% of ChatGPT's citations originate from search results ranking beyond position 21, indicating AI systems access broader content pools than traditional search engines. [1] This research contributes novel insights into the evolution of digital marketing from traditional Search Engine Optimization (SEO) to Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), providing actionable strategies for businesses seeking enhanced visibility in AI-powered search environments.

title: Guardian Sense-AI Powered Home Security
authors: K.Madhavi, M Divyasri, G Akshara, K.Poojitha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Security of homes and properties needs smart means that can detect threat in the right way. The design proposal presents the introduction of a smart human detection alarm system by applying AI camera surveillance and object recognition. It constantly scans the environment and raises an alarm only in case it sees unauthorized or suspicious persons. The system is able to distinguish between the human beings, animals and pets and avoid false alarms due to innocent motions. Also, it incorporates the facial recognition with a database of known members of the family, so that only unknown and unauthorized people are indicated. This is the least false positive, and it is more reliable, and identifies threats accurately. Smart homes, remote locations and sensitive premises are an ideal environment where the proposed solution can be used to provide efficient, accurate and intelligent monitoring.

title: An Optimal Evaluation of Entanglement Depth in Variational Quantum Classifiers for Efficient Deployment of Machine Learning Applications
authors: Aryan Sahni, Krishnesh Mathur, Yashvi Diya Singh, Kowsigan M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Variational Quantum Classifiers (VQCs) are a common class of hybrid quantum-classical models developed for nearterm quantum machine learning applications. Despite growing focus, it is still unclear how circuit design choices, particularly entanglement depth, affect learning performance and optimisation stability when compared to reliable classical baselines [1]. In this research, we provide a controlled empirical analysis of entanglement depth in VQCs for medical image classification utilizing pre-extracted deep feature representations. Under a predetermined experimental design, we assessed VQCs with no entanglement, single-layer entanglement, and two-layer interleaved entanglement with different training set sizes. Our findings demonstrate that in low-data regimes, increasing entanglement could negatively impact accuracy due to optimization instability [2] and may not always enhance performance. Since classical machine learning baselines consistently match or surpass quantum models, there is no performance advantage of variational quantum classifiers was observed under the evaluated experimental conditions and preprocessing constraints. These results emphasize the significance of thorough, comparative benchmarking for real-world use and draw attention to the expressivity optimization trade-offs inherent in variational quantum learning.

title: Sign Language Gesture Recognition via Temporal Attention Networks
authors: S Poonguzhali, N. Sarika, D. Meenakshi, R. Muthulakshmi, G. Elakkiyaselvam, S. Sowmyayani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: A Temporal Attention Network (TANet) is proposed for robust sign language gesture recognition in both isolated and continuous video settings. TANet extracts frame-level spatio-temporal features using a two-stream backbone (appearance + pose), fuses them and applies a learnable attention module that emphasizes frames most discriminative for a given gloss/word. The model is trained end-to-end and evaluated on standard sign language benchmarks (WLASL, RWTH-PHOENIX, ASLLVD). Experimental results show TANet improves recognition accuracy over baselines (ResNet3D+LSTM, TCN, standard Transformer) while requiring comparable compute. The studies demonstrate the contribution of temporal attention, pose stream fusion and multi-scale temporal context. The TANet achieves Top 1 accuracies of 91. 4 %, 85. 2 % and 9 2. 6 % on the respective datasets, outperforming existing CNN-RNN, GCN and Transformer-based methods by 3-4 %. Furthermore, the proposed model operates at nearly real-time speed (~48 fps), making it suitable for assistive communication systems and human-computer interaction.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Cross-Attention for Multi-Modal Image-Text Retrieval
authors: Gnanajeyaraman Rajaram, M Surya Narayanan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Multi-modal systems for vision-language tasks of- ten struggle with semantic ambiguity. Overlapping or context- dependent features in cross-modal representations lead to poor alignment and performance. To tackle this, we introduce Quantum-Enhanced Fusion (QE-Fusion), a new cross-attention framework inspired by quantum interference principles. QE- Fusion works in a complex-valued domain. This allows it to capture both magnitude and phase information in cross-modal attention scores.By representing queries and keys as complex-valued tensors, QE-Fusion simulates both constructive and destructive interference through the magnitude of their complex dot product. This mechanism enables the model to dynamically boost relevant cross-modal correlations while reducing irrelevant ones. As a result, it achieves better feature disambiguation. Unlike standard attention mechanisms, which use real-valued scores, QE-Fusion’s phase-aware computation offers more flexibility for modeling complex feature interactions.The framework is implemented as a modular PyTorch layer, making it easy to integrate into existing multi-modal Transformer architectures. We validated our approach using the Flickr8k image-text retrieval benchmark. While initial training showed some instability, we improved this by adjusting the learning rate. After tuning, the stabilized QE-Fusion model showed clear and consistent performance gains compared to the standard attention baseline. These results position QE-Fusion as a strong and efficient method for strengthening the reasoning abilities of multi-modal systems. They also highlight the potential of quantum-inspired principles in traditional deep learning models.

title: ST-Think: How Multimodal Large Language Models Reason About 4D Worlds from Ego-Centric Videos
authors: Peiran Wu, Yunze Liu, Miao Liu, Junxiao Shen
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Humans excel at spatial-temporal reasoning, effortlessly interpreting dynamic visual events from an egocentric viewpoint. However, whether multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can similarly understand the 4D world remains uncertain. This paper explores multimodal spatial-temporal reasoning from an egocentric perspective, aiming to equip MLLMs with human-like reasoning capabilities. To support this objective, we introduce Ego-ST Bench, a novel benchmark containing over 5,000 question-answer pairs across four categories, systematically evaluating spatial, temporal, and integrated spatial-temporal reasoning. Additionally, we propose ST-R1 training paradigm, a video-based reasoning model that incorporates reverse thinking into its reinforcement learning process, significantly enhancing performance. We combine long-chain-of-thought (long-CoT) supervised fine-tuning with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) reinforcement learning, achieving notable improvements with limited high-quality data. Ego-ST Bench and ST-R1 provide valuable insights and resources for advancing video-based spatial-temporal reasoning research.

title: Optimized AI Models for Multi-Class Malware Detection: Balancing Datasets with O-SMOTE and Detecting Ransomware with 1D-CNN
authors: Harshitha B K, Priya Nandihal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Because of the enormous harm it does to people's finances, reputations, and operations, ransomware has arisen as a major cybersecurity concern in recent years. Criminals online will always find a way to circumvent security measures put in place, and they will use these new platforms to steal vital information from unsuspecting consumers. This might lead to disastrous cyberattacks such as monetary losses, reputational damage, ransomware, or the transmission of contagious software. Ransomware is among the most destructive forms of malware since it encrypts user data and demands money to decrypt its contents. Loss of critical data, interruption of company operations, and substantial financial harm are all possible outcomes of ransomware attacks. The detection of Android ransomware attacks has been greatly improved by AI-based techniques, including machine learning (ML). Nevertheless, there has been a lack of thorough investigation into ensemble models and DL models. To efficiently detect multi-class threats such as adware, ransomware, rootkit, SMS malware, and scareware, this work proposes a multi-stage learning model that uses a 1 dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) and decision tree-based classification. Using the stadium spectator's optimizer (SSO) algorithm to optimally select the sample rate, the optimized synthetic minority oversampling procedure (O-SMOTE) is used to balance the dataset. In order to build cyber systems that are resistant to cyber threats, our symmetrical research in AI and cybersecurity analytics will improve our capacity to identify, analyze, and mitigate malware. Using a dataset that includes recentlyreleased dangerous software, the study proved that our technique is effective and efficient in meeting real-world needs, thus validating the model. Combining behavior-based deep learning with heuristic-based approaches beats static deep learning tactics for malware discovery and classification, according to the experimental data.

title: Zero-Day Detection of AI-Generated Images Using Perturbation-Response Analysis and Zero-Shot CLIP Embeddings
authors: Pooja S, Ezhil Arasi V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The advancement of AI-generated image synthesis models, such as GANs and Diffusion Models, has quickly progressed and has continued to not only blur but eventually remove the lines between real and synthetic images, particularly in cases where the image generator is not known. Existing detection techniques often require retraining, thus lacking generalizability to all attacks and thus are only applicable to non-zero-day attacks. The paper introduces a retrain-free, generator-agnostic detection system that combines perturbation-response analysis with zero-shot CLIP semantic embeddings. The method adds controlled pixel-level and semantic-level perturbations to input images and checks the stability of their CLIP embeddings to discriminate between real and synthetic images. The system utilizes CLIP's strong cross-domain generalization to identify effectively AI-generated images without having been trained on the generator. Large-scale experiments carried out on benchmark datasets such as FFHQ, StyleGAN2, DIRE, PromptBench, and Spectral exhibit higher accuracy, robustness, and flexibility. The results verify that integrating perturbation-response analysis with semantic embedding evaluation offers a scalable, retrain-free, and highly reliable framework for zero-day detection of synthetic images, with implications for visual forensics, media authenticity verification, and AI-generated content monitoring.

title: DNA: Dual-branch Network with Adaptation for Open-Set Online Handwriting Generation
authors: Tsai-Ling Huang, Nhat-Tuong Do-Tran, Ngoc-Hoang-Lam Le, Hong-Han Shuai, Ching-Chun Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Online handwriting generation (OHG) enhances handwriting recognition models by synthesizing diverse, human-like samples. However, existing OHG methods struggle to generate unseen characters, particularly in glyph-based languages like Chinese, limiting their real-world applicability. In this paper, we introduce our method for OHG, where the writer’s style and the characters generated during testing are unseen during training. To tackle this challenge, we propose a Dual-branch Network with Adaptation (DNA), which comprises an adaptive style branch and an adaptive content branch. The style branch learns stroke attributes such as writing direction, spacing, placement, and flow to generate realistic handwriting. Meanwhile, the content branch is designed to generalize effectively to unseen characters by decomposing character content into structural information and texture details, extracted via local and global encoders, respectively. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our DNA model is well-suited for the unseen OHG setting, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

title: Food Image Generation on Multi-Noun Categories
authors: Xinyue Pan, Yuhao Chen, Jiangpeng He, Fengqing Zhu
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating realistic food images for categories with multiple nouns is surprisingly challenging. For instance, the prompt "egg noodle" may result in images that incorrectly contain both eggs and noodles as separate entities. Multi-noun food categories are common in real-world datasets and account for a large portion of entries in benchmarks such as UEC-256. These compound names often cause generative models to misinterpret the semantics, producing unintended ingredients or objects. This is due to insufficient multi-noun category related knowledge in the text encoder and misinterpretation of multi-noun relationships, leading to incorrect spatial layouts. To overcome these challenges, we propose FoCULR (Food Category Understanding and Layout Refinement) which incorporates food domain knowledge and introduces core concepts early in the generation process. Experimental results demonstrate that the integration of these techniques improves image generation performance in the food domain.

title: Narrating For You: Prompt-guided Audio-visual Narrating Face Generation Employing Multi-entangled Latent Space
authors: Aashish Chandra K, Aashutosh A V, Abhijit Das
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present a novel approach for generating realistic speaking and talking faces by synthesizing a person’s voice and facial movements from a static image, a voice profile, and a target text. The model encodes the prompt/driving text, the driving image, and the voice profile of an individual and then combines them to pass them to the multi-entangled latent space to foster key-value pairs and queries for the audio and video modality generation pipeline. The multi-entangled latent space is responsible for establishing the spatiotemporal person-specific features between the modalities. Further, entangled features are passed to the respective decoder of each modality for output audio and video generation. Our experiments and analysis through standard metrics demonstrate the effectiveness of our model. All model checkpoints, code, and the proposed dataset can be found at https://github.com/narratingForYou/NarratingForYou.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Optimization of Turbo Decoder Iterations for Low-Power IoT Devices
authors: P.Balakrishna, Pramod Kumar P
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The exponential growth of Internet of Things (IoT) networks demands low-power and highly reliable data transmission despite constrained energy budgets and dynamic wireless channels. Traditional turbo decoders, although proven in 4G and 5G systems, rely on fixed iteration counts that cause unnecessary energy consumption in favorable channel conditions and inadequate correction in adverse ones. This paper presents a Reinforcement Learning-Based Optimization Framework that adaptively controls the number of decoding iterations in real time based on channel observations. Using a Q-Learning model integrated with a Log-MAP turbo decoder, the system learns an optimal iteration policy that minimizes power consumption without degrading bit-error-rate (BER) performance. The framework is implemented entirely in MATLAB and Python, and results under AWGN and Rayleigh fading channels show up to 34 % energy savings and 28 % latency reduction compared to conventional decoders. This approach provides a scalable software solution for energy-aware, intelligent IoT communication and forms a foundational step toward autonomous physical-layer adaptation in future 6G systems.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning for Early Cardiovascular Risk Prediction: A Systematic Review and the OC-GRU-RAE Framework
authors: J. Jerin Jose, T. Prem Jacob
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is a significant cause of death in the globe and its early and correct diagnosis is extremely significant in the appropriate clinical treatment of the disorder. Despite the success of deep learning models with a single clinical data modality such as tabular electronic health records, physiological time-series signals, and cardiac imaging, the existing ones generally. Besides that, another set of issues, such as sub-optimal representation of high-cardinality categorical data, low temporal modeling capacities, and low interpretability remain undermining clinical acceptance. The paper will summarize 40 peer-reviewed publications about early CVD detection with the application of deep learning models on unimodal and multimodal data that have been published since 2018. Based on the gaps of the conceptual framework, one can consent to the suggestion of the proposal of a conceptual framework called the Optimized CatBoost-Gated Recurrent Unit Recursive Autoencoder (OC-GRU-RAE) as the direction of the forthcoming research. The proposed framework combines sequential categorical representation, category-sensitive temporal coding and recursive multimodal representation learning to offer powerful, explainable and clinical consistent early cardiovascular risk prediction.

title: FARF-Net: Frequency-guided Adaptive Receptive Field Network for Edge-enhanced Polyp Segmentation
authors: Xue Li, Aiwen Jiang, Hongqian Yu, Yang Xiao
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of colorectal polyps plays a vital role in the early diagnosis and prevention of colorectal cancer. Despite notable progress, existing methods struggle with limited region adaptability due to fixed receptive fields, lack explicit boundary modeling, and are prone to interference from background noise, leading to suboptimal segmentation results. To address these issues, we propose FARF-Net, a novel edge-aware segmentation framework that leverages frequency-domain adaptive receptive fields. Built upon the Pyramid Vision Transformer v2 backbone, FARF-Net introduces three tailored components. Specifically, EdgeKAN module applies Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks for channel-wise nonlinear modeling, enhancing local edge semantics and boundary detail representation. Adaptive Receptive Field module adjusts spatial receptive fields based on localized frequency energy, boosting sensitivity to high-frequency boundaries. Frequency-Guided Dual-Supervision Decoder integrates high-frequency structural features and boundary priors to refine edge predictions and suppress irrelevant high-frequency background noise. Extensive experiments on five public polyp segmentation benchmarks demonstrate that FARF-Net consistently surpasses state-of-the-art methods. Notably, it achieves superior boundary reconstruction and robustness in challenging cases such as blurred contours and small polyps.

title: FLoMo-Net: A Novel Task-Adaptive Mixture of Experts Routing Framework with Frequency and Uncertainty Correction for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Md Rayhan Ahmed, Patricia Lasserre
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Medical image segmentation (MIS) is challenged by anatomical variability, ambiguous boundaries, and subtle textures, demanding an efficient balance between fine local details and global context. Existing architectures often suffer from suboptimal fusion of spatial and frequency-domain features, limiting their ability to capture richer structural and textural representations. To specifically address these challenges, we introduce FLoMo-Net, a modular MIS architecture organized around a principled route → select → refine → correct pipeline: (1) The Local–Global Mixture of Experts encoder adaptively routes features across specialized convolutional branches to capture scale-appropriate context; (2) The Dual-Attention Selective Aggregator then jointly selects informative channels and spatial regions using frequency-guided modulation; (3) At the bridge, the Frequency-Aware Multi-Scale Refinement module refines edges and textures through explicit low/high-frequency decomposition; (4) Finally, the False Positive/Negative Corrective Attention Module leverages uncertainty, derived from entropy and cosine dissimilarity, to produce a residual corrective mask that suppresses semantic drift and improves boundary delineation in the decoder stages. Across four MIS benchmarks, FLoMo-Net achieves superior boundary-aware performance and faster inference with fewer parameters than prior state-of-the-art 2D MIS models. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/rayhan-ahmed91/FLoMo-Net.

title: UAV Based Precise Multi Payload Dropping System Using Vision Guidance and RTK Positioning
authors: Bodnayakuni Tarun Kumar, Sanju Kumar NT, Syam Narayanan S, Raju Santhani, Pavan Sai Kumar Reddy T, P. Rajalakshmi
abstract: The precision dropping of payloads using unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) has gained critical importance in both military and civilian contexts. Accurate target positioning can determine mission success in defence operations, medicine, and disaster-affected areas. However, conventional UAV-based payload release systems face significant challenges in limited accuracy for targeting position, imprecise release mechanisms, and poor adaptability to diverse mission requirements. To overcome the challenge, this paper presents the development of a precise multi-payload drop system integrated with a drone, capable of carrying and deploying up to three payloads with a total capacity of 10–15 kg. The system first navigates to the target waypoint, then activates a vision-based precision drop routine. A downward-facing camera identifies the ground marker, and the drone adjusts its position until alignment is achieved. Once within the accuracy threshold, the payload is released, while target coordinates and telemetry are streamed to the ground station in real time. The system combines vision guidance with real-time kinematic (RTK) positioning to achieve sub-meter level accuracy in payload deployment. Comparative analysis is conducted in three operational model manual releases with camera-based target localisation, GPS-assisted release, and vision-RTK fusion. The system employs a servo-driven release mechanism connected to the flight controller, enabling manual operation via radio transmitter and autonomous triggering. Designed with a modular plug-and-play architecture. Experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid approach significantly improves precision and reduces error rates compared to conventional methods. The proposed VTOL-based multi-payload system demonstrates strong potential for tactical and civilian applications, offering accuracy, flexibility, and reliability in complex operational environments.

title: Key Frame Optimization of Videos Using Image Captioning Model
authors: Mohammed Inayathulla, A Lakshman, V Sujay, SMP Qubeb, KR Thukaram, G Raju
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: One of the most reliable information mediums is video, and in recent years, both online and offline video consumption has risen to previously unheard-of heights. In contrast to images, where context can be understood from a single frame, videos require the viewer to watch the entire clip in order to be understood. This presents a fundamental problem for information extraction from videos. To reduce the length of the video an optimized method to extract key frames of the video is proposed. Transformers based image captioning model is implemented as the first step in our work. After extracting the key frames using an unsupervised clustering technique, we have used the captioning model and a scoring function to determine how similar a frame is to a manually compiled corpus of videos belonging to a particular category. Next, we choose only those frames from the entire video that have similarity scores that are higher than the threshold. We conducted experiments on different categories of videos from TVSUM50 dataset and the findings were satisfactory.

title: Preliminary Evaluation of Polymer-Based Mechanical Intracranial Pressure Sensor With Ultrasound Readout
authors: Colleen Chemerka, Lukas Selzer, Florian Solzbacher
abstract: Intracranial hypertension requires continuous monitoring to improve patient outcomes. However, existing implantable sensors rely on electronic or semiconductor components that require active power, limiting long-term stability and compatibility with necessary diagnostic tools. This study introduces a novel working principle of a fully mechanical, passive, polymer-based pressure sensor that utilizes ultrasound as a readout method. The sensor translates exerted pressure into a geometrical change within the micromachined structure, thereby altering ultrasound transmission or reflection properties. In a water tank, experiments showed that ultrasound transmission was significantly modulated between different sensor configurations or states. The pressure sensors representing no applied pressure showed, on average, more than twice the transmission loss compared to the simulated pressure. Experimental data are in good agreement with a one-dimensional mathematical model. Compared to current alternatives that rely on resonance-based behavior, the proposed transmission approach is less sensitive to manufacturing variation and material aging. These findings suggest a promising alternative to current pressure sensors with the prospect of allowing long-term monitoring and implantation.

title: DRTA: Dynamic Reward Scaling for Reinforcement Learning in Time Series Anomaly Detection
authors: Bahareh Golchin, Banafsheh Rekabdar, Kunpeng Liu
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: Anomaly detection in time series data is important for applications in finance, healthcare, sensor networks, and industrial monitoring. Traditional methods usually struggle with limited labeled data, high false-positive rates, and difficulty generalizing to novel anomaly types. To overcome these challenges, we propose a reinforcement learning-based framework that integrates dynamic reward shaping, Variational Autoencoder (VAE), and active learning, called DRTA. Our method uses an adaptive reward mechanism that balances exploration and exploitation by dynamically scaling the effect of VAE-based reconstruction error and classification rewards. This approach enables the agent to detect anomalies effectively in low-label systems while maintaining high precision and recall. Our experimental results on the Yahoo A1 and Yahoo A2 benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms state-of-theart unsupervised and semi-supervised approaches. These findings show that our framework is a scalable and efficient solution for real-world anomaly detection tasks.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>Code is available at GitHub Repository.

title: Learnify-Massively Online Open Courseware
authors: Archana Das, Neetu, Aditya U, Deekshith, K Sinchana, Kavya Vasant Saraf
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This study presents Learnify, a cutting-edge MOOC platform that uses AI-driven customization and engagement to revolutionize online learning. Using collaborative filtering to recommend courses and reinforcement learning algorithms to adjust quiz difficulty, the system tailors each user's learning experience according to their behavior and performance. Sentiment analysis enhances the mood-based user interface by increasing its emotional responsiveness. The platform is more responsive and scalable thanks to its modular design, which integrates React.js, Node.js, and MongoDB. Compared to standard MOOCs, the review revealed a 32% increase in learner engagement and a 27% increase in course completion. This approach would transform the way personalized e-learning operates by fusing AI intelligence with game-like and community-driven element

title: TihanFly: A Multi-Drone Ground Control Station for Scalable UAV Operations
authors: Pavan Sai Kumar Thummala, Mohan Kumar J, Syam Narayanan S, B. Tarun Kumar, Raju Santhani, P. Rajalakshmi
abstract: Advanced Air Mobility (AAM) and multi UAV operations demand ground control systems that scale beyond the single-drone paradigm. Conventional ground control stations (GCS) face limitations in scalability, real time responsiveness, and integration with emerging use cases such as Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) and heavy payload flights. We introduce TihanFly, an indigenous GCS designed for multi-drone connectivity, real-time monitoring, and mission planning. The architecture employs a producer consumer threading model for parallel telemetry handling and a link manager that assigns each UAV a unique identifier for efficient retrieval, control, and scalable multi-UAV management. TihanFly has been validated in BVLOS and heavy payload trials, demonstrating robust performance and scalability. Beyond core command and control, it integrates external services including weather and traffic data enhancing situational awareness and flight safety. These features position TihanFly as an AAM-ready GCS capable of enabling swarm coordination, proactive airspace monitoring, and mission critical applications in high density aerial environments.

title: Automated Feature Selection and Hyperparameter Optimized XGBoost for Multi-Domain Disease Prediction
authors: G.V. Vidya Lakshmi, Sanjay Mandru, Deepak Reddy Avuthu, Anitha Rani Palakayala, Kommineni Madhavi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The proposed method aims to enhance patient out-comes and optimization of healthcare resources by early and accurate prediction of chronic diseases, based on patient real-world data, through an integrative machine learning framework of multi-disease prediction, specifically heart disease, breast cancer and diabetes. The data processing commenced with the preprocessing of the data, including imputation. For missing values, encoding of categories, feature scaling, class balancing with the use of SMOTE, and correlation analysis. Five standard machine learning models-Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors, XGBoost, and Decision Tree-were trained and tested. The improvements to model interpretability included the following visual tools like feature heatmaps, ROC curves, performance comparison charts and confusion matrices. Comparative analysis between diseases revealed good and interpretable results, and the optimized feature selection was a success. This framework is easily generalizable to the modular designed to an increasing number of disease domains, which is a possible solution to comprehensive multi-disease prediction in real-world healthcare settings.

title: Exploring UAV-Based Multispectral Crop Yield Analysis: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Sreenivasa Chakravarthi Sangapu, M Sai Deekshith, P Chandrasena Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Combining UAVs with multispectral imaging technology has enabled high-resolution, real-time monitoring of crops and prediction of crop yields, revolutionizing precision farming. This research focuses on UAV yield estimation, along with machine and deep learning models with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) for multispectral imaging. The research has deployed UAVs to gather other relevant data with multispectral imaging and generated orthomosaics along with vegetation indices, which are NDVI, EVI, and GNDVI, as well as different agronomic and environmental data. Afterward XGBoost, LASSO regression predictive models, and dimensionality reduction were compared to Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). The spatial and spectral Ubisoft models were also incorporated, where CNNs provided the most predictive power with a validation R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.8486. The XAI tools proposed, SHAP and Grad-CAM, determined which vegetation indices and spatial patterns apportioned at the field level influenced yield forecasting. The research has shown predictive AI for actionable insights in precision farming. The research aims to develop hybrid deep learning models for rapid, scalable, real-time use on a multi-crop dataset. The research proposes focusing on its real-time applications.

title: Evaluation and Future Exploration of Lightweight Cryptography on FPGA: Challenges and Opportunities for AES, TEA, and PRESENT
authors: Sandhya C P, Manjith B C
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In this digital era, the security and privacy of transmissive data are critically important. AES, TEA, and PRESENT are three block-based lightweight ciphers that can be used for encrypting sensitive data. To understand the desired goal of security and privacy, this paper focuses on modeling of major lightweight ciphers, namely Advanced Encryption Standard (AES), Tiny Encryption Algorithm (TEA), and PRESENT algorithms, using the Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) hardware platform. This paper presents the implementation and pseudocode for the AES, TEA, and PRESENT ciphers in Xilinx Vivado 2016. The evaluation performance is based on the metrics namely resource utilization, area, throughput, maximum frequency, latency, and energy consumption. The results demonstrate that the PRESENT cipher performs better in terms of resource efficiency compared to AES and TEA ciphers by 32% and 60%, respectively. Regarding maximum frequency, latency, and throughput, the TEA cipher performs better. However, when compared to energy efficiency, the PRESENT cipher consumes less than AES and TEA ciphers. AES and TEA ciphers consume 68.82% and 96.6% of the energy, respectively. In conclusion, considering resource utilization and energy consumption, the PRESENT cipher is recommended, and the TEA cipher is recommended for performance in IoT domain applications.

title: Development of a Multimodal Virtual Agent Utilizing a Fine-Tuned Large Language Model for Transformative Mental Health Support at Chinhoyi Provincial Hospital
authors: Ropafadzo Makaza, Shakemore Chinofunga, Fungai Jacqueline Kiwa, Tavengwa Masamha
publish: 2025 4th Zimbabwe Conference of Information and Communication Technologies (ZCICT)
abstract: Access to mental health services remains a significant challenge for many Zimbabweans, particularly in underserved regions. This study investigates the development of an intelligent, multimodal conversational agent(CA) to improve mental health support at Chinhoyi Provincial Hospital. Given the limited availability of mental health resources in Zimbabwe, the research focuses on creating a contextually relevant AI-driven solution that provides accessible and affordable assistance. While text-based chatbots have been deployed in low-resource settings, there remains a gap in exploring multimodal virtual agents powered by large language models (LLMs) for mental health applications. The study employs the Cross-Industry Standard Process for Data Mining (CRISP-DM) methodology, beginning with an in-depth analysis of existing challenges and system requirements. A fine-tuned DistilBERT model was developed, selected for its robustness and efficiency compared to larger LLMs. The model was integrated with a 3D avatar to enable multimodal interaction (text, audio, and visual) and evaluated for effectiveness. Findings indicate that fine-tuned AI models can successfully simulate natural human conversation, with empathy, personalization, and user-centered design emerging as critical factors in enhancing engagement and mental well-being. This research demonstrates the potential of AI to transform mental health service delivery in resource-constrained settings. Through the mitigating of barriers such as stigma and a shortage of mental health professionals, the virtual agent could help bridge critical gaps in care. The study ultimately contributes to a sustainable, technology-driven support system that empowers individuals to proactively manage their mental health, fostering improved well-being in Zimbabwean communities.

title: RhythmSense-DSTNet: A Dual-Stream Spatiotemporal Learning Framework with Selective Attention for Robust ECG Arrhythmia Detection
authors: Md. Shamshad Begum, Nimmagadda Padmaja
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In this paper, RhythmSense-DSTNet, a multi-class ECG signal arrhythmia detection hybrid deep spatiotemporal neural network, is proposed. The model combines the dual-stream time refinement with specific emphasis on features, making it possible to highly discriminate in a wide range of the cardiac rhythms despite noise and morphological distortion. RhythmSense-DSTNet reaches 98.92% accuracy, 98.61% sensitivity, 99.15% specificity, and F1-score of 98.74, which is 24000% worse than the state-of-the-art baselines. The high-resolution ROC curves also demonstrate that the AUC is close to 0.99 in all the major rhythm classes, which is a good indicator of a robust generalization ability. The findings reveal that RhythmSense-DSTNet can be used to screen arrhythmia and help in clinical decision-making in a real-time manner.

title: Network Packet Sniffer and Traffic Analyzer for Real-Time Data Monitoring
authors: C R Rathish, Sooraj Naik, Rahul Patil, Srushti Navale, Ravikumar A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The exponential growth in digital communication has made modern networks increasingly complex and vulnerable to performance degradation and security threats. This paper presents the design and development of a real-time network packet sniffer and traffic analyzer aimed at enhancing data flow visibility, improving operational efficiency, and identifying potential cybersecurity risks. Leveraging robust packet-capturing libraries and advanced traffic analysis algorithms, our system captures, decodes, and visualizes live network traffic to extract critical parameters such as protocol type, IP addresses, packet size, and payload content. The tool incorporates real-time anomaly detection and customizable filters to flag irregularities, offering actionable insights to network administrators. Designed with scalability and usability in mind, it provides a graphical interface for monitoring bandwidth utilization, protocol distribution, and security alerts. By enabling proactive management and deep analysis of network activity, this system addresses the increasing demand for secure, reliable, and transparent digital infrastructure across enterprises and institutions.

title: Quantum Machine Learning–Driven AI Predictive Model for Cluster-Level Optimization of Manufacturing Line
authors: Rajan Sharma, Nasir Khan, Pushyamitra Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: This paper presents a Quantum Machine Learning– Driven AI Predictive Model (QML–AI PM) for cluster-level optimization of manufacturing lines. The proposed framework integrates Quantum Features Encoding (QFE), Variation Quantum Circuits (V.Q.C.), and the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) with advanced AI models to address uncertainty, nonlinear behavior, and high-dimensional interactions in industrial environments. A combined dataset of multi-cluster manufacturing operations was used to evaluate the system, and performance was compared with classical models such as SVM, RF, LSTM, and XGBoost. Results show that the proposed model achieves 94.6% prediction accuracy, 93.4% optimization efficiency, and lower computation time, outperforming all baseline techniques. The hybrid architecture significantly improves scheduling, resource allocation, and predictive maintenance at the cluster scale. Overall, the QML–AI PM demonstrates substantial potential for next-generation smart manufacturing and establishes a foundation for future quantum-enabled industrial optimization.

title:
DM
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    Net: Dual-Camera Super-Resolution via Domain Modulation and Multi-scale Matching
authors: Cong Guan, Jiacheng Ying, Yuya Ieiri, Osamu Yoshie
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Dual-camera super-resolution is highly practical for smartphone photography that primarily super-resolve the wide-angle images using the telephoto image as a reference. In this paper, we propose DM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>Net, a novel dual-camera super-resolution network based on Domain Modulation and Multi-scale Matching. To bridge the domain gap between the high-resolution domain and the degraded domain, we learn two compressed global representations from image pairs corresponding to the two domains. To enable reliable transfer of high-frequency structural details from the reference image, we design a multi-scale matching module that conducts patch-level feature matching and retrieval across multiple receptive fields to improve matching accuracy and robustness. Moreover, we also introduce Key Pruning to achieve a significant reduction in memory usage and inference time with little model performance sacrificed. Experimental results on three real-world datasets demonstrate that our DM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>Net outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches.

title: HiGlassRM: Learning to Remove High-prescription Glasses via Synthetic Dataset Generation
authors: Sebin Lee, Heewon Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Existing eyeglass removal methods can handle frames and shadows but fail to correct lens-induced geometric distortions, as public datasets lack the necessary supervision. To address this, we introduce the HiGlass Dataset, the first large-scale synthetic dataset providing explicit flow-based supervision for refractive warping. We also propose Hi-GlassRM, a novel pipeline whose core is a network that explicitly estimates a displacement flowmap to de-warp distorted facial geometry. Experiments on both synthetic and real images show that this flowmap-centric approach, trained on our data, significantly improves identity preservation and perceptual quality over existing methods. Our work demonstrates that explicitly modeling and correcting geometric distortion via flowmap estimation, enabled by targeted supervision, is key to faithful eyeglass removal. Project page: https://higlassrm.github.io/

title: Towards Intelligent Traffic Signal Control in Internet of Vehicles Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Satish Kumar Negi, Suraj Sharma, Pushpendra Kumar Chandra, Niranjan Kumar Ray
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Traffic jams in cities and more cars connecting to the internet mean that traffic signal control systems need to be smart and adaptable. This paper provides an extensive examination of artificial intelligence-based methodologies for intelligent traffic signal management in Internet of Vehicles contexts. There are four main types of existing methods: rule-based, optimization-based, learning-based, and hybrid. A comparison of average delay, queue length, throughput, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions shows that reinforcement learning reduces delays by about 30% to 40%, deep reinforcement learning boosts performance by up to 45% to 50%, and IoV-enabled hybrid approaches achieve the biggest gains of over 55%. We talk about open questions and future research directions for intelligent traffic signal control systems that are safe, scalable, and easy to use.

title: AllerSafe: Real-Time Allergen Detection Using Knowledge Graph-Enhanced Transformer Classification
authors: Samanvitha L, Varun Aditya, Tanisha R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Problem: Globally allergies caused by food has affected 8% of children and 11% of adults, and due to barcode-only lookup only 40-70% coverage gaps exist for regional markets. Method: This paper AllerSafe, combines OCR-based extraction of text, a domain-specific knowledge graph (AllerGraph: 847 nodes, 2,156 edges), and transformer-based DistilBERT classification for detection of allergens in real-time. AllerGraph addresses semantic blindness by encoding ingredient-allergen derivation paths. Results: Evaluation demonstrates 94.3% OCR accuracy, 96.7% classification F1-score (95% CI: [96.2%, 97.1%]) across nine FDA allergen categories, and 4.7s average response time. The full system improves F1 by 18.4% over keyword matching, with AllerGraph contributing +7.3% over DistilBERT alone. Field deployment with 2,847 users achieved 76% repeat usage and 98.2% product coverage. Conclusion: The proposed system demonstrates practical utility for real-world allergen detection.
