title: Stabilizing the Memristive Liu System Using PI-Based Sliding Mode Control: Implications for Autonomous Vehicle Dynamics
authors: Pallav, Rani Kumari
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In modern years, there has been a rising focus in the study of chaotic systems, predominantly in exploring methods to stabilize their unpredictable behavior. Chaos theory, known for its ability to describe the dynamics of highly complex and nonlinear systems, has emerged as a valuable tool in modeling real-world phenomena such as traffic flow where irregular and sudden changes are common. This work explores the stabilization of a memristive Liu system as a conceptual analog for modeling the complex and unpredictable dynamics of urban traffic flow. By applying PI-based sliding mode control (PI-SMC), the chaotic dynamics are stabilized in simulation, providing insights into robust control strategies for autonomous vehicles navigating uncertain and nonlinear traffic environments. This approach bridges chaos theory and adaptive vehicle control, offering a foundation for future advancement of intelligent transport systems competent of handling practical-world traffic complexities.

title: A Current Sensor Fault Tolerant Technique for Pole-Changing Induction Motors
authors: Shubham Dabral, Saptarshi Basak
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Pole-changing induction motors (PCIMs), have been considered as potential drives for electric vehicle (EV) applications due to their ability to function at a wide constant power speed range along with higher starting torque. Vector control techniques are commonly implemented for PCIM drives where accurate value of stator current is essential for proper control and decoupling of flux and torque. Therefore, identifying and mitigating current sensor faults in PCIMs offers significant challenges, particularly during pole transitions and under varying loads. This is because the motors exhibit a complex and dynamic behavior, which makes it difficult to detect and mitigate such sensor faults. A failure detection and mitigation approach for PCIMs is provided in this work. The technique is based on the unique features of the transformation matrices used in a PCIM drive. Through the provision of consistent performance throughout all operational modes, including pole-changing events, the suggested approach enhances the reliability and safety of electric vehicle drive systems. The MATLAB/Simulink software is utilized in order to carry out simulations and validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Ground Fault Detection in RES Integrated Power System Networks Using Clustering Algorithm
authors: Mandadhi Venkateswarlu, Rakesh Kumar Panda, N. Kumaresan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Accurate detection of Line-to-Ground (LG) faults in power systems integrated with Inverter Interfaced Sources (IISs) remains challenging due to their limited contribution to negative and zero-sequence currents. This paper proposes an unsupervised learning approach for LG fault detection using Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) within a time-series K-Means clustering framework. The WSCC 9-bus system is used as the testbed to validate the effectiveness of the algorithm. Various fault scenarios are simulated by varying source combinations and fault locations across different buses. Voltage and current phasors are recorded using Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs), while sequence components are extracted using sequence analyzers. To improve clustering performance, dimensionality reduction techniques—including Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) are employed to map the data into a reduced feature space. MATLAB is utilized for simulation and data generation, while Python is used to implement the unsupervised clustering algorithm. Simulation results confirm that the proposed DTW-based method effectively distinguishes between faulted and non-faulted conditions, even under scenarios involving high IIS penetration.

title: PiGraphNet: A Novel Physics-Informed Graph Neural Network for Remaining Useful Life Prediction of Lithium-Ion Batteries in Electric Vehicles
authors: Manoranjan Gandhudi, Gangadharan G. R
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Accurate prediction of the Remaining Useful Life of lithium-ion batteries is essential for enhancing the performance, safety, and reliability of electric vehicles (EVs). Existing model-based approaches often require extensive parameter identification and lose accuracy under unmodeled operating conditions, whereas purely data-driven methods may overlook spatial-temporal dependencies and violate physical constraints. To address these limitations, we propose PiGraphNet, a novel Physics-Informed Graph Neural Network (GNN) that unifies graph-based spatial-temporal modeling with embedded battery degradation physics. PiGraphNet dynamically constructs battery interaction graphs from voltage, current, and temperature data to capture evolving inter-cell dependencies, while integrating electrochemical degradation laws, such as Peukert's law, into the message-passing mechanism for physically consistent predictions. Two domain-specific graph strategies—the Voltage-Current-Temperature (VCT) Graph and the Electrode Degradation Graph (EDG) enable targeted modeling of real-time electro-thermal coupling and long-term aging patterns. The model is trained with a composite loss function combining mean squared error with physics-informed constraints, ensuring both accuracy and interpretability. Experimental results on the NASA lithium-ion battery degradation dataset demonstrate that PiGraphNet outperforms LSTM, GRU, TCN, and physics-informed baselines, achieving an MSE of 251.60 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 93.29%.

title: Performance Analysis of LSVF Equivalent Output Feedback I-PD Control for Non-Minimum Phase Systems
authors: Rajeev Kumar, Jayati Dey, Tapas Kumar Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: The advantages of the LSVF (Linear State Variable feedback) control are twofold because the response and loop performance of systems can be improved by virtue of arbitrary closed-loop pole-placement, and no transmission zero arises in the closed-loop transfer function due to controller dynamics. However, LSVF control needs measurement of all the state variables. This work establishes an equivalence between LSVF integral error feedback and output feedback-based I-PD control for second order systems. The performance of the developed IPD control is investigated for non-minimum phase Boost and Buck-Boost Converters, which inherently contain right half plane zeros. The I-PD control necessitates only the output voltage measurement for the converters, and achieves the same desirable performance as LSVF one. The controller is designed to achieve a satisfactory time domain response and robust stability. The I-PD controller parameters are designed with linear quadratic regulator (LQR) control technique to obtain the optimal solution. Gain margin (GM), Phase margin (PM), and Peak of the Sensitivity transfer function are employed here as measures of the robustness. The I-PD control is shown to maintain reference output voltage under varying load and set point conditions. Further, the LQR based I-PD control is shown to achieve a lesser settling time and overshoot as compared to two other output feedback control schemes, Lead-PI and Internal Model Control (IMC).

title: Gradient-Based Optimizer for Short-Term Hydrothermal Generation Scheduling
authors: Aman Kumar, Manoj Kumawat, Ujjwal Kumar Kalla, Ashok Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper explores the application of the Gradient-Based algorithm (GBO) to discover the best way to manage and schedule power generation from both hydro and thermal plants over a short period of time, involving the coordinated operation of one thermal unit and two hydro units over a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}$</tex>-hour period. The hydro unit operates with a fixed-head water reservoir. The main objective is to reduce the total fuel cost of thermal generators by utilizing the water volume of the reservoir to the maximum extent while satisfying system and operational constraints. The GBO is inspired by gradient-based search strategies to effectively navigate the solution search space and determine optimal hydrothermal power generation schedules for 24 hours. The algorithm balances exploration and exploitation capabilities to handle the complexity of the Short-term Hydro-Thermal Scheduling problem. Comparative results show that the GBO is capable of solving complex hydrothermal power generation problems, providing more economical solutions. Furthermore, statistical analysis confirms the robustness and consistency of the algorithm, demonstrating its suitability and efficiency in addressing the challenges associated with short-term hydrothermal generation scheduling

title: A Comparative Analysis of Various Deep Learning Models for State-of-Charge Estimation in Lithium-Ion Batteries
authors: Sourabh Joshi, Arghya Mitra
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) have emerged as a key technology driving recent advancements in electrical and electronic systems. They are widely used in electric vehicles, renewable energy storage systems, portable electronic devices and even in orbiting satellites due to their high energy density, fast charging capabilities, long operational lifespan, and minimal maintenance requirements. Accurate estimation of the State-of-Charge (SoC) is obligatory to ensure the safe, reliable, and efficient performance of LIBs. Conventional approaches like Coulomb counting and Kalman filters are commonly used, but they often face issues in handling nonlinear battery behaviour, high-dimensional data, and complex operational conditions. In this study, we perform a comparative analysis of three deep learning architectures - Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-LSTM (CNN-LSTM) for datadriven SoC estimation using voltage, current, and temperature as input parameters. Using a publicly available Li-ion battery dataset, we preprocess the time-series data into fixed-length windows and train each model under identical conditions. The models are then evaluated using Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and the coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}})$</tex>. To ensure a statistically sound assessment, each model's performance was evaluated over 10 independent runs. The results show that all three architectures are capable of learning time-based patterns for SoC estimation, but their performance and stability vary significantly.

title: Analysis of Modified Leaky-LMS Algorithm Based Single-Stage Three-Phase Grid-Tied PV System Using Current Error Space Phasor Hysteresis Controller
authors: Urvashiben Rathod, Uday Patel, Siddharthsingh K. Chauhan, M. T. Shah, P. N. Tekwani
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: In this paper, analysis of Modified leaky-LMS (MLLMS) algorithm based single-stage three-phase grid-tied photovoltaic (SSGTPV) system that employs a current error space phasor (CESP) hysteresis controller (HC) is presented. The system employs an incremental conductance (InC) maximum power point tracking (MPPT) method for extraction of maximum power from the PV array. At the same time, it maintains the grid current stable at the point of common coupling (PCC). The CESP based HC uses two hexagonal hysteresis bands - one for selecting voltage vectors and the another to identify the corresponding sector. During each fundamental cycle, the CESP frequently crosses the fixed hexagonal boundary. It reaches the outer hysteresis band to enable precise sector identification. The CESP has advantages such as easy implementation, fast transient behavior, adjacent voltage vector switching. Additionally, it helps prevent random voltage vector switching by ensuring that only adjacent vectors within the same sector of the voltage vectors are selected. Analysis of MLLMS algorithm based SSGTPV system using CESP HC is evaluated through MATLAB simulation. The system is analyzed for steady-state, phaseout condition, and dynamic behavior for irradiance change.

title: Poisson-Based Modeling of Arc-Induced Spike Events for Low-Voltage Distribution Systems
authors: Ratnakar Nutenki, Haraprasad Badajena, Aurobinda Routray, Ashok Kumar Pradhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Arcing faults in low-voltage distribution systems exhibit stochastic and load-dependent temporal patterns that challenge conventional detection techniques. This paper presents a systematic analysis of arcing currents generated under various household appliances, including rheostat, kettle, fan, LED, and drill machines. A slip-difference-based method is employed to extract transient signatures from the current waveforms, revealing distinct spike trains associated with zero-current periods and rapid excursions. For each load, the inter-spike intervals are statistically analyzed and shown to follow an exponential distribution, validating a Poisson point process model for arc event occurrence. This physically interpretable and data-driven framework supports the development of adaptive, load-aware arc detection schemes suitable for smart circuit breakers and protective devices.

title: Control Strategy of Bidirectional AC-DC Converter using Modified RBFNN Algorithm to Improve PQ in Grid Connected PV-EV based Charger
authors: Sudhanshu Mittal, Manoj Badoni, Prakash Chittora, Gaurav Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: A grid connected EV with a PV charging system is proposed in this paper. An RBFNN is designed to control a bidirectional AC-DC Converter (BADC), and the system is designed for single-phase. The DC link of BADC is connected with a bidirectional DC-DC converter (BDDC) with a battery for smooth operation under charging and discharging conditions. This paper presents a fast charging control during dynamic load conditions in G2V as well as V2G modes. The solar Photovoltaic (SPV) injects power to support the grid, load and battery. Also, it provides an improved power quality by applying the proposed RBFNN controller. The simulation results of G2V and V2G are well presented and analyzed their dynamic waveforms.

title: A Synergistic Neural-Kalman Hybrid Approach for Real-Time State Estimation
authors: Tasadeek Hassan Dar, Satyavir Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This study introduces a new framework for estimating the state of charge (SOC). It blends optimization, deep learning, and adaptive filtering techniques. First, the Cuckoo Search Algorithm finds the best settings for a secondorder RC circuit model. This ensures accurate battery characterization. The parameters obtained is fed to the Adaptive Extended Kalman Filter (AEKF) to produce training data for a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network. The BiLSTM model predicts SOC estimation errors. It integrates with a Squared Gain EKF (SGEKF) to adjust SOC estimates in real-time. We assess the BiLSTM-SGEKF method with real driving cycle data. We also compare it to traditional methods like AEKF and EKF. The results show that BiLSTMSGEKF has the lowest RMSE at 0.047 %, MAE at 0.041 %, and ME at 0.14%. It significantly outperforms AEKF and EKF. This hybrid approach adjusts easily to changing conditions. So, it's a great option for real-time SOC estimation.

title: Barrier Method Optimized MTPA and AFSTSMOCDSC-SRF-PLL Techniques for Sensorless Operation of SPV-Battery-Charger Integrated PMaSynRM Based LEV
authors: Sushant Kumar, Shailendra Kumar, Shashank Kurm
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents an advanced control framework for a solar photovoltaic (SPV) and battery-powered permanent magnet-assisted synchronous reluctance motor (PMaSynRM) drive, it is designed for the operation of light electric vehicles (LEVs) and battery charging topology. The SPV array is primarily utilized to extend the driving range by providing supplementary energy for battery charging, promoting cleaner transportation. Similarly, a bidirectional DC-DC converter (BDDC) is employed to recover energy during regenerative braking, further enhancing the range of LEV. DC bus voltage is regulated using a second-order sliding mode controller (SOMSC), ensuring system stability under load transients. To achieve optimal torque generation with minimized copper losses, the Maximum Torque Per Ampere (MTPA) strategy is implemented. The associated constrained optimization problem is effectively solved using the Barrier method. For accurate flux estimation under dynamic conditions, an improved active fluxbased supertwisting sliding mode observer (AFSTSMO) is proposed. A cascaded delayed signal cancellation (CDSC)-based filter is applied to eliminate harmonics and enable precise rotor position estimation from the active flux. Furthermore, a gridintegrated charger topology based on a three-phase inverter is incorporated, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{d}-\mathbf{q}$</tex> axis current regulation. The current controllers employ a second-order sliding mode control strategy for high accuracy and robustness. The integration of these advanced control and estimation techniques leads to distortionfree, dynamically stable, and energy-efficient operation of the PMaSynRM drive system in LEV applications.

title: Metaheuristic Optimization of UAV Mission Planning with a Novel Cost Function
authors: Persdeep Kaur, Naresh Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper addresses the complex problem of mission optimization by efficiently assigning tasks to a fleet of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), or drones, operating within an environment that includes predefined threat zones. The primary objective is to minimize the mission makespan, defined as the maximum path length traversed by any single drone, while ensuring complete task coverage, avoidance of threat areas, and efficient utilization of all available drones. The study evaluates the performance of seven widely recognized metaheuristic algorithms, namely, Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), Cuckoo Search (CS), Special Forces Algorithm (SFA), Puma Optimizer (PO), Firefly Algorithm (FA), and Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO). Experimental results indicate that the PO achieved the lowest makespan, demonstrating superior solution quality, albeit with a longer execution time. Conversely, the ACO exhibited the fastest execution time, offering a balance between speed and solution quality. The findings provide valuable insights into the suitability of these metaheuristic algorithms for real-world drone mission planning in dynamic and constrained environments.

title: A 13-,19-, and 25-Level Switched-Capacitor Multilevel Inverter with Threefold Gain for Renewable Energy Applications
authors: Md Ashraf Ali Khan, M Saad Bin Arif, Faraz Ahmad, Abu Athar
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel asymmetric multilevel inverter (AMLI) topology for photovoltaic (PV) applications, capable of producing 13-, 19-, and 25-level output voltages with a three-fold voltage gain. The proposed design employs 16 switches, 2 diodes, and 4 switched capacitors (SCs), ensuring reduced component count. The SCs achieve natural voltage self-balancing without sensors or complex control circuitry, thereby simplifying implementation. A three-DC-source selection strategy is introduced to enhance operational flexibility across different configurations. The topology is analyzed using sinusoidal pulse width modulation (SPWM) and evaluated for power loss and efficiency through thermal modeling in PLECS. Simulation results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed AMLI under both steady-state and dynamic load conditions, maintaining an efficiency of 96.91 % at 1000 W and 94.41 % at 5 kW. Comparative analysis highlights its superior gain, low device-per-level ratio, and component count, making it a promising solution for high-performance renewable energy conversion systems.

title: A New Converter Topology to Suppress Common-Mode Voltage in Open End Pmsm Drives Based on Three-Phase Unfolder
authors: Brojogopal Majhi, Anandarup Das
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel dual T-type threelevel inverter based unfolder converter topology for Open-End three-phase surface-mounted permanent magnet synchronous motor(PMSM). Novel circuit topology consist of two T-type inverter in a novel fashion with Common-dc link has been proposed in this paper. Existing Unfolder topology possibly eliminate high frequency Common-Mode-Voltage(CMV), with dual T-type inverter the suppression of high frequency CMV concept further extended for Open-End(OE) Drives. Dual T-Type operates over a line-frequency cycle with sector-wise switching sequences to achieve nearly pure sine wave across the motor phase winding with simplified modulation strategy. Using the unfolded voltage waveform in the motor phase windings, the proposed topology inherently eliminate high frequency CMV and almost eliminate motor bearing current. Simulation results show that the proposed approach maintains low harmonic distortion, regulated motor currents, and stable torque production under both dynamic and steady-state conditions.

title: Directional Relaying using Fundamental Harmonic Sensitivity in Phase Currents
authors: Srijita Saha, Biman Kumar Saha Roy, Rajesh Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC)
abstract: Estimation of the fault direction is an important feature of protective relays, primarily in power systems where the fault current direction is not fixed. Traditional directional relays are dependent on both current and voltage signals. Although it is a common practice, relying on voltage implies the usage of additional equipment such as potential transformers, making the system more expensive and difficult to maintain. In this paper, a novel method is proposed for directional relaying in which only the current signals at the relay point are used. The timederivative graphs of the fundamental harmonic current have been analyzed to design an algorithm to distinguish between forward and reverse faults in transmission systems. The proposed method is applicable for reverse power flow as well. The efficiency of the scheme has been checked through extensive simulations.

title: FB-4D: Spatial-Temporal Coherent Dynamic 3D Content Generation with Feature Banks
authors: Jinwei Li, Huan-Ang Gao, Wenyi Li, Haohan Chi, Chenyu Liu, Chenxi Du, Yiqian Liu, Mingju Gao, Guiyu Zhang, Zongzheng Zhang, Li Yi, Yao Yao, Jingwei Zhao, Hongyang Li, Yikai Wang, Hao Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: With the rapid advancements in diffusion models and 3D generation techniques, dynamic 3D content generation has become a crucial research area. However, achieving high-fidelity 4D (dynamic 3D) generation with strong spatial-temporal consistency remains a challenging task. Inspired by recent findings that pretrained diffusion features capture rich correspondences, we propose FB-4D, a novel 4D generation framework that integrates a Feature Bank mechanism to enhance both spatial and temporal consistency in generated frames. In FB-4D, we store features extracted from previous frames and fuse them into the process of generating subsequent frames, ensuring consistent characteristics across both time and multiple views. To ensure a compact representation, the Feature Bank is updated by a proposed dynamic merging mechanism. Leveraging this Feature Bank, we demonstrate for the first time that generating additional reference sequences through multiple autoregressive iterations can continuously improve generation performance. Experimental results show that FB-4D significantly outperforms existing methods in terms of rendering quality, spatial-temporal consistency, and robustness. It surpasses all multi-view generation tuning-free approaches by a large margin and achieves performance on par with training-based methods. Our code and data will be publicly available to support future research.

title: Diverse Source-Aware Context Retrieval for Multi-Hop Queries: LLM-Powered Keyword Filtering and Cross-Source Ranking
authors: Yuta Tsuchiya, Masayoshi Mase, Hiroya Matsuba
abstract: We propose a novel Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach that addresses complex queries requiring evidence from multiple documents with similar content but different specifications. Industrial equipment in energy, rail transport, and manufacturing often remains in service for over 20 years. This results in an extensive collection of maintenance manuals and troubleshooting guides covering different product generations and numerous customer-specific configurations. Although these documents may appear similar, they often have critical differences in specifications. Therefore, referencing the wrong manual can lead to serious misunderstandings and critical operational errors for maintenance engineers. We propose a lightweight, agentic RAG approach that consists of three components: 1. LLM-Based Keyword Extraction: LLM is used to extract salient keywords from the user's query, separating them into “topical” keywords and “source” keywords, avoiding generating hallucinated content; 2. Document Routing via Keyword Filtering: the extracted “source” keywords are used to filter the document corpus, effectively narrowing the search space and ensuring evidence is gathered from appropriate sources; 3. Keyword-Weighted Hybrid Retrieval with Source-wise Ranking: in addition to dense vector similarity and sparse lexical relevance, we incorporate a score based on the extracted “topical” keywords to better capture query intent, and create a ranking for each source to ensure diverse and relevant context for the generator LLM. The method was evaluated on the MultiHop-RAG benchmark, a dataset designed for complex queries requiring evidence from multiple sources. Compared to standard hybrid search and metadata filtering, our method improved Recall@4 by 29.5% and achieved better generation performance (F1 score: 0.7523 vs. 0.7156). The method is currently in production use as a maintenance inquiry agent for troubleshooting support at a service center.

title: Incipient Location and Fault Detection in Underground Cable Through Traveling Waves and Integrated AI Systems Using Enhanced CNN and LSTM Model
authors: V Puvikko Chezhian, K Uma Maheswari, Dr Ajai Venkatesh, R Divya Dharsan, Kc Guruprasath, S Saran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Identifying early-stage cable defects aims to pinpoint local faults by analyzing high-frequency signals, detecting remote faults and specifying their exact locations along underground cables for more efficient maintenance operations. The paper proposes a method for fault detection to the first stage and the identification of the location of those faults in underground cables by combining signal processing with deep learning techniques. it obtains the voltage and current transients from the distribution cables and applies Continuous and Discrete Wavelet Transforms (DWT) to fetch the features of very high resolution which are the early fault behavior. Multi-resolution analysis helps to separate the traveling waves thus the estimation of the fault location becomes more accurate. A Clarke transformation is used to reduce the interference, and adaptive thresholds are used to distinguish faults from normal load variations. The pre-processed time windows are then input to a hybrid Convocational Neural Network (CNN)- Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM) model, where CNN layers learn spatial-temporal signatures and LSTM layers track fault progression.

title: A Machine Learning Approach for Crop Recommendation Using Soil Nutrients and Climate Features
authors: Omm Prakash Senapaty, Rajalaxmi Padhy, Sanjit Kumar Dash
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: In many countries where agriculture is a primary livelihood, farming is central to ensuring food availability and supporting economic growth. Traditionally, decisions about crop selection have been based largely on farmers' intuition and past experience. Such approaches often overlook critical parameters, including soil fertility, climatic conditions, and rainfall patterns, which strongly influence agricultural productivity. The present research presents a machine learning based crop recommendation system with the use of algorithms like Random Forest, XGBoost, and SVM (Support Vector Machine) with the goal to enhance this procedure. These models aid in the study of huge agricultural data sets and recommend the most advantageous crops for specific conditions. These mathematical models significantly lower incorrect classification rates and boost prediction efficiency when paired with successful pre processing and feature extraction methods. With a small amount of variations, the algorithm was able to accurately forecast every class in relation to its actual group distributions. With an accuracy of 99.32%, Random Forest outperformed each of the other two, followed by XGBoost at 98.60% and SVM at 96.82%. Having better decisions about what to grow is made easier by this research, This leads to more environmentally friendly farming, better utilization of resources, and greater crop yields. It displays how technology can improve agriculture's abilities, output, and capacity for future challenges.

title: Mapping Marine Biodiversity Vulnerability for Offshore Wind Development: Insights from Pantelleria Island
authors: Giovanna Cilluffo, Gianluca Sottile, Laura Ciriminna, Geraldina Signa, Agostino Tomasello, Salvatrice Vizzini
abstract: Offshore wind energy is rapidly emerging as a cornerstone of the global transition to renewable power. Yet, its deployment in marine environments demands careful ecological scrutiny to avoid unintended biodiversity impacts. This study presents a spatially explicit vulnerability assessment to support offshore wind farm (OWF) planning around Pantelleria Island, a pilot site in the central Mediterranean. We selected seven marine species of high conservation concern, based on the Marine Strategy Framework Directive (2008/56/EC), and compiled occurrence records from the Global Biodiversity Information Facility. Using nine environmental predictors, we applied four Ecological Niche Modeling algorithms—Generalized Linear Models, Random Forest, MaxEnt, and Support Vector Machines—to generate habitat suitability maps. These were integrated into a composite vulnerability map to highlight ecologically sensitive areas. Model performance was robust (AUC: 0.86–0.99), and the resulting maps offer actionable insights for minimizing biodiversity risks in OWF siting. Our approach demonstrates the value of predictive modeling in reconciling renewable energy development with marine conservation goals.

title: A Solar-Powered IoT-Based Outdoor Air Quality Monitoring System with Dual Connectivity and Data Quality Validation
authors: B. H. Sudantha, M. A. L. S. K. Manchanayaka, C. Premachandra, Chan-Yun Yang, D. M. U. Nethmina, W. M. P. G. B. C. Bandara
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a solar-powered, IoT-enabled outdoor air quality monitoring system capable of real-time measurement of key air pollutants and environmental parameters. The system integrates an ESP32 microcontroller with multiple sensors to monitor PM2.5, PM10, CO, CO2, temperature, humidity, and pressure. Dual communication using Wi-Fi and GSM ensures reliable operation in both urban and remote environments [1]. Field deployment results demonstrate stable operation, accurate measurements, and effective AQI computation.

title: Can Image Splicing and Copy-Move Forgery Be Detected by the Same Model? Forensim: An Attention-Based State-Space Approach
authors: Soumyaroop Nandi, Prem Natarajan
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We introduce Forensim, an attention-based state-space framework for image forgery detection that jointly local-izes both manipulated (target) and source regions. Unlike traditional approaches that rely solely on artifact cues to detect spliced or forged areas, Forensim is designed to capture duplication patterns crucial for understanding context. In scenarios like protest imagery, detecting only the forged region—e.g., a duplicated act of violence inserted into a peaceful crowd—can mislead interpretation, highlighting the need for joint source-target localization. Forensim outputs three-class masks (pristine, source, target) and supports detection of both splicing and copy-move forgeries within a unified architecture. We propose a visual state-space model that leverages normalized attention maps to identify internal similarities, paired with a region-based block-attention module to distinguish manipulated regions. This design enables end-to-end training and precise localization. Forensim achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks. We also release CMFD Anything, a new dataset addressing limitations of existing copy-move forgery datasets. Project page and code.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Approaches for Automated Detection of AI-Generated Text in Academic Content
authors: Nayanika Sinha, Sreyas Saravindu, Tanmoy Maitra, Debasis Giri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: This study looks at different machine learning methods to spot academic papers written by AI. We tested TFIDF using BERT, and a hybrid model that combines publication metadata (H-Score) and BERT. Based on 2,000 text samples, we find that deep learning models that is BERT and hybrid provide a significant improvement in accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(87 \%$</tex> and 94 % respectively), while lightweight model achieves only 78 % accuracy. The investigation finds that the linguistic patterns can differentiate AI-generated content and also can suggest a tiered AI-detection framework for educational settings. Moreover, the proposed hybrid model achieves a balance between computation and accuracy while maintaining the crucial human oversight.

title: AI Powered Smart Farming Ecosystem
authors: Krishnav Mahanta, Shaik Shahid Hussain, G Abinaya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: A platform was created, which utilizes AI to assist small farmers in expanding their growth during farming. It operates on real-time data of the IoT devices, has machine learning models to anticipate crop diseases and weather, and even financial planning. The primary need here is to provide farmers with good data-driven advice to make good decisions and manage their resources. This is based on a Random Forest machine learning model. It takes into account several elements such as soil, weather among other patterns of the environment to suggest the most suitable crops at a particular time of the year. Transfer learning models were also trained on Google Vertex AI Studio with a large body of categorized images of farmland and surroundings data. In this manner, the proposed platform is able to be aware of the initial signs of trouble which is quite impressive. Weather forecasting has also been implemented with the help of Weathercast API. It implies that farmers will receive alerts that may actually make them make the most of the irrigation and protect their crops. Coming to the financial aspect of this, the platform analyzes previous yield and market data to provide ROI estimates on alternative crop selections. This has provided the farmers with a better idea of their possible profit levels. There has been an excellent response due to either better disease control, smarter crop choice and better financial planning. This is all translated into the presence of high yields and higher profits to the small farmers. Anyway, it is not one smooth sailing. Acquiring quality data of sensors in those large remote locations may be challenging and unreliable connections may just inconvenience the whole process. Nevertheless, in general, this platform is an enormous support to the smallholder agriculture.

title: Shape-Constrained Segmentation of Anatomical Structures Using Graph-Based Deep Learning in Low-Contrast CT Images
authors: Rajib Guha Thakurta, Anitha Christy Angelin. P, Mukesh Soni, Durgesh Nandan, Rajit Nair, M. W. P. Maduranga
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: Weak boundaries, intensity ambiguity, and structural noise make accurate segmentation of anatomical structures in low-contrast CT images challenging. This paper describes a shape-prior-constrained, graph-based deep learning framework that utilizes a unique combination of convolutional feature extraction, graph neural network (GNN) relational reasoning, and spectral graph filtering with statistical shape anatomical templates. This framework is proposed to tackle a variety of segmentation conditions and achieve anatomically-prior-consistent segmentation. We consider CT volumes as weighted graphs, which allows us to perform feature propagation by neighborhood and model long-range dependencies. We encapsulate shape priors from eigen-shape representation in energy-based optimization to promote the anatomical plausibility of a segmentation while preserving variability across different patients. Anisotropic spectral filtering on the graph Laplacian is used to enhance the coherence of boundaries and to attenuate high-frequency noise, especially in low-contrast areas. With the introduction of viable alternatives to U-Net, Attention U-Net, nnU-Net, Swin-UNet, and GNN-only models, the proposed method, through thorough and extensive experimentation, has continued to show consistent success. Achieving a Dice similarity coefficient of 0.825, Jaccard index of 0.702, and boundary F1-score of 0.768, the method outperformed the previous best baseline (nnU-Net) by about 0.8-1.4% on most of the metrics. The average symmetric surface distance (ASSD) and 95% Hausdorff distance (HD) also reflect surface accuracy, which was previously (and consistently) underreported, at 1.62 mm and 8.9 mm respectively. Its robustness is demonstrated through a volumetric overlap error of 0.298 with corroborating true positive and true negative rate values of 0.851 and 0.932 respectively. A sensitivity score of 0.851 reinforces the explanation of this method showing the highest clinical acceptability score of 3.9 The attributed boundary realism, shape plausibility, and radiologist confidence, reflect increasing clinical acceptability attributed to even limited soft signage. These results confirm the highly clinically relavent segmentation combined the explicit shape constraints and graph based learning for low-contrast CT imaging.

title: Intelligent Monitoring System of Staircase Using Computer Vision
authors: Priyanka Vyas, Rutuja Gopale, Hrishikesh Bawale, Anjali Yadav, Ashutosh Ubhe
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Human safety is the biggest concern of a safe environment for young adults, middle-aged adults, and older people. An intelligent monitoring system that understands motion patterns and distraction behaviors, such as mobile phone usage, is required in today's environment this paper presents a ComputerVision based approach, which includes two things at a time: human motion detection and mobile phone usage recognition using the YOLOv8 architecture. The proposed system is real-time based on the detection capabilities with the simultaneous identification of human presence, posture, and handheld phone activity. A comparative evaluation with the existing detection frameworks, YOLOv8 reveals better results in 92.1 dependable solution for smart surveillance and public safety applications, is further equipped with. The proposed model, by concentrating on staircases, which are a significant cause of distracted movement accidents, helps raise people's awareness of the situation and prevents them from falling.

title: Hybrid Attention-Driven and Explainable AI for Multi-Label Retinal Disease Diagnosis
authors: Anusha P K, Nayanaa Ajith, Loveline Zeema J, Jayapriya J
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The retinal fundus imaging is an important factor in early diagnosis of ophthalmic disease where early diagnosis is vital in avoiding the loss of vision inherent in common and rare diseases. Despite the high potential of deep learning in this area, the majority of existing models are not multi-disease predictors, do not predict rare diseases because of class imbalance, and are not interpretable, which decreases clinical acceptance and use. To deal with these issues, this study suggests a two-level, explainable, multi-label diagnostic system over the Retinal Fundus Multi-Disease (RFMiD) data. In Tier One, disease detection is conducted at the high-level with methods of class imbalance mitigation to maximize the recognition of the rare diseases. In Tier Two, fine classifications carried out to identify several ocular pathologies that take place simultaneously in a single image. The framework uses a hybrid architecture, which involves EfficientNetB0, attention mechanism to acquire local features and global dependencies. Explainable techniques like Grad CAM++ and Integrated Gradients produce valuable clinically interpretable visualizations of model prediction. Macro and micro ROC-AUC, per class accuracy and F1-scores are used to evaluate performance with special attention paid to rare disease detection. This will address the problems of multi-label classification, imbalance of classes, and interpretability that a powerful, clinically certain AI system may be delivered that are capable of improving real-life retinal screening and augmenting AI-based healthcare clinician confidence.

title: Smart Pollination System
authors: Amrutha Y N, Priyanka S, Chandana T S, Honnu Shree L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: This paper describes a Smart Electrostatic Robotic Pollination system designed for vertical farming environments where natural pollinators are not present. The system uses a Pi Camera and environmental sensors, including a DHT22 for temperature and humidity and an LDR sensor for light intensity, to capture real-time data about plants and their surroundings. A Raspberry Pi acts as the main processing unit, running a machine learning model based on convolutional neural networks (CNN) to detect the presence of tomato flowers and analyze their flowering stages for the best pollination timing. The decision engine uses both sensor and image data to automatically determine when the flowers are ready to be pollinated. In suitable conditions, the actuation module, which includes a servo motor and an electrostatic robotic arm, delivers pollen to flowers with great accuracy. All operational data, such as tier status, flower stage, environmental readings, and pollen charge level, are sent to an IOT cloud and displayed on a web dashboard for remote monitoring and manual control. This system provides an efficient, sustainable, and scalable way to automate pollination in indoor farms, increasing fruit yield and resource use while lessening the need for manual labor.

title: Hybrid Solar-Wind Energy with Automatic Load Sharing and Priority Switching
authors: Adithiyan S, Ebinesh Joie E J, Navayuugan N, Suresh. K. P
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In rural and remote regions, power supply has remained a big challenge; in terms of the continuity and reliability. Solar and wind energy with battery storage is a feasible and sustainable solution that can be used as hybrid renewable energy systems. This paper will develop a hybrid solar-wind system that incorporates an intelligent control strategy to make certain that the power delivery is stable even with the variable weather conditions. Automatic Load Sharing and Priority Switching are a major characteristic of the system with the available energy being allocated among loads automatically with regard to the real-time supply and demand. The system favors all the time the most economic and plentiful source and in this order that is solar, wind, and lastly the battery, which means that there is an efficient use of energy and a continuity of power during the fluctuations. It has been designed with MPPT (Maximum Power Point Tracking) of both the solar and wind units to draw maximum power out of any given moment. A fuzzy logic controller makes smart decisions in times of rapid weather changes and prevents the battery over- and undercharging as well as over and under discharging. The system behaviour is modelled, simulated, and tested with the help of MATLAB/Simulink. All in all, the system is supposed to provide a low cost, dependable and green power system to homes, small industry and agricultural use.

title: SCAdapter: Content-Style Disentanglement for Diffusion Style Transfer
authors: Luan Thanh Trinh
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Diffusion models have emerged as the leading approach for style transfer, yet they struggle with photo-realistic transfers, often producing painting-like results or missing detailed stylistic elements. Current methods inadequately address unwanted influence from original content styles and style reference content features. We introduce SCAdapter, a novel technique leveraging CLIP image space to effectively separate and integrate content and style features. Our key innovation systematically extracts pure content from content images and style elements from style references, ensuring authentic transfers. This approach is enhanced through three components: Controllable Style Adaptive Instance Normalization (CSAdaIN) for precise multi-style blending, KVS Injection for targeted style integration, and a style transfer consistency objective maintaining process coherence. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate SCAdapter significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both conventional and diffusion-based baselines. By eliminating DDIM inversion and inference-stage optimization, our method achieves at least 2× faster inference than other diffusion-based approaches, making it both more effective and efficient for practical applications.

title: Advanced Mobility Management In 5G Networks using a Multi-Layered Convolutional LSTM for Proactive Handover Optimization
authors: Leela Priya Inturu, P M Benson Mansingh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Effective mobility management in 5G networks is essential for ensuring uninterrupted connectivity and efficient cell transitions, particularly as user mobility, device density, and service diversity increase. Traditional mobility management solutions face limitations in adapting to the complex, dynamic mobility patterns and scalability demands of 5G environments. This study introduces a multi-layered Convolutional LSTM model to enhance mobility management by accurately predicting future user locations and mobility trends. By leveraging Convolutional LSTMs, which combine convolutional layers with LSTM units, the model captures spatial and temporal dependencies from user mobility data, enabling predictive handover decisions and optimized resource allocation. Unlike conventional threshold-based or LSTM-only approaches, in this paper, ConvLSTM model combines both spatial dependencies (e.g., cell-to-cell correlation, geographical trajectories) and temporal dependencies (e.g., movement sequences over time). The process begins with collecting mobility datasets containing location coordinates, signal strength values, and timestamps, followed by preprocessing through normalization and sequence construction. The processed data is fed into the ConvLSTM network, where convolutional layers extract spatial mobility patterns while stacked LSTM layers learn temporal transitions. The trained model predicts the next probable user location (cell ID or coordinates) and mobility trend. These predictions are then used to enable proactive handover management by reserving resources in the predicted target cell, minimizing latency, and avoiding ping-pong effects. Finally, the model output is combined into the 5G mobility management framework to provide real-time handover decisions and resource allocation, ensuring seamless connectivity and enhanced user experience.

title: Rug Pulls in Blockchain-Based NFT Markets: Detection Techniques and Risk Mitigation Strategies
authors: Sridevi S, Riyazulla Rahman J, Jobin Thomas, Komalavalli C, Swati Sharma, Gnanaprakash V
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The rapidly evolving NFT (Non-Fungible Tokens) ecosystem has brought about a wealth of opportunities for investors and creators alike. However, the allure of this burgeoning market has also attracted a host of malicious actors, who have exploited vulnerabilities to perpetrate rug pull incidents, a form of exit scam where project developers abruptly abandon their project, absconding with investors' funds. To safeguard oneself against these pernicious schemes, it is crucial to understand the mechanisms underlying rug pulls, as well as the strategies for detecting and avoiding them. In this paper, we discuss the anatomy of an NFT rug-pull and a detailed investigation and study on how these rug-pulls are executed. In this study, we have made an attempt to investigate the common signs along with the cautionary steps to identify and avoid them. Finally, we have also designed and proposed an effective mitigation strategy for a rug pull with deterministic mathematical modelling.

title: Deep Learning-Based Depression Detection: Insights from Twitter data through Hashtag Text Analysis
authors: R Usha, N. Balakrishna, S Jahnavi, P Sai Kalyan, K Bhavitha Reddy, K Madhivadhani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Depression has emerged as a significant worldwide issue for mental well-being, and innovative strategies are required for early detection and intervention. To address this critical challenge, this project seeks to utilize the vast amounts of Twitter data by developing a robust deep learning model aimed at identifying the initial signs of depression. By training on a dataset specifically focused on depression, The suggested system is painstakingly made to identify the minute differences in evidence of a mental health condition extracted from individuals’ online posts. A key innovation of this research lies in the development of tailored word embeddings tailored for a depression-focused corpus, improving the model's capacity to identify subtle linguistic patterns that may indicate a tendency toward depression. Project also integrates a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with a Message level Sentiment Analysis framework to bolster feature extraction and enhance model performance. Experimental findings indicate that CNN–BILSTM with Attention outperforms other models in nearly all evaluation measures for the several classifier architectures under consideration include accuracy, recall, precision, and F1 macro scores. This innovative approach underscores the potential of synergistically employing advanced NLP techniques, including sentiment analysis and attention mechanisms, for more accurate and rapid mental health diagnostics. As a result, this work marks a substantial breakthrough in automated depression identification using deep learning and paves the way for future enhancements in deep learning to support mental health research and potential interventions.

title: Deep learning based skin cancer classification: A comprehensive evaluation
authors: A. Chandra Sekhar, D. Venkatesh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Deep learning techniques, especially the Convolution neural networks have transformedthe field of skin cancer diagnosis, offering automated and highly accurate methods for lesion classification. CNNs operate by progressively extracting hierarchical features from raw pixel data, with early layers detecting basic structures like edges and lines, and deeper layers capturing complex patterns such as textures and object parts. These networks are capable of processing large volumes of dermoscopic images, with CNN-based models consistently achieving high accuracy, often surpassing human-level performance. However, challenges persist, such as the limited diversity of current datasets, which primarily focus on melanocytic lesions, and the need for improved model interpretability. The paper discusses the integration of these advanced models into clinical practice, highlighting their potential to assist dermatologists in providing quicker and more reliable diagnoses, thus improving early detection and patient outcomes. Despite the advancements, further research is needed to address data imbalance, enhance model generalization, and ensure the integration of these models into clinical workflows.

title: A Vision-Guided, IoT-Integrated Pill Dispensing System for Intelligent Medication Adherence
authors: Kailash Shaw, Shreyash Chilip, Kushal Chaudhari, Sahil Jadhav, Shubham Gaikwad, Shiv Wagh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Failure to take medications as prescribed is a major problem in the management of chronic disease, leading to avoidable complications and generally increasing costs of care worldwide. This project proposes a new Enhanced Intelligent Pill Dispenser backed with embedded hardware, computer vision, and IoT Services that recognize geometry, dispense automatically, and monitor adherence in real-time. A precise vision pipeline for pill dimensions is set up based on a pixel-to-centimetre mapping of a known reference. This allows for compartment and dispensing enabled by geometry selection. The system can be designed with modules, friendly for geriatrics, clinically extensible to improve safety, and reduce the routine workload for caregivers.

title: Electrical Reliability Analysis of TSVs with Structural Defects: Spherical Voids vs. Pinhole Imperfections
authors: Deepali Gupta, Chanchal Ijardar, Baljit Kaur, Manoj Kumar Majumder, Dhandapani Vaithiyanathan
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: In recent years, Through-Silicon Via (TSV) based three-dimensional integrated circuits (3D ICs) have evolved as a promising solution to meet the growing demand for electronic device requiring higher bandwidth, reduced power consumption, and compact form factors. Despite their advantages, the intricate manufacturing processes and stress-induced effects make TSVs highly susceptible to defects such as voids, misalignment, cracks, and electrical failures factors that critically affect reliability and yield of 3D ICs. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of TSVs under two common defect scenarios: spherical voids and pinhole defects. A Carbon Nanotube FET (CNTFET) based Coupled Driver-Via-Load (DVL) setup is employed at the 7 nm technology node to evaluate performance. A distributed electrical model is developed to capture the defect conditions, and key performance metrics propagation delay, crosstalk delay, power dissipation, and peak noise are examined. The study reveals that pinhole defects have a more severe impact on TSV performance, with propagation delay, power dissipation, and peak noise worsening by $6.57 \%, 7.08 \%$, and $6.84 \%$, respectively, compared to spherical void defects. These findings underscore the critical influence of defect type on TSV signal integrity and reliability, offering insights for designing robust 3D ICs.

title: Video and Language Alignment in 2D Systems for 3D Multi-object Scenes with Multi-Information Derivative-Free Control
authors: Jason Armitage, Rico Sennrich
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Cross-modal systems trained on 2D visual inputs are presented with a dimensional shift when processing 3D scenes. An in-scene camera bridges the dimensionality gap but requires learning a control module. We introduce a new method that improves multivariate mutual information estimates by regret minimisation with derivative-free optimisation. Our algorithm enables off-the-shelf cross-modal systems trained on 2D visual inputs to adapt online to object occlusions and differentiate features. The pairing of expressive measures and value-based optimisation assists control of an in-scene camera to learn directly from the noisy outputs of vision-language models. The resulting pipeline improves performance in cross-modal tasks on multi-object 3D scenes without resorting to pretraining or finetuning.
